courses.name: Contemporary Native American Writers
courses.description: A study of the fiction and poetry of Native American writers covering the period from 1945 to the present. Selections may include works by Sherman Alexie, Joseph Bruchac, Louise Erdrich, Joy Harjo, N. Scott Momaday, and Leslie Marmon Silko, as well as other contemporary Native American writers.

courses.name: American Gothic
courses.purpose: This course examines the American Gothic as a literary and cultural mode shaped by historical anxieties in the United States. It aims to help students understand how Gothic texts engage fear, violence, race, gender, science, religion, and social change.
courses.description: This course explores Gothic short stories, novels, poems, and films by American authors. It connects Gothic tropes such as hidden corpses, disembodied voices, and labyrinthine spaceships to historical developments in science and religion, as well as ideologies of race and gender. The reading list may include works by Charles Brockden Brown, Edgar Allan Poe, Nathaniel Hawthorne, Charlotte Perkins Gilman, Flannery O'Connor, Henry James, and H.P. Lovecraft. Films may include Alien, Event Horizon, or Rosemary's Baby.
learning_outcomes: able to identify Gothic conventions and comprehend the historical developments that gave rise to the genre
able to recognize the gendered and racial tensions and problems that underlie Gothic texts
able to produce prose analyzing those issues.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: What is the Gothic?
Hafta 2: Colonial and Early Republican Gothic
Hafta 3: Dark Romantic Gothic (Poe)
Hafta 4: Dark Romantic Gothic (Hawthorne)
Hafta 5: Dark Romantic Gothic (Melville)
Hafta 6: Gothic and the Civil War (Bierce)
Hafta 7: Realism and the Female Gothic (Freeman/Gilman)
Hafta 8: The Gothic and Race (Chesnutt)
Hafta 9: The Gothic and “Weird Fiction” (Lovecraft)
Hafta 10: Southern Gothic and Modernism (Faulkner)
Hafta 11: Southern Gothic and Modernism (O'Connor)
Hafta 12: Cold War Domestic Gothic (Jackson/Plath)
Hafta 13: Horror and Gothic Science Fiction (Butler)
Hafta 14: Dystopian Gothic (Mieville)
resources: All readings posted on Moodle

courses.name: Hemispheric American Studies
courses.purpose: A hemispheric approach to American Studies aims to examine North, Central, and South America as an interconnected whole rather than as separate national units. The course is designed to expose students to a wide range of literary texts, films, and public policy materials from across the Americas and to provide a theoretical framework for understanding the United States in relation to its neighbors.
courses.description: The course covers literary texts, films, and public policy/legislative materials from various countries in the Americas. Readings include U.S. legal documents such as HB 56 and SB 1070 alongside authors such as Jorge Luis Borges, Richard Rodriguez, Junot Diaz, Adolfo Caminha, Sandra Cisneros, Esmeralda Santiago, Pablo Neruda, Gabriel Garcia Marquez, and Isabel Allende. The course also addresses the major languages of the Americas, texts in translation, films in their original languages with English subtitles, and a dedicated section on Border Studies in the U.S. and beyond.

courses.name: Theories of American Culture: An Ethnographic Approach
courses.purpose: This course uses anthropology and ethnography to examine how "American culture" is understood and represented through everyday life in the United States. It aims to move beyond a single definition and instead help students analyze the diverse social, political, and economic forces that shape different forms of life across America.
courses.description: The course draws on readings in anthropology and ethnography to explore questions about American culture through accounts of everyday life in recent American history. It focuses on how racial violence, militarism, environmental injustice, socioeconomic marginalization, gendered violence, neoliberal labor regimes, addiction, and empire shape life in different social and economic spaces of the US.

courses.name: Freedom and Philosophy in Anglo-America
courses.purpose: This course examines the origins and meanings of freedom and liberty in Anglo-American thought and culture. It asks how these concepts developed and how they shaped political culture in early modern Europe and America.
courses.description: This course explores different theories of freedom and liberty in the early modern era, from the European Renaissance to the American Revolution. It links the development and growth of American political culture to major social and intellectual movements such as the Protestant Reformation, the English Civil Wars, the Scientific Revolution, and the Enlightenment.

courses.name: Gender Studies in American Culture
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the critical role that gender has played in the structure of American society and to the ways gender theory has developed in recent years.
courses.description: This course offers an introduction to the critical role that gender has played in the structure of American society. It examines theories of gender and society as they have evolved in recent years. Students consider how feminism and other gender-sensitive critical practices help us to understand problems in U.S. society.
learning_outcomes: Students will demonstrate advanced efficiency in analyzing gender as a social construct in American society.
Students will evaluate the strengths and weaknesses of research project in critical gender identity issues.
Students will analyze the processes and patterns of gender oppression and resistance in the contemporary gender relations of USA.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Gender Studies. Reading: David Graeber & David Wengrow on schizmogenesis; start readings for next week.
Hafta 2: Gender from a Pre-Historical Perspective. Reading: Graeber & Wengrow, selection from "Gardens of Adonis"
Hafta 3: Richard Reeves on Boys and Men and their condition today.
Hafta 4: Reeves continued
Hafta 5: Women and Gender from an Anthropological Perspective. Reading: Gayle Rubin, "The Traffic in Women"
Hafta 6: "Deconstructing" Gender. Reading: Nancy Chodorow and Judith Butler
Hafta 7: Sex and Gender Today. Reading: Amia Srinivasan, "The Right to Sex"
Hafta 8: Midterm exam: March 24th. Reading: Meridel Le Sueur, "I Was Walking."
Hafta 9: Feminist Issues Today. Reading: Amia Srinivasan, "Sex, Carceralism, and Capitalism"; Audre Lorde, "The Master's Tools Will Never Dismantle the Master's House"
Hafta 10: The New Man. Eva Illouz, "A New Emotional Stratification."
Hafta 11: The Plight of Men today. Reading: Susan Faludi, selection from STIFFED.
Hafta 12: Faludi reading continued.
Hafta 13: Gender, secularism, and religion. Reading: Joan Wallach Scott, SEX AND SECULARISM.
Hafta 14: Scott reading continued.
resources: Readings available on Moodle

courses.name: Architectural Building Systems
courses.purpose: To introduce students to construction materials, their properties, production, and construction methods, and to develop an understanding of how these systems are applied in architectural design.
courses.description: Introduction to construction materials, their properties, production and construction methods, application and use in architecture; integration of design and building technology; building sustainability; development of a set of detail drawings.

courses.name: Statics and Strength of Materials
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamental concepts and principles of statics and strength of materials, with emphasis on their application in architectural design and building behavior.
courses.description: Introduction to the basic concepts and principles of statics; vector mechanics; geometrical properties; free body diagrams; internal forces: shear and moment diagrams. Analysis of simple load-carrying structures; columns; concepts of stress, strain and deformation.
learning_outcomes: Recognize, define and distinguish the basic terminologies of statics and their applications in building design
Statically analyze simple column/beam systems under the given loads and interpret the building behavior with respect to these analyses
Draw shear-moment diagram for simple structural systems and use these diagrams in the related architectural design
Design a small-scale structural project by calculating the stresses generated under the relevant loading conditions, then construct a model structure to test the behavior.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction / Force / Stress
Hafta 2: Loads / Equilibrium / FBD
Hafta 3: Retaining Walls
Hafta 4: Trusses
Hafta 5: Cables
Hafta 6: Arches / Midterm
Hafta 7: Strength of Materials
Hafta 8: Center of Gravity & Centroid
Hafta 9: Moment of Inertia
Hafta 10: Shear and Bending Diagrams
Hafta 11: Bending Stresses
Hafta 12: Shear Stresses
Hafta 13: Revision / Project Evaluation
Hafta 14: Final Evaluations
resources: Statics and Strength of Materials For Architecture and Building Construction, Onouye B. and Kane K., 2013, Prentice Hall
The Structural Basis of Architecture, Sandaker B.N. , Eggen A.P. , Cruvellier M.R., 2011, Routledge
Structures, Schodek D.L., 2008, Prentice Hall

courses.name: Creative Writing Workshop
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' creative English writing voice through reading, writing, sharing, and critical conversation. It also introduces students to the diversity of creative expression in English and helps them build confidence in their own writing.
courses.description: This is a workshop-based seminar that includes writing exercises and readings in poetry, short fiction, creative nonfiction/personal essay, and cross-genre writing. The course also covers how to create a writing portfolio, independent, small-press, and self-publishing, how to submit/query for publication, and how to identify publications and presses that are a good fit for a writer's work.
learning_outcomes: Develop a creative English writing voice
Explore different forms of creative expression in English
Produce writing through workshop-based exercises and readings
Understand how to build a writing portfolio
Learn basic strategies for publication, querying, and identifying suitable presses/publications

courses.name: Computerized Presentation Techniques
courses.purpose: To develop advanced skills in using computerized programs for the representation and presentation of architectural projects. The course aims to integrate traditional and digital media in order to improve visual communication of design ideas.
courses.description: Advanced usage of computerized programs for representation of projects by integrating traditional and computer media.

courses.name: Parametric Design Studio
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to parametric design approaches and the use of building information systems (BIM) in architectural design. It also develops the ability to use related software packages efficiently.
courses.description: Different parameters of design are examined; skills are acquired in establishing building information systems (BIMs) and using them efficiently through the utilization of related software packages.

courses.name: Architecture and Society
courses.purpose: To investigate the societal and environmental aspects and determinants that shape architectural design.
courses.description: Investigation of societal and environmental aspects and determinants of architectural design; analysis of case studies.

courses.name: Form and Structure
courses.purpose: Provides an understanding of the behavior of structures in relation to their form.
courses.description: Studies spatial structures such as shells, domes, cable-nets and fabrics using computer programs, where the relationship between the geometrical form and the structural behavior is observed and explained through structural simulations.

courses.name: Architectural Lighting and Acoustics
courses.purpose: To introduce students to the fundamental principles of daylighting and artificial lighting in buildings, as well as the basics of architectural acoustics. The course also aims to familiarize students with fire prevention, life-safety systems, and their architectural applications.
courses.description: Study of fundamental daylight and artificial lighting principles; building lighting performance; lighting design methods. Introduction to concepts of architectural acoustics; sound behavior in buildings; noise control; fundamental design principles and methods. Fire prevention in buildings; life-safety systems and architectural applications; standards and regulations.

courses.name: Environmental Technology
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to environmental technology in architectural design, with emphasis on how building systems support comfort, performance, and sustainability. It aims to help students understand and evaluate the integration of mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems within buildings.
courses.description: Introduction to contemporary mechanical systems and equipment for heating, cooling, ventilation and plumbing. Consideration of environmental sustainability. Integration of water supply and heating systems into the building design; waste water disposal; electrical systems, wiring. Evaluation of building service systems performance; technical drawings and documentation of the proposed designs.
learning_outcomes: Analyze, evaluate, and assess building enviroments
Integration of passive and mechanical system design
Integration of safety, construction, efficiency of environmental system
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, Passive systems, Sustainability & Site Layout Design Strategies
Hafta 2: Passive Environmental Systems, Natural & Artificial Light in Architecture
Hafta 3: Lighting Design
Hafta 4: Electrical Services; Electrical systems; supply, generation, distribution, Electrical plans
Hafta 5: Solar Shading; Solar shading and screening; glazing systems
Hafta 6: Passive heating & cooling methods
Hafta 7: HVAC Systems and Air Quality
Hafta 8: Air Distribution & Air Quality; Ducting and ceiling systems; Ventilation systems; Indoor Air Quality (IAQ)
Hafta 9: Water Management
Hafta 10: Fire Safety & Planning
Hafta 11: Accessibility
Hafta 12: Waste Management
Hafta 13: Critics
Hafta 14: Presentations
resources: Sun, Wind & Light: Architectural Design Strategies, G. Z. Brown, Mark DeKay, 3rd ed. 2014, John Wiley & Sons.
Mechanical and Electrical Equipment for buildings, Stein, Benjamin and Reynolds, John S., 8th Ed., 1992, John Wiley & Sons Inc.
Architectural Interior Systems: Lighting, Acoustics, Airconditioning, Flynn, Kremers, Arthur, Gary, 3rd Ed., 1992, Van Nostrand Reinhold
Architectural Design: Integration of structural and environmental systems, Bovil, Carl, 1991, Van Nostrand Reinhold
Architectural Lighting (2nd Edition), M. David Egan, McGraw Hill, Paperback, 2002
The Architecture of Light. A Textbook of Procedures and Practices for the Architect, Sage Russell, Publisher Conceptnine, 2012

courses.name: Structural Design II
courses.purpose: This course aims to build students' understanding of reinforced concrete structural behavior and design principles. It also introduces advanced structural systems and analysis methods used in architectural practice.
courses.description: Introduction to concrete, design and analysis of reinforced concrete members. Exploring pre-stressed and post tension techniques. Case studies of historic and contemporary examples. Earthquake: Importance and role in structural design. Introduction to finite element modeling (FEM) and computer analysis.
learning_outcomes: Define and describe the properties of reinforced concrete.
Analyze and design simple reinforced concrete systems.
Define and analyze the structural behavior of post-tensioned and pre-tensioned structural systems.
Distinguish the importance of earthquake design and the application in structural design.
Design and analyze a small-scale RC beam and then construct a model structure to test the behavior.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Reinforced Concrete
Hafta 2: Introduction to Reinforced Concrete
Hafta 3: Reinforced Concrete Axial Members
Hafta 4: Reinforced Concrete Axial Members
Hafta 5: Reinforced Concrete Beams: Flexure
Hafta 6: Reinforced Concrete Beams: Flexure
Hafta 7: Reinforced Concrete Beams: Shear
Hafta 8: Reinforced Concrete Beams: Shear
Hafta 9: Reinforced Concrete Columns under Axial load & Bending
Hafta 10: Reinforced Concrete Columns under Axial load & Bending
Hafta 11: Pre-stressed Concrete Concepts: Pre-tensioning and Post-tensioning
Hafta 12: Earthquake Resistant Design Principles
Hafta 13: Revision
Hafta 14: Jury Week

courses.name: Urban Metabolism: tools from the social sciences for architects and designers
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip architecture and design students with social-science-based tools for understanding cities as dynamic and contested spaces. It helps students analyze urban change, power relations, and the transformation of resources into the built environment.
courses.description: City as a dynamic, contested space, marked by reconfiguration and change. Literatures of actor-network theory, urban political ecology, urban regime theory; links between transformation of resources into built environment, with a focus on housing, and accompanying flows (people, resources, waste, power); dynamics of socio-political power and domination in cities.

courses.name: Advanced Visualization Studio
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' ability to produce advanced computer-aided visualizations for design-related disciplines. It also introduces the use of virtual environments, internet-based platforms, and software packages as tools for communicating and presenting design ideas.
courses.description: Advanced techniques in computer aided visualization. Virtual environments, internet based platforms and software packages for design related disciplines are utilized for creating design visualizations.

courses.name: Conservation of Historical Environments
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the principles and techniques of conservation and restoration. It aims to develop an understanding of the problems and developments related to conservation, especially in Turkey.
courses.description: General introduction to the principles and techniques of conservation and restoration; focuses on the problems and developments related to conservation in Turkey; covers analysis of restored historic sites and buildings.
learning_outcomes: Identify and evaluate the problematic issues in the protection of the historical environment and the critical sides of conservation policies
Demonstrate knowledge of past and contemporary issues in the theory and practice of restoration
Demonstrate skills of research and observation through analyses and criticism of different case studies
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Conservation of architectural heritage: An introduction to theory, principles and terminology.
Hafta 3: The Charters in a Larger Context and the Concept of Value
Hafta 4: Discussion: Venice Resolutions and Burra Charter
Hafta 5: International organizations and their role
Hafta 6: Heritage Management/ Heritage Interpretation; Assignment 1 Submission
Hafta 7: The concept of "layers" and Multi-layered Historical Towns
Hafta 8: Conservation in and for archaeological sites
Hafta 9: Review and Midterm Exam
Hafta 10: Architectural conservation in Turkey: A brief history
Hafta 11: Conservation in Turkey: Legal issues and administrative concerns
Hafta 12: Conservation and urban context from a sociopolitical perspective: Current issues and discussions
Hafta 13: A new life? Issues concerning reuse. Design in conjunction with historic environments: Approaches and discussions; Assignment 2 Submission
Hafta 14: Support, funding and conflicts: a discussion on economics and ethics; Research Paper submission

courses.name: Special Topics in Architectural Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop a design project with social content, encouraging students to engage with architectural details and collaborative construction processes.
courses.description: Creation of a design project with a social content. Focus on architectural details and collaborative construction.
learning_outcomes: Able to develop a design project in response to different contexts and utilizing different methods
Demonstrate knowledge in different design methods and methodologies
Able to recognize totality of a design work, while controlling different components of design individually
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and Studio Construction
Hafta 2: Introduction to Semiotics and Representation; Materialization of Signs and Symbols + Visual and Spatial Forms & Modes of Representation
Hafta 3: Relational Aesthetics; source-based & context-based & participatory production in the contemporary art and architecture + cross-disciplinary arts
Hafta 4: Introduction to Contemporary Art and Architecture; Parameters, Paradigms and Themes
Hafta 5: Archive and Archeology of Knowledge; Flexible Archives & Archival Constructions
Hafta 6: Mid-Term: Archival Construction Workshop
Hafta 7: Museum Objects: Understanding the roles of the museum and the value of the cultural objects within institutional frameworks
Hafta 8: Presentation Lab (Final Production until the end of the semester)
Hafta 9: Lab-work
Hafta 10: Presentation
Hafta 11: Lab-work
Hafta 12: Presentation
Hafta 13: Lab-work
Hafta 14: Final Review

courses.name: Medieval Architecture in the Eastern Mediterranean
courses.purpose: Presents and evaluates the Byzantine architectural heritage through a comparative approach. It also aims to situate this heritage within the broader architectural context of the Eastern Mediterranean and related medieval cultures.
courses.description: Investigates the architectural context of the Byzantine world that expanded throughout the Eastern Mediterranean and emerged as one of the prominent architectural layers of the Anatolian peninsula. The course also opens comparative windows into the architectural characteristics of related medieval cultures such as Western Medieval, Early Islamic, Crusader, Seljuk, and Beyliks.

courses.name: Modern Turkish Architecture
courses.purpose: This course aims to examine the development of architecture in Turkey from 1900 to the present within its socio-cultural and political context. It also seeks to help students understand the reciprocal relationship between architecture, political change, and urban development.
courses.description: Study of architectural developments in Turkey from 1900 to the present within a socio-cultural framework; particular emphasis on the interrelationship of architecture and political developments; survey of important buildings, key figures of architecture and urban design; extensive readings on the subject.

courses.name: Contemporary Architecture and Theory
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the major developments in Western architectural culture from the turn of the twentieth century to the present. It also seeks to develop students' ability to critically engage with contemporary architectural movements and theories.
courses.description: Review of the developments in Western architectural culture from the turn of the twentieth century to the present; contemporary architectural movements and theories.
learning_outcomes: Analyze contemporary movements and theories in Western architecture from the turn of the twentieth century to the present
Demonstrate advanced critical thinking and communication skills examining prevailing theories and debates in architecture
Evaluate current issues in contemporary architecture recognizing key theories, buildings and architects
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Aims and contents
Hafta 2: Lecture 01: Ciam and Team X
Hafta 3: Lecture 02: New Monumentality
Hafta 4: Lecture 03: Autonomy of Architecture
Hafta 5: Lecture 04: Post modern narratives
Hafta 6: Lecture 05: Critical Regionalism
Hafta 7: Lecture 06: Architecture of the Digital
Hafta 8: Midterm Exam
Hafta 9: In class discussion. Assignment 01
Hafta 10: Assignment 01 Review
Hafta 11: In class discussion. Assignment 02
Hafta 12: Assignment 02 Review 1
Hafta 13: Assignment 02 Review 2
Hafta 14: Final review
resources: A Critical History of Contemporary Architecture. 1960-2010, Haddad Elie and Rifkind David, 2014, Routledge
Modern Architecture, Alan Colquhoun, 2002, Oxford History of Art
Modern Architecture A Critical History, Kenneth Frampton, 1992, thames & hudson
Architecture and Modernity, Hilde Heynen, 1999, Cambridge: MIT Press

courses.name: Professional Practice
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the professional administration of architectural design projects and the construction process. It also develops awareness of architectural practice, financial planning, contracts, ethics, and the legal responsibilities of architects and clients.
courses.description: Professional administration of architectural design projects and the construction process; conduct of architectural practice; financial planning and contracts; professional ethics; rights and responsibilities of architects and clients; role of client and user; codes and legal aspects of building construction.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate understanding of professional administration of architectural design projects
Demonstrate understanding of rights and responsibilities of architects and clients
Able to develop professional contracts
Show commitment to professional ethics
Have knowledge about principles, laws, regulations and standards related to the field

Evaluate current knowledge in the field with a critical and dialectical approach; use existing knowledge, understanding and skills with a professional approach required by the discipline, in the light of ethical principles, professional codes of conduct, criteria, and standards by considering possible social, environmental and ethical consequences according to legislative frameworks
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to ARCH 418 / Seminar 01 / Roadmap / Seminar 02 / Pre-project stages & Project stages
Hafta 2: Discussion 01 _WHO & WHAT / Discussion 02 _HOW & WHEN
Hafta 3: Workshop 1 (1/3) / Guest Lecture 1
Hafta 4: Presentations ASS 1
Hafta 5: Workshop 1 (2/3 & 3/3)
Hafta 6: Debates
Hafta 7: Debates / Guest Lecture 2
Hafta 8: Guest Lecture 3 / MIDTERM EXAM
Hafta 9: NO CLASS
Hafta 10: GEUST LECTURE 4
Hafta 11: GUEST LECTURE 5
Hafta 12: Workshop 02 (1/2) / PORTFOLIO & CV CRITICS
Hafta 13: workshop 02 (2/2) / guest lecture 6
Hafta 14: critics for final project

courses.name: Architectural Theory and Criticism
courses.purpose: To introduce students to architectural history, theory, and criticism through focused topics and key theoretical texts. The course aims to develop critical awareness of the social, cultural, and political forces that shape architecture.
courses.description: Investigation of architectural history, theory and criticism on special topics; social, cultural and political influences on architecture; readings of important theoretical and critical writings about a broad range of topics.

courses.name: Analytical Chemistry I
courses.purpose: Fundamental principles and theories of analytical chemistry.
courses.description: Qualitative and quantitative analysis by gravimetric, volumetric and electrochemical methods.

courses.name: Organic Chemistry I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic principles of organic chemistry and builds a foundation for understanding the structure and behavior of organic compounds.
courses.description: A survey of the principal classes of organic compounds. Synthesis and characteristic reactions of various functional groups.

courses.name: Materials Science and Technology
courses.description: Selected characteristics of materials. Mechanical, thermal, electrical properties. Atomic structure and chemical bonding. Structure of solids. Crystals, noncrystals. Metals, semiconductors. Alloys, polymers, polymeric properties. Ceramics. Electromagnetic and mechanical behavior of ceramics. Multiphase equilibria. Mechanical and physical properties of multiphase materials. Thermal processing. Corrosion of metals.
learning_outcomes: By the end of this course, students are expected to be able to describe, compare and identify concepts of atomic structure, chemical bonding theories, and electronic structure of atoms.
By the end of this course, students are expected to be able to successfully name main classes of materials, compare and contrast their properties
By the end of this course, students are expected to be able to analyze the structure of materials, when a set of materials are given, predict and estimate their mechanical properties
By the end of this course, students are expected to be able to extend their basic knowledge on materials to unknown, unusual examples and solve out-of the box problems on materials

By the end of this course, students are expected to be able to recognize defects in materials, calculate diffusion in solids when the parameters are given and prepare phase diagrams from a given set of chemical information.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, Definition and Classification of Materials, Atomic structure, periodic table, molecular structure, bonding
Hafta 2: Introduction, Definition and Classification of Materials, Atomic structure, periodic table, molecular structure, bonding
Hafta 3: Structure of Crystalline Solids
Hafta 4: Structure of Crystalline Solids (II)
Hafta 5: Structure of Polymers
Hafta 6: Imperfections in Solids
Hafta 7: Diffusion in Solids
Hafta 8: Mechanical Properties of Metals I (Test methods; Stress-strain curves)
Hafta 9: Mechanical Properties of Metals II (Strength, ductility, toughness, resilience)
Hafta 10: Mechanical Properties of Ceramics and Polymers
Hafta 11: Dislocations and Strengthening Mechanisms
Hafta 12: Phase Diagrams I (basics)
Hafta 13: Phase Diagrams II (Iron-carbon)
Hafta 14: Review
resources: Materials Science and Engineering, W. D. Callister, Jr. and D. G. Rethwisch, 10th, Wiley
General Chemistry, R.H.Petrucci, F.G.Herring, J.D.Madura, C.Bissonette, 11th Ed, Pearson
Polymer Chemistry, Timothy P. Lodge (Author), Paul C. Hiemenz (Author), International Student Edition 3rd Edition, CRC

courses.name: Organic Chemistry II
courses.purpose: This course builds on the basic principles of organic chemistry and develops students’ ability to understand the major classes of organic compounds and their reactions.
courses.description: Basic principles of organic chemistry. A survey of the principal classes of organic compounds. Synthesis and characteristics reactions of various functional groups.
learning_outcomes: Able to apply knowledge of fundamental principles in organic chemistry to complex problems.
Able to apply NMR spectroscopy to organic chemistry problems.
Able to propose and draw reaction mechanisms for complex organic reactions.
Able to describe, compare and identify concepts in organic synthesis and functional group interconversion.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Nuclear Magnetic Resonance and Mass Spectrometry: Tools for Structure Determination-I
Hafta 2: Nuclear Magnetic Resonance and Mass Spectrometry: Tools for Structure Determination-II
Hafta 3: Radical Reactions-I
Hafta 4: Radical Reactions-II
Hafta 5: Alcohols and Ethers-I
Hafta 6: Alcohols and Ethers-II
Hafta 7: Alcohols from Carbonyl Compounds-I
Hafta 8: Alcohols from Carbonyl Compounds-II
Hafta 9: Conjugated Unsaturated Systems-I
Hafta 10: Conjugated Unsaturated Systems-II
Hafta 11: Aromatic Compounds-I
Hafta 12: Aromatic Compounds-II
Hafta 13: Reactions of Aromatic Compounds-I
Hafta 14: Reactions of Aromatic Compounds-II
resources: Organic Chemistry, Solomons, Fryhle and Snyder, 12th Edition, Wiley

courses.name: Principles of Organic Chemistry I
courses.purpose: To introduce the basic principles of organic chemistry and provide a foundation for understanding the major classes of organic compounds.
courses.description: Basic principles of organic chemistry. A survey of the principal classes of organic compounds. Synthesis and characteristic reactions of various functional groups.

courses.name: Analytical Chemistry II
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce modern instrumental methods used in chemical analysis and to build the conceptual foundation needed to understand electrochemical and spectroscopic techniques.
courses.description: Modern instrumental methods of chemical analysis based upon electrochemical and spectroscopic methods.
learning_outcomes: Able to apply knowledge of fundamental principles of chemistry to instrumental methods of chemical analysis
Able to describe and identify the applications of potentiometric methods
Able to describe, compare and classify different spectrochemical methods such as molecular absorption and fluorescence spectrometry, and atomic spectroscopy
Able to describe, compare and classify analytical separation methods including gas chromatography, high performance liquid chromatography and thin layer chromatography
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Ch.18 Introduction to Electrochemistry
Hafta 2: Ch.19 Applications of Standard Electrode Potentials
Hafta 3: Ch.20 Applications of Oxidation/Reduction Titrations
Hafta 4: Ch.21 Potentiometry / Chapter 22 Bulk Electrolysis: Gravimetry and Coulometry
Hafta 5: Ch.23 Voltammetry
Hafta 6: Ch.24 Introduction to Spectrochemical Methods
Hafta 7: Ch.25 Instruments for Optical Spectrometry
Hafta 8: Ch.26 Molecular Absorption Spectrometry
Hafta 9: Ch.26 Molecular Absorption Spectrometry
Hafta 10: Ch.27 Molecular Fluorescence Spectroscopy
Hafta 11: Ch.28 Atomic Spectroscopy
Hafta 12: Ch.30 Introduction to Analytical Separations
Hafta 13: Ch.31 Gas Chromatography
Hafta 14: Ch.32 High Performance Liquid Chromatography
resources: Fundamentals of Analytical Chemistry, Skoog, West, Holler and Crouch, 8th Ed., Brooks/Cole, Cengage Learning

courses.name: Physical Chemistry Laboratory
courses.description: Critical Point Determination, Neutralization Enthalpy, Adiabatic Coefficients of Gases, Joule-Thomson Effect, Differential Thermal Analysis, Phase Equilibria in a Ternary System, Viscosity Measurements, Conductivity of Strong and Weak Electrolytes, Electrolysis, Surface Energy Measurements.

courses.name: Processing and Applications of Materials
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the principles of processing and applications of different classes of materials, and to show how processing affects material performance. It also provides a foundation for understanding how science and engineering are used to design materials for a wide range of applications.
courses.description: Principles of processing and applications of various types of materials ranging from metal alloys and ceramics to polymers and composites. Processing of materials and their applications in various fields. Annealing, casting, fundamentals of heat treatment, powder handling, and powder pressing. Relationship between processing and performance. Materials used particularly in developing fields of materials science such as optical communication, fuel cells, superconductors, light-emitting diodes, lasers, and information storage. Introduction to how science and engineering can be engaged to design materials for many applications.
learning_outcomes: to compare and contrast the general processing and applications of materials.
to identify alloys, ceramics, polymers, and composites, which are commonly used in industry and technology.
to extend their basic knowledge on the application of materials.
to evaluate different processing methods of materials in industry
to focus and extend their knowledge on a specific type of conventional material.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and Review of CHEM 201
Hafta 2: Phase Transformations
Hafta 3: Phase Transformations II
Hafta 4: Thermal Processing of Metal Alloys I
Hafta 5: Thermal Processing of Metal Alloys II (Precipitation Hardening)
Hafta 6: Failure Mechanisms I (Fracture)
Hafta 7: Failure Mechanisms II (Fatigue and Creep)
Hafta 8: Metal Alloys and Applications I
Hafta 9: Metal Alloys and Applications II
Hafta 10: Composite Materials
Hafta 11: Corrosion and Degradation of Materials
Hafta 12: Electrical Properties
Hafta 13: Magnetic Properties
Hafta 14: Optical Properties
resources: Materials Science and Engineering: An Introduction, William D. Callister, 2020, John Wiley & Sons Inc

courses.name: Physical Chemistry I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental principles of physical chemistry needed to understand and analyze chemical systems quantitatively.
courses.description: Chemical thermodynamics. Solution and phase equilibria. Electrochemistry. Solid and liquid states.

courses.name: Principles of Physical Chemistry I
courses.description: Chemical thermodynamics. Solution and phase equilibria. Electro chemistry. Solid and liquid states.

courses.name: Principles of Physical Chemistry II
courses.description: Chemical kinetics. Transport phenomena. Surface chemistry. Chemistry of macromolecules.

courses.name: Quantum Chemistry I
courses.description: Review of matrix algebra and linear vector spaces. Basic notions of probability theory. Basic concepts of classical mechanics. Axioms of quantum mechanics: state vectors and wavefunctions, eigenvalues and eigenstates of observables, probability interpretation, time-dependent Schrodinger equation. Bound and continuum states of one-dimensional models. Approximation methods: Variation and perturbation methods. Bound states of Hydrogen atom.

courses.name: Quantum Chemistry II
courses.description: Intrinsic angular momentum (spin) of particles. Fermions and bosons. Permutational symmetry of identical particles and Pauli Principle, Slater determinants. Hartree-Fock equations and introduction to atomic structure. Born-Oppenheimer approximation, potential energy surfaces and vibrational-rotational states of molecules. Valence-bond and molecular-orbital approaches to molecular electronic structure. Qualitative molecular-orbital description. Ab-initio and semi-empirical methods.
learning_outcomes: Able to apply knowledge of variational and perturbation methods to quantum chemical systems and processes
Able to apply knowledge of angular momentum theory and coupling of spin and orbital angular momenta to multiplet states in atoms and molecules
Able to understand the principles and applications of potential energy surfaces, rotational and vibrational structures of molecules
Able to follow the literature about modern quantum mechanical methods for electronic structure of molecules
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review of Wave Functions, Operators , Expectation Values
Hafta 2: Approximate Methods
Hafta 3: Variaational Method
Hafta 4: Perturbation Theory
Hafta 5: First and Second Order Perturbation Theory
Hafta 6: Atomic Units and Many Electron Atoms
Hafta 7: He-Atom: Singlet and triplet states
Hafta 8: Many Electron wavefunctions, Hartree-Fock equations, SCF procedure
Hafta 9: Atomic structure. Spin orbit coupling. Fine and hyperfine structure. Hund's rules
Hafta 10: Introduction to Molecular Structure: Born-Oppenheimer approximation, potential energy surface, vibrational and rotational structure
Hafta 11: Molecular Orbitals of Hydrogen molecular ion. LCAO approximation.
Hafta 12: Qualitative MO theory for diatomic molecules. MO correlation diagrams, symmetries. Huckel method.
Hafta 13: Hartree-Fock method for molecules. Basis sets, LCAO-MO-SCF approach. Population analysis. Semi-empirical methods.
Hafta 14: Quantum Mechanics and Spectroscopy
resources: Quantum Chemistry, D.A. McQuarrie, 2nd, University Science Books
Introduction to Quantum Mechanics in Chemistry, Ratner and Schatz, 2001, Prentuce Hall
Elementary Methods of Molecular Quantum Mechanics, V. Magnasco, free e-book through Bilkent Library
Quantum Chemistry, I. N. Levine, Prentice Hall

courses.name: Physical Chemistry II
courses.description: Chemical kinetics. Transport phenomena. Surface chemistry. Chemistry of macromolecules.
learning_outcomes: Able to apply knowledge of physical chemistry principles
Able to design and conduct physical chemistry experiments as well as to analyze data associated with physical chemistry
Able to contribute to experimental work
Able to describe, compare and identify concepts of transport, molecular motion, kinetics and reaction dynamics
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Chapter 20 Molecules in Motion
Hafta 2: Chapter 20 Molecules in Motion
Hafta 3: Chapter 20 Molecules in Motion
Hafta 4: Chapter 20 Molecules in Motion
Hafta 5: Chapter 21 Rates of Chemical Reactions
Hafta 6: Chapter 21 Rates of Chemical Reactions
Hafta 7: Chapter 21 Rates of Chemical Reactions
Hafta 8: Chapter 21 Rates of Chemical Reactions
Hafta 9: Chapter 21 Rates of Chemical Reactions
Hafta 10: Chapter 22 Reaction Dynamics
Hafta 11: Chapter 22 Reaction Dynamics
Hafta 12: Chapter 22 Reaction Dynamics
Hafta 13: Chapter 23: Catalysis
Hafta 14: Chapter 23: Catalysis
resources: Physical Chemistry, Peter Atkins, J. Paula, 9th Ed, Oxford University Press

courses.name: Inorganic Chemistry I
courses.description: The nature of chemical bond. Shapes of molecules. Acid-Base chemistry. Chemistry in aqueous and nonaqueous solutions. Hydrogen bonding. General properties of the elements in the periodic table.

courses.name: Inorganic Chemistry Laboratory
courses.description: Crystal growth in gels. Synthesis of isomorphous compounds. Structural, electrical and magnetic properties of perovskite ceramics. Preparation and characterization of polythionates. Interchalogenes: synthesis of iodine trichloride. Isopolyvanadates. Acid-base chemistry: determination of the nature of bonding in DMSO complex of copper(II). Spectrophotometric determination of the formula of a coordination complex and equilibrium constant. Spectrochemical series of Co(III) complexes. Microscale determination of magnetic susceptibility with Evans balance. Geometric isomers of coordination complexes. Linkage isomers of coordination complexes.
learning_outcomes: Be able to perform experiments under acidic and basic conditions
Be able to experimentally determine electronic and chemical structures and magnetic properties of Inorganic complexes
Be able to effectively report the experimental results
Be able to design experiental set ups for independent laboratory project
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Isopolyvanadates: Synthesis and Characterization
Hafta 2: Spectrophotometric Determination of the Formula of a Thiocyano Complex of Fe(III) and its Formation Constant
Hafta 3: Acid-Base Chemistry. Determination of the Nature of Bonding in DMSO Complex of Copper(II)
Hafta 4: Preparation and Characterization of the Geometric Isomers of Coordination Complex: cis- and trans-bis(Glycinato)copper(II) Monohydrates.
Hafta 5: Determination of Lewis Acidity Using 31P NMR
Hafta 6: Midterm Exam
Hafta 7: Synthesis and Characterization of Linkage Isomers: Nitro and Nitrito Complexes of Aqua Pentaamine Cobalt(III)
Hafta 8: Spectrochemical Series of Co(III) Complexes and Determination of Slitting Energy of Co(III) Complexes.
Hafta 9: Microscale Determination of Magnetic Susceptibility with Evans Balance
Hafta 10: Self Study (Project)
Hafta 11: Self Study (Project)
Hafta 12: Self Study (Presentation)
Hafta 13: Self Study
Hafta 14: Self Study

courses.name: Inorganic Chemistry II
courses.purpose: This course aims to build students' understanding of advanced inorganic chemistry topics, especially the structure and properties of coordination compounds and solids. It also develops the ability to relate bonding, stereochemistry, and physical properties in inorganic systems.
courses.description: Bonding, stereochemistry and spectra of coordination compounds. Electronic, magnetic and optical properties of solids.
learning_outcomes: Demostrate competence on fundementals of Solid State Chemistry
Demonstrate competence on relations between energy and acid-base concepts
Demonstrate basic knowledge on structural and bonding concepts of coordination compounds
Able to evaluate the electronic and magnetic properties of coordination compounds
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Structures and Energetics of Metallic and Ionic Solids
Hafta 2: Structures and Energetics of Metallic and Ionic Solids
Hafta 3: Structures and Energetics of Metallic and Ionic Solids
Hafta 4: Acid Base Chemistry
Hafta 5: Acid Base Chemistry
Hafta 6: d-block Metal Chemistry: General Considerations
Hafta 7: d-block Metal Chemistry: General Considerations
Hafta 8: d-block Metal Chemistry: Coordination Complexes
Hafta 9: d-block Metal Chemistry: Coordination Complexes
Hafta 10: d-block Metal Chemistry: Coordination Complexes
Hafta 11: d-block Metal Chemistry: Coordination Complexes
Hafta 12: d-block Metal Chemistry: the first row metals
Hafta 13: d-block Metal Chemistry: the first row metals
Hafta 14: d-block Metal Chemistry: the heavier metals
resources: Recommended - Textbook: Inorganic Chemistry, J. E. Huheey, E. A. Keiter and R. L. Keiter, 4th, Harper Collions College
Required - Textbook: Inorganic Chemistry , C. E. Housecroft and A. G. Sharpe, 5th, Pearson

courses.name: Food Chemistry
courses.purpose: This course introduces the scientific issues connected with food and food preparation, with emphasis on the chemical and physical processes involved in cooking.
courses.description: Topics include the composition of the human body in terms of chemical elements and tissues, energy needs, components of food such as fats, proteins, carbohydrates, and vitamins, as well as food preparation and preservation, taste, diet, toxins, food additives, and the industrialization of food.

courses.name: Introduction to Statistical Thermodynamics
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the fundamental principles of statistical thermodynamics and show how macroscopic thermodynamic behavior emerges from microscopic molecular descriptions. It prepares students to apply statistical methods to physical and chemical systems.
courses.description: Review of classical thermodynamics, including the fundamental equation, maximum entropy principle, Legendre transforms, and thermodynamic potentials. Basic notions of probability theory, ensemble theory (microcanonical, canonical, and grandcanonical ensembles), partition functions, fluctuations, equivalence of ensembles, classical phase-space approach, and quantum statistics. Applications include ideal gases, chemical equilibrium, and models for adsorption and elasticity.
learning_outcomes: Be able to articulate the basic postulates of statistical thermodynamics and basic concepts such as Boltzmann equation, microcanonical/canonical/grandcanonical probability distributions, and fluctuations.
Be able to apply the methods of statistical thermodynamics to monoatomic, diatomic and polyatomic gases.
Be able to apply Fermi-Dirac and Bose-Einstein distributions to quantum ideal gases.
Be able to apply the statistical thermodynamic methods to chemical equilibrium, and to models of elasticity and adsorption.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review of Classical Thermodynamics: Fundamental relation, maximum entropy principle, Legendre transform, thermodynamic potentials
Hafta 2: Review of Classical Thermodynamics: Extremum principles for equilibrium, Maxwell relations, response functions, stability of thermodynamic systems
Hafta 3: Basic results of combinatorics and elementary notions of probability, information-theoretic entropy
Hafta 4: Postulates of Statistical Mechanics: Ergodic hypothesis, principle of a priori equal probabilities. Ensembles. Microcanonical ensemble
Hafta 5: Applications: Einstein model of crystals, simple model of paramagnetism, negative absolute temperature.
Hafta 6: Canonical ensemble. Calculation of canonical partition function for simple models.
Hafta 7: Grandcanonical ensemble. General ensemble theory.
Hafta 8: Fluctuations. Equivalence of ensembles
Hafta 9: Classical statistical mechanics.
Hafta 10: Systems composed of identical particles. Quantum statistics. Classical limit of quantum statistics.
Hafta 11: Classical ideal gases: mono-atomic and diatomic molecules. Nuclear-spin and statistics; ortho-para hydrogen.
Hafta 12: Vibrational and rotational partition functions for polyatomic molecules. Symmetry number.
Hafta 13: Introduction to lattice statistics: Simple models of adsorption, polymer chains.
Hafta 14: Chemical equilibrium in ideal gas mixtures
resources: Statistical Mechanics, McQuarrie, 2000, University Science Books
H. Callen, Thermodynamics and an Introduction to Thermostatistics, 1985/2nd, Wiley
An Introduction to Statistical Thermodynamics, T.L. Hill, 1986, Dover

courses.name: Organic Chemistry of Carbonyl Compounds
courses.purpose: This course introduces the chemistry of carbonyl compounds and related functional groups, with emphasis on their structures, reactivity, and synthetic transformations. It aims to build students' ability to understand and apply reaction mechanisms in organic synthesis.
courses.description: Aldehydes and ketones. Carboxylic acids and their derivatives. Reactions at the alpha-carbon of carbonyl compounds. Condensation and conjugated addition reactions of carbonyl compounds.
learning_outcomes: Able to apply independent and analytical thinking.
Able to identify classes of organic compounds and reaction mechanisms.
Able to apply the knowledge to synthesis and characterization of organic compounds and derivatives.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Revision of topics covered in CHEM 232
Hafta 2: Revision of topics covered in CHEM 232
Hafta 3: Aldehydes and Ketones
Hafta 4: Carboxylic acids and their derivatives
Hafta 5: Reactions at the alpha-carbon of carbonyl compounds
Hafta 6: Reactions at the alpha-carbon of carbonyl compounds
Hafta 7: Condensation and conjugated addition reactions of carbonyl compounds
Hafta 8: Amines
Hafta 9: Phenols and aryl halides
Hafta 10: Carbohydrates
Hafta 11: Carbohydrates
Hafta 12: Lipids
Hafta 13: Amino acids and proteins
Hafta 14: Illustration of the role of organic chemistry in biology, medicine, and industry
resources: Organic Chemistry, Solomons, Fryhle and Snyder, 11th Edition, Wiley
Organic Chemistry, Clayden, Greeves, Warren and Wothers, Oxford University Press

courses.name: Applied Quantum Chemistry
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the quantum mechanical basis of molecular structure and properties, and to develop the ability to apply quantum chemical methods to chemical problems.
courses.description: Quantum mechanical calculations of various properties of molecules using semi-empirical as well as ab-initio methods.

courses.name: Principles and Chemical Applications of Thermodynamics
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental laws and principles of thermodynamics and shows how they are applied to chemical systems. It aims to build a conceptual and mathematical basis for analyzing equilibrium, stability, and thermodynamic behavior in chemistry.
courses.description: The course covers the zeroth, first, second, and third laws of thermodynamics, the fundamental equation, maximum entropy principle, Legendre transforms and thermodynamic potentials. It also includes extremum principles for equilibrium states, stability of thermodynamic systems, the LeChatelier principle, thermodynamic surfaces, thermodynamics of systems in external fields, and basic notions of irreversible thermodynamics.

courses.name: Advanced Instrumental Analysis
courses.purpose: The course objective is to study and discuss the principles, instrumentation and applications of modern instrumental methods, including spectroscopic techniques such as AAS, ICPMS, FTIR, Raman spectroscopy, Luminescence Spectroscopy, Mass Spectrometry.

courses.name: Environmental Chemistry
courses.description: Chemical problems related to environment. Energy balance of earth, ozone in the upper atmosphere, greenhouse effect, micrometeorology. SO2 and CO2 cycles, photochemical smog, aerosols, trace elements in the environment, particle size distribution.

courses.name: Fundamentals of Biochemistry
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental principles underlying the structure and function of biomolecules and their roles in biological systems.
courses.description: Basic discussion of the structure and properties of biomolecules with special emphasis on proteins, enzymatic catalysis, membrane assembly and functions, bioenergetics.

courses.name: Structure and Reactivity of Organic Compounds
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain how the structure of organic compounds influences their reactivity. It focuses on the factors that control chemical behavior and reaction pathways in organic chemistry.
courses.description: Discussion of the relationship between the structure and reactivity of organic compounds, with special emphasis on conformational and stereo-electronic effects, reaction mechanisms and physical organic chemistry.

courses.name: Media Studies II
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to major critical theories in media and cultural studies and to help them develop a critical perspective on media institutions, technologies, audiences, and professions.
courses.description: Introduces major critical theories in media and cultural studies. Social, technological and aesthetic contexts of visual and mass media. Surveillance, mediated environments, media technologies, media industries, audiences and spectatorship, media professions.
learning_outcomes: Defining the basic concepts and fundamental debates in the communication theory.
Understand and explain the importance of ownership structure and government regulation in the media industry.
Developing a critical understanding of new media technologies and be able to identify their limitations and strengths.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Course agenda, goals and procedures
Hafta 2: Media technology
Hafta 3: Media effect
Hafta 4: Media effect v2
Hafta 5: Media influence/influencers
Hafta 6: Communication as practice, action, and interaction
Hafta 7: Media audiences
Hafta 8: Media as business
Hafta 9: Media as business V2
Hafta 10: Media and surveillance
Hafta 11: Media as spectacle
Hafta 12: research project presentations
Hafta 13: research project presentations
Hafta 14: research project presentations
resources: Communication Theory for Humans, O'Boyle, N., 2022
The Media Student's Book, Gill Branston and Roy Stafford, 2016, Routledge

courses.name: Media Studies I
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the study of media content and the social implications of mass media in contemporary life. It aims to develop students' ability to analyze, interpret, and critique media texts using close reading and content analysis.
courses.description: The course examines media and communication studies with a focus on media content analysis. Topics include semiotics, narrative, genre, stardom, celebrity, authorship, discourse, ideology, hegemony, and representation, with attention to how media texts produce meaning within cultural contexts.
learning_outcomes: Define key terms, theories, and practices related to media production and media studies
Design and complete a research project using close reading or content analysis
Strengthen oral and written communication skills
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course Introduction / Syllabus / Overview of basics around studying media
Hafta 2: Research Methods & Skills in Media Studies: Jane Stokes, "Analysing Media and Cultural Texts", How To Do Media & Cultural Studies
Hafta 3: Semiotics-1: Daniel Chandler, SEMIOTICS FOR BEGINNERS, "Introduction"
Hafta 4: Semiotics-2: Daniel Chandler, SEMIOTICS FOR BEGINNERS, “Signs” & “Denotation, Connotation and Myth”
Hafta 5: Narratives-1: Gill Branston & Roy Stafford, "Narratives," The Media Student’s Book, 5th ed.
Hafta 6: Narratives-2: “Transmedia Storytelling 101” & C. A. Scolari, "Transmedia Storytelling: Consumers, Narrative Worlds, and Branding in Contemporary Media Production”

Hafta 7: Genre-1: Gill Branston & Roy Stafford, “Genres and Other Classifications”, The Media Student’s Book, 5th ed., Thomas Schatz, “Film Genre and the Genre Film”, Richard Dyer, “Entertainment and Utopia”
Hafta 8: Genre-2: Screening TBD & midterm review / midterm

Hafta 9: Stardom, Celebrity & Authorship: Christine Geraghty, “Re-examining Stardom”, Frame By Frame: Auteur Theory (online), Frame By Frame: Auteur Theory, ONLINE: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mWzPIuXUwpI
Hafta 10: Discourse, Ideology & Hegemony-1: Gill Branston & Roy Stafford, “Ideologies and Discourses”, The Media Student’s Book, 5th ed.
Hafta 11: Discourse, Ideology & Hegemony-2: Stuart Hall, "Encoding/Decoding" & Judith Williamson, "Meaning and Ideology" & Screening TBD

Hafta 12: Representation-1: Paul Long and Tim Wall, “Media Representations”, Media Studies: Texts, Production, Context, 2nd ed. & Stuart Hall, Representation and the Media ONLINE: https://vimeo.com/191647636
Hafta 13: Representation-2: Gender Inequality Report (films 2007-2014) & Group Workshop – Content analysis & close reading group project meetings
Hafta 14: POSTER and ORAL PRESENTATION SESSIONS – conference style, during class hours
resources: Consult the course Moodle page for a complete required bibliography.

courses.name: History of Film and Media
courses.purpose: To introduce students to the historical development of cinema and media from the invention of film to the digital era, and to help them understand how major movements, genres, styles, and techniques shape film meaning.
courses.description: History of cinema from its invention to the digital era. Major breakthroughs, significant movements and genres in cinema, as well as style and meaning, elements of film narrative, and filmmaking techniques. Historical, political and cultural context of the movies.

courses.name: Polymer Chemistry
courses.purpose: To introduce the fundamental concepts of polymer chemistry, including polymer chain structure, polymerization statistics and kinetics, and the relationship between polymer structure and properties.
courses.description: Polymer chains and characterization. Statistics and kinetics of polymerization reactions. Structure and properties of bulk polymers. Materials and synthetic polymers. Rubber elasticity and entropy.

courses.name: Introduction to Screenwriting
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamentals of classical narrative screenwriting and to develop their ability to transform ideas into a structured audiovisual script. It also helps students practice pitching and proposal writing for screen-based projects.
courses.description: Basic notions of classical narrative scriptwriting such as character, structure, plot, dialogue, genre, and theme, as well as textual elements of audio-visual forms such as documentary, experimental and multimedia presentations. Proper script formatting, dialogue writing, pitching ideas, and proposal writing. Completion of one short script and a proposal for a second audio-visual project.
learning_outcomes: Design a visual and written presentation and prepare a pitch proposal for a completed screenplay
Demonstrate knowledge and experience in various forms of creative writing
Produce an original classical narrative short screenplay. Demonstrate knowledge of and apply various storytelling techniques.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Screenwriting
Hafta 2: Idea Generation / Premise
Hafta 3: Creating a Character / Writing Character Descriptions
Hafta 4: Classical Narrative Structure / Conflict
Hafta 5: Engaging Openings / Setting the Scene
Hafta 6: Theme / Genre / Pitching Technique
Hafta 7: Story Presentations and Class Feedback
Hafta 8: Script Formatting / Story Ideas Progress Session
Hafta 9: Dialogue: How People Really Speak / Subtext
Hafta 10: First Draft Screenplays Assesment
Hafta 11: Second Draft Presentations and Feedback
Hafta 12: A/V Script Formatting
Hafta 13: Script Progress Meetings
Hafta 14: Practice Pitch Meetings
resources: Recommended - Textbook: See list posted on Moodle

courses.name: Advanced Screenwriting
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' understanding of storytelling for the screen by focusing on narrative construction, memory, observation, and interpersonal relationships. It also explores how language can be used to shape audience response.
courses.description: Art of story-telling, increasing awareness of memory, observation, and interpersonal relationships through the construction of narrative form for the screen. The ways in which an audience can be manipulated through the use of language. Themes with stories drawn from experience and conventions, problems, and possibilities of screenwriting.

courses.name: Principles of Visual Communication Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' understanding of visual communication design as an effective means of communication. It also seeks to build awareness of visual identification through the use of typography, layout, and expressive design within a design thinking process.
courses.description: Exploration of visual design together with typography used as an effective key element of communication based on theoretical and practical knowledge. Visual communication design and typographic principles, layout and expressive typography are covered by implementing design thinking process. An awareness on visual identification is explored through lectures, discussions, screenings, critiques and presentations.
learning_outcomes: Able to integrate theoretical and historical knowledge of visual communication design practice with typography.
Able to analyze, interpret, and engage in constructive criticism.
Able to produce innovative works of visual design.
Able to use methods and techniques related to the field of design in focus.
Able to define his/her target audience and effectively presents work to them.
Produce work in a limited time, test problem solving skills and previously learned information.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course Introduction >> COMD 212 is a studio-based graphic design course where students explore how graphic styles—both historical and contemporary—can be appropriated and transformed to develop a distinctive design voice. Through studio projects at the intersection of visual design and typography, they translate conceptual ideas into concrete visual outcomes across packaging, poster design, book graphics, logo design, film title sequences, and other print applications. By re-adapting, re-interpreting, and re-contextualizing existing graphic styles, students create conceptual projects that visualize new creative possibilities and contribute to their own personal design approach.

Hafta 2: Project #1 Introduction: Horror Movie Poster Design (Design a a set of three horror movie posters that portray extreme violence in a visually palatable, symbolic way — inspired by Saul Bass’s minimalist graphic style and the dramatic aesthetics of German Expressionism)
Hafta 3: Project 1 Workday/Process Review
Hafta 4: Project 1 Workday/ Final Critique

Hafta 5: Project #2 Introduction: Promotional Items for Punk Rock Shows (Design a series of publicity materials—posters, flyers, and related visual assets—for a Punk Rock music band, by combining the raw, rebellious ethos of Punk Rock with the clarity and structure of Swiss Modernism)
Hafta 6: Project 2 Workday/Process Review
Hafta 7: Project 2 Workday/ Final Critique

Hafta 8: Project #3 Introduction: Book Cover and Spread Design for a Fairy Tale for Adults (Design a book cover and introductory spread for a classic fairy tale — using Surrealism and Psychedelic Style to create dreamlike, fluid, and fantastical imagery that reimagines and reinterprets the story for an adult audience)
Hafta 9: Project 3 Workday/ Process Review/ Final Critique

Hafta 10: Project #4 Introduction: Typographic Poster and Title Sequence Design for a Nexflix Series (Design a typographic promotional poster and title sequence for an existing Netflix series — inspired by the clarity and structure of the International Typographic Style and the geometric abstraction of De Stijl)
Hafta 11: Project 4 Workday/Process Review
Hafta 12: Project 4 Workday/ Final Critique

Hafta 13: Project #5 Introduction: Identity Design for a French Vintage Café (Design the visual identity—logo, signage, menus, dinnerware, packaging, business cards, coasters etc—for a café/restaurant with a vintage French bistro theme, inspired by Ornamental and Victorian Styles)
Hafta 14: Project 5 Workday/ Process Review (Submission Date TBA)
resources: Laptop computer with Adobe Photoshop (or equivalent) and Adobe Illustrator (or equivalent), CC 2020, Adobe Systems (or equivalent design software)

courses.name: News Reporting and Writing
courses.purpose: To prepare students for professional news reporting positions by developing their ability to gather information, edit, and write under strict deadlines.
courses.description: News reporting and production techniques of radio and television. Gathering information, editing and writing under strict deadlines in order to prepare the student for a professional position. News values, and responsibilities. Basic news writing and style principles, interviewing techniques.

courses.name: Film Theory and Criticism
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to major debates in film theory and criticism. It aims to develop students' ability to think critically about how films produce meaning and how theoretical approaches can be used to analyze film texts.
courses.description: Key debates in film theory, covering concepts such as genre, auteurism, ideology, psychoanalysis, subjectivity, national and transnational cinemas, spectatorship and reception discussed in relation to film language, including narrative, mise-en-scene, cinematography, sound, and editing.
learning_outcomes: Develop basic literacy in film theory
Apply critical and theoretical concepts to film texts
Analyze and interpret films using various theoretical approaches
Strengthen critical thinking, discussion, and writing skills
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Film as Art and Language: An Introduction
Hafta 2: Munsterberg, Psychology, and Expressionism
Hafta 3: The Face, the Emotive Images, and Impressionism
Hafta 4: Soviet Montage: Image and Thought
Hafta 5: Realism, Reality, and the Real
Hafta 6: The Theater and the Spectator: the Apparatus Theory
Hafta 7: The Gaze, Desire, and Cinematic Psychoanalysis
Hafta 8: Auteur Politics
Hafta 9: National and Transnational Cinema
Hafta 10: The Limits and Possibilities of Genre
Hafta 11: Modernism and Postmodernism
Hafta 12: Posthumanism and Environmental Humanities
Hafta 13: Posthumanism and Feminism
Hafta 14: New Media and The Digital Turn
resources: Film Screenings
Required readings and bibliography on the course Moodle page

courses.name: News and Society
courses.purpose: To examine the function of news in contemporary society and its role and impact on social and political affairs.
courses.description: A critical understanding of news through core debates in press theories, patterns of news consumption, changes to news industries, and the social consequences of news making.

courses.name: Science Writing and Journalism
courses.purpose: Improvement of communication skills to cover science news and reach general public.
courses.description: Analysis of scientific journal findings, reporting and writing science/technology news stories to inform the lay audience.

courses.name: Analysis of Moving Image
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' ability to understand and critically analyze moving image texts by introducing key concepts of cinematic language and visual media analysis.
courses.description: Cinematic language, which has extended into a variety of visual media, including television. Mise-en-scene, cinematography, editing, sound-image relationships, narrative and non-narrative forms, with attention to both dominant practices (Hollywood) and alternatives. Use of these concepts in conjunction with critical writing skills to analyze moving image texts.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate proficiency in terminology related to film form and analysis
Analyze and interpret moving image texts; effectively present analysis to peers
Demonstrate knowledge of basic concepts regarding visual literacy and analytical skills
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Mise-en-scène
Hafta 3: Cinematography
Hafta 4: Editing
Hafta 5: Sound
Hafta 6: Narrative
Hafta 7: MIDTERM EXAM
Hafta 8: Videographic Criticism
Hafta 9: Genre & Ideology
Hafta 10: Documentary & Essay
Hafta 11: WORKSHOP - VIDEO ESSAYS (In groups)
Hafta 12: Social Contexts
Hafta 13: Television: Turkish TV
Hafta 14: COURSE REVIEW
resources: The Film Experience: An Introduction, Patricia White and Timothy Corrigan, 2012 (3rd Edition)
Film: A Critical Introduction, Maria Pramaggiore and Tom Wallis, 3rd Edition (2005), Laurence King
Film Art: An Introduction, David Bordwell & Kristin Thompson, 8th Edition, 2008, New York: McGraw- Hill
Introduction to Film Studies, Jill Nelmes, 5th Edition, 2011, London: Routledge

courses.name: Media and Society
courses.purpose: This course examines the role of media as a major social institution and explores the relationship between media and society. It aims to help students understand how media production, circulation, and reception shape culture, institutions, and individual behavior.
courses.description: The media as a major social institution, the relationship between media and society. The production and reception of media content, the impact of media over other institutions, society and culture as well as the effects over individual behavior. Questions of control and ownership, public and private media. Mass culture and popular culture. Different forms and genres of media, fictional and news material. Propaganda and ideological influence. The impact of new technologies of communication.

courses.name: Popular Culture
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop an awareness of how popular culture operates in specific ways and to encourage a critical understanding of its forms and effects.
courses.description: Popular narrative and entertainment forms in contrast with "high culture." Impact of cultural forms on audiences and a critical study of theories of popular culture. Significant cases from literature, press, film, television, and new media.

courses.name: Introduction to Advertising
courses.purpose: To introduce the basic functions of advertising and how advertisements are planned and produced. The course also aims to explain the role of advertising within marketing communications and its institutional, media, retail, economic, ethical, and social dimensions.
courses.description: Basics of advertising: its functions, and how to plan and produce advertisements. Role of advertising in marketing communications, advertising institutions and media, and its retail aspects, with a theoretical foundation in economics, ethics, and social aspects.

courses.name: Game Design and Research
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to games as a cultural phenomenon and a media form within a historical context. It also aims to develop an analytical perspective on game mechanics and dynamics while supporting iterative game design and development practices.
courses.description: Introduction to games as a cultural phenomenon and a media form in a historical context. An analytical approach to game mechanics and dynamics. Fundamentals of game development with the implementation of iterative design methodologies.
learning_outcomes: Participate in work with teammates from other disciplines
Demonstrate knowledge on the field and contemporary issues regarding games and gaming
Use appropriate media to communicate ideas throughout the design process
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course. Brief Description of Video Games and other media. Discussions.
Hafta 2: Definition of Video Games and introduction to gaming terminologies.
Hafta 3: History of Video Games and Gaming
Hafta 4: Discussing Game Mechanics / Game Dynamics with examples from different video game genres
Hafta 5: Experience: Agency and Immersion
Hafta 6: Narrative in games. Linearity / Non-Linearity / Multi-Linearity in video games
Hafta 7: Student Presentations
Hafta 8: Spring Break
Hafta 9: Midterm Exam
Hafta 10: Forward to Game Analysis
Hafta 11: Critical Approach to Gameplay.
Hafta 12: Fundamentals of Game Design
Hafta 13: Final Project, Experience Design. First Iteration. Group Testing. GDD (Alpha)
Hafta 14: Final Project, Second Iteration. Testing. GDD (Beta)

courses.name: Digital Marketing Communication
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the strategic role of digital marketing in contemporary communication practice. It helps students understand how digital channels, tools, and data can be used to reach audiences, build brand narratives, and develop effective campaigns.
courses.description: Digital marketing strategies, SEO, content, paid media, and e-commerce tools. Focus on audience, storytelling, and real-world campaign development.
learning_outcomes: Understanding the fundamental principles of digital marketing and its evolution
Knowing effective strategies using owned, earned, and paid media
Analyzing key performance indicators (KPIs) and optimize digital campaigns
Applying SEO, content marketing, and AI-driven search optimization
Designing social media and influencer marketing strategies
Implementing e-commerce growth and conversion optimization techniques
Creating a data-driven digital strategy for a real-world brand
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The Digital Marketing Landscape & Fundamentals
Hafta 2: Consumer Behavior & Market Segmentation
Hafta 3: Content Marketing, Storytelling & Brand Voice
Hafta 4: SEO & Organic Growth Strategies, AI Search Optimization
Hafta 5: Social Consumers/Digital Identity/Roger's Diffusion of Innovations/Wired World
Hafta 6: Social Media Strategy & Performance Metrics
Hafta 7: MIDTERM EXAM
Hafta 8: Influencer Marketing & User-Generated Content (UGC)
Hafta 9: Social Media Advertising (Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube)
Hafta 10: E-Commerce Growth & Conversion Optimization
Hafta 11: Digital Strategy Development
Hafta 12: Digital Strategy Development
Hafta 13: Digital Strategy Development
Hafta 14: Final Project Presentations
resources: Digital Marketing: Strategy, Implementation and Practice, Dave Chaffey, Fiona Ellis-Chadwick, 8th Edition, Pearson Education

courses.name: Digital Culture
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to selected media theories and help them understand how digital media and communication technologies shape political, social, economic, and cultural change.
courses.description: The course examines the rapid development of information and communication technologies and their impact on media culture, social change, information monopolies, and disinformation. It discusses selected media theories in the context of societal transformation to a media culture.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of the key concepts and theoretical debates in the study of digital media.
Demonstrate critical understanding of the role of digital media in shaping and reshaping identity, culture, and society.
Analyze, criticize and evaluate everyday practices of digital media.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Mediated Worlds
Hafta 3: The Network Society
Hafta 4: Platform Architecture and Digital Power
Hafta 5: Digital Identity and Performance Culture
Hafta 6: Platform Capitalism and Digital Economy
Hafta 7: Algorithmic Culture and Power
Hafta 8: Structure and Agency
Hafta 9: Digital Identity and Virtual Communities
Hafta 10: Visual Cultures
Hafta 11: Recommendation Systems
Hafta 12: Chatbots and Ai-Language Models
Hafta 13: Digital and Social Transformations
Hafta 14: Future of Digital Culture
resources: Understanding Digital Culture, Vincent Miller, 2020, Sage
Digital Media and Society : Theories, Topics and Tools, Simon Lindgren, 2022, Sage
The Data Revolution: A Critical Analysis of Big Data, Open Data & Data Infrastructures, Rob Kitchin, 2022, Sage

courses.name: Radio Programming and Production
courses.purpose: This course aims to acquaint the student with the techniques and aesthetics of audio production including radio broadcasting, audio for television, and multimedia production such as podcasts. It emphasizes scripting, editing, and the production of several program forms, while building a foundation in broadcasting and multimedia production.
courses.description: The course introduces the concepts, technology, and skills behind audio production. It covers radio broadcasting, audio for television, and multimedia production such as podcasts, with attention to scripting, editing, and producing different program formats.

courses.name: Multimedia Journalism
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide an understanding of digital journalism by introducing current discussions in online media, emerging digital technologies, and data visualization. It also aims to develop students' online and multimedia reporting skills through practical production and publication work.
courses.description: An understanding of digital journalism by introducing current discussions in online media, emerging digital technologies, data visualization. Development of online and multimedia reporting skills by actively contributing to class website, writing beat blogs, taking photographs, and creating short video and audio components for reports. Access to a smart phone or audio and video equipment required.
learning_outcomes: Discuss current issues in digital journalism.
Create and manage an internet blog publication that demonstrates working knowledge of journalistic research and practice.
Complete medium-specific journalistic assignments such as photo-essays, audio-slides and video news pieces using a variety of multimedia and social media tools.
Use a selection of current online user-friendly editing and story-telling software platforms.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Web and Multimedia Journalism. Trends in Online News. Shift of Newspapers from paper to Net - Blogs & Social Media & Multimedia in News Outlets.
Hafta 2: Shift in Journalistic Reporting Styles Writing Techniques: Tumbled Pyramid as Opposed to Reverse Pyramid writing
Hafta 3: Visual literacy: Planning and Designing a Beat Blog
Hafta 4: Reporting Skills: Research, Sources, Interview
Hafta 5: Visual Storytelling for the Web: Photography & Photo Journalism
Hafta 6: Visual Storytelling for the Web: Creating Photo-Essays
Hafta 7: Audio Storytelling for the Web: Audio and Podcasting
Hafta 8: Audio Storytelling for the Web: Audio Slideshows
Hafta 9: Video Journalism for the Web: Documentary Journalism vs. Broadcast News
Hafta 10: Video Journalism for the Web: Shooting and Editing Video
Hafta 11: Social Media Journalism: Micro-blogging. Live Reporting
Hafta 12: Social Media Journalism: User-generated Content. Content- Curation
Hafta 13: Data Journalism & Interactive Story-telling
Hafta 14: Presentation of Class Blogs
resources: The Online Journalism Handbook: How to Survive and Thrive in the Digital Age. Bradshaw, Paul and Liisa Rohumaa. 2011. London: Routledge.
Journalism Next: A Practical Guide to Digital Reporting and Publishing. Briggs, Mark. 2012. 2nd Ed. New York: CQ Press.
Multimedia Journalism: A Practical Guide. Bull, Andy. 2010. London: Routledge.

courses.name: Sound Design II
