courses.purpose: Continuation of Sound Design I. The course develops advanced audio post-production skills for video and film and explores the creative potential of sound design to enhance communication through artistic and expressive uses of sound.
courses.description: Advanced audio post-production techniques for video and film. Components and creative potential of sound design in order to enhance communication through artistic and expressive uses of sound.

courses.name: Sound Design I
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the role of sound in relation to visual media and develops practical skills in audio production and post-production for video and film.
courses.description: Audio in relation to visual media through basic audio production and post-production techniques for video and film. Skill development in sound recording both in-studio and on location, covering signal processing, digital audio editing, and mixing.
learning_outcomes: Students will develop and further recording, editing and mixing audio for audio-visual program skills
Students will demonstrate basic technical audio knowledge
Students will demonstrate knowledge of image and sound relationship
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introducing the course
Hafta 2: Sound and Image Part 1
Hafta 3: Sound and Image Part 2
Hafta 4: Fundamentals of Audio Production and Post-Production Part 1
Hafta 5: Fundamentals of Audio Production and Post-Production Part 2
Hafta 6: Project #1 In Progress
Hafta 7: Project #1 Evaluation
Hafta 8: Project #2 In Progress
Hafta 9: Project #2 In Progress
Hafta 10: Project #2 Evaluation
Hafta 11: Project #3 In Progress
Hafta 12: Project #3 In Progress
Hafta 13: Project #3 Evaluation
Hafta 14: Review of the Term

courses.name: Music and Media
courses.purpose: To examine how media shapes the production, distribution, and consumption of music, and to introduce key theories and practices related to music and media technologies.
courses.description: The course covers theories and practices of music and related technologies in media. It addresses music from a variety of perspectives, including artistic, industrial, and cultural dimensions.

courses.name: Advanced Broadcast Studio
courses.description: Expands on the skills developed in the design and production related courses of the curriculum including multi-camera and live streaming. Building on the camera work, editing, writing, and producing, work to create and produce original online programming and production including live streaming.

courses.name: Media Theory and Methods
courses.purpose: To introduce advanced methods and approaches used in the study of media and to support students in designing and conducting an independent research project in a specific area of media studies.
courses.description: Various advanced methods of studying media, including semiology, content analysis, theories of identification, audience studies and ethnographies, and economics of media. The course also involves the design and conduct of an independent research project in a specific area of media studies.

courses.name: Advanced Issues in Communication Studies
courses.purpose: This course is designed to introduce students to advanced issues in communication studies and recent research.
courses.description: Topics to be covered may include: revisiting the communication process; audience groups and different interpretations of media messages; ratings system; media practice and democracy; issues of identification, image and visuality; internet, cyberspace and the impact of recent technologies; digital cinema and digital television; interactive media and media art.
learning_outcomes: Understanding the political and economic forces driving media institutions, messages and technologies
Critically analyzing media content
Cultivating awareness of media environments
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction & Course Overview
Hafta 2: Thinking about the field of communication research
Hafta 3: Studying contemporary media problems
Hafta 4: Basics of communication research methods 1
Hafta 5: Basics of communication research methods 2
Hafta 6: Concepts, methods, and digital media
Hafta 7: Algorithms, platforms, and research challenges
Hafta 8: Midterm exam
Hafta 9: Digital populism, authoritarianism, and self-censorship
Hafta 10: Project development
Hafta 11: Activism on TikTok
Hafta 12: Voicing grievances, affects, and the problem of silence
Hafta 13: Final project proposal pitches
Hafta 14: Final project proposal pitches

courses.name: Gender and Media
courses.purpose: This course examines how media represents gender and how those representations shape and reflect social understandings of masculinity and femininity. It also aims to develop critical awareness of gender bias, stereotypes, and inequality in media contexts.
courses.description: Representation of masculinity and femininity in the media. Gender bias and stereotypes in the portrayal of gender in film, television, internet and print media. Gendered audiences and gender differences in media reception. Issues of pornography and censorship, in the use of women's images in advertising and marketing. The role of media transforming as well as perpetuating gender inequality.

courses.name: Video Production for Non-majors
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce non-majors to the fundamental technical and practical skills needed for digital moving image production. It helps students understand the basic workflow of video production and develop hands-on familiarity with the tools and methods used in creating video content.
courses.description: Fundamental technical skills for digital moving image production including lighting, sound, cinematography, and basic editing. Production of several video projects during the semester.
learning_outcomes: Students will have a profound and consistent overview of the process of media production - concepts, principles and methods.
Students will have a broad overview over the technological issues of video production
Students will be able to develop skills in appropriately implementing techniques of scenario-based design
Students will be able to develop skills for finding, selecting and adapting the appropriate methods and techniques
Students will develop and foster skills of interdisciplinary cooperation as well as dialogue culture.
Students will demonstrate an understanding of professional and ethical responsibility
Students will undertake complete responsibility of a project from idea development to finishing and presentation.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course and video production workflow
Hafta 2: Introduction to Video Technology
Hafta 3: Lenses, Camera Angles and Framing
Hafta 4: In class activity: Shooting exercises
Hafta 5: Continuity in shooting, practice on camera usage
Hafta 6: Continuity in Shooting
Hafta 7: Continuation in class works and tutorials
Hafta 8: Getting started with video editing
Hafta 9: Editing in Practice
Hafta 10: Editing audio and sound design
Hafta 11: Lighting for video
Hafta 12: Critiques
Hafta 13: Final project, pre production session
Hafta 14: Film submissions and screenings.
resources: Required - Hardware: Personal lap top with an editing software

courses.name: Special Topics in Journalism
courses.purpose: This course aims to deepen students' critical understanding of journalism by examining advanced issues and debates in the field.
courses.description: Advanced topics in journalism to deepen students' critical understanding of the field. The complicated nature of fact, truth and evidence; journalism contexts; privacy and publicity; ethics; news agendas; technological change; and journalism applied to specific areas like science, culture, business, and politics.
learning_outcomes: gather news for both traditional and new media
interview “people on the street”
write and edit news stories for both traditional and new media
demonstrate active participation in class through critical discussion, peer interaction, and informed contributions to journalistic debates
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: News production process / News gathering
Hafta 3: Storytelling - News examples
Hafta 4: News writing
Hafta 5: News Assignment #1
Hafta 6: Shooting & Editing
Hafta 7: How to do a news interview
Hafta 8: Interview Assignment- Presentation of proposals & questions
Hafta 9: Presentation of Interview assignments
Hafta 10: News Assignment #2 - Presentation of proposals
Hafta 11: Presentation of news assignments #2
Hafta 12: News Assignment #3 - Presentation of proposals
Hafta 13: Presentation of news assignments #3
Hafta 14: Overall evaluation

courses.name: Documentary
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a basic understanding of the history and forms of documentary cinema. It also introduces the evolution of documentary sub-genres and modes from its emergence to the digital era, with particular attention to newly emerging digital documentary practices.
courses.description: Basic knowledge of the history and forms of documentary cinema. Sub-genres and modes of documentary from its emergence to the digital era, with particular attention to newly emerging digital modes of documentary.

courses.name: Television Genres
courses.purpose: To examine television as a cultural, social, political, and industrial force and to develop critical approaches to understanding it as a medium.
courses.description: Television's role as a cultural, social, political, and industrial force. Evolution of television and strategies for critical inquiry into its nature as a medium, exploring the uses and limitations of genre theory as applied to television, format adaptations, and interactive television.

courses.name: Adaptation in Media
courses.purpose: This course examines why and how stories and ideas are adapted across different media forms, and how adaptation shapes contemporary media culture and franchises. It also introduces students to major debates in adaptation studies, including cross-cultural adaptation and critical approaches to adapted texts.
courses.description: This course focuses on adaptation of content across media: film, literature, television, theater, games, comics, and graphic novels. It explores how adaptation has helped build media franchises with global influence, as well as issues in cross-cultural adaptation, developments in adaptation theory, and critical approaches to adapted texts.
learning_outcomes: Define key critical and theoretical approaches to adaptation and adapted texts
Identify conventions of transmedia adaptation
Design and produce a major project related to adaptation / adaptation studies
Strengthen academic writing, project design, and discussion skills
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course Introduction: New approaches to adaptation
Hafta 2: Fidelity discourse
Hafta 3: Identifying adaptations
Hafta 4: Medium specificity
Hafta 5: Beyond literature
Hafta 6: Cross-cultural adaptation & appropriation
Hafta 7: Cross-cultural adaptation & appropriation (cont'd - Turkish examples)
Hafta 8: Parody & Pastiche
Hafta 9: Remakes, Sequels, & More
Hafta 10: Fan Appropriations
Hafta 11: CASE STUDY: Watchmen (Moore & Gibbons 1987 / Snyder 2009 / Lindelof 2019)
Hafta 12: CASE STUDY: Watchmen (Moore & Gibbons 1987 / Snyder 2009 / Lindelof 2019) cont'd
Hafta 13: EXAM
Hafta 14: Project Presentations & Feedback
resources: Required - Reading: See course Moodle page for a detailed list of course reading and film screenings

courses.name: International Public Relations
courses.purpose: The course examines the role and impact of public relations in international settings, with attention to community and nation building, relationship management, and multinational organizations.
courses.description: The course covers public relations in an international context. It includes case studies and examples from different countries to show how cultural context can influence public relations practices.

courses.name: Creative Producing
courses.purpose: To introduce students to the basic techniques of creative project design and development, with attention to efficiency, creativity, and working within time and budget constraints. The course also aims to help students understand how changing media environments affect funding, marketing, and distribution strategies.
courses.description: Exploration of basic creative project design and development techniques in terms of process efficiency and the role of creativity when working with specific limitations and restrictions of time and budget. Consideration of the drastic change currently taking place in the methods of funding, marketing and distribution due to the effects of a new generation of media. Completion of a research project on various aspects of creative project design and also working on the development of a visual project, from conception of an idea or acquisition of the rights to a text, to correctly identifying the target audience and outlet in order to create the marketing/distribution strategy for the project. Presentation of final projects in the form of complete project proposals.

courses.name: Directing Actors
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the fundamental skills of directing actors for the camera and to develop their understanding of the actor's creative process. It also prepares students to work collaboratively with actors and crew in film, TV, and related media production contexts.
courses.description: Directing Actors course offers instruction and practice in the basics of directing actors for the camera and will guide the students to understand the actor's creative process of building the character. It will introduce students to on-camera performance in various genres, and will address the technical requirements of directing the TV and film actor such as blocking, playing to the camera, shooting out of sequence, and other production considerations. The course includes significant on-camera scene-work, character development, script analysis; breaking down the screenplay into a lined script, a storyboard and a shot list. Casting, rehearsal technique, and blocking issues will be explored in depth. Readings, exercises, screenings and other assignments are designed to sharpen the director's narrative and visual awareness. Through observations and reviewing of the video recordings of exercises following scene study practices, this course will familiarize students with the collaborative and creative director/crew/actor relationship on set.
learning_outcomes: Students who are intending to work in the film and TV sectors will prepare a portfolio to submit during their job applications.
Students who are intending to work in the film and TV sectors will acquire the fundamental skills of directing actors for the camera.
Students will advance their project development and production skills.
Students will undertake complete responsibility of a project from idea development to finishing and presentation.
Students will further their skills in artistic practice, making creative contributions in cinema, TV, digital media and arts as well in relation to creative industries.
Students will learn to collaborate with actors on set as directors and principal crew members.
Students will demonstrate an understanding of professional and ethical responsibility.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to course / Opening Discussion / Outline of Course
Hafta 2: Acting Exercises / Lecture / Monologues
Hafta 3: Acting Exercises / Lecture / Rehearsals and Design for Monologues
Hafta 4: The Shoot for Monologues
Hafta 5: Screening for Monologues / Script Analysis for Midterm Project
Hafta 6: Auditions and Rehearsals for Midterm Project
Hafta 7: The Shoot for Midterm Project
Hafta 8: Finalizing Post for Midterm Project
Hafta 9: Screening for Midterm Project
Hafta 10: Script Analysis for Final Project
Hafta 11: Auditions and Rehearsals for Final Project
Hafta 12: The Shoot for Final Project
Hafta 13: Finalizing Post for Final Project
Hafta 14: Final Screening

courses.name: Post-production Techniques
courses.purpose: To introduce students to a variety of film and media post-production techniques and to help them apply these techniques in a group project.
courses.description: A variety of film and media post-production techniques, including 3D integrations, animation, and green-box installations. Completion of a group project that puts these techniques into practice.
learning_outcomes: Integrate theory with practice through multifaceted communication and design projects.
Design a composition to demonstrate new post-production skills.
Show how post-production techniques work in practice.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course and Syllabus Introduction: What is Post-Production?
Hafta 2: Retrospective on Traditional Filmmaking - Digital Film, Video and Cinema - Project 1: Editing Analysis
Hafta 3: Best Practices for Production - DIT and Data Management
Hafta 4: Editing and Post-Production I
Hafta 5: Editing and Post-Production II
Hafta 6: Multi-cam Editing
Hafta 7: Sound and Integration
Hafta 8: Fundamentals of Color Science
Hafta 9: Color Correction/Grading & Styles
Hafta 10: HDR and Advanced Color Management
Hafta 11: Advanced Techniques and Final Project
Hafta 12: Best Practices Troubleshooting- Final Project 1st Feedback
Hafta 13: Work-In-Progress: Discussion of project drafts / Rough Cut
Hafta 14: Work-In-Progress: Discussion of project drafts / Fine Cut
resources: Modern Post - Workflow and Techniques for Digital Filmmakers, Scott Arundale, 2014, Focal Press
In the Blink of an Eye: A Perspective on Film Editing, Walter Murch, 2001, Silman-James Press

courses.name: Public Relations and Communication Campaigns
courses.purpose: This course introduces the theory and practice of public relations and public communication, with an emphasis on developing effective communication campaign strategies.
courses.description: Theory and practice of public relations and public communication. PR and opinion research, communication process and building effective campaign strategy. Case studies in PR and public communication, commercial and political advertising, humanitarian campaigns. Ethical considerations and impact on society.

courses.name: Special Topics in Advertising
courses.purpose: Study of selected advanced topics in advertisement production and research focusing on various media application and future developments.
courses.description: Creativity and diversity, advertising research and planning of campaigns, generating ideas and strategy, copywriting for television, radio and direct marketing.

courses.name: Media Ethics
courses.purpose: This course examines why ethical issues arise in media practice and how moral principles and legal regulations guide professional decision-making in reporting, editing, and broadcasting.
courses.description: A survey of the current ethical problems and issues in reporting, editing and broadcasting moral principles, legal regulations and their application to these problems. Examination of case studies with special emphasis on questions of privacy and freedom of information.

courses.name: Individual Research Study
courses.purpose: This course aims to acquaint students with research and develop their ability to explore and investigate a topic independently. It also helps students exercise critical thinking and conduct research activities under faculty guidance.
courses.description: Getting acquainted with research. Exploring and investigating a topic. Exercising critical thinking. Performing research activities in the area of computer engineering and science. Doing individual research study, under the guidance of a faculty member. Preparing an academic report or article, and presenting study results.
learning_outcomes: Develop research skills
Able to identify and formulate a complex engineering problem.
Solves complex engineering problems by applying principles of engineering, science, and mathematics.
Able to acquire new knowledge using appropriate learning strategies.
Able to apply new knowledge as needed.
Prepare reports with high standards in terms of content, organization, style and language
Able to make presentations with high standards in terms of grammar, vocabulary, body language, voice quality, understandability, organization, focus and use of visual tools.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Literature survey
Hafta 2: Literature survey
Hafta 3: Research Activity
Hafta 4: Research Activity
Hafta 5: Research Activity
Hafta 6: Research Activity
Hafta 7: Research Activity
Hafta 8: Interim Report
Hafta 9: Research Activity
Hafta 10: Research Activity
Hafta 11: Research Activity
Hafta 12: Research Activity
Hafta 13: Research Activity
Hafta 14: Final Report Preparation

courses.name: Introduction to Microeconomics
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the basic concepts and analytical tools of microeconomics. It aims to help students understand how individual consumers and firms make decisions, how markets function, and how economic policy affects welfare.
courses.description: Introduces microeconomic concepts and analysis, supply and demand analysis, theories of the firm and of individual behavior, competition and monopoly, welfare economics. Application to problems of current economic policy.
learning_outcomes: Can have command of fundamental concepts in microeconomics (K2)
Can have fundamental conceptual knowledge in economics so as to analyze and evaluate real life problems (K6)
Can communicate field related knowledge and skills in speaking. (C1)
Can have the capacity to interpret real life events and improve views about social and behavioral facts by using the body of methodologies and information provided within the field (F4)
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Consumer Theory
Hafta 2: Consumer Theory
Hafta 3: Exchange Economies: General Equilibrium
Hafta 4: Exchange Economies: Welfare
Hafta 5: Demand
Hafta 6: Elasticity
Hafta 7: Producer Theory
Hafta 8: Producer Theory
Hafta 9: Supply
Hafta 10: Competitive Equilibrium
Hafta 11: Economic Efficiency and Government Policies
Hafta 12: Market Power: Monopoly, Monopolistic Competition, Oligopoly
Hafta 13: Markets for Labor and Other Factors of Production
Hafta 14: Externalities and Coase Theorem
resources: Economics, R. Glenn Hubbard abd Anthony Patrick O'Brien, 6th Edition, Pearson

courses.name: Introduction to Macroeconomics
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the main issues and tools of macroeconomics. It aims to build a foundation for understanding how aggregate economic variables are determined and how policy affects the economy.
courses.description: An overview of macroeconomic issues: the determination of output, employment, unemployment, interest rates, and inflation. Monetary and fiscal policies are discussed as well as international economic issues. Introduces basic models of macroeconomics and illustrates principles with the experience of Turkish and foreign economies.
learning_outcomes: Have basic conceptual knowledge of the fundamental concepts of the field. (K1)
Develop solid understanding of the causal relationships in the theoretical models of the field. (K4)
Can follow, analyze and interpret the basic theoretical relationships in the field related data. (S1)
Develop the capacity to utilize and apply the knowledge learned in further education, in life-long analysis of events and decision making. (C2)
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Macroeconomics; Measuring Total Output and Income
Hafta 2: Balance of Payments
Hafta 3: Inflation/Unemployment
Hafta 4: Growth Facts/Solow Model
Hafta 5: Cyclical Facts and Goods Market Equilibrium: Aggregate Expenditure and Output
Hafta 6: Money and Banking
Hafta 7: Midterm
Hafta 8: Term structure of interest rates/Cryptoassets
Hafta 9: Money Market and Central Banking
Hafta 10: IS/LM
Hafta 11: Exchange Rates
Hafta 12: AS/AD
Hafta 13: Fiscal Policy/Phillips Curve
Hafta 14: Phillips Curve and Policy Analysis
resources: Economics, R. G. Hubbard and A. P. OBrien, 5th Edition, Pearson/ Prentice Hall
Macroeconomics, Dornbusch, R., S. Fischer and R. Startz, 11th Edition

courses.name: Creative Sound Design and Synthesis
courses.purpose: To introduce the aesthetic, theoretical, and technical foundations of sound design and sound synthesis, and to develop students' ability to apply them in creative and research-oriented contexts.
courses.description: Aesthetic, theoretical and technical aspects of sound design and sound synthesis. Exploration of artistic approaches to sound in visual and immersive environments, and linear and non-linear media. Hands-on work as both a creative sound practice and an interdisciplinary research pursuit.

courses.name: Principles of Economics
courses.purpose: Introduces students to the basic concepts of microeconomics and macroeconomics and builds a foundation for understanding economic conditions and government decisions.
courses.description: Introduces the basic concepts of microeconomics and macroeconomics: supply and demand analysis, production factors, market structures, GDP, unemployment, inflation, fiscal policy, monetary policy, and the banking system.
learning_outcomes: Can grasp the fundamental concepts and analytical framework of current economic theory.
Can obtain information from the field and analyse the decisions and behavior of firms and consumers in the markets.
Can analyse and critically evaluate the government policies involving economic issues.
Can formulate and defend a personal stance involving economic issues by utilizing the knowledge acquired with a critical approach by investigating casual relationships.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Economics: Foundations and Models; Trade-offs, Comparative Advantage, and the Market System
Hafta 2: Where Prices Come From: The Interaction of Demand and Supply
Hafta 3: Economic Efficiency, Government Price Setting, and Taxes
Hafta 4: Elasticity: The Responsiveness of Demand and Supply
Hafta 5: Technology, Production, and Costs
Hafta 6: Firms in Perfectly Competitive Markets
Hafta 7: Monopolistic Competition: The Competitive Model in a More Realistic Setting; Oligopoly: Firms in Less Competitive Markets; Monopoly and Antitrust Policy
Hafta 8: GDP: Measuring Total Production and Income
Hafta 9: Unemployment and Inflation
Hafta 10: Aggregate Expenditure and Output in the Short Run
Hafta 11: Aggregate Demand and Aggregate Supply Analysis
Hafta 12: Money, Banks, and the Federal Reserve System
Hafta 13: Monetary Policy; Fiscal Policy
Hafta 14: Macroeconomics in an open Economy
resources: Economics, Hubbard&O'Brien, 8th Ed., Pearson Prentice Hall

courses.name: Principles of Economics I
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the basic concepts and methods of microeconomics and explains how economists analyze issues at the individual, household, and firm levels.
courses.description: Introduction to basic microeconomic concepts. What microeconomics is about. Economists' approach to current microeconomic issues at the individual household and firm level. Government's micro policies in providing incentives for production, consumption, and technology choice. Current debates on firm behavior, the new economy, privatization, and efficient use of resources. Market organizations, welfare, and firm objectives.

courses.name: Principles of Economics II
courses.purpose: This course provides an overview of macroeconomic issues and the main concepts used to analyze them. It aims to help students understand how national income, employment, investment, and foreign trade are determined and influenced by government policies.
courses.description: An overview of macroeconomic issues. The concept of the gross national product and its determination. Theories of macroeconomic equilibrium and government policies to effect the gross national product, employment, investment, and foreign trade. Current issues on the global economy, international economic organization and Turkey's role in a changing world macroeconomic environment.

courses.name: Microeconomics: Competitive Markets
courses.purpose: This course is the first part of an intermediate level microeconomics sequence. It aims to build a solid foundation for analysing consumer and firm behaviour and understanding competitive market outcomes.
courses.description: Consumer theory, theory of the firm, and partial equilibrium theory are studied in depth. Applications of the partial equilibrium model to public finance and trade are also covered.
learning_outcomes: Use the techniques in differential calculus to analyse the behaviour of economic agents, such as consumers and firms (K1)
Analyse changes in equilibrium outcomes due to changes in exogenous variables (K2)
Characterise the partial equilibrium of a perfectly competitive market (W2)
Interpret the solution of a consumer's or firm's optimisation problem (C1)
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Preference, Choice, and Utility
Hafta 2: Preference, Choice, and Utility
Hafta 3: Demand Functions
Hafta 4: Demand Functions
Hafta 5: Demand Functions
Hafta 6: Production; Cost Minimization; Profit Maximization
Hafta 7: Production; Cost Minimization; Profit Maximization
Hafta 8: Production; Cost Minimization; Profit Maximization
Hafta 9: Consumer and Producer Surplus; Social Welfare
Hafta 10: Consumer and Producer Surplus; Social Welfare
Hafta 11: Partial Equilibrium Competitive Model
Hafta 12: Partial Equilibrium Competitive Model
Hafta 13: Edgeworth Box Economies and the General Equilibrium
Hafta 14: Edgeworth Box Economies and the General Equilibrium

courses.name: Macroeconomics: Business Cycles
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to intermediate macroeconomic analysis with a focus on business cycles. It aims to develop an understanding of how national income, inflation, unemployment, aggregate supply, and policy tools interact in the short and medium run.
courses.description: This course is the first part of an intermediate level macroeconomics sequence. Besides the analysis of national income determination, inflation and unemployment, analysis of aggregate supply and growth theories will be introduced. Within the frameworks of basic business cycle models, demand side equilibrium, and the IS/LM models (including its open economy version) fiscal and monetary policy effectiveness will be studied.
learning_outcomes: Can formulate and solve the optimization problems of various economic agents
Can formulate and solve the long-run optimization problems of various economic agents
Analyze the long-run general equilibrium
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Two-Period Consumption-Saving Model in Endowment Economy
Hafta 2: Two-Period Consumption-Saving Model in Endowment Economy (cont'd)
Hafta 3: Theories of Consumption Determination
Hafta 4: Production Economy
Hafta 5: Fiscal Policy
Hafta 6: Money in utility
Hafta 7: Real Business Cycle Models: Shocks
Hafta 8: Real Business Cycle Models: Empirical Relevance
Hafta 9: From Flexible to Sticky Prices
Hafta 10: Intertemporal Theory of the Current Account
Hafta 11: Current Account Determination in a Production Economy
Hafta 12: Determinants of Real Exchange Rates
Hafta 13: Deteminants of Real Exchange Rates (cont'd)
Hafta 14: Nominal Rigidity, Exchange Rate Policy, Unemployment
resources: Intermediate Macroeconomics, Julio Garlin, Robert Lester and Eric Sims, 2021
International Macroeconomics, Stephanie Schmitt-Grohe, Martin Uribe, Micheal Woodford, 2022, Princeton University Press
prerequisites: ECON 225 / MATH 220 / MATH 223 / MATH 227 / MATH 241 / MATH 225

courses.name: Macroeconomics: Growth
courses.purpose: This course is the second part of the intermediate macroeconomics sequence and aims to build the microeconomic foundations needed to study macroeconomic models. It introduces the analytical tools required to understand long-run growth and related macroeconomic policy issues.
courses.description: The course focuses on micro foundations for macro models. Topics include consumption and investment theories, the inflation-unemployment trade-off, fiscal and monetary policy design and institutions, open economy macroeconomics, exchange rate models, current account dynamics, and intertemporal optimization by the representative agent.
learning_outcomes: Analyze dynamic general equilibrium using Overlapping Generations Framework (K1, S1, L1, F4).
Analyze the long-run macroeconomic issues using Overlapping Generations Framework.
Overview of the Growth Models (K1, S1, L1, F4).
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Growth Facts
Hafta 2: Introduction: Growth Empirics
Hafta 3: Introduction: Growth Accounting
Hafta 4: The Solow-Swan Model
Hafta 5: The Solow Growth Model with Technological Progress
Hafta 6: The Solow growth Model with Human Capital
Hafta 7: Overlapping Generations Model: Competitive Equilibrium
Hafta 8: Overlapping Generations Model: Optimality
Hafta 9: Ramsey-Cass-Koopmans Models
Hafta 10: Optimal Growth and Dynamic Programming
Hafta 11: The AK Model
Hafta 12: The AK Model with Physical and Human Capital
Hafta 13: Endogenous Technological Change
Hafta 14: Innovation and Growth
resources: Intermediate Macroeconomics, Julio Garin, Robert Lester and Eric Sims
Introducing Advanced Macroeconomics: Growth and Business Cycles, Peter Birch Sorensen and Hans Jorgen Whitta-Jacobsen
Macroeconomic Theory: A Dynamic General Approach, M. Wickens, 2011
Economic Growth, D.N. Weil, Pearson
Introduction to Economic Growth, C.I. Jones and D. Vollrath, Norton & Company
Economic Dynamics, G. Gandolfo, 1995, Springer-Verlag

courses.name: Economic Theory for Engineers
courses.purpose: This course is designed to introduce Engineering students to the fundamentals of economic analysis. It aims to provide students with a foundation for understanding how economic principles can be applied to engineering-related decision making.
courses.description: This course provides an introduction to micro and macroeconomic principles and concepts. It also covers intermediate microeconomic analysis topics, including the analysis of the consumer, the firm, and the market.
learning_outcomes: Familiarity with core concepts of economics, being able to think clearly using these concepts.
Ability to use economic theory to verbally, graphically and analytically analyze household and firm decisions, market outcomes and policy choices.
Ability to analyze changes in equilibrium outcomes due to changes in exogenous variables.
Ability to analyze social science questions using quantitative methods, understanding their uses and limitations.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Chp3: Preferences and Utility
Hafta 2: Chp4: Utility Maximization and Choice
Hafta 3: Chp5: Income and Substitution Effects
Hafta 4: Chp9: Production Functions
Hafta 5: Chp10: Cost Functions
Hafta 6: Chp11: Profit Maximization
Hafta 7: Chp12: The Partial Equilibrium Competitive Model
Hafta 8: Chp12: The Partial Equilibrium Competitive Model
Hafta 9: Chp7: Uncertainty
Hafta 10: Chp7: Uncertainty; Chp8: Game Theory
Hafta 11: Chp8: Game Theory; Chp14: Monopoly
Hafta 12: Chp14: Monopoly; Chp15: Imperfect Competition
Hafta 13: Chp15: Imperfect Competition
Hafta 14: Chp19: Measuring Total Production and Income; Chp20: Unemployment and Inflation
resources: Microeconomic Theory: Basic Principles and Extensions, Walter Nicholson and Christopher Snyder
Economics, R. Glenn Hubbard and Anthony P. O’Brien
Microeconomics: An Intuitive Approach with Calculus, Nechyba, Thomas, 2nd, Cengage Learnin

courses.name: Probability and Statistical Distributions
courses.description: Descriptive statistics. Fundamental concepts of probability. Combinatorial methods. Conditional probability. Random variables, univariate and bivariate distribution. Expectation, variance, covariance and correlation. Some commonly used discrete and continuous probability distributions, the law of large numbers and the central limit theorem.
learning_outcomes: Correctly derive summary statistics for both theoretical and real world random variables.
Correctly conduct statistical inference using numerical and graphical methods in both theoretical and real data settings.
Correctly apply basic probability concepts and results to accurately derive the likelihood of occurrence of events of interest.
Correctly conduct univariate statistical inference using discrete and continuous probability distributions in theoretical and real data settings.
Correctly conduct multivariate statistical inference using discrete and continuous probability distributions in theoretical and real data settings.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Describing data: Taxonomy of data, Representation of distributions
Hafta 2: Describing data: Measures of central tendency, Measures of dispersion, Chebyshev’s inequality
Hafta 3: Describing data: Measures of association for bivariate data, Recap
Hafta 4: Probability basics: Modeling a random experiment, Probability postulates, Methods of assigning probability
Hafta 5: Probability basics: Classical probability, Relative frequency probability, Subjective probability
Hafta 6: Probability basics: Conditional, Joint and Marginal probabilities, Independence of events, Bayes theorem
Hafta 7: Random variables: Definition, CDF, PDF
Hafta 8: Random variables: Expected value, Variance
Hafta 9: Random variables: Discrete probability laws: Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Hypergeometric, Geometric, Negative Binomial, Discrete Uniform, Triangular
Hafta 10: Random variables: Discrete probability laws: Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Hypergeometric, Geometric, Negative Binomial, Discrete Uniform, Triangular
Hafta 11: Random variables: Continuous probability laws: Uniform, Exponential and Normal
Hafta 12: Random variables: Continuous probability laws: Uniform, Exponential and Normal
Hafta 13: Sampling distributions: Chebyshev’s theorem, Law of Large Numbers theorem, Central Limit Theorem
Hafta 14: Sampling distributions: Sample mean, Sample proportion and Sample variance
resources: Teaching page, Mustafa Eray Yucel, Intermittently updated, Published online at Instructor's web page

courses.name: Microeconomics: Strategic Interactions
courses.purpose: This course aims to build students' understanding of how strategic interactions shape economic outcomes in markets and other settings. It prepares students to analyze equilibrium, competition, uncertainty, and game-theoretic behavior in intermediate microeconomics.
courses.description: This course is the second part of an intermediate level microeconomics sequence. Topics covered are: general equilibrium, welfare economics, monopoly, imperfect competition, externalities and public goods, uncertainty and information, and game theory.
learning_outcomes: Can distinguish between the economic implications of perfectly versus imperfectly competitive markets (K2)
Can apply the tools equipped for the study of markets and economic behavior under certainty to the world with uncertainty (S1)
Can come up with predictions for the outcome of strategic interactions (K1)
Have an eclectic attitude towards theories of economic behavior (F3)
Use field-related knowledge while making real-life decisions (S4)
Identify deficiencies in knowledge and practice related to the field (L3)
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Choice and preferences
Hafta 2: Choice and preferences
Hafta 3: Competitive equilibrium in housing markets
Hafta 4: Welfare Theorems and special topics
Hafta 5: Normal Form Games and Nash equilibrium
Hafta 6: Cournot duopoly
Hafta 7: Bertrand price competition
Hafta 8: Special Topics: Applications
Hafta 9: Dominant Strategy Equilibrium and Auctions
Hafta 10: Applications such as the marriage problem
Hafta 11: Applications such as the marriage game
Hafta 12: Extensive form games and Subgame Perfect Nash Equilibrium
Hafta 13: Applications: Ultimatom game and bargaining
Hafta 14: Special Topics
prerequisites: ECON 225 / MATH 220 / MATH 223 / MATH 227 / MATH 241 / MATH 225

courses.name: Statistical Estimation and Inference
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamental concepts and methods of statistical estimation and inference used in economics and related fields. It prepares students to analyze data, draw conclusions from samples, and interpret statistical results in empirical applications.
courses.description: Sampling and sampling distributions. Introduction to inference. Point and interval estimation. Hypothesis testing. Small sample distributions (t, X2, F). Introduction to analysis of variance, regression and distribution free methods. Applications using statistical computer programs.
learning_outcomes: Students can formally describe the theoretical details of the statistical methods they have to utilize for a given task. (K1)
Students can correctly apply hypothesis testing methods in order to investigate unobservable properties of economic and financial datasets. (S1)
Students can utilize the concepts of correlation and covariance in order to formally analyse the co-movements between pairs of economic or financial variables. (S1)
Students can conduct introductory level econometric analysis using the least squares estimation method in order to investigate linear relationships between economic and financial variables. (S1)
Students can correctly interpret and report the empirical results of a statistical procedure. (C1)
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Topics of ECON 221 deferred to ECON 222 if any, Point estimators: Derivation, Properties
Hafta 2: Confidence intervals: One population
Hafta 3: Confidence intervals, Hypothesis testing: One population
Hafta 4: Hypothesis testing: One population
Hafta 5: Confidence intervals: Two populations
Hafta 6: Confidence intervals, Hypothesis testing: Two populations
Hafta 7: Hypothesis testing: Two populations
Hafta 8: Recap: Confidence intervals and Hypothesis testing
Hafta 9: Extents of Linear Regression Analysis; econometric modeling; Occam's razor and the principle of parsimony
Hafta 10: Model of mean, Simple Linear Regression (SLR) model, Multiple Linear Regression (MLR) model; functional forms and elasticity calculations
Hafta 11: Deriving the estimators of the linear regression parameters, in the model of mean, in SLR and in MLR
Hafta 12: Goodness of fit; Modeling examples
Hafta 13: Inference in Linear Regression Analysis: relating the t tests and F tests
Hafta 14: Model building, reduction and inference
resources: Teaching page, Mustafa Eray Yucel, Intermittently updated, Published online at Instructor's web page

courses.name: Mathematics for Economists
courses.description: Multivariate calculus, constraint optimization, Hessians, implicit function theorem, difference equations.
learning_outcomes: Find the span of a set of vectors. Determine if a set of vectors is linearly independent. Determine is a set of vectors forms a basis (K1, L2)
Setup and solve system of linear equations (K1, L2)
Perform various calculations with matrices. Find eigenvalues and eigenvectors (K1, L2)
Find limits. Decide if a set is closed/open, compact, and convex. (K1, L2)
Set and solve unconstrained and constrained optimization problems (K1, L2)
Set and solve difference and differential equations (K1, L2)
Set and solve dynamic optimization problems (K1, L2)
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Euclidean (vector and metric) spaces.
Hafta 2: Introduction to Euclidean (vector and metric) spaces.
Hafta 3: Unconstrained optimization.
Hafta 4: Unconstrained optimization.
Hafta 5: Constrained optimization.
Hafta 6: Constrained optimization.
Hafta 7: Constrained optimization.
Hafta 8: Matrix algebra
Hafta 9: Matrix algebra
Hafta 10: Matrix algebra
Hafta 11: Difference equations.
Hafta 12: Difference equations.
Hafta 13: Dynamic programming.
Hafta 14: Dynamic programming.

courses.name: Seminar in Energy Economics
courses.purpose: The purpose of the course is to make students get acquainted with the current problems/challenges of energy markets. The course will evolve around a single project that each group will carry their project throughout the semester on selected energy issues.
courses.description: The course focuses on selected energy issues through a semester-long group project. Students examine the structure and functioning of an energy market of their choice, its interrelations with other energy and financial markets, and the major problems and challenges facing that market.
learning_outcomes: Know the structure and workings of an energy market of their choice.
Understand the interrelations between energy markets and other energy/financial markets.
Be aware of important problems and challenges of the selected energy market.

courses.name: Econometrics
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the core ideas and methods of econometrics so they can analyze economic data rigorously. It emphasizes how regression models are used to study economic relationships and to make statistical inference in applied settings.
courses.description: Introduction of linear multiple regression model, inference, hypothesis testing; and maximum-likehood methods. Illustration from economics and application of these concepts to economic problems will be emphasized. The course covers Gauss-Markov assumptions and violation of the assumptions such as heteroskedasticity, serial correlation and errors variables.
learning_outcomes: Correctly communicate the difference between causality and correlation and the idea of ceteris paribus in linear regression analysis.
Correctly interpret the relationships between dependent and independent variables in simple and multiple linear regression.
Correctly conduct statistical inference using linear regressions analysis in theoretical settings.
Correctly conduct statistical inference using linear regressions analysis using statistical software packages on real world data.
Correctly demonstrate the theoretical statistical properties for estimators obtained in linear regression analysis.
Correctly identify and communicate using examples, the conditions under which linear regression fails or needs to be modified.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review of Statistics, Simple Linear Regression and Multiple Linear Regression
Hafta 2: Review of Statistics, Simple Linear Regression and Multiple Linear Regression
Hafta 3: Violations to Gauss-Markov assumptions: Random Sampling
Hafta 4: Violations to Gauss-Markov assumptions: Multicollinearity
Hafta 5: Violations to Gauss-Markov assumptions: Heteroskedasticity
Hafta 6: Violations to Gauss-Markov assumptions: Serial Correlation
Hafta 7: Violations to Gauss-Markov assumptions: Endogeneity
Hafta 8: Simultaneous Equation Models
Hafta 9: Time Series: ARMA Models
Hafta 10: Time Series: VAR Models
Hafta 11: Panel Data Estimation
Hafta 12: Maximum Likelihood Estimation
Hafta 13: Limited Dependent Variables: Binary Choice Models
Hafta 14: Limited Dependent Variables: Binary Choice Models
resources: Introductory Econometrics: A Modern Approach, Jeffrey M Wooldridge

courses.name: Monetary Economics
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a solid understanding of the role of money, banking, and monetary policy in the economy. It also helps students connect monetary theory with current policy issues and financial developments.
courses.description: Basic models of money and monetary economics; the role of expectations; asset pricing models with special reference to equities and the term structure of interest; the Phillips curve; banking and financial intermediaries; monetary and fiscal policies.
learning_outcomes: Master the newest developments in monetary theory and monetary policy practices
Tie the learned material to recent developments
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Why study money?
Hafta 2: Financial markets and money
Hafta 3: Understanding interest rates, the behavior of interest rates.
Hafta 4: The risk and term structure of interest rates
Hafta 5: An economic analysis of financial structure, banking and the management of financial institutions
Hafta 6: First midterm examination
Hafta 7: Financial crises in emerging economies
Hafta 8: Tools of monetary policy
Hafta 9: The conduct of monetary policy
Hafta 10: Foreign exchange markets and interest parity condition
Hafta 11: International financial system
Hafta 12: Monetary Policy Theory and the Role of Expectations
Hafta 13: Transmission Mechanisms of Monetary Policy
Hafta 14: Central Banking and Monetary Policy in Turkey
resources: Economics of Money, Banking and Financial Markets, Frederic S. Mishkin, Global Edition, 12/E, 2019, Pearson
The Economics of Central Banking, Livio Stracca, 2019, Routledge

courses.name: Economics of Monetary Union
courses.description: Contemporary issues of monetary integration; the European Monetary Union experience; Euro zone; monetary and fiscal policies for monetary union targets; the Maastricht Criteria; Central Bank Independence and Price Stability.

courses.name: Advanced Econometrics
courses.description: Identification and estimation of simultaneous equation models. Advanced topics such as Generalized Least Squares, instrumental variables, non-linear regression techniques and limited dependent variable models. An introduction to time-series analysis such as stationary and nonstationary processes, VARs, unit roots, and cointegration.

courses.name: International Trade
courses.purpose: To introduce the theory of international trade and show how it is applied in commercial policy.
courses.description: Theory of international trade and applications in commercial policy.

courses.name: International Macroeconomics and Finance
courses.purpose: To examine how international economic relations are adjusted, with particular attention to foreign exchange markets, the balance of payments, and the international monetary system.
courses.description: Adjustment in international economic relations with attention to foreign exchange markets, balance of payments, and the international monetary system.

courses.name: Fiscal Economics I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the main theories and tools used to analyze fiscal policy and government finance. It aims to help students understand how taxation and public spending affect economic behavior and economic outcomes.
courses.description: First and second best fiscal theory. Incidence models. Economic response to taxation. Quantitative analysis of economic effects of fiscal instruments and fiscal changes. Government finance statistics; tax and expenditure structures; the budget and government financing; fiscal management in Turkey and abroad.

courses.name: Analytical Writing for Economist
courses.purpose: For students in the department of economics, writing analytically and effectively is important. The course aims to develop students' analytical writing and presentation skills, with emphasis on discussing, presenting, and conveying ideas in the field of economics.
courses.description: The coursework consists of writing exercises and presentations/discussions on economic topics and issues.

courses.name: Topics in Public Finance
courses.purpose: Introduces the fundamentals of deficit financing and examines the role of the bureaucracy and its relations with the government, with particular emphasis on the Treasury and its functioning.
courses.description: This course covers fundamentals of deficit financing, the role of the bureaucracy and its relations with the government, the role of the Treasury and how it functions, new orientalism in government financial management, the government balance sheet approach, and expenditure management and control issues.

courses.name: Contemporary Issues in Turkish Economy
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the contemporary problems confronting the Turkish economy and to provide an understanding of the structural adjustment reforms and macroeconomic policies used to address them.
courses.description: Contemporary problems confronting the Turkish economy, structural adjustment reforms and macroeconomic policies.

courses.name: Contemporary Issues in Turkish Economy II
courses.purpose: A problem solving-based investigation of the contemporary socio-economic problems of the Turkish economy with a special focus on the post-2000 period. It aims to develop project-based resolutions to current problems in Turkey by strengthening students' ability to define problems and generate workable policy proposals.
courses.description: The course covers contemporary socio-economic problems of the Turkish economy, with emphasis on the post-2000 period. It focuses on the problems to be covered, solution approaches, and policy perspectives in a global context, using scientific method and project development as guiding tools.
learning_outcomes: Define contemporary socio-economic problems of the Turkish economy.
Develop project-based and workable policy proposals for current problems in Turkey.
Apply scientific method and project development techniques to economic issues.
Improve professional toolset and communication skills.

courses.name: Development of Economic Thought
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the historical development of economic thought and to help them understand how major economic ideas evolved over time. It also connects classical and modern theories to contemporary economic issues.
courses.description: The main topic of this course is the developments in the theoretical aspects of economics after the marginal utility revolution. The course starts from the 1870s with the contributions of major economists to marginal utility theory and its applications. It proceeds with general and partial equilibrium analyses. Finally, Keynesian income determination, Monetarism, New Classical economics, and post-Keynesian approaches are examined.
learning_outcomes: Can analyze the origins of economic differences between developed and developing nations and evaluate various theories for these reasons.

Can distinguish the evolution of economic thought and concepts throughout history and compare various theoretical ideas in economics, such as Classical, Neoclassical, Keynesian, Monetarist, and Post-Keynesian schools.

Can derive lessons from history and relate these issues to today's problems, including Ricardo's rent, the Great Depression of 1929, the Great Recession of 2007, demographic transition, population problems, and international monetary systems.
Can analyze international and national political economy in light of various macroeconomic theories, especially Keynesian, Monetarist, Rational Expectationist, and Supply-Sider approaches.
Can analyze recent trends and changes in economic theory and application, including behavioral economics, institutional economics, and evolutionary economics.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Types of Histories in Economics: Economic History and History of Economic Thought
Hafta 2: Emergence of Markets and Capitalism: Industrial Revolution: Why Did It Occur in England and not in France?
Hafta 3: Institutional Explanations: Why Nations Fail by D. Acemoglu and Comparison with Another Institutionalist: K. Marx
Hafta 4: Mercantilism and The Rise of Classical School: Idea of Economic Growth in A. Smith, T. Malthus, D. Ricardo and K. Marx; General Tendencies and Applications related with Economic Inequality
Hafta 5: Rise of Classical School Continues
Hafta 6: Fall of Classical School and Rise of Marginalist Revolution in Europe (Cournot, Menger, Jevons) and Late Marginalism: A. Marshall
Hafta 7: Fall of Classical School and Rise of Marginalist Revolution in Europe (Cournot, Menger, Jevons) and Late Marginalism: A. Marshall Continues
Hafta 8: Midterm
Hafta 9: Demographic Transition
Hafta 10: The Great Depression
Hafta 11: John Maynard Keynes and Keynesian Economics
Hafta 12: Keynesian Macroeconomics Continues
Hafta 13: Contemporary Macroeconomics and a Documentary Movie
Hafta 14: Recent Trends in Economics
resources: A Little History of Economics, Niall Kishtainy, 2018, Yale Press
History of Economic Thought and Method, Robert Ekelund and Robert Hebert, 2010
Economic History of Europe, Karl Gunnar Persson, 2012
Why Nations Fail?, D. Acemoğlu and J. A. Robinson, 2012, Crown Business
Visions of Inequality, Branko Milanovic

courses.name: Environmental Economics
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the economic analysis of environmental problems and policy responses. It focuses on how economic tools can be used to evaluate environmental damage and the costs and benefits of alternative abatement strategies.
courses.description: Analysis of environmental problems, cost-benefit analysis, techniques, costs of abatement.

courses.name: Public Sector Governance
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the theoretical and practical dimensions of quality and performance management in public sector service delivery. It also examines the transition from public administration culture to public sector management and the implications of new public management approaches.
courses.description: The course covers quality and performance management in the public sector, including the advantages and disadvantages of new public management culture. It explores the shift from public administration culture to public sector management and elaborates on strategic and performance management, public sector management reforms, financial management, personnel management, quality management, audit issues, and citizen participation in decision-making processes.

courses.name: Applied Econometrics
courses.purpose: The course emphasizes the application of econometric estimation techniques and bridges the gap between the theoretical presentation of the econometrics introductory course and the use of econometrics to answer questions in various research agendas. It is designed to provide students with a hands-on environment in which they learn how to apply economic theories to real data by selecting the most appropriate estimation technique.
courses.description: Various case studies from empirical applications of topics in microeconomics and macroeconomics are examined. Students are expected to build up their abilities in combining theory courses with statistical modeling and estimation. The course also aims to develop students' abilities in conducting empirical research and in evaluating published empirical work.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic fundamental principles of empirical research and the methodology of published research output.

Develop skills to follow the details of the empirical estimation method, and learn the limitation of each technique, acquire a comprehensive understanding of econometric software, STATA in the present semester.
Develop skills that will enable students to independently follow and develop a critical understanding of empirical research.
Acquire skills that will both help critically to evaluate existing research and to perform sound empirical analysis.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review of Multiple Regression I
Hafta 2: Review of Multiple Regression and Stata introduction
Hafta 3: Panel Data FEI
Hafta 4: Panel Data FE II and Stata Application
Hafta 5: Instrumental Variable Estimation I
Hafta 6: Instrumental Variable Estimation II
Hafta 7: Limited Dependent Variable Models I, Logit and Probit
Hafta 8: Limited Dependent Variable Models II, Tobit and Stata Application
Hafta 9: Causal Effects I (Diff on Diff)
Hafta 10: Causal Effects II, stata applications
Hafta 11: Event Study Design
Hafta 12: Non Parametric Techniques
Hafta 13: Presentations of Projects
Hafta 14: Presentations of Projects, Review
resources: Introductory Econometrics, Jeffrey M. Wooldridge, 2015, Nelson Education
Introduction to Economics, Stock Watson, 2nd edition, Pearson
Microeconomics Using Stata Revisited, Cameron Triverdi, 2010, Stata Press
Microeconometrics: Methods and Applications, Cameron and Triverdi, 2010, Cambridge Press

courses.name: Industrial Organization
courses.purpose: This course will survey the current thinking and issues in industrial organization and regulation. It aims to examine in some depth how firms behave in a market economy and to review basic forms of state intervention in modern capitalist economics.
courses.description: The course provides background on firm behavior and the legal environment in which firms operate. It reviews the rationale and experience of antitrust and competition policies. The second semester concentrates on the regulation of private monopolies.

courses.name: Game Theory
courses.purpose: This course introduces the theory of games and the analytical tools used to model strategic interactions among decision-makers such as consumers, firms, politicians, and governments. It aims to build a foundation in noncooperative game theory and its applications.
courses.description: This course covers the basic theory of noncooperative game theory, including normal form games, dominant and undominated strategies, mixed strategies, Bayesian games, extensive form games with perfect information, subgame perfect equilibrium, repeated games, general extensive form games, and special topics. A variety of applications of game theory will also be discussed.
learning_outcomes: Understanding the concept of games and strategic interactions.
Understanding the concept of equilibrium in games.
Understanding normal form games, domination, and Nash equilibria.
Understanding extensive form games and subgame perfect equilibria.
Understanding Bayesian equilibria, Bayesian games and repeated games.
Understanding the application of game theory tools to real world examples and how these help in assessing mechanisms and policies.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Normal form games
Hafta 2: Normal form Games
Hafta 3: Dominant and undominated strategies - Iterated elimination of dominated strategies
Hafta 4: Mixed strategies
Hafta 5: Mixed Strategies
Hafta 6: Bayesian Games
Hafta 7: Bayesian Games
Hafta 8: Extensive form Games with Perfect Information
Hafta 9: Subgame Perfect Equilibrium
Hafta 10: Repeated Games
Hafta 11: Repeated Games
Hafta 12: General Extensive form games
Hafta 13: General Extensive form games
Hafta 14: Special Topics

courses.name: Advanced Macroeconomic Theory
courses.purpose: This course introduces advanced topics in microeconomics and macroeconomics to help students understand how macroeconomic outcomes are shaped by policy, market behavior, and equilibrium interactions.
courses.description: Special topics in micro and macroeconomics. Topics include inflation and unemployment, theories of macroeconomic policies, economic stabilization, theories of the consumer and the firm, welfare, and general equilibrium analysis.

courses.name: Topics in Game Theory
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of ECON 439-Game Theory I and aims to deepen students' understanding of advanced game theory topics. It focuses on sequential and repeated games, Bayesian games, equilibrium refinements, and cooperative game theory, while also discussing theoretical implications and applications.
courses.description: This course covers advanced topics in game theory, including sequential and repeated games, games with imperfect/incomplete information, Bayesian games, equilibrium refinements such as sequential equilibrium and trembling hand perfect equilibrium, and cooperative game theory. It also introduces coalitional games with and without transferable payoffs, Shapley value, core, kernel, and nucleolus, together with their implications and applications.
learning_outcomes: Have advanced level of fundamental conceptual knowledge so as to consider its reflections in practice.
Analyze theoretical knowledge and evaluate its reflections in practice.
Solve a field-related problem both as a team member and as an independent individual.
Aware of what and how to learn.
Communicate field related knowledge and skills in writing and speaking.
Have command of fundamental concepts in social and behavioral sciences and relationships between concepts.
Use field related knowledge to make decisions, implement and apply them.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Allocation in claims and bankruptcy problems.
Hafta 2: Allocation in claims and bankruptcy problems.
Hafta 3: Allocation in claims and bankruptcy problems.
Hafta 4: Allocation in claims and bankruptcy problems.
Hafta 5: Allocation when preferences are single-peaked.
Hafta 6: Allocation when preferences are single-peaked.
Hafta 7: Allocation when preferences are single-peaked.
Hafta 8: Allocation when preferences are single-peaked.
Hafta 9: Social choice/voting.
Hafta 10: Social choice/voting.
Hafta 11: Social choice/voting.
Hafta 12: Coalitional form games.
Hafta 13: Coalitional form games.
Hafta 14: Coalitional form games.
resources: An Introduction to Game Theory, M.J. Osborne, 2009, Oxford University Press
Lecture Notes

courses.name: Turkish and World Economy Between Industrial Revolution and 1950
courses.description: Growth and developments in Turkey and the World from World War I to present. The application of economic analysis to historical issues. Topics: Great Depression, the interwar period, the developments in Soviet type economies since 1917, the Bretton Woods System, the collapse of the Bretton Woods System, the collapse of communism, Turkish economy during the Great Depression and thereafter.

courses.name: Contract Theory and Mechanism Design
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to analyze asymmetric information in markets and organizations.
courses.description: Selective topics from mechanism design, contract theory, principal-agent problems, global games and information aggregation will be covered.

courses.name: Bargaining Theory and Experiments
courses.purpose: Our objective is to introduce students, including those from potentially different majors, to the key concepts in bargaining, the major approaches used in modelling and studying bargaining, and the interactions between these approaches. The course also aims to familiarize students with special topics, current trends, and future directions in bargaining research.
courses.description: The course covers bargaining in economics and in other fields, and introduces the axiomatic approach, strategic approach, and experimental approach. It presents the theoretical foundations of bargaining, followed by experimental and behavioral methods, and occasionally includes research from social psychology and management. In-class and field experiments may also be conducted to support understanding through practice.
learning_outcomes: Have advanced level of knowledge on theories of bargaining so as to consider its reflections in practice (K1)
Analyse theoretical knowledge and evaluate its reflections in practice (S1)
Access to authentic resources for information (S2)
Use bargaining theories and stylized facts from bargaining experiments while negotiating in real-life (S4)
Identify deficiencies in knowledge and practice related to the field (L3)
Solve a field-related problem both as a team member and as an independent individual (W2)
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Bargaining. Study of bargaining in economics as well as in other fields. Why is it so multi-disciplinary? Why is it so well-studied? Different methods/approaches within economics.
Hafta 2: Introduction to Bargaining Cont'd. Introduction to Axiomatic Approach, Strategic Approach, and Experimental Approach.
Hafta 3: Axiomatic Approach to Bargaining: Basic Models
Hafta 4: Axiomatic Approach to Bargaining: Basic Models Cont'd
Hafta 5: Axiomatic Approach to Bargaining: Extensions. Critique of the Approach
Hafta 6: Strategic Approach to Bargaining: Basic Models
Hafta 7: Strategic Approach to Bargaining: Basic Models Cont'd
Hafta 8: Strategic Approach to Bargaining: Extensions
Hafta 9: Strategic Approach to Bargaining: Special Topics
Hafta 10: Strategic Approach to Bargaining: A Critique
Hafta 11: Experimental Approach to Bargaining: Early Experiments
Hafta 12: Experimental Approach to Bargaining: Special Topics
Hafta 13: Experimental Approach to Bargaining: Special Topics
Hafta 14: Future Directions in Bargaining Research
resources: Bargaining and markets, Martin Osborne and Ariel Rubinstein, 1990, Academic Press

courses.name: Dynamic Systems and Control
courses.description: Introduction to modern theories of growth; the neoclassical growth model; the golden rule of accumulation; transitional dynamics and the steady state; exogenous versus endogenous growth modeling; sources of growth and the convergence of nations; empirics of growth. Introduction to endogenous growth.
learning_outcomes: Have advanced level of fundamental conceptual knowledge so as to consider its reflections in practice.
Have command of fundamental concepts in social and behavioral sciences and of relationships between concepts.
Analyse theoretical knowledge and evaluate its reflections in practice.
Evaluate the knowledge acquired in the field with a critical approach by investigating causal relationships
Have the capacity to share knowledge and learn collectively.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Dynamic Optimization: Calculus of variations
Hafta 2: Calculus of variations: The Euler equation
Hafta 3: Calculus of variations: Variable endpoints, infinite planning horizon and the Transversality condition
Hafta 4: Optimal control theory: Pontryagin's maximum principle
Hafta 5: Optimal control theory: Constraints on state and control variables
Hafta 6: Optimal control theory: Sufficient conditions of optimality
Hafta 7: Dynamic programming: Separability and the principle of optimality
Hafta 8: Dynamic programming: Bellman equation, contraction mapping theorem
Hafta 9: Dynamic Systems and Stability: Linear systems
Hafta 10: Dynamic Systems and Stability: Nonlinear systems, local theory
Hafta 11: Dynamic Systems and Stability: Saddles, nodes, foci, and centers
Hafta 12: Dynamic Systems and Stability: Limit sets and attractors, periodic orbits and limit cycles
Hafta 13: Economic Applications
Hafta 14: Economic Applications

courses.name: Alternative Theories of Growth and Distribution
courses.purpose: To examine alternative approaches to economic growth and their implications for development macroeconomics, with particular attention to distribution, long-run equilibrium, and the policy conditions needed for sustained and stable growth.
courses.description: The course covers classical, neoclassical, and new theories of growth and their implications for development macroeconomics, with a direct focus on distribution. It studies the determinants of the wealth of nations, national policies for sustained growth, stylized facts and empirical regularities of growth across countries, traditional growth models, endogenous growth theories, the role of technological change and market structure, and alternatives to the neoclassical vision such as Marxian and (Neo)-Ricardian growth models.

courses.name: Institutions and Development
courses.purpose: This course aims to examine how institutions shape economic development and macroeconomic outcomes. It introduces students to the main ideas of New Institutional Economics and the institutional dimensions of fiscal and monetary policy.
courses.description: A synopsis of earlier development theories. Analysis of the linkages between economic development and macroeconomic institutions in the framework of New Institutional Economics. Empirical analysis of institutional designs related to fiscal and monetary policy making. Specifically, the course examines the economic effects of fiscal decentralization, budgetary rules and procedures, central bank independence, and inflation targeting.
learning_outcomes: Acquire critical thinking about the gaps in the neoclassical approach to macroeconomic outcomes by viewing the role of institutions
Ability to model and assess macroeconomic issues from the angle of new development theory
Acquire proficiency in expressing a topic effectively via use of graphs and tables
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Institutional Economics
Hafta 2: Earlier development theories; definition of an institution; main approaches to institutional analysis
Hafta 3: New Institutional Economics Approach
Hafta 4: Collective Action Theory
Hafta 5: Modeling Institutional Evolution; Measurement and Estimation issues
Hafta 6: Review and Mid-Term
Hafta 7: Fiscal Institutions : Budgetary Procedures
Hafta 8: Fiscal Institutions : Fiscal Decentralization
Hafta 9: Monetary Institutions and Development: Theoretical underpinnings
Hafta 10: Central Bank Independence; Deficit and Inflation relationship
Hafta 11: Currency Boards; Currency Unions; Inflation Targeting
Hafta 12: Review and Mid-Term
Hafta 13: Bank Regulation and Supervision; International Financial Institutions
Hafta 14: Student Presentations
resources: Macroeconomic Institutions and Development, Bilin NEYAPTI, 2010, Edward Elgar
PP - Articles posted in Moodle

courses.name: Global Trading
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamentals of global currency trading, with particular emphasis on the foreign exchange spot market. It also seeks to develop students' ability to analyze market behavior and interpret short- and long-term movements in currency markets.
courses.description: The course covers global currency trading, especially trading in the foreign currency spot market. It examines buying and selling patterns, volatility behavior, technical analysis tools such as Japanese candlesticks and Fibonacci numbers, and the major currencies of the world. The course also includes practical global currency trading exercises using up-to-date trading platforms.

courses.name: Labor Economics
courses.purpose: This course introduces the economics of earnings determination and labor allocation. It develops the theory of labor supply and labor demand and applies these tools to issues such as income distribution, unions, government intervention in labor markets, and discrimination.
courses.description: The course covers the economics of labor markets, including labor supply and labor demand, labor market equilibrium, compensation and wage differentials, human capital, worker mobility, pay and productivity, discrimination, unemployment, and inequality in earnings. If time allows, the relationship between unemployment and inflation is also discussed.
learning_outcomes: Explain the theory of labor supply.
Explain the theory of labor demand.
Analyse the labor market equilibrium by using the theory of labor supply and labor demand.
Analyse the effectiveness of labor market policies by using the relevant techniques.
Explain return to schooling, training, and migration by using human capital theory.
Explain sources of measured and unmeasured earnings differences.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Labor Market - Introduction (Chapter 1) - Overview of the Labor Market (Chapter 2)
Hafta 2: Labor Demand - The Demand for Labor (Chapter 3) - Labor Demand Elasticity (Chapter 4) - Frictions in the Labor Market (Chapter 5)
Hafta 3: Labor Demand - The Demand for Labor (Chapter 3) - Labor Demand Elasticity (Chapter 4) - Frictions in the Labor Market (Chapter 5)
Hafta 4: Labor Supply - Supply of Labor to the Economy: The Decision to Work (Chapter 6) - Labor Supply: Household Production, the Family, and the Life Cycle (Chapter 7)
Hafta 5: Labor Supply - Supply of Labor to the Economy: The Decision to Work (Chapter 6) - Labor Supply: Household Production, the Family, and the Life Cycle (Chapter 7)
Hafta 6: Midterm Exam I (10 March 2015)
Hafta 7: Compensation - Compensation and Wage Differentials (Chapter 8)
Hafta 8: Human Capital - Investment in Human Capital: Education and Training (Chapter 9)
Hafta 9: Labor Mobility - Worker Mobility: Migration, Immigration, and Turnover (Chapter 10)
Hafta 10: Pay and Productivity - Pay and Productivity: Wage Determination within the Firm (Chapter 11)
Hafta 11: Midterm Exam II (14 April 2015)
Hafta 12: Discrimination - Gender, Race, and Ethnicity in the Labor Market (Chapter 12)
Hafta 13: Unemployment (Chapter 14)
Hafta 14: Inequality and Earnings (Chapter 15)
resources: Modern Labor Economics: Theory and Public Policy, Ehrenberg, R. G. and Smith, R.S., 11th Ed., Pearson-Prentice Hall
Mostly Harmless Econometrics. An Empiricist's Companion., Angrist, J. and Pischke, J., 1st, Princeton University Press

courses.name: Growth Theory
courses.description: Modern theories of endogenous growth; convex growth; models based on R and D spillovers and other sources of externalities. Degree of openness and growth. Alternative theories of growth and development: neo-Keynesian growth and structuralist macroeconomics. Modern theories of the real business cycle literature.

courses.name: Introduction to Turkish Literature I
courses.purpose: Designed to give the student an overview of Turkish Literature.
courses.description: The course will deal with the earliest poems, the Orkhon Inscriptions, major early works (Divan-i Lugati't-Turk, Kutadgu Bilig, Dede Korkut, etc.), highlights of Seljuk Literature (especially Yunus Emre), and the most important works of Divan poetry and prose from the 14th century to the mid-19th century.

courses.name: Introduction to Turkish Literature II
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with an overview of Turkish literature from the Tanzimat era to the present and to help them understand the development of modern literary genres and major literary movements.
courses.description: This course will provide an overview of Turkish literature from the Tanzimat era to the present. Emphasis will be on the development of such literary genres as the novel, short story, drama, poetry, essay, and criticism in the modern era. The ethical and aesthetic arguments of major literary movements, key literary debates, and the social impact of literature will be discussed and evaluated. Readings will include major works in various genres.

courses.name: Written Expression
courses.purpose: This course aims at enhancing the appreciation and control of written Turkish at a high level. It will equip students with the fundamental skills of writing and editing.
courses.description: After a review of the basic elements of composition (thesis, organization, style, tone), the techniques of narration (summary, paraphrase, quotation), the rules of punctuation, citation, etc., numerous examples of printed works will be discussed in class. Regular writing and rewriting assignments will be given, with emphasis on non-fictional prose, including scholarly and critical writing.

courses.name: Ottoman Turkish II
courses.purpose: This course will enhance the students' comprehension of the Ottoman script and the fundamentals of Ottoman-Turkish grammar through readings and writing exercises.

courses.name: Theoretical History of Western Civilization
courses.purpose: This course aims to examine western civilization theoretically from preliterate societies to modern times. It also seeks to provide a framework for understanding major political, economic, religious, technological, and artistic transformations across history.
courses.description: In this course, western civilization, from preliterate societies to modern times, will be examined theoretically. Political, economic, religious, technological and artistic transformations on various levels are to be dealt with from different theoretical points of view. Ancient Greek and Roman contributions with respect to philosophy and law are also on the agenda of this course. Historical backgrounds of Humanism, Renaissance, Scientific Revolution, Reformation, Enlightenment and Romanticism are, inter alia, part of the basic problematic to be discussed. Furthermore, numerous important questions will be raised, among them: 'Are primitive/civilized differences tenable?', 'On what basis can there be periodization of human history?', and 'How the human mind passes from myth to Logos?'

courses.name: Sources of Literature: Nature and Culture
