courses.name: Operations Analysis and Design
courses.purpose: This course introduces traditional industrial engineering and the fundamental concepts and functions used in the design, improvement, and analysis of man-machine systems, mainly in a manufacturing environment.
courses.description: Design and improvement of manufacturing systems. Time study, work measurement, material handling systems, and layout design. A term project on simulating manufacturing systems.

courses.name: Engineering Economic Analysis
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the principles and methods used to evaluate engineering decisions economically. It helps students compare alternative designs, equipment, and projects under uncertainty and make rational investment decisions.
courses.description: Analysis of engineering decisions; principles and methodology of comparing decision alternatives, such as various engineering designs, manufacturing equipment, or industrial projects. Dealing with uncertainty and risk; rational decision making when future outcomes are uncertain. Concepts of time value of money. Effects of depreciation, inflation, and taxation on economic decisions. Cost-benefit analysis of public projects. Replacement analysis. Introduction to financial engineering.
learning_outcomes: Understand the time value money and use interest formulas
Do analysis of equivalent cash flows
Do analysis of economics of investment alternatives
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Ch 1: Engineering Economic Decisions, Ch 3: Time Value of Money, Economic Equivalence
Hafta 2: Ch 3: Single Cash Flows, Equal Payment Series, Gradient Series, Unconventional Equivalence Calculations
Hafta 3: Ch 4: Nominal and Effective Interest Rates, Equivalence Analysis using Effective Interest Rates
Hafta 4: Ch 4: Debt Management, Investing in Financial Assets: Stocks and Bonds
Hafta 5: Ch 5: Payback Period, Discounted Cash Flow Analysis, Variations of Present Worth Analysis
Hafta 6: Ch 5: Comparing Mutually Exclusive Alternatives Ch 6: Annual Equivalent Worth Criterion
Hafta 7: Ch 6: Applying Equivalent Worth Analysis Ch 7: Rate of Return Analysis, Finding RoR
Hafta 8: Ch 7: Internal Rate of Return Criterion, Incremental Analysis
Hafta 9: Ch 9: Asset Depreciation, Book Depreciation Methods
Hafta 10: Ch.9: Tax Depreciation Methods, Ch.9: Corporate Income Taxes
Hafta 11: Ch 10: Developing Project Cash Flows, Ch 11: Meaning and Measure of Inflation
Hafta 12: Ch 11: Equivalence Calculation under Inflation, Effects of Inflation Project Cash Flows
Hafta 13: Ch 12: Decision Tree Analysis
Hafta 14: Ch.14: Replacement Analysis Fundamentals, Replacement Decision Models, Ch.16: Benefit-Cost Ratio
resources: Contemporary Engineering Economics, Park, C. S., 2016 / 6th edition, Prentice Hall

courses.name: Production Planning
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the design and analysis of production planning systems using mathematical, computational, and other modern analytical techniques. It prepares students to model and solve core planning problems encountered in manufacturing and operations.
courses.description: Design of production planning systems using mathematical, computational and other modern analytical techniques. Forecasting; integrated production-inventory systems; deterministic inventory and lot-sizing models; multi-echelon supply networks; machine scheduling and capacity planning.
learning_outcomes: Learn pull and push production systems and other planning and control concepts
Learn and apply techniques for demand forecasting
Construct aggregate production planning models
Formulate and solve inventory problems
Learn and apply operations scheduling algorithms
Understand and appreciate professional and ethical responsibilities

Hafta 2: Forecasting

courses.name: Quality Control and Improvement
courses.description: Various philosophies and definitions of quality. Statistical process control. Process capability. Experimental design and the Taguchi method for quality improvement. Acceptance sampling.
learning_outcomes: Compare and contrast various philosophies and definitions of quality
Construct and analyze control charts to establish statistical control of a process
Assess the process capability
Use the design of experiments as an offline quality control tool
Use the Taguchi methods for quality improvement
Develop acceptance sampling plans
Calculate the reliability of a complex system as a function of the constituent component reliabilities
Do effective teamwork
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Terminology and Overview
Hafta 2: Pioneers of Quality
Hafta 3: Inferential Statistics
Hafta 4: Inferential Statistics
Hafta 5: Foundations of Control Charts
Hafta 6: Control Charts for Variables
Hafta 7: Control Charts for Attributes
Hafta 8: Process Capability
Hafta 9: Experimental Design
Hafta 10: Experimental Design
Hafta 11: Experimental Design
Hafta 12: Taguchi Method
Hafta 13: Acceptance Sampling
Hafta 14: Acceptance Sampling
resources: Statistical Quality Control: A Modern Introduction, Douglas C. Montgomery, 2013 / Seventh Edition, John Wiley & Sons

courses.name: Quantitative Risk Management
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic concepts and methods of risk management and develops the quantitative tools used to measure and incorporate risk in decision-making.
courses.description: Basic concepts and methods of risk management; the structure of risk and copulas; how to measure risk: VaR, coherent measures of risk, expected utility theory, the concept of stochastic dominance; extreme value theory; how to incorporate risk measures into stochastic optimization problems; applications in management, finance, and energy.

courses.name: Introduction to Financial Engineering
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts and tools used in financial engineering. It aims to build students’ ability to model financial markets, analyze securities and derivatives, and apply quantitative methods for pricing, hedging, and portfolio decision-making.
courses.description: Financial markets (bonds, stocks, futures, forwards, options, interest rates and their term structures), models of security prices (Brownian motion, geometric Brownian motions, Ornstein-Uhlenbeck processes, Cox-Ross-Rubinstein binomial model, Merton-Black-Scholes model), pricing and hedging financial derivatives (Ito's rule, stochastic integration, diffusion processes, probabilistic solutions of PDEs, no-arbitrage pricing in a complete market of futures, forwards, European and American type options, pricing in incomplete markets), hedging with futures and options, bond hedging, numerical methods (pricing using trees, Monte-Carlo simulations, finite-difference methods), mean-variance analysis of portfolios, value at risk, optimal consumption and portfolio strategies (formulations and solutions of appropriate dynamic programming models and Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equations).

courses.name: Multi-Objective Decision Analysis
courses.purpose: To introduce the principles and methods of quantitative decision analysis for problems with multiple objectives. The course aims to develop the ability to structure objectives, build value hierarchies, and evaluate preferences and trade-offs under certainty and uncertainty.
courses.description: Quantitative decision analysis. Structuring of objectives and value hierarchies, and determination of value functions. Introduction to consistent characterization of preferences under certainty. Value analysis under uncertainty including expected value analysis, utility theory, multi-attribute risk aversion, certainty equivalent calculations and scalarization methods for biobjective optimization.

courses.name: Production Information Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the role of computers and databases in production systems and to explain how integrated production management systems are developed and used in practice.
courses.description: The course covers fundamental concepts in production information systems, including ERP, lean production, JIT, inventory management, lead time, work-order management, shop floor control, and group technology-based parts classification and coding systems.
learning_outcomes: Identify fundamental concepts of integrated production management systems
Assess the complexity of production planning and control problems and emphasize the role of information systems in production planning and control
Apply a systems approach by considering the interactions between different subsystems
Integrate different tools and techniques applicable to production management systems
Conduct capacity planning
Do effective teamwork
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Material Requirements Planning – MRP
Hafta 3: Lot sizing
Hafta 4: Master Production Scheduling
Hafta 5: Capacity Planning
Hafta 6: Supply Chain Management
Hafta 7: Supply Chain Management
Hafta 8: Shop Floor Control and Scheduling
Hafta 9: Enterprise Resource Planning – ERP
Hafta 10: Theory of Constraints (TOC) Systems
Hafta 11: Just in Time - JIT
Hafta 12: Just in Time - JIT
Hafta 13: Group Technology and Cellular Manufacturing
Hafta 14: Kanban Systems
resources: Manufacturing Planning and Control for Supply Chain Management, Jacobs, F.R., Berry, W.L., Whybark, D.C., Vollmann, T.E., 6th edition, 2011, McGraw-Hill, Inc.

courses.name: Operations Research in Finance
courses.description: Bonds, interest rates, duration and convexity, bond portfolios, options, binomial model, early exercise options, dynamic programming in finance, portfolio optimization, value-at-risk, robust portfolio optimization.

courses.name: Financial Issues in Engineering Projects
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the financial perspective needed to evaluate and manage engineering projects from the viewpoint of top management. It focuses on how financing, valuation, and risk considerations affect project decisions in corporate settings.
courses.description: Analysis of investment and financial decisions from the perspective of top management in a corporation; financing methods and techniques including working capital, project finance, shareholder, export credit agency loans and derivatives; optimization of debt/equity ratios; business and risk analysis to meet the requirements of the financiers; corporate valuation methods and business plans; mergers and acquisitions. Case studies covering topics such as energy, infrastructure, privatizations and buyouts.
learning_outcomes: Understand the decision-making mechanism in a corporation for the investments
Understand the techniques for the financial analysis of the corporations
Identify the drivers in a corporation in managing the financial resources to optimize the risk and return
Understand the techniques to maximize the corporate value for the stake holders
Use quantitative methods to prepare business plan
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, review of basics of CF and definitions.
Hafta 2: Cost of capital, leverage, types of capital, Financial and FX markets.
Hafta 3: Financing methods and sources, types of loans (Working Capital, Share Holder, Bridge, Export Credit Agency Loans).
Hafta 4: Balance Sheet Finance, Project Finance, Debt/Equity Ratios.
Hafta 5: Negotiations with the financiers, Loan Agreements, Default, cross default, covenants, pledges, negative pledges.
Hafta 6: Case Study (Energy Project), Students’ Project selection, Risks (Financial, FX, Market, Political/Country), Derivatives, Hedging.
Hafta 7: Business Plans/Models, Cash Flows, IRR.
Hafta 8: Company valuations, Discounting of Cash Flow, EBITDA Multiplier.
Hafta 9: Public Offerings, Pre-Public Offerings, Privatization.
Hafta 10: Case Study (Privatization, PO), Mergers&Acquisitions (M&A), Due Dilligence.
Hafta 11: Corporate Governance, Corporate Structures, Case Study (M&A).
Hafta 12: Private Equity Funds, Major International and local PEs, Venture Capital. Case study (PE Transaction).
Hafta 13: Project Presentation-Panel Discussion
Hafta 14: Project Presentation-Panel Discussion
resources: Principles of Corporate Finance, Richard A. Brealey, Stewart C. Myers, Franklin Allen, 11th edition or newer, McGraw-Hill Irwin

courses.name: Energy Systems Planning
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the analytical and modeling tools needed to support decision making in energy systems planning. It focuses on understanding energy supply and demand dynamics, evaluating alternative energy resources, and addressing uncertainty, reliability, and sustainability issues in energy operations.
courses.description: Historical trends of energy supply and demand; alternative energy resources and related technologies. Global climate change, security, reliability and economic issues in energy operations and planning problems. Energy demand and supply forecasting, energy modeling under uncertainty, intermittency and role of storage, grid operations and reliability, smart grid, and generation expansion planning. Case studies and problem solving techniques and methods applied in the pursuit of improved decision making in energy systems.
learning_outcomes: Explain the historical trends of energy supply and demand
Evaluate alternative energy resources and related technologies
Develop a thought process about global climate change, security, reliability and economic issues in energy operations and planning problems
Develop and use a wide range of problem-solving techniques and methods in the pursuit of improved decision making in energy systems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Alternative Energy Resources
Hafta 3: Time Series of Energy Supply and Demand
Hafta 4: Matching Energy Density of Supply and Demand
Hafta 5: Challenges with Modeling Renewable Energy Sources
Hafta 6: Energy Modeling under Uncertainty
Hafta 7: Role of Storage and Intermittency
Hafta 8: Energy Flow Optimization Modeling
Hafta 9: Power Systems Modeling- Generation, Transmission and Distribution
Hafta 10: Applications of Combinatorial Optimization
Hafta 11: Energy Modeling under Uncertanity
Hafta 12: Energy Use and Climate Change
Hafta 13: Grid Operations and Reliability
Hafta 14: Smart Grid
Hafta 15: Capacity Expansion Investment Planning

courses.name: Sustainable Operations
courses.purpose: To introduce the concepts of sustainability and sustainable operations and to develop the ability to evaluate and implement sustainability measures in operations settings.
courses.description: Concepts of sustainability and sustainable operations. Evaluation and implementation of sustainability measures. Analysis of sustainable operations from the value chain perspective. Sustainable actions and policies at different phases in the product life cycle. The use of quantitative operations models and techniques to analyze and evaluate the viability of sustainable opportunities. Topics include Triple Bottom Line, Environmental Product Differentiation, Lean and Six Sigma, Life Cycle Assessment, Design for Environment, Remanufacturing, Take-Back Legislation, and Closed-Loop Supply Chains.

courses.name: Quantitative Models in Supply Chain Management
courses.description: Economies of scale in the supply chain, quantity and transportation discounts. Multi-stage inventory and production systems, risk pooling, decentralized and centralized modeling approaches, bullwhip effect. Supply chain coordination, strategic games in supply chains, and contracting. More complicated examples from industry.

courses.name: Introduction to Advanced Manufacturing Technologies
courses.description: A study of the present impact of computers and automation on manufacturing. product design technologies. Concepts and principles of computer aided design and process planning; automated production lines; group technology; cellular manufacturing; flexible manufacturing systems; automated material handling. Introduction to robotics technology, work cell design and applications.
learning_outcomes: Study of the present impact of computers and automation on manufacturing
Overview of the different manufacturing techniques
Introduction to robotics technology, work cell design and applications
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Review of Manufacturing Processes
Hafta 3: Computer Aided Manufacturing
Hafta 4: Design for Manufacturing
Hafta 5: Geometric Tolerancing (GDT)
Hafta 6: Advanced Manufacturing Processes
Hafta 7: Surface Integrity
Hafta 8: Surface Topography Analysis
Hafta 9: Additive Manufacturing
Hafta 10: Flexible Manufacturing
Hafta 11: Industrial Robotics
Hafta 12: Material handling systems
Hafta 13: Manufacturing as a Service
Hafta 14: Term Project Presentations
resources: Automation, Production Systems, and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing, Mikell P. Groover

courses.name: Service Systems
courses.purpose: To introduce the role of service industries and the main concepts used to manage service systems. The course aims to develop the ability to design, analyze, evaluate, and improve service systems using Operations Management and Operations Research techniques.
courses.description: Service industries, managing service systems, design and analysis of service systems using Operations Management/Operations Research (OM/OR) techniques, evaluation and optimization of the performance of service systems, service industry applications.

courses.name: Operations Scheduling
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the fundamental concepts and methods used in operations scheduling. It aims to develop the ability to model and solve scheduling problems arising in manufacturing and service systems.
courses.description: Introduction to scheduling. Exact and approximate techniques and approaches to modeling and solving problems from a variety of manufacturing and service applications including production scheduling, assembly systems, reservation systems, timetabling problems, and workforce and crew scheduling. Overview of the current trends and future directions.
learning_outcomes: Learn exact and approximate scheduling techniques
Overview of scheduling applications in production and service systems
Discuss the current trends and future directions
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Scheduling examples
Hafta 3: Single machine models
Hafta 4: Heuristic approaches
Hafta 5: Heuristic approaches
Hafta 6: Project planning and scheduling
Hafta 7: Project planning and scheduling
Hafta 8: Job shop scheduling
Hafta 9: Job shop scheduling
Hafta 10: Local search algorithms
Hafta 11: Interval Scheduling, Reservations, and Timetabling
Hafta 12: Scheduling applications in production and service systems
Hafta 13: Scheduling applications in production and service systems
Hafta 14: Scheduling systems overview
resources: Operations Scheduling with Applications in Manufacturing and Services, Michael Pinedo and Xiuli Chao

courses.name: Inventory Planning Models
courses.description: Inventory control problems in service and manufacturing environments. Introduction to single-item and multi-item inventory models. Extensions of economic-order-quantity model, power-of-two type policies, coordinated replenishment, economic lot scheduling problem, (r, Q) policies, (s, S) policies, models with lost sales, exchange curves, and multi-period problems.

courses.name: Pricing and Revenue Optimization
courses.description: Estimation and optimization for pricing and revenue optimization. Review of price-demand functions. Pricing under constrained supply. Price differentiation. Customized pricing. Dynamic pricing and markdown management. Revenue management. Network revenue management. Overbooking.
learning_outcomes: Identify fundamental drivers of customer choice and a product’s demand
Identify important models for price-demand relationship
Use statistical tools to estimate the effect of price and segmentation decisions on demand
Use optimization models to determine profit maximizing prices under a variety of settings
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Basic Price Optimization
Hafta 3: Basic Price Optimization
Hafta 4: Basic Price Optimization
Hafta 5: Price Differentiation

Hafta 7: Pricing with Constrained Supply

Hafta 13: Network Revenue Management
Hafta 14: Network Revenue Management
resources: Pricing and Revenue Optimization, Phillips, Robert, 2005, Stanford University Press

courses.name: Industrial Applications of Operations Research
courses.purpose: To introduce students to optimization models used in industrial engineering and to show how these models are applied in real-world decision-making problems.
courses.description: Survey of optimization models in manufacturing, logistics and project management. Applications to real production contexts in project management, lot sizing, location, finite-capacity scheduling, and cutting stock. Use of Excel optimization tools.

courses.name: Humanitarian Logistics
courses.purpose: To introduce students to humanitarian operations and the decision problems involved in meeting society’s ongoing needs during disasters and relief efforts. The course aims to build awareness of humanitarian logistics challenges and the use of quantitative methods in this context.
courses.description: Introduction to humanitarian operations; decision problems related to meeting the ongoing needs of the society; relief logistics; disaster management cycle; decision problems faced in preparing for, responding to, and recovering from disasters; the activities and challenges in the supply chain for the procurement, delivery, warehousing and distribution of the aid; practical examples, projects and case studies.
learning_outcomes: Create an awareness of worldwide problems
Apply the tools and techniques gained through IE curriculum in case studies and projects
Use quantitative operations models and techniques in humanitarian applications
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: World Overview
Hafta 2: Humanitarian Concepts
Hafta 3: NGOs
Hafta 4: Location Models
Hafta 5: Hierarchical Location, Preventive Healthcare
Hafta 6: Big Depot Project
Hafta 7: Disasters
Hafta 8: Organ Transplantation Logistics
Hafta 9: The Broccoli Project
Hafta 10: VRP Based Applications
Hafta 11: Case Readings
Hafta 12: Case Readings
Hafta 13: Blood Logistics
Hafta 14: Earthquake Project
Hafta 15: Malaria
Hafta 16: Review and Project Presentations

courses.name: Decision Making in Health Care
courses.description: Applications of decision sciences in health-care industry; methods to allocate health-care resources; decision making for health economics and medicine; decision analysis using Markov processes, Monte Carlo simulations; cost-effectiveness analysis; quality-adjusted life years.

courses.name: Introduction to Research in IE and OR I
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to research techniques in industrial engineering and operations research. It is designed to support independent study under faculty supervision and to prepare students for communicating research findings effectively.
courses.description: Introduction to research techniques in industrial engineering and operations research. Independent study under the supervision of a departmental faculty member. Written and oral presentations to report research findings.
learning_outcomes: Develop an understanding of an appreciation for scholarly research
Review library and internet based resources to identify the literature
Analyze a given problem, and investigate and evaluate different solution methodologies
Demonstrate and improve analytical and critical thinking abilities
Develop the students’ technical writing and presentation skills

Hafta 2: Problem Definition
Hafta 3: Literature Search
Hafta 4: Literature Search
Hafta 5: Project Proposal & Presentation

Hafta 13: Independent Research Study
Hafta 14: Final Report & Presentation
resources: Writing Scientific Research Articles: Strategy and Steps, Margaret Cargill, 2013, Wiley
From Research to Manuscript: A Guide to Scientific Writing, Michael Jay Katz, 2009, Springer

courses.name: Distribution Logistics
courses.description: Logistics network design; strategic, tactical, operational level decision making; location models, planning and controlling freight transportation; long-haul, short-haul, practical examples; projects and case studies.

courses.name: Introduction to Research in IE and OR II
courses.purpose: Continuation of IE490 Introduction to Research in IE and OR. Students deepen and extend the same research problem under the supervision of the same faculty advisor as in IE490.
courses.description: Continuation of IE490 Introduction to Research in IE and OR. The same research problem under the supervision of the same faculty advisor as in IE490 is investigated to obtain deeper and extended results.
learning_outcomes: Develop an understanding of and an appreciation for scholarly research
Review library and internet based resources to identify the literature
Analyze a given problem, and investigate and evaluate different solution methodologies
Demonstrate and improve analytical and critical thinking abilities
Develop the students’ technical writing and presentation skills
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Problem Definition
Hafta 2: Problem Definition
Hafta 3: Literature Search
Hafta 4: Literature Search
Hafta 5: Project Proposal & Presentation
Hafta 6: Independent Research Study
Hafta 7: Independent Research Study
Hafta 8: Independent Research Study
Hafta 9: Independent Research Study
Hafta 10: Independent Research Study
Hafta 11: Independent Research Study
Hafta 12: Independent Research Study
Hafta 13: Independent Research Study
Hafta 14: Final Report & Presentation
resources: Required - Advisor: Students have to contact an advisor prior to the course registration period, and fill an override form to enroll.

courses.name: Diplomatic History
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the major developments in European diplomatic history from the Peace of Westphalia to the outbreak of World War I. It also helps students understand how key concepts such as the balance of power, hegemony, and the concert of powers evolved over time.
courses.description: Diplomatic history from the Peace of Westphalia until World War I.
learning_outcomes: Understand and evaluate the significance of key events, people, and processes in European international relations from 1648 until 1919
Understand the meaning and historical development of central issues in the history of European international relations, including hegemony, the balance of power, and the concert of powers
Evaluate the questions being asked when academics write about European international relations and the broader objectives that shape those questions
Be able to think critically about how historical analysis and historical comparisons influence our understanding of contemporary international relations
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: General introduction and information on course requirements
Hafta 2: Westphalia
Hafta 3: Asian Orders
Hafta 4: The Age of Revolutions
Hafta 5: The Concert of Europe
Hafta 6: Liberalism and Nationalism
Hafta 7: A world of freer trade
Hafta 8: Nationalism in power
Hafta 9: The Gold Standard
Hafta 10: Rising Powers
Hafta 11: Sovereign debt
Hafta 12: The balance of power breaks down
Hafta 13: Immigration restrictions
Hafta 14: Final Exam review
resources: Diplomacy, Henry Kissinger, 1994, New York: Simon & Schuster

courses.name: Research Methods in International Relations
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to how to design an IR research paper and gradually write one throughout the semester. It builds a basic background in research philosophies and methods so that students can later produce good research papers.
courses.description: The course covers the basic concepts of research philosophies and methods, including formulating a good research question, developing a theory and hypotheses, and using data collection and analysis techniques. It also introduces applied and empirical research, with firsthand experience in library research and consultation with a library advisor.
learning_outcomes: Develop original research projects, correctly demonstrate an understanding of research methods and research design, and complete the assignment within deadlines.
Use the terms related to the research process in their proposals correctly.
Analyze theoretical knowledge on research methods and philosophy of science and evaluate its reflections in practice.
Obtain, assess, and classify data to be used in the project and develop skills to conduct research in the library and online databases.
Correctly demonstrate a clear understanding of theoretical and practical debates in research methods in International Relations.
Correctly identify and define important concepts in qualitative and quantitative research methods.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to IR Methods
Hafta 2: Research Philosophy & Ethics
Hafta 3: Research Question
Hafta 4: Literature Review & Theory Building
Hafta 5: Theory Building (continued)
Hafta 6: Research Design & Measurement
Hafta 7: Quantitative Methods in IR
Hafta 8: Quantitative Methods in IR (Software use)
Hafta 9: Hands-on Empirical Analysis
Hafta 10: Public Opinion Data & Survey Method in IR
Hafta 11: Experiments & Quasi-Experiments in IR
Hafta 12: Game Theory in IR
Hafta 13: Qualitative Methods and Case Studies in IR
Hafta 14: Qualitative Methods and Case Studies in IR (cont'd)

courses.name: International Law
courses.purpose: This course introduces the first part of a comprehensive survey of international law as a normative factor in international relations. It aims to explain the basic legal concepts and prepare students to understand prospective developments in the field.
courses.description: The course covers the basic concepts, sources, and subjects of international law, as well as treaties, state responsibility, international organizations, international criminal law, the law of territory, the law of the sea, the law of air and outer space, the use of force, peaceful settlement of disputes, the International Court of Justice, diplomatic law, and international migration and refugee regime. It also places legal rules in their political, economic, and cultural contexts while emphasizing legal reasoning.
learning_outcomes: Can define major concepts in the field and distinguish between them.
Can identify major theoretical perspectives in the field and compare and contrast.
Can plan the process and schedule of an independent project.
Can evaluate the knowledge acquired in the field with a critical approach by investigating causal relationships.
Can communicate field related knowledge and skills in writing and speaking.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course introduction
Hafta 2: Introduction to international law
Hafta 3: International Law vs. Municipal Law; The Sources of International Law
Hafta 4: The Law of Treaties; The Subjects of International Law: State
Hafta 5: The Subjects of International Law: International Organizations; State Responsibility
Hafta 6: International Criminal Law
Hafta 7: Revision
Hafta 8: Revisions, Mid-Term Examination
Hafta 9: Spring Break / Holiday period
Hafta 10: The Law of Territory; The Law of the Sea
Hafta 11: The Law of Air and Outer Space; Use of Force
Hafta 12: The Settlement of Disputes by Peaceful Means; The International Court of Justice
Hafta 13: Diplomatic Law
Hafta 14: International Migration and Refugee Regime (cont.)
Hafta 15: International Migration and Refugee Regime (cont.)
Hafta 16: Review of the former weeks, Q&A
