International Law, Malcolm Shaw, 2009, 6th edition, Cambridge University Press
International Law, Vaughan Lowe, 2007, Oxford University
International Law, Antonio Cassese, 2005, Oxford University Press
Uluslararası Hukuk, Hüseyin Pazarcı, 2016, Turhan Kitabevi

courses.name: Turkic/Muslim People of the Former USSR
courses.description: Russian Rule over the Turco-Muslim lands, the enlightenment and national revival of the Turco-Muslim peoples: establishment of Soviet power, communist rule and the road to national independence.

courses.name: International Organizations
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the development of international organizations and their role in contemporary world politics. It also introduces the main institutions, their functions, and their significance in global governance.
courses.description: A comprehensive study of the development of international organization and its role in the contemporary world. The central part of the course deals with the United Nations, its structure, performance and prospects, both in the maintenance of peace and in the economic and social field. Then specialized agencies and the regional organizations are treated on a descriptive basis.
learning_outcomes: Understand and apply different theoretical perspectives to the analysis of International Organizations
Comprehend the function, role and influence of different International Organizations in world politics
Appreciate the challenges International Organizations face, and the impact political and economic change has on their function
Be in a position to apply acquired knowledge to the study of International Organizations through systematic, structure-based research
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course introduction
Hafta 2: Introduction to International Organizations
Hafta 3: Theorizing International Organizations & Research Methods
Hafta 4: The United Nations I: Law and Administration
Hafta 5: The United Nations II: International Peace and Security
Hafta 6: The World Trade Organization
Hafta 7: The International Monetary Fund & the World Bank
Hafta 8: The International Monetary Fund & the World Bank (cont.), Review of the former weeks, Q&A
Hafta 9: Mid-Term Week, Revisions, Q&A
Hafta 10: The International Labour Organization
Hafta 11: Refugees and International Migration: UNHCR, the IOM and the 1951 Convention
Hafta 12: The International Court of Justice
Hafta 13: The International Criminal Court
Hafta 14: The EU and Regional Organizations
Hafta 15: EU (cont.)
Hafta 16: Review of the former weeks, Q&A
resources: International Organizations: Politics, Law, Hurd, Ian, 2021, 4th Edition, Cambridge University Press
International Organizations, Archer, Clive, 2001, Routledge
International Organization: Theories and Institutions, Barkin, J. Samuel., Springer

courses.name: Russian History
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide a historical survey of Russia from the rise of the Kievan Confederation in the 9th century to the Bolshevik Revolution. It also seeks to help students understand the major forces that shaped Russian political and social development, especially reform, revolution, ideology, and society.
courses.description: A survey of Russian History from the rise of Kievan Confederation (9th century) to the Bolshevik Revolution. Focus will be on reform, revolution, ideology and society.

courses.name: 20th Century World Politics
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the major events, transformations, and dynamics that shaped world politics in the 20th century. It helps students understand how international and domestic developments interacted and influenced the contemporary international system.
courses.description: This course introduces students to critical events and dynamics of the 20th Century, including the League of Nations, the inter-war period, World War II, the United Nations, the Cold War, super-power conflict and the Non-Aligned Movement, détente, the Second Cold War, the revolutions of 1989, and post-Cold War challenges to the international system.
learning_outcomes: Outline the scope of world politics in the 20th Century, with an eye on interstate relations, mass ideologies and counter-cultures that transcended local boundaries and became ubiquitous at one point or another
Set forth the interaction between domestic politics and foreign affairs

Measure the impact of super-power rivalry (both during the Interbellum and Cold War years) on local conflicts in the third world and assess how global politics shaped counter-cultures in different geographical settings.
Explore underlying historical motives that continue to guide international relations
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Course Overview
Hafta 2: World War I
Hafta 3: Revolutions
Hafta 4: The Great Depression
Hafta 5: Interwar Fascism
Hafta 6: World War II
Hafta 7: Midterm Week
Hafta 8: Decolonization
Hafta 9: The Cold War
Hafta 10: Postwar Economic Growth
Hafta 11: The Second World
Hafta 12: The 1970s
Hafta 13: The Third World
Hafta 14: Collapse of the Soviet Union and its Aftermath

courses.name: International and European Protection of Human Rights
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the international and European systems of human rights protection. It aims to provide a framework for understanding the main institutions, legal instruments, and practical mechanisms that shape human rights protection at the global and European levels.
courses.description: The course covers the global human rights protection regime in the first half, including relevant international instruments and institutions, as well as the legal, political, and practical issues involved. It examines the major human rights treaties and how they are interpreted and applied by human rights bodies in practice. The second half focuses on the European system of human rights protection under the OSCE, the Council of Europe, the European Convention on Human Rights, the mechanism of the European Court of Human Rights (ECtHR), and ECtHR case law.

courses.name: War, Peace and Security
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the core concepts of war, peace, and security in world politics and helps them develop a comprehensive basis for understanding contemporary international dynamics. It also aims to strengthen students’ oral, written, and research skills through critical engagement with complex and sometimes contradictory material.
courses.description: This course examines three central concepts in world politics: war, peace, and security. It presents a wide variety of intellectual traditions and contemporary ideas related to these concepts, and discusses the causes and significance of war, alternative meanings and practices of peace, and competing conceptions and practices of security.
learning_outcomes: Communicate advanced level of knowledge on war, peace and security (C1)
Apply security related knowledge to world politics (S1)
Have knowledge on research planning related to war, peace and security (K5)
Obtain basic knowledge about war, peace and security with reference to contemporary security problems in world politics (K1)
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Fear, Power, Security
Hafta 2: Conflict, Cooperation, Statecraft
Hafta 3: Security: Definitions and Redefinitions
Hafta 4: Power, Peace and Security: The Proliferation of Concepts
Hafta 5: War and Change in World Politics
Hafta 6: Security and Identity
Hafta 7: The production of danger and insecurities
Hafta 8: War, Conflict and Trauma in World Politics
Hafta 9: Human In/security
Hafta 10: Critical Approaches to Security
Hafta 11: Security as Emancipation?
Hafta 12: Values, Ethics and Choices in the study of War, Peace and Security
Hafta 13: How to seek security?
Hafta 14: Conspiracy Theories and World Politics
resources: Critical Approaches to International Security, Karin Fierke, 2nd ed., Polity

courses.name: Foreign Policy Analysis
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the main theoretical and methodological approaches used to analyze foreign policy. It also helps students understand how foreign policy is made and implemented in practice.
courses.description: The making and implementation of foreign policy in theory and practice. The theoretical literature on foreign and security policymaking. Case studies.
learning_outcomes: Develop an in-depth understanding of major theoretical and methodological issues in the study of foreign policy

Compare and contrast different FPA theories in terms of their assumptions about the making of foreign policy, and the conceptual frameworks used to study foreign policy making as a process, outcome, output, resultant and so on
Apply FPA theories to the study of specific case studies
Relate FPA theories and conceptual frameworks to recent/current regional and global developments
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Foreign Policy Analysis
Hafta 2: Studying Foreign Policy Comparatively
Hafta 3: Rational Actors National Interest
Hafta 4: Systemic and Situational Explanations of Foreign Policy
Hafta 5: Sources of Explanation: Domestic Politics and Domestic-International Interaction
Hafta 6: Decision -Making
Hafta 7: Decision Units: Leaders
Hafta 8: Decision Units: Groups and Coalitions
Hafta 9: Public Opinion
Hafta 10: Culture and Role Theory
Hafta 11: IR Theories and FPA, Integration: Overview
Hafta 12: Group Presentations in Class
Hafta 13: Group Presentations
Hafta 14: Group Presentations
resources: Studying Foreign Policy Comparatively: Cases and Analysis, Laura Neack, 2018, New Millenium Books in International Studies

courses.name: International Political Economy
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to provide students with a comprehensive introduction to International Political Economy (IPE) as a field of study.
courses.description: The course focuses on recent developments and current trends in the world political economy, various theoretical IPE-perspectives, as well as more specific topics such as international monetary affairs, global finance, foreign debt, international trade, global production, foreign direct investment, transnational corporations, and development.
learning_outcomes: Understand and evaluate diverse theoretical perspectives and views regarding the global political economy
Know historical developments and current trends in the world economy, and how politics interact with economic interests.
Understand structural and actor driven challenges in world trade, transnational production, global financial system, and economic development.
Be able to apply the acquired course knowledge and theoretical perspectives to discuss and conduct research on the historical developments and recent challenges in the global political economy.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Theoretical perspectives
Hafta 3: Pre-1945 history of the world political economy
Hafta 4: The post-1945 world political economy
Hafta 5: International trade
Hafta 6: Transnational production
Hafta 7: Midterm examination
Hafta 8: Global financial system I
Hafta 9: Global financial system II
Hafta 10: Global division of labour
Hafta 11: Gender
Hafta 12: Economic development
Hafta 13: Global environmental change
Hafta 14: Global economic governance
resources: Global Political Economy: Evolution and Dynamics (6th ed.), Robert O'Brien and Marc Williams, 2020, Palgrave Macmillan

courses.name: International Relations in Movies
courses.purpose: This course uses movies as a medium to understand and explain international issues. It aims to deepen students' understanding of international affairs by connecting film content with concepts and debates from international relations.
courses.description: Through classroom discussions, readings, and films dealing with international issues, the course analyzes how movies shed light on international affairs and present different perspectives on them. The course covers 6 to 8 movies during the semester and relates their content to topics studied in international relations, including foreign policy making, war, terrorism, ethnic conflict, identity, environmental issues, revolutions, civil wars, foreign intervention, international institutions, espionage, and the Cold War.

courses.name: Negotiation and Mediation in Politics
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the theory and practice of negotiation and mediation in legal and political conflicts, and to develop their ability to analyze and participate in such processes.
courses.description: This course covers theories of bargaining, negotiation outcomes, negotiation processes, psychological dynamics affecting negotiations, effective communication strategies, the role of language, culture, and power in negotiations, and the role of third parties. It also includes applied negotiation and mediation skills through simulations, role plays, and case discussions on topics such as the Turkish Constitution, Cyprus, and Jerusalem.

courses.name: Globalization
courses.purpose: This introductory course aims to build a foundation of knowledge, analytical skills, and conceptual vocabulary for understanding globalization and engaging with the broader concerns of International Relations. It also seeks to help students analyze and anticipate emerging dynamics in global politics across transnational and domestic dimensions.
courses.description: The course examines globalization as a concept encompassing the transnational linkages that increasingly characterize today's world. It situates globalization in historical context and explores its relationships with culture, cross-border people flows, nationalism and ethnicity, security, democracy, religious fundamentalism, gender, the environment, and economics. The course also considers how these concepts interact in the Turkish case.

courses.name: International Relations Theory
courses.purpose: This course provides students with a comprehensive introduction to contemporary international relations theory. It is designed for students with no prior knowledge of international theory.
courses.description: This course introduces major contemporary theories of international relations, including realism, liberalism, the English School, Marxism, critical theory, constructivism, feminism, poststructuralism, postcolonialism, and green theory.
learning_outcomes: Knowledge of different theories, and ability to compare, contrast and analyse them.
Ability to think theoretically and apply theories to real-world cases.
Ability to communicate about theory both verbally in debates and in written essays.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course intro
Hafta 2: What is IR theory?
Hafta 3: Realism
Hafta 4: Liberalism
Hafta 5: English School
Hafta 6: Marxism
Hafta 7: Midterm exam
Hafta 8: Critical Theory
Hafta 9: Constructivism
Hafta 10: Feminism
Hafta 11: Poststructuralism
Hafta 12: Postcolonialism
Hafta 13: Green Theory
Hafta 14: Course review
resources: International Relations Theories: Discipline and Diversity, Tim Dunne et al., 2016 (4th ed.), Oxford University Press

courses.name: Energy Security and Foreign Policy
courses.purpose: This course examines the challenge of energy security in relation to foreign policy analysis.
courses.description: The course has three parts. The first part outlines the continuities and changes in the global energy market to underline the emerging challenges in securing energy supplies, access to resources and the environment. The second part presents different theoretical approaches to facilitate a conceptual framework in analyzing how energy security relates to foreign policy. The third part focuses on selected issues and cases to analyze and discuss energy security and foreign policy in the light of the different conceptual frameworks presented in the second part of the course.

courses.name: Public Policy Making in the European Union
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the knowledge, skills, and research experience needed to analyze policy processes and decision-making challenges in the European Union.
courses.description: This course covers policy processes in the EU, with emphasis on understanding the policy making process through the most complicated policy areas. It also focuses on the current financial crisis and contemporary debates on the future integration of the EU, as well as the policy implementing process and supranational policy problems and controversies.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the complex EU policy making and policy implementing processes.
Examine supranational policy problems and controversies.
Develop solutions in the field of European public policy.
Analyze the political environment of European public policy.
Form effective strategies related to EU public policy.

courses.name: Introduction to Middle East Politics
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with an overview of the main political developments in the Middle East and to introduce them to a major center of world affairs. It also seeks to help students understand the complexity of the region and the dynamics that shape its politics.
courses.description: This is an introductory course on key issues in domestic and regional politics in the Middle East. It covers the main political developments of the region and introduces students to the conceptual frameworks through which the Middle East is studied and understood.
learning_outcomes: Appraise the value of the Middle East in world politics
Engage critically with debates on the Middle East while using suitable theoretical and empirical evidence
Review developments in the Middle East and comprehend their salience
Recognize the geographical salience of bordering states in the Middle East
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction – the Middle East: geographical, religious, and ethnic perspectives
Hafta 2: Middle East and International Relations theory
Hafta 3: Modern nationalism, Decline of the Ottoman Empire and WWI
Hafta 4: European Involvement in the Middle East – Britain and France
Hafta 5: Jewish settlement in Palestine and the rise of the Palestinian National Movement
Hafta 6: End of the British Mandate and Arab-Israeli wars
Hafta 7: Arab-Israeli wars cont. (1967-1973)
Hafta 8: Egyptian Revolution (1952) and Nasserism
Hafta 9: Arab-Israeli peace process – Egypt and Jordan
Hafta 10: Arab-Israeli peace process – Palestinians and Syria
Hafta 11: Oil – a blessing or a curse (Saudi Arabia and the Persian Gulf)
Hafta 12: Iran, the Islamic Revolution and its influence on the Middle East
Hafta 13: The Arab Spring – was it a revolution? Why did it fail?
Hafta 14: Future challenges for the Middle East

courses.name: Game Theory and International Politics
courses.purpose: This course is designed to study rational behavior related to conflict.
courses.description: Substantively, it focuses on strategic rationality underlying bargaining, deterrence, and surprise attack.

courses.name: International Security
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a strong foundation in the dominant theories of international security and the key issues currently being debated. It is designed to help students understand how the concept of security has expanded beyond the state to include a wide range of actors and issue areas.
courses.description: International security is an ever-expanding subfield of International Relations. Originally applied solely to the security of the state, the concept of security understood as the absence of threats has been broadened in recent years to include international and non-governmental organizations, individuals, the environment, cyberspace, energy, food, health, or cultural identity, among many other topics. The course also addresses the renewed importance of traditional security studies in light of the rise of China and the resurgence of Russia.
learning_outcomes: Understanding the complex security challenges of 21st century
Develop analytical skills to follow contemporary security developments
Accumulate knowledge and skills to conduct graduate level work on international security
Can obtain, assess, and classify data on contemporary security developments
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introductions & What is Security?
Hafta 2: Anarchy, Power, and the Security Dilemma
Hafta 3: Coercion and War
Hafta 4: Great Power War
Hafta 5: Alliances
Hafta 6: Nuclear Weapons
Hafta 7: Simulation & Midterm
Hafta 8: Economics and Security
Hafta 9: Ethnic Conflict, Civil Wars, and Terrorism
Hafta 10: Migration and Refugees
Hafta 11: Emerging Technologies: Cyber, Drones, AI
Hafta 12: Energy Security
Hafta 13: Environmental Change and Security
Hafta 14: Gender and Security
resources: Security Studies: An Introduction, Williams, Paul D., and Matt McDonald, 2018, Third Edition, Routledge
Conflict after the Cold War: Arguments on Causes of War and Peace, Betts, Richard, 2017/6th, Longman Pearson
The Oxford Handbook of International Security, Gheciu, Alexandra, and William Curti Wohlforth, 2018, Oxford University Press

courses.name: Use of Force in International Law
courses.purpose: The course aims to equip students with a detailed and critical understanding of the legal regime governing the use of force, its limitations, and the challenges of contemporary warfare.
courses.description: The course examines the manner in which international law regulates the use of force among States and non-State actors (jus ad bellum). By studying the law within its strategic context, the course focuses on the legal framework governing the use of force and its application in contemporary international relations.

courses.name: International Development Assistance
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with an understanding of the historical evolution of development assistance and the theoretical frameworks used to analyze it. It also examines the role of development assistance in foreign policy and introduces the main elements of international development assistance practice.
courses.description: Analyses of the historical evolution and our theoretical understanding of development assistance. Its role in foreign policy and main elements of the practice of international development assistance (actors, policy paradigms and modalities of intervention), through a methodology combining analysis and access to real-life examples and practitioners.

courses.name: Turkish Foreign Policy
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a historical and analytical understanding of Turkish foreign policy and its major turning points. It helps students examine continuity and change in Turkey’s external relations and connect domestic, regional, and global factors to foreign policy making.
courses.description: Analysis of Turkish Foreign Policy from the beginning of the Republic until 1950.
learning_outcomes: Comparing patterns of continuum and change between foreign policy machinations of successive Turkish governments.
Evaluating field-related issues in a transdisciplinary manner.
Analyzing theoretical knowledge and evaluating its reflections in practice.
Obtaining, assessing and classifying determinants of contemporary Turkish diplomacy.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: INTRODUCTION: SYALLBUS & FINAL YEARS OF THE OTTOMAN EMPIRE
Hafta 2: ATATÜRK'S FOREIGN POLICY
Hafta 3: WWII AND THE POST-BELLUM ORDER, 1939-1949
Hafta 4: TURKEY DURING THE EARLY COLD WAR, 1950-1960
Hafta 5: TURKISH FOREIGN POLICY DURING DÉTENTE, 1961-1980
Hafta 6: FROM MARGINALITY TO CENTRALITY, 1981-2001
Hafta 7: TURKISH FOREIGN POLICY SINCE THE AKP I
Hafta 8: TURKISH FOREIGN POLICY SINCE THE AKP II
Hafta 9: NEIGHBORS AND RIVALS
Hafta 10: TURKISH-AMERICAN RELATIONS
Hafta 11: TURKEY’S EU VOCATION
Hafta 12: TURKEY, RUSSIA, AND EURASIA
Hafta 13: TURKEY AND THE MIDDLE EAST
Hafta 14: CURRENT CRISES
resources: Turkish Foreign Policy since 1774, William Hale, 2012, Routledge

courses.name: International Environmental Politics
courses.purpose: This course provides a comprehensive and critical account of international environmental politics.
courses.description: The first part covers the conventional way of analyzing the causes, consequences and resolvability of a range of international environmental problems, and focuses on regimes established by state and non-state actors to deal with issues like ozone depletion, hazardous waste, toxic chemicals, climate change, biodiversity loss, deforestation, and resources depletion. The second part of the course engages with strands of green political thought, and explores what a more ecological approach implies for how we think about issues like security, economy, the state, global governance, development, and sustainability.

courses.name: Political Economy of Turkey
courses.purpose: This class is designed to give an overview of the political economy of modern Turkey.
courses.description: It begins with an introduction to the history of social coalitions dominating Turkey's political economy between 1908 and 1980. It then proceeds with a thematic focus on the current issues of Turkish political economy. Among the current issues discussed are: politics of economic reforms; political economy of state-market relations; political economy of Turkey's relations with the IMF/World Bank, EU and USA; political economy of state-society relations; political economy of Turkish democracy; and political economy of income distribution, and corruption.

courses.name: International Terrorism and Transnational Crime
courses.purpose: This course is designed to give students a comprehensive understanding of international terrorism and transnational crime from both conceptual and practical perspectives.
courses.description: On the conceptual side, the course explores the theories, origins, definitions, forms, strategies and tactics, international relations, and countering strategies of international terrorism and transnational crime. On the practical side, it includes guest speakers who are active counter-terrorism and organized crime professionals, visits to relevant departments of the national and military police commands, and a simulation activity of an international terrorist act involving all class members.

courses.name: Latin American Politics
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a general understanding of Latin American politics and society. It also helps students examine the dominant regional patterns across the continent and the national variations in political and societal development.
courses.description: A general introduction to Latin American politics and society. The course covers the dominant regional patterns across the continent, both historically and in contemporary times, as well as national variations in political and societal developments.
learning_outcomes: Can understand the historical development of Latin American societies.
Have detailed knowledge of contemporary theoretical and policy debates on Latin American societies and apply them to specific cases.
Assess the political trajectory of different countries in the region.
Can analyze recent political developments in Latin America theoretical knowledge and evaluate its reflections in practice.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: No required readings
Hafta 2: Quijano, Anibal. "Coloniality of power and Eurocentrism in Latin America." International sociology 15.2 (2000): 215-232.
Hafta 3: Macias, Anna. "Women and the Mexican Revolution, 1910-1920." The Americas 37, no. 1 (1980): 53-82.

Hafta 4: Selections: Chapters 1 & 2: The Development of Underdevelopment and Sociology of Development and Underdevelopment of Sociology in Gunder Frank, A., 1969, Latin America: Underdevelopment or Revolution. Monthly Review Press.
Hafta 5: Encarnación, O., 2016. “American Caudillo.” Foreign Affairs.
Hafta 6: Selections: Chapter 1: The History of the OAS, in Herz, M., 2011. The Organization of American States (OAS): Global governance away from the media. Routledge.
Hafta 7: Midterm no readings

Hafta 8: Selections: Chapter 1: State Terror in the U.S.–Latin American Interstate Regime in Menjívar, C. and Rodriguez, N. eds., 2009. When states kill: Latin America, the US, and technologies of terror. University of Texas Press.
Hafta 9: Gonzalez-Perez, M., 2006. Guerrilleras in Latin America: domestic and international roles. Journal of Peace Research, 43(3), pp.313-329.
Hafta 10: Sikkink, K., 2008. From pariah state to global protagonist: Argentina and the struggle for international human rights. Latin American politics and society, 50(1), pp.1-29.

Hafta 11: González Villamizar, J. and Bueno-Hansen, P., 2021. The promise and perils of mainstreaming intersectionality in the Colombian peace process. International Journal of Transitional Justice, 15(3), pp.553-575. Why Brazil’s Yanomami are being decimated by disease, mining. January 2023. Elénore Hughes and Edmar Barros. AP News: https://apnews.com/article/jair-bolsonaro-technology-politics-health-brazil-government-beb55045d93c3152c9ec8e8c79b32cfc.
Hafta 12: Waylen, G., 1993. Women's movements and democratisation in Latin America. Third World Quarterly, 14(3), pp.573-587. Recommended: read ~15-20 pages per day of PP

Hafta 13: China’s Evolving Presence in Latin America. January 2023. Felipe Larraín and Pepe Zhang. Americas Quarterly: https://www.americasquarterly.org/article/china-is-here-to-stay-in-latin-america/. Recommended: read ~15-20 pages per day of PP
Hafta 14: Mexico: reporters and activists hacked with NSO spyware despite assurances (The Guardian October 2022) Recommended: read ~15-20 pages per day of PP
Hafta 15: Payne, L.A. and de Souza Santos, A.A., 2020. The right-wing backlash in Brazil and beyond. Politics & Gender, 16(1). Recommended: read ~15-20 pages per day of PP
Hafta 16: PP must be completed
resources: Various scholarly journal and current events articles
The Parrot's Perch, Karen Kelit, 2019, She Writes Press

courses.name: Political Economy of Natural Resources
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the importance of natural resources in the international political economy.
courses.description: It explores how previously marginal actors on the energy scene, such as China, India, Russia, Turkey and their Caucasian and Central Asian neighbors, have become more central; the extent to which European Union energy requirements are driving this shift; how this shift is reshaping multinational business strategies; how ethnic conflict and terrorism are posing salient risks to major hydrocarbon supplies and supply lines; and the degree to which the need to preserve environmental integrity will ultimately limit fossil-fuel consumption.

courses.name: Gender in International Relations
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to highlight the role played by gender in world politics. The course locates a concern with gender in the broader context of International Relations as a field of study, and seeks to uncover and critically assess the gender dimension of key IR-issues such as war, peace, security, international political economy, development, and human rights.

courses.name: History of the Cold War
courses.purpose: This course examines why and how the two superpowers gradually became entrapped on opposing sides in different corners of the world.
courses.description: For nearly half a century, the Cold War rivalry between the United States and the Soviet Union created a glacial divide that haunted international politics, exacerbating preexisting local conflicts or producing new ones.

courses.name: European Union
courses.purpose: This course is designed to introduce the students to the history, institutions and policies of the European Union. It aims to develop an understanding of the basic dynamics of the European integration with a view of national and global contexts in which the EU has evolved towards an "ever closer union". The course does not require previous study on the EU.
courses.description: This course covers the history, institutions, policies, and decision-making processes of the European Union. It also examines major theories of European integration, Europeanization, the roles of key EU institutions, interest groups, Economic and Monetary Union, the Euro crisis, Brexit, and the political economies of selected Eurozone countries.
learning_outcomes: Can define major concepts in the field and distinguish between them.
Can examine the salience of data and evaluate its salience by use of theoretical and empirical data.
Can conduct independent research on the subject by use of different data sources.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Welcome, Course Policies and Procedures
Hafta 2: EU History
Hafta 3: Theories I: Neo-functionalism, intergovernmentalism and beyond
Hafta 4: Theories II: Governance and Europeanization
Hafta 5: The Council and European Council
Hafta 6: The Commission, the EP and the Court of Justice
Hafta 7: The Policy Making Process and Interest Groups
Hafta 8: EMU
Hafta 9: Euro Crisis I: Causes
Hafta 10: Euro Crisis II: Consequences, Brexit and Beyond
Hafta 11: Greece: a special case?
Hafta 12: Ireland: the ‘Celtic Tiger’ and its discontents
Hafta 13: Spain: real estate and other stories
Hafta 14: Portugal: austerity and beyond
resources: Required - Textbook: Crisis in the Eurozone Periphery, Parker, O. and Tsarouhas, D., 2018/1st, Palgrave
Recommended - Textbook: European Union Politics, Cini, M., 2016/5th, Oxford UP

courses.name: Causes and Prevention of War
courses.purpose: This course examines the causes and prevention of war. It aims to help students discover and assess why interstate and intrastate (civil) wars take place and how they can be prevented or at least controlled.
courses.description: The course covers theoretical aspects of war in the first part and uses various case studies in the second part to build an empirical understanding of war. It includes the study of the two world wars, ethnic cleansing in states such as Rwanda, and possible causes of future wars, especially terrorism, along with means to prevent such wars.
learning_outcomes: Be familiar with basic theories of the causes of war.
Understand and analyze cases associated with the causes of war.
Evaluate possible causes of future wars, including terrorism.
Discuss possible means to prevent or control wars.
Develop skills in critical essay writing and class presentation.

courses.name: Oil and Gas Law
courses.purpose: The primary goal of the course is understanding the basics of antitrust law, regulation theories, contract law, environment law, law of the sea, and labor law, and how they apply to the oil and natural gas industries.

courses.name: World Energy Politics
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the political, economic, and strategic dimensions of global energy issues. It also seeks to help students understand how energy resources, policies, and international actors shape world politics.
courses.description: Energy resources and their geographical distribution. Advances in energy technologies. Reference and alternative scenarios on energy. Supply and demand. Factors and actors affecting oil prices. Major actors in the energy scene (states, organizations, etc.). Main principles of energy policy. Energy policies and strategies of principal actors (U.S., Russian Federation, E.U., China and others). Concept of energy security. Turkey's resources and energy policies. Whether Turkey can be an energy bridge. The importance of international relations on energy policies.

courses.name: Landscape Representation
courses.purpose: This course aims at exploring new ways to interpret, conceive and express landscape by means of different techniques required to develop ideas from the early stages of design projects to the deployment phase.
courses.description: Topics include mastering ways of seeing, strategies of representation, finding of graphic style, creating materials and images to show design, and targeting different presentations.

courses.name: Flora of Turkey
courses.purpose: Building a general framework to familiarize students with the variety of Turkey's flora while understanding effects of climate, topography and soil on plant communities.
courses.description: The course covers the flora of Turkey and examines how climate, topography, and soil influence plant communities.

courses.name: Landscape Plants
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to landscape plants and their use in design through basic plant biology, classification, botanical naming, visual characteristics, and ecological requirements.
courses.description: Beginning level lecture and field study course covering basic biological structure of plants, their classification and botanical (Latin) names, plants' visual characteristics, ecological requirements and their design use.
learning_outcomes: Identify and differentiate physical characteristics, ecological requirements and landscape uses of landscape plants
Correctly use plants in landscape design
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction of the course
Hafta 4: Class Presentations
Hafta 5: Student presentations (Assignment 2)
Hafta 6: Introduction to Angiosperms / Urban green spaces
Hafta 7: Angiosperms (cont'd) / Principles of planting design (1)
Hafta 8: Shrubs, ground covers, vines and perennials / The Olmsted legacy: Central Park
Hafta 9: Pre-Jury of Park Design Project
Hafta 10: Principles of Planting Design (2)
Hafta 11: Desk critics
Hafta 12: Desk critics
Hafta 13: Student presentations (Assignment 3)
Hafta 14: Feast of Ramadan Holiday (No class)
resources: Dış Mekan Süs Bitkileri ve Peyzajda Kullanımları, Ceylan, G., 2000, Flora Yayınları
Principles of Horticulture, Adams, C. R., 1999, Oxford; Boston : Butterworth-Heinemann
Landscape Graphics, Reid, Grant W., 1997, Whitney Library of Design
Trees, Allen J. Coombes, 1992, Eyewitness handbooks, London : Dorling Kindersley
Bitki Materyali 1-2-3, Pamay, B., 1992, İstanbul Üniversitesi
Atatürk Arboretumu Ağaç ve Çalıları, Şengönül,S., Yılmaz, H., 2008, Bahçeköy Orman İşletme Müdürlüğü
Professional Planting Design, Scarfone, Scott, C., 2007, John Wiley & Sons, New Jersey.
The Planting Design Handbook, Robinson, N., 2004, Ashgate Publishing Limited
Introduction to Landscape Design, Motloch, John L., 2000, Wiley, New York
Residential Landscape Architecture: Design Process for the Private Residence, Booth, Norman K. , 1991, Pearson

courses.name: Introduction to Landscape Theory
courses.purpose: This course aims to build a theoretical basis for understanding the meaning and scope of landscape architecture and urban design.
courses.description: An introductory lecture-based course covering the major components of landscape architecture, including environmental conditions, human factors, landform and landscape elements such as topography, vegetation, water, and built material.

courses.name: Introduction to Urban Concepts
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to visual language, various concepts of urbanism, and different tools and approaches for understanding the urban environment.
courses.description: The course creates relationships between concepts across spatial disciplines such as architecture, landscape architecture, and urban design.

courses.name: Morphology and Topology of Urban Spaces
courses.purpose: To introduce students to the physical principles, functions, relationships, and elements that shape urban spaces, and to develop their ability to analyze these spaces typologically and morphologically.
courses.description: Discussion and analysis of physical principles, functions, relationships, and physical elements of urban spaces. Various techniques and methods are used to make a typological and morphological study of urban spaces.

courses.name: Site Design Techniques
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamental principles and techniques of site engineering as applied to landscape architecture and urban design. It focuses on developing the ability to analyze sites and translate that analysis into practical design and construction decisions.
courses.description: Principles of site engineering especially knowledge on site grading. Land surveying, office procedures for calculating cut and fill volumes for a construction project, site drainage and erosion control techniques.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate use of fundamentals of site design: site selection and resource analysis, topographic maps, site cross-section, grading, interpolation and slope analysis
Prepare contour maps, perform cut and fill calculation
Demostrate knowledge of materials and techniques used in site drainage and erosion control
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course
Hafta 2: Site Selection and Resource Analysis; Land Use and Circulation
Hafta 3: Landforms and Contours; Interpolation and Slope Analysis
Hafta 4: Grading and Earthwork calculations; Site Drainage, Storm Water Management
Hafta 5: Sketch Problem
Hafta 6: Paving materials
Hafta 7: Public Holiday
Hafta 8: Midterm Exam
Hafta 9: Site Structures (steps, ramps, walls, fences, curbs and edgings); Site furnitures and fixtures
Hafta 10: Sport facilities, Playgrounds, Rooftop Gardens
Hafta 11: Plant Material
Hafta 12: Lighting, Pools, Ponds, Fountains, Irrigation
Hafta 13: Research Project: Presentations
Hafta 14: Research Project: Presentations
resources: Basic Elements of Landscape Architectural Design., Booth, N.K. , 1990
Site Design and Construction Detailing., Theodore, W. , 1991
Site Engineering for Landscape Architects, Steven, S. , 1998
Peyzaj Yapıları II., Seçkin, B., 1997
Peyzaj Uygulama Tekniği., Seçkin, B. , 2003
A Guide to Site and Environmental Planning., Rubenstein, H. , 1987
Time Saver Standards for Landscape Architecture., Harris, C. & Dines N. , 1998

courses.name: Making of Urban Landscape
courses.purpose: A field study oriented elective course aiming to reveal the 'spirit' of the place and everyday life.
courses.description: Survey, analysis, diagnosis and design of the city's components to see and understand the urban form, its elements and related conditions. Public open spaces, squares, clusters of houses, transportation hubs, cultural nodes, recreational areas, etc.

courses.name: Analysis of Urban Environment I
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the analysis of urban environments through historical, theoretical, and methodological perspectives. It helps students understand how cities are shaped by economic, social, and physical forces.
courses.description: Urban settlements throughout different historical periods. Economic, social and physical structure of cities with the guidance of 20th century urban theories, investigations and evaluation methods. Urban-rural, urbanization-urbanism dichotomies, classification of urban settlements and finally variations in urban form and structure.

courses.name: Planting Design Studio
courses.purpose: This course introduces planting design and explains its significance in landscape architecture. It focuses on how plants contribute through structural characteristics, visual properties, symbolic meanings, psychological effects, and sensual experience.
courses.description: Introduction to planting design and its significance in landscape architecture considering its structural characteristics, visual properties, symbolic meanings, psychological effects and sensual experience.
learning_outcomes: Identify structural characteristics, visual properties and symbolic meanings of plants
Demonstrate use of plants in design with regard to their psychological effects and sensual experiences
Able to communicate design ideas and proposals to clients and the public
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to “Planting Design”; ‘Analogy Between Landscape Architecture and Abstract Painting – Modernism in Landscape Architecture’ by HK
Hafta 2: ‘PROJECT 1: 'AN URBAN POCKET PARK DESIGN INSPIRED BY AN ARTIST’; Lecture on ‘Abstract Art’ by Ayşegül Tokol; Selection of the Art Work; In-class Work 1: Initial sketches on selected art work
Hafta 3: ‘Contemporary Approaches in Planting Design’ by HK; Critiques on Project 1; In-class Work 2: Preparation of “Plant List” and “Seasonal Daigram”
Hafta 4: ‘Planting Design’ by Tülin Munzur Göçer; Critiques; In-class Work 3: Planting Plan
Hafta 5: Critiques on Project 1
Hafta 6: Submission and Presentation of Project 1
Hafta 7: ‘A THEMATIC PARK DESIGN’; Presentation of “Inspiration Boards”; Presentation of “Festival du Domaine Chaumont sur Loire”
Hafta 8: Spring Break
Hafta 9: critiques on the general design layout
Hafta 10: Critiques on plant composition; WORKSHOP: MY MOOD! MY GARDEN!
Hafta 11: in-class work ‘benig piet oudolf’; critiques plant selection and composition
Hafta 12: Preliminar Jury
Hafta 13: Final Critiques on Plant Composition
Hafta 14: Workshop 2: PRINCIPLE LIGHTING by Pınar Onat
resources: Garden Design, Asensio, Paco, 2003, Teneues, New York
Austin, Richard, Designing with Plants, 1982, Van Nostrand Reinhold, New York
Moods in the Landscape, Bye, A.E., 1999, Washington, DC
Garrett Eckbo: Modern Landscape for Living, Treib, Marc and Imbert, Dorothee, 1997, University of California Press
Creating a Garden for the Senses, Pavia, Jerry, 1993, Abbeville Press, New York
Landscape Design with Plants, Clouston, B., 1994, Oxford: Butterworth Architecture
Festival du Domaine de Chaumont sur Loire

courses.name: Senior Design Research
courses.purpose: This course provides a knowledge basis for the Senior Design Studio and prepares students for a variety of professional careers. It also aims to strengthen critical and analytical thinking, creative problem solving, and social awareness.
courses.description: A seminar format research course focused on building a foundation for the Senior Design Studio. The course includes research methods, locating academic resources, seminar discussions, presentations, writing workshops, tutorials, and the development of a research paper/report.
learning_outcomes: Analyze and produce solutions to situations and problems of professional practice
Reflect on various opinions raised by professionals and put forward their own viewpoint on certain projects
Able to demonstrate an understanding of professional and ethical responsibility
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course and discussion of LAUD402 themes
Hafta 2: Introduction to research methods + searching for and locating academic resources
Hafta 3: Place: Seminar discussion + presentations
Hafta 4: Experience: Seminar discussion + presentations
Hafta 5: Event: Seminar discussion + presentations
Hafta 6: Time: Seminar discussion + presentations
Hafta 7: Place-making: Summative discussion
Hafta 8: Writing workhops 1-2
Hafta 9: Writing workshops 3-4
Hafta 10: Tutorials for Research Feedback
Hafta 11: PechaKucha presentations
Hafta 12: Tutorials for research feedback
Hafta 13: Tutorials for research feedback
Hafta 14: No class: Ramazan Bayramı
resources: Required - Reading: reading list will be provided in class

courses.name: Landscape Construction: Techniques, Materials and Structures
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to provide students with basic information about materials and structures used in landscape design, their design potential and limitations, design details, and construction methods. It also aims to develop creative abilities and skills in project preparation and the construction of small forms in civil engineering solutions for the realization of the urban environment.

courses.name: Analysis of Urban Environment
courses.purpose: This course is intended to form a laboratory for students in which they can explore urban analysis techniques at different scales by using urban sociology and geography concepts, and to associate these analyses with different design problems.
courses.description: This course explores urban analysis techniques at different scales through the use of urban sociology and geography concepts. It also focuses on the association and use of these analyses with different design problems.
learning_outcomes: Apply different analysis techniques to existing cities
Able to conduct surveys, as well as to analyze and interpret spatial data
Understand the impact of design solutions in a global, economic, environmental, and societal context
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: INTRODUCTION: CONTEXT & CONCEPTS. SIZE & SCALE (MACRO- MEZO- MICRO ANALYSIS)
Hafta 2: METHODS & TECHNIQUES of URBAN ANALYSIS: SPATIAL, STATISTICAL & THEMATIC. PROCESS of URBAN ANALYSIS: SURVEY & DOCUMENTATION, ANALYSIS & EVALUATION
Hafta 3: No class
Hafta 4: PHOTOGRAPHY. AN INTEGRATED TOOL IN URBAN DESIGN. SKETCH PROBLEM01.
Hafta 5: TOOLS & TECHNIQUES for VISUALIZATION in URBAN STUDIES. GUEST LECTURER Dr. ECEM KUTLAY
Hafta 6: INTEGRATED URBAN ANALYSIS. REPRESENTATION of Assignment 01
Hafta 7: THEMATIC STUDIES: RESEARCH QUESTION & RESEARCH DESIGN. WORLD EXAMPLES, STANDARDS & LEGAL FRAMEWORK
Hafta 8: KEYNOTES: PRINCIPALS, CRITERIAS & OBJECTIVES. SKETCH PROBLEM02: SPATIAL SWOT ANALYSIS, INNOVATIVE THINKING & PROBLEM
Hafta 9: Spring Break
Hafta 10: Ramadan feast
Hafta 11: DESIGN GUIDELINES. SERIES OF DESIGN STATEMENTS AND IMAGES
Hafta 12: DESIGN BRIEF: EVAULATION & ASSESSMENT, DEFINING PROGRAM AREAS, IDENTIFYING INTERVENTION MODES, TRANSLATING GUIDELINES INTO A DESIGN BRIEF
Hafta 13: GUIDELINE–PROJECT INTEGRATION. AI-ASSISTED URBAN DESIGN GUIDELINES
Hafta 14: REVIEW & REFINEMENT. FROM IMAGE TO DIAGRAM: INFOGRAPHICS, GUEST LECTURER Dr. EREN EFEOĞLU
resources: Social Justice and the City, David HARVEY, 1983, 2009, Georgia Press
Uneven Development, Nature, Capital and the Production of Space, Neil Smith, 2008, University of Georgia Press.
Mobility innovation at the urban margins, Brand, P., Dávila, J.D., 2011,12 December publishing- September 08, 2014 web.
The Production of Space, Lefebvre, H., 1991, Blackwell Publshing
Can We Make the Cities We Want?. Centre for Liveable Cities, Feinstein,S., 2013, Lecture Series, 22 February 2013, MND Auditorium, Singapore.

courses.name: Contract Documents
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' understanding of how contract documents are organized and compiled for landscape and urban design projects. It prepares students to handle the technical and practical requirements of producing a complete documentation package for tender and contract processes.
courses.description: A lecture/studio course studying the technical and practical aspects of organizing the components of a full set of contract documents for a landscape and urban design project. All working drawings, bills of quantity, organization of the specifications and relationship of these components to the conditions of tender and contract are prepared and compiled in a portfolio.

courses.name: Realization of Urban Projects
courses.purpose: This course aims to examine the conditions, factors, partners, actors, and elements involved in the realization of urban projects in order to support the creation of better urban environments.
courses.description: This is an interactive course that addresses the legal, institutional, and financial problems that may arise at different stages of urban project realization. It also discusses various approaches to urban problems and exposes students to different case studies presented by guest lecturers.

courses.name: Professional Practice
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the practical aspects of professional practice in landscape architecture. It focuses on the social, legal, technical, ethical, and financial dimensions of working in the profession.
courses.description: A lecture/seminar course studying the practical aspects of professional practice with its social, legal, technical, ethical and financial factors. It covers relations between the designer, contractor and client, office management and organization, tendering and contracting procedures, and project management and supervision.
learning_outcomes: Identify stages of landscape design process and design management
Analyze and evaluate sample documents concerning design management
Able to demonstrate an understanding of professional and ethical responsibility
Able to use design techniques, skills, and tools necessary for professional practice
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Kick-Off Session - Introduction
Hafta 2: Initial Site Analysis: Confronting the Site and Its Memory
Hafta 3: Guest Lecturer / Site Analysis Critiques
Hafta 4: Site Analysis Submission + Preliminary Design Critiques
Hafta 5: Preliminary Design Package Submission + Hard Landscape Constr. Critiques
Hafta 6: Guest Lecturer / Hard Landscape Constr. Critiques
Hafta 7: Hard Landscape Constr. + Soft Landscape Constr. Critiques
Hafta 8: Guest Lecturer / Hard Landscape Construction Drawing Submission + Soft Land. Const. Critiques
Hafta 9: NO CLASS (Spring Break + Feast of Ramadan Holiday)
Hafta 10: Preliminary Jury
Hafta 11: Soft Landscape Construction Drawing Submission
Hafta 12: Guest Lecturer + Setting-Out Project Submission + Rendering Critiques
Hafta 13: Details + Sections + Technical Report Package Submission (Quantity Surveying & Cost Estimate)
Hafta 14: Final Critiques
resources: Time Saver Standarts for Landscape Architecture, Harris, Charles W., Dines Nicholas, 1988, Hill Arc. & Scien. Pub.
Spon`s Landscape Contract Manual, Hugh Clamp, 1986, E&F.N. Spon Ltd.

courses.name: Urban Sociology
courses.purpose: Compulsory fourth year course to provide a better conceptualization of the components of the city.
courses.description: Dynamics of urbanization process and the economic, social, and cultural aspects of urban phenomena. Spatial repercussions of different aspects of the urbanization process in different environments.

courses.name: Recent Issues in Human Geography
courses.purpose: This course aims to examine the relationships between society and space in a changing world, with particular attention to how social science approaches explain spatial processes and human geography issues.
courses.description: Relations between society, space and social science in a changing world. Recent approaches in analyzing the relationships between society and space in different time and space contexts with an emphasis on time-space geography, agency and structure relationships, local/global dynamics, images and symbols in different cultural contexts.

courses.name: Experiencing the City
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' understanding of human experience in urban environments, with particular attention to the relationship between private and public spaces.
courses.description: Human experience in various urban settings focusing on the nature of private and public spaces. Analysis of spatial and social factors that define the context of urban life and experience with selected cases.

courses.name: Space, Culture and Identity
courses.purpose: To examine the cultural and political contradictions of urban design and its role in shaping cities as sites of knowledge, modernity, and identity formation.
courses.description: Cultural and political contradictions of urban design. Urban design turning the city into a scientific object of knowledge, a laboratorium of modernity, and a site for the formation of new experiences and identities. Cases of urban design and its politics, historically and theoretically, from different places across the globe.

courses.name: Occupancy in Urban Areas
courses.purpose: To examine the relationship between people and the physical settings, particularly urban public spaces, and to develop an understanding of how occupancy patterns shape and are shaped by urban environments.
courses.description: Relationship between people and the physical settings, particularly urban public spaces. Field studies to analyze cases in different contexts.

courses.name: Cinema and Space
courses.purpose: To explore the relationship between cinema and spatial design through the theory of cinematic montage and imagery. The course aims to develop an understanding of how interior and exterior spaces can be read, designed, and perceived through cinematic principles.
courses.description: Multiple readings of interior and exterior spaces within the framework of the theory of cinematic montage and cinematic imagery. An analogy between cinema and space design, and the eye and the camera. Design as a cinematographic process. Both creative acts are organized in time through space and perceived through time in space.

courses.name: Visual Politics of Space
courses.purpose: This course examines how visual representation systems are connected to social power relations through the relationship between image and space. It also considers how visual representations produce meaning and the political implications of those meanings.
courses.description: The course focuses on the social functions of visual representations from the mid-19th century to the end of the 20th century. It addresses the role of painting, photography, and cinema in shaping the ways space is perceived, conceptualized, and socially defined.

courses.name: Landscape Ecology Studio
courses.purpose: To introduce students to ecology, ecosystems, environmental problems, and the limits of the environment in relation to human activities on land, with an emphasis on spatial theories and their applications at site scale.
courses.description: Ecology, ecosystems, environmental problems, limits of environment in reference to human activities on land; and tools of analysis, synthesis and assessment methods of landscape structure, function, change and processes to achieve sustainable environments. Focus on spatial theories and their applications on site.

courses.name: Introduction to Law
courses.description: Law and social order; the sources of law and the hierarchy of the norms; characteristics of common law and the continental legal systems; main branches of public and private law; organization of the Turkish judicial system; the application of norms and the methods of interpretation; transactions and rights.

courses.name: Constitutional Law
courses.description: Definition of State, Sovereignty, Constitution, Constituent Power, Hierarchy of Norms, Constitutional Review, Political Systems (totalitarian, authoritarian, democratic systems), Governmental Systems (legislative, presidential parliamentarian, semi-presidential systems) (in Turkish).

courses.name: Environment Philosophy and Ethics
courses.purpose: To develop students' understanding of the meaning of environment and human attitudes toward nature and other living things, and to build awareness, responsibility, and skills for addressing environmental conflicts.
courses.description: The course covers the meaning of environment and human attitude towards nature and other living things. It examines related environmental topics and issues in the light of environmental philosophy and ethics.
learning_outcomes: Understand the meaning of environment and human attitudes toward nature and other living things.
Develop consciousness and responsibility regarding environmental issues.
Gain skills to understand and resolve environmental conflicts.

courses.name: Civil Law: Introductory Provisions and Natural Persons
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic structure of civil law and explains how its rules are interpreted and applied. It also aims to build a foundation for understanding rights, legal transactions, good faith, and the legal protection of personality.
courses.description: Subject matter, historical background, sources and application of civil law; basic concepts such as rights, acquisition of rights and legal transactions; good faith (bonafides); protection of rights; natural persons, including the beginning and end of personality, capacities, protection of personality; and legal persons.
learning_outcomes: Categorize civil law by using the history and sources of the Turkish Civil Code.
Evaluate methods of interpretation and application of legal rules by considering Articles 1 and 4 of the Turkish Civil Code.
Explain the concept of rights, types of rights, ways of acquiring rights, and types of legal transactions.
Analyze the principle of good faith and the concept of bona fides through their fields of application.
Discuss the legal ways of protecting personality rights.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Meaning and subject matter of civil law, its history, systems; sources; Civil Code and Code of Obligations
Hafta 2: Application of legal rules: literal and opposite meaning; in terms of place, time, and nature; interpretation of legal rules
Hafta 3: Judicial discretion; gaps in law and legal gaps; customary law; judge-made law; doctrine and case law: TMK m.1, m.4
Hafta 4: Rights in private law: concept of right; types of rights
Hafta 5: Types of rights
Hafta 6: Acquisition of rights: original, derivative, constitutive acquisition; legal event / legal act / legal transaction; types of legal transactions
Hafta 7: Types of legal transactions
Hafta 8: Invalidity
Hafta 9: Principle of good faith and abuse of rights: TMK m.2; bona fides: TMK m.3
Hafta 10: Bona fides: TMK m.3; capacity
Hafta 11: Capacity
Hafta 12: Personality rights and their protection
Hafta 13: Personality rights and their protection
Hafta 14: Personality rights and their protection

courses.name: Turkish Constitutional Law
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the historical development and core structure of Turkish constitutional law. It aims to help students understand the constitutional framework of the Republic of Turkey and the functioning of its main state organs and fundamental principles.
courses.description: General overview of Ottoman-Turkish Constitutional Developments, General overview of Constitution of 1982, Characteristics of the Republic, Fundamental Rights and Freedoms, Legislative Power, Elections, Members of Parliament, Parliamentary Immunities, Executive Power, Judiciary, Constitutional Court and Constitutional Review.
learning_outcomes: Students who successfully complete the course can classify the structure and functioning of the legislative, executive, and judicial functions within the framework of the separation of powers principle.
Students who successfully complete the course can evaluate the functioning and procedures of the legislative body and the legal status of members of parliament.
Students who successfully complete the course can analyze the functioning of the executive body, its procedures, and the state of emergency regime.
Students who successfully complete the course can explain the judicial function and constitutional review in a constitutional state.
Students who successfully complete the course can discuss the powers of the Constitutional Court and the types of cases in light of court decisions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: presentation of the course content and resources; the scope of the constitution; key milestones in Ottoman-Turkish constitutional history.
Hafta 2: Overview of Ottoman-Turkish constitutional developments (1839-1908).
Hafta 3: Historical background and normative content of the 1921, 1924, and 1961 Constitutions.
Hafta 4: Historical background of the 1982 Constitution; scope and content of constitutional amendments made to date; meaning of the major change after the 16 April 2017 referendum.
Hafta 5: Characteristics of the Republic: the concept of republic, introductory provisions, and commitment to Atatürk nationalism.
Hafta 6: Principle of secularism / secular state.
Hafta 7: State respectful of human rights: basic principles of the fundamental rights and freedoms regime; classification, regulation, and limitation of fundamental rights and freedoms.
Hafta 8: Rule of law.
Hafta 9: Democratic state, social state.
Hafta 10: Principle of equality.
Hafta 11: Legislative body: structure and powers; legal status of members of parliament.
Hafta 12: Executive body: structure and powers.
Hafta 13: Constitutional review; binding force and supremacy of the Constitution; hierarchy of norms and the problem of norm review.
Hafta 14: Constitutional review (continued); general course evaluation.
resources: Türk Anayasa Hukuku, Ergun Özbudun, 2025/25, Yetkin
1982 Anayasasına Göre Türk Anayasa Hukuku, Bülent Tanör/Necmi Yüzbaşıoğlu, 2024/22, Beta
Anayasa Yargısı Dersleri, Şeref İba/Abbas Kılıç, 2025/8, Turhan
Türk Anayasa Yargısı, İsmail Köküsarı/Ömer Gedik, Şubat 2024, Yetkin
Anayasa Yargısı, Bülent Yücel, 2025/1, Seçkin
Osmanlı-Türk Anayasal Gelişmeleri, Bülent Tanör, 2025/39, Yapı Kredi Yayıncılık
İki Anayasa- 1961-1982, Bülent Tanör, 2019/5, Oniki Levha Yayınları
Anayasa Mahkemesi Kararlarının Yorum Yöntemleri Açısından İncelenmesi, Ömer Gedik, Temmuz 2022, Filiz
Anayasa Yargısında İptal Kararlarının Etkileri, Özen Ülgen, 2016, On İki Levha
Hukuk Eğitiminde Etkili Çalışma Yöntemleri & Hukukta Kariyer Plânlaması, Tamer Bozkurt, 2022, Yetkin

courses.name: Legal Persons and Family Law
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the legal framework governing legal persons and family law, with emphasis on the formation, structure, effects, and termination of legal persons, as well as the main institutions of family law. It also helps students develop the ability to identify and analyze legal issues arising in marriage, divorce, filiation, and guardianship.
courses.description: The course covers the general theory of legal persons, types of legal persons, association law, and foundation law. It also examines the purpose, sources, and principles of family law; engagement; marriage and its validity requirements and impediments; invalidity of marriage; divorce and its grounds and consequences; general provisions of marriage; matrimonial property regimes, especially participation in acquired property; establishment and effects of filiation; and custody/guardianship-related institutions.
learning_outcomes: Explain the purposes, establishment, structure, and grounds for termination of associations and foundations.
Determine whether an engagement or marriage has been validly established.
Discuss whether divorce grounds have occurred and the effects of divorce on spouses and children.
Distinguish between legal and optional matrimonial property regimes.
Evaluate the ways filiation is established and the legal consequences attached to it.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the law of legal persons; general theory of legal personality; types of legal persons; association law
Hafta 2: Association law and foundation law
Hafta 3: Purpose, sources, and functions of family law; principles governing family law; family in the narrow sense; engagement; marriage
Hafta 4: Marriage, capacity to marry, and impediments to marriage
Hafta 5: Invalidity of marriage
Hafta 6: Divorce (grounds)
Hafta 7: Divorce (consequences)
Hafta 8: General provisions of marriage
Hafta 9: Matrimonial property regimes, especially the participation in acquired property regime
Hafta 10: Establishment of filiation
Hafta 11: Establishment of filiation
Hafta 12: Effects of filiation
Hafta 13: Custody, etc.
Hafta 14: Custody, etc.
resources: Türk Özel Hukuku Cilt III Aile Hukuku, Mustafa DURAL/ Tufan ÖĞÜZ/ Alper GÜMÜŞ, Son Bası, Filiz Kitabevi

courses.name: Law of Obligations I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts, principles, and sources of the law of obligations. It aims to help students understand how obligation relationships arise and how legal transactions, especially contracts, are formed and assessed for validity.
courses.description: Concepts of "obligational relationship" and "obligation", sources of obligations, formation and validity of legal transactions; particularly contracts, representation, torts and unjust enrichment.
learning_outcomes: Identify the sources of obligation relationships by considering the basic principles and concepts of the law of obligations.
Determine whether a contract has been formed by understanding the rules on contract formation.
Explain the form of contracts, the principle of freedom of contract, and the cases in which contracts become invalid.
Explain representation and unauthorized representation.
Determine whether fault-based or non-fault-based tort liability exists in a concrete case.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Borçlar Hukuku'nun İlkeleri, Kaynakları ve Temel Kavramları
Hafta 2: Borç İlişkileri ve Hukuki İşlemler
Hafta 3: Sözleşmelerin Kuruluşu
Hafta 4: Sözleşmelerin Kuruluşu, Yorumlanması ve Tamamlanması
Hafta 5: Genel İşlem Koşulları
Hafta 6: Sözleşmelerin Şekli ve Sözleşme Özgürlüğü
Hafta 7: İrade Sakatlığı Halleri
Hafta 8: İrade Sakatlığı Halleri ve Gabin
Hafta 9: Temsil
Hafta 10: Temsil
Hafta 11: Kusura Dayalı Haksız Fiil Sorumluluğu
Hafta 12: Kusura Dayalı Haksız Fiil Sorumluluğu
Hafta 13: Kusura Dayalı Olmayan Haksız Fiil Sorumluluğu
Hafta 14: Haksız Fiil ile İlgili Diğer Meseleler

courses.name: Law of Obligations II
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a systematic understanding of the law of obligations, focusing on the performance, non-performance, and termination of obligations, as well as special issues such as conditional obligations, joint obligations, transfer of debt, and assignment of obligations.
courses.description: Performance of obligations, violation of obligations and its consequences, cease of obligations and some specialties related to obligations; namely conditional obligation, joint obligation, transfer of debt and assignment of obligation.
learning_outcomes: Students who successfully complete this course list the situations constituting unjust enrichment and explain the scope of the duty of restitution.
Students who successfully complete this course explain the modalities of performance and identify the reasons and consequences of non-performance of obligations.
Students who successfully complete this course determine whether an obligation has been terminated based on its legal grounds.
Students who successfully complete this course distinguish special types of obligations from one another and explain their legal consequences.
Students who successfully complete this course distinguish changes of parties in obligations and explain their legal consequences.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Sebepsiz Zenginleşme
Hafta 2: Edime Dayalı Sebepsiz Zenginleşme ve Edim Dışı Sebepsiz Zenginleşme
Hafta 3: Sebepsiz Zenginleşmenin Benzer Taleplerle İlişkisi ve Sebepsiz Zenginleşmenin Hukuki Sonuçları
Hafta 4: Borçların İfası
Hafta 5: Borçların İfası

Hafta 7: Borçlunun Temerrüdü
Hafta 8: Borçlunun Para Borcunda ve Tam İki Tarafa Borç Yükleyen Sözleşmelerde Temerrüdü
Hafta 9: Borcun Sona Ermesi
Hafta 10: Borcun Sona Ermesi ve Zamanaşımı

Hafta 12: Özellik Arz Eden Borç İlişkileri
Hafta 13: Borç İlişkilerinde Taraf Değişiklikleri
Hafta 14: Borç İlişkilerinde Taraf Değişiklikleri
