Recommended - Textbook: Borçlar Hukuku Genel Hükümler (6098 sayılı TBK’na Göre Hazırlanmış), Prof. Dr. Fikret Eren, 2024 , Yetkin Yayınevi
Recommended - Textbook: Borçlar Hukuku Genel Hükümler: Yeni Borçlar Kanunu'na Göre Hazırlanmış, Prof. Dr. Ahmet M. Kılıçoğlu , 2024 , Turhan Kitabevi
Recommended - Textbook: Borçlar Hukuku Pratik Çalışmaları, Baki İlkay Engin, Işık Önay, Tülay Aydın Ünver, 2023, On İki Levha Yayıncılık
Recommended - Textbook: Borçlar Hukuku Genel Hükümler , Haluk Nâmi Nomer, 2024/20. Bası, Beta

courses.name: Administrative Law
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the basic political and legal principles of Turkish administrative law and to provide students with an understanding of how administrative power is structured and exercised.
courses.description: Basic political and legal principles of Turkish administrative law; structural and functional aspects of central and local administrations, rule-making power of administration, unilateral administrative acts and contracts, and the legal regime of public personnel and public domain.

courses.name: Criminal Law II
courses.purpose: Bu ders, ceza hukukunda suçun oluşumu, suça iştirak, teşebbüs, suçların içtimaı ve cezalandırılabilirlik gibi temel kurumları inceleyerek öğrencilerin ceza hukukunun genel yapısını kavramasını amaçlar. Ayrıca ceza ve güvenlik tedbirlerinin niteliği, amacı ve uygulanması hakkında analitik düşünme becerisi kazandırır.
courses.description: Suçun oluşumunu engelleyen sübjektif nedenler; suça etki eden nedenler; teşebbüs, işlenemez suç ve gönüllü vazgeçme; suça iştirak; suçların içtimaı; isnat yeteneği; isnat yeteneğini kaldıran sebepler; tehlikelilik hali ve TCK'daki düzenlemeler; cezanın tanımı, işlevi, özellikleri ve türleri; cezanın belirlenmesi; kısa süreli cezalara alternatif tedbirler, erteleme ve koşullu salıverme; güvenlik tedbirleri; cezalandırılabilirliği kaldıran nedenler.
learning_outcomes: Dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler, suça etki eden nedenleri inceleyebilir ve ceza hukukunda teşebbüs ve iştirak kurumlarını kategorize eder.
Dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler, somut olayda suçların içtimaının söz konusu olup olmayacağını tartışır.
Dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler, ceza hukukunun genel ilkeleri ışığında suçun fail veya faillerinin kimler olabileceğini ortaya koyar.
Dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler, ceza hukukunun gelişiminde etkili olan okulları analiz eder.
Dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler, ceza ve güvenlik tedbirlerinin ne olduğunu, bunların amacını, niteliğini ve türlerini dikkate alarak, uygulanacak müeyyideyi yorumlar.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Suçun oluşumunu engelleyen sübjektif nedenler
Hafta 2: Suça etki eden nedenler
Hafta 3: Teşebbüs, işlenemez suç, gönüllü vazgeçme
Hafta 4: Suça iştirak
Hafta 5: Suça iştirak
Hafta 6: Suçların içtimaı
Hafta 7: Genel olarak isnat yeteneği
Hafta 8: İsnat yeteneğini kaldıran sebepler
Hafta 9: Tehlikelilik hali ve TCK'da yer alan düzenlemeler
Hafta 10: Cezanın tanımı, işlevi, özellikleri ve türleri; cezanın belirlenmesi
Hafta 11: Kısa süeli cezalara alternatif tedbirler, erteleme, koşullu salıverme
Hafta 12: Güvenlik tedbirleri
Hafta 13: Cezalandırılabilirliği kaldıran nedenler
Hafta 14: Cezalandırılabilirliği kaldıran nedenler
resources: Ceza Hukuku Genel Kısım, Nevzat Toroslu-Haluk Toroslu, 21. Baskı, Savaş Yayınevi
Türk Ceza Hukukuna Giriş, Nur Centel-Hamide Zafer-Özlem Çakmut, 8. Baskı, Beta Yayınevi

courses.name: Criminal Law I
courses.description: Legality, sources and application of the criminal laws; General theory of crime; elements of a crime; Actus reus and defenses; superior orders, necessity; Mens rea and crimes of negligence; mistake and ignorance of the laws.

courses.name: Administrative Judiciary
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the basic principles of judicial control of administration and the structure of administrative justice. It also helps students understand the legal remedies available against administrative acts and the liability of the administration and its agents.
courses.description: Basic principles of judicial control of administration. Organization of administrative courts and Council of State; judicial remedies (action for annulment and full remedy action); liability of the administration and its agents.
learning_outcomes: Students who successfully complete the course determine the scope of administrative jurisdiction by interpreting legal regulations and court decisions.
Students who successfully complete the course identify the existence of conditions for filing a lawsuit in concrete cases by examining case law and legal regulations.
Students who successfully complete the course assess the legality of an administrative act subject to litigation by interpreting the grounds for annulment.

Students who successfully complete the course determine the grounds of liability of the administration accepted by case law and identify whether the administration has compensation liability in concrete examples.
Students who successfully complete the course analyze Constitutional Court and Council of State decisions by using the basic principles governing administrative justice.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: General principles
Hafta 2: Organization of administrative justice
Hafta 3: Scope of administrative jurisdiction
Hafta 4: Administrative Court of Conflicts, positive and negative jurisdictional disputes
Hafta 5: Types of administrative actions
Hafta 6: Conditions for annulment action
Hafta 7: Results of annulment action
Hafta 8: Conditions for full remedy action
Hafta 9: Results of full remedy action
Hafta 10: Principles related to administrative litigation
Hafta 11: Jurisdiction, venue, and parties in administrative justice
Hafta 12: Conditions related to filing a lawsuit and its form
Hafta 13: Consequences of filing a lawsuit
Hafta 14: Legal remedies
resources: İdare Hukuku, Turgut Tan, Bahar Bayazıt, 2023, 11. Bası, Turhan Kitabevi
İdare Hukuku Cilt 2, İdari Yargılama Hukuku, Gözübüyük Şeref- Tan Turgut, 2022, 10. Bası, Turhan Kitabevi
İdari Yargılama Hukuku, Ender Ethem Atay, 2021, Seçkin
Türk İdari Yargılama Hukuku, Akyılmaz-Sezginer-Kaya, 6. Baskı, Ekim 2021, Savaş Yayınevi
İdari Yargılama Hukuku, Ali Ulusoy, 2020, 2. Baskı, Yetkin
İdari Yargılama Hukuku, Gürsel Kaplan, 2020, 7. Baskı, Ekin

courses.name: Basic Concepts of Law
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic concepts and structure of law. It aims to help students understand how law functions as a system of social regulation and how legal rules, rights, obligations, and remedies are organized.
courses.description: Sources of law. Social life and the law. Definitions and concepts. Division of public and private law. Branches of law.
learning_outcomes: Differentiate legal rules from other social rules and evaluate the hierarchical structure within legal rules.
Understand the concept of person in law and explain how persons can exercise their rights and the limits of those rights.
Reason about how a dispute may be resolved and evaluate how the enforcement of the solution is carried out after the dispute is settled.
Discuss the sources and subjects of international law and understand the limits international law places on the jurisdiction of the state.
Characterize acts performed by the state based on public power, identify the remedies against such acts, and explain the basic limits of the state's power to punish.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction - definition and purpose of law
Hafta 2: Characteristics of legal rules as rules regulating social life
Hafta 3: Sources of law
Hafta 4: Branches of law
Hafta 5: Application of legal rules
Hafta 6: Types of cases
Hafta 7: Judicial organization
Hafta 8: Concept of right, types, and protection of rights
Hafta 9: Concept and types of sanctions
Hafta 10: Concept of personality: natural and legal persons
Hafta 11: Concept of liability and types of liability
Hafta 12: Concept of obligation: sources of obligations, consequences of obligations, and termination of obligations
Hafta 13: Right of ownership: scope, content, types, and acquisition of ownership
Hafta 14: Right of inheritance: heirs, dispositions upon death, acquisition of inheritance, and division of inheritance
resources: Hukukun Temel Kavramları, Kemal Gözler, 2024, Ekin Yayınevi
Hukuka Giriş, Kemal Gözler, 2024, Ekin Yayınevi
Hukuka Giriş, İhsan Erdoğan, 2024, Seçkin Yayınevi

courses.name: Introduction to Turkish Public Law
courses.description: Sources of Constitutional Law; Main characters of the state under the 1982 Constitution; Separation of powers; Legislative, executive and judicial functions; General principles of criminal and administrative law.

courses.name: Civil Procedure I
courses.description: Courts' structure, competence and venue, status of the plaintiff and the defendant, filing the lawsuit, submissions exchange, the trial, investigation, oral trial and judgment.

courses.name: Private International Law
courses.description: Conflict of laws, international procedure law, private international law, citizenship and foreigners' law, recognition and enforcement of foreign judgments and arbitral awards, international jurisdiction of Turkish courts.

courses.name: Law of Property I
courses.description: Concept, types and characteristics of property, legal characteristics of possession; acquisition, termination and protection of possession and related claims, land registry; particularly the structure and principles of registry, legal consequences of registration and correction of entries in land registry.

courses.name: Civil Procedure II
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' understanding of civil procedure beyond the basics, focusing on proof, evidence, legal remedies, and post-judgment review mechanisms. It also introduces temporary legal protection measures and alternative dispute resolution mechanisms within civil litigation.
courses.description: The proof of claims, burden of proof, evidence, legal remedies, intermediate appeal and appeal procedures, arbitration.
learning_outcomes: Students who successfully complete the course can identify the evidence related to the plaintiff's claim and the defendant's defense, explain how a disputed fact can be proven, and determine who bears the risk if it cannot be proven.
Students who successfully complete the course can understand the concepts of interlocutory decisions, final decisions, and legal remedies, and explain the scope of review in appeal and cassation.
Students who successfully complete the course can determine the meaning and effects of res judicata and discuss how it may be removed based on procedural or substantive law grounds.

Students who successfully complete the course can identify which temporary legal protection measures may be used to prevent possible loss of rights until the final legal protection sought is achieved, and determine their conditions.
Students who successfully complete the course can identify the duties and procedures of specialized courts and the matters falling within non-contentious jurisdiction.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: İspat ve İlgili Kavramlar, İspat Hakkı ve Sınırları
Hafta 2: Kesin Deliller; Senet, Yemin, Kesin Hüküm
Hafta 3: Takdiri Deliller; Tanık, Bilirkişi, Keşif; Uzman Görüşü; Delil Sözleşmesi
Hafta 4: Yargılama ve Hüküm, Dava Arkadaşlığı
Hafta 5: Davaya Müdahale, Davanın İhbarı, Ön Sorun ve Bekletici Sorun, Davanın Geri Alınması, Pratik Çalışma
Hafta 6: Davaların Birleştirilmesi ve Ayrılması, Dosyanın İşlemden Kaldırılması, Dava Konusunun Devri, Islah, Davaya Son Veren Taraf İşlemleri, Pratik Çalışma
Hafta 7: Vize Haftası
Hafta 8: Bahar Tatili
Hafta 9: Geçici Hukuki Korumalar
Hafta 10: Kanun Yolları- İstinaf
Hafta 11: Kanun Yolları- Temyiz
Hafta 12: Kesin Hüküm, Yargılamanın İadesi
Hafta 13: Yargılama Giderleri, Adli Yardım, Basit Yargılama Usulü, Çekişmesiz Yargı
Hafta 14: Tahkim, Arabuluculuk, Pratik Çalışma
resources: 6100 sayılı Hukuk Muhakemeleri Kanunu ve İlgili Mevzuat, Arslan, Ramazan/Yılmaz, Ejder/Taşpınar Ayvaz, Sema/Hanağası, Emel, 12. Baskı 2025, Yetkin Yayınevi
Medeni Usul Hukuku Pratik Çalışmalar, Arslan, Ramazan/Yılmaz, Ejder/Taşpınar Ayvaz, Sema/Hanağası, Emel, 22. Baskı, 2024, Yetkin Yayınevi
Medeni Usul Hukuku Ders Kitabı, Atalı, Murat/Ermenek, İbrahim/Erdoğan, Ersin, 8. Baskı, 2025, Yetkin Yayınevi
Medeni Usul Hukuku, Budak, Ali Cem/Karaaslan, Varol, 8. Baskı, 2024, Filiz Kitabevi
Medeni Usul Hukuku, Görgün, Şanal/Börü, Levent/Kodakoğlu, Mehmet, 14. Baskı, 2025, Yetkin Yayınevi
Medeni Usul Hukuku Ders Kitabı, Kuru, Baki/Aydın, Burak, 8. Baskı, 2023, Yetkin Yayınevi
Medeni Usul Hukuku Ders Kitabı, Pekcanıtez, Hakan/Atalay, Oğuz/Özekes, Muhammet, 13. Bası, 2025, On İki Levha Yayınları
Medeni Usul Hukuku Dersleri, Tanrıver, Süha, 1. Baskı, 2025, Filiz Kitabevi

courses.name: Commercial Enterprise Law
courses.description: Commercial enterprise, business transaction, commercial provisions, commercial jurisdiction, merchant, trade name, trademark, enterprise name, unfair competition, books of account, checking account, merchant assistants.

courses.name: Law of Property II
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' understanding of the legal structure of property rights and the rules governing ownership. It also introduces the acquisition, loss, and limitation of ownership and other limited real rights.
courses.description: Concept and scope of ownership, protection of ownership, ownership of movable and immovable property; particularly acquisition and loss of ownership, legal characteristics, types, acquisition and termination of limited real rights (servitudes, land charges and pledge).
learning_outcomes: Students who successfully complete the course understand the powers granted by ownership and distinguish the distinctive characteristics of co-ownership and joint ownership.
Students who successfully complete the course understand the acquisition and loss of movable and immovable property and determine the scope of ownership rights.
Students who successfully complete the course analyze the concept of condominium ownership and evaluate the rights and obligations prescribed by law for condominium owners.
Students who successfully complete the course classify limited real rights and explain their establishment and legal provisions.
Students who successfully complete the course understand the restrictions on immovable property arising from law and legal transactions and analyze the liability of the immovable property owner.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Types of ownership, co-ownership, joint ownership
Hafta 2: Subject matter of immovable property, acquisition, acquisition by registration
Hafta 3: Acquisition before registration, inheritance, court decision, compulsory execution, occupation, expropriation, other ways of acquisition, formation of new land
Hafta 4: Ordinary prescription, extraordinary prescription
Hafta 5: Loss of immovable property, scope of immovable property, horizontal scope, vertical scope, material scope, structures
Hafta 6: Unlawful construction, right of superstructure, conduits, encroaching construction, planted vegetation, sources
Hafta 7: Restrictions on immovable property, restrictions arising from legal transactions, public law restrictions arising from law
Hafta 8: Private law restrictions, liability of the immovable property owner
Hafta 9: Condominium ownership
Hafta 10: Condominium ownership and movable property ownership
Hafta 11: Types and characteristics of limited real rights, ranking, easement rights, appurtenant easements
Hafta 12: Personal easements, usufruct, right of residence, irregular easements, right of superstructure, source right
Hafta 13: Immovable burden, mortgage on immovable property
Hafta 14: Mortgage on immovable property, pledge on movable property
resources: Eşya Hukuku, Oğuzman/Seliçi/Oktay Özdemir, 2014/17, Filiz Kitabevi
Eşya Hukuku, A. Lale Sirmen, 2014/2, Yetkin Yayınları
Mülkiyet Hukuku, Fikret Eren, 2014/3, Yetkin Yayınları
Taşınır Eşya Hukuku, Rona Serozan, 2014/3, Filiz Kitabevi
Eşya Hukuku, Aybay/Hatemi, 2014/4, Vedat Kitapçılık
Eşya Hukuku, Şeref Ertaş, 2014/11, Fakülteler Kitabevi
Eşya Hukuku Dersleri, Hasan Erman, 2014/5, Der Yayınları
Eşya Hukuku, Esener/Güven, 2012/5, Yetkin Yayınları

courses.name: Public International Law
courses.description: Normative factor of international relations; particularly international agreements and international customary law, jurisdictional aspects of international law, settlement of disputes.

courses.name: Company Law
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the legal structure and functioning of business partnerships and companies. It helps students distinguish among different types of partnerships and understand the basic principles governing their formation, organization, and liability regimes.
courses.description: Ordinary partnerships, collective companies, commandite companies, joint stock companies, limited companies.
learning_outcomes: Distinguish commercial partnerships from private law legal entities such as associations and foundations by considering their distinctive features.
Understand that the ordinary partnership relationship forms the basis of partnership law and evaluate the rules on its formation, internal and external relations, and termination.
Identify the main differences between personal and capital partnerships and explain the scope of liability of partners in personal and capital companies.
Analyze the legal structure of commercial partnerships regarding their formation, organizational structure, and relations with partners and stakeholders, and propose solutions to related problems.
Analyze the organizational structures and operating principles of collective, commandite, joint stock, and limited partnerships in light of legal bases and fundamental principles.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Concept and elements of partnership; comparison of partnership with similar concepts; classification of partnerships
Hafta 2: Types of partnerships; ordinary partnership
Hafta 3: Ordinary partnership; general provisions of commercial partnerships
Hafta 4: General provisions of commercial partnerships
Hafta 5: General provisions of commercial partnerships
Hafta 6: Commercial partnerships in the nature of personal companies
Hafta 7: Commercial partnerships in the nature of personal companies; joint stock company
Hafta 8: Joint stock company
Hafta 9: Joint stock company
Hafta 10: Joint stock company
Hafta 11: Joint stock company
Hafta 12: Joint stock company
Hafta 13: Limited company
Hafta 14: Limited company; affiliated enterprises (group of companies)
resources: Ortaklıklar Hukuku I, Reha Poroy/ Ünal Tekinalp/ Ersin Çamoğlu, 2025 (16. Baskı), Vedat Kitapçılık
Ortaklıklar Hukuku II, Reha Poroy/ Ünal Tekinalp/ Ersin Çamoğlu, 2023 (15. Baskı), Vedat Kitapçılık

Şirketler Hukuku Cilt III: Sermayesi Paylara Bölünmüş [Paylı] Komandit Şirket ile Limited Şirket, Abuzer Kendigelen/ İsmail Kırca, 2022 (1. Baskı), On İki Levha Yayıncılık (Yalnızca belirli konuları kapsamaktadır.)

courses.name: Law of Obligations Special Provisions
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the special provisions of the Turkish Code of Obligations and the legal framework governing specific contract types.
courses.description: Scope and characteristics of special provisions of Turkish Code of Obligations, nominate and innominate contracts, sales contract, rental contract, contract of mandate, bailment contract.

courses.name: World Trade Organization Law
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the basic features of WTO law and its dispute settlement system, and examines the interaction between Turkish law and WTO law.
courses.description: The course provides an overview of the agreements annexed to the WTO Agreement and examines in detail the Agreement on Technical Barriers to Trade, the Agreement on Sanitary and Phytosanitary Measures, the Anti-Dumping Agreement, the Agreement on Subsidies and Countervailing Measures, and the Agreement on Safeguards. It also explains the WTO dispute settlement system and concludes with the main issues of importance to Turkey under WTO law.

courses.name: Law of Negotiable Instruments
courses.description: Negotiable instruments, drafts, bonds, bills of exchange.

courses.name: Inheritance Law
courses.description: Concept of inheritance, deceased, heirship, functions and intentions of inheritance law, the terminology of inheritance law, general principles of inheritance law, legal heirship, testamentary heirship, forced heirship, falcidian portion, protection of falcidian portion, abridgement, discretionary portion, testamentary disposition, the types of testamentary disposition, invalidity of testamentary disposition, interpretation of testamentary disposition, testament, testamentary contract, calculation of succession, hotchpot, protection and administration of succession, co-ownership of heirs, partition of the succession.

courses.name: International Human Rights Law
courses.purpose: The course aims to introduce students to the protection of human rights at the United Nations and European levels and to develop their analytical understanding of the main institutions, systems, and debates in international human rights law.
courses.description: The course focuses on the protection of human rights at United Nations and European levels. It covers the UN Charter-based system, the UN treaty-based systems, and the European regional human rights system. The syllabus also includes core themes such as sources of international human rights law, universalism versus cultural relativism, civil and political rights, economic, social and cultural rights, women’s rights, children’s rights, refugees’ rights, business and human rights, environment and human rights, and technology and human rights.
learning_outcomes: Familiarized with the basic concepts, standards and debates within international human rights law and will develop their analytical thinking on issues concerning international human rights law.

Learned UN bodies and procedures for the promotion and protection of human rights; international human rights treaties; limitation of human rights and constraints in their realization; differing views on the concept of human rights such as the issue of universality versus cultural specificity.
Able to compare European regional human rights protection systems, present the admissibility criteria to the ECtHR and discuss about national implementation of human rights protection in Turkey.

Able to solve case studies and examine specific rights including equality and non-discrimination, the right to life; the prohibition of torture and other cruel, inhuman or degrading treatment or punishment, freedom of expression and right to a fair trial based on relevant jurisprudence
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to International Human Rights Law: The Idea of Human Rights - History, development and institutions
Hafta 2: Sources of International Human Rights Law: Human rights treaties, customary law, and non-binding human rights norms
Hafta 3: Universalism vs. Cultural Relativism
Hafta 4: United Nations Human Rights Protection System (Charter-Based and Treaty-Based Machinery)
Hafta 5: Civil and Political Rights (ICCPR)
Hafta 6: Economic, Social and Cultural Rights (ICESCR)
Hafta 7: Regional Human Rights Systems (with a particular focus on Europe)
Hafta 8: Women’s Rights
Hafta 9: Rights of the Child
Hafta 10: Refugees, Migrants and Non-Nationals’ Rights
Hafta 11: Business and Human Rights
Hafta 12: Environment and Human Rights
Hafta 13: Technology and Human Rights
Hafta 14: Revision
resources: International Human Rights Law, Moeckli D., Shah S., Sivakumaran S. (eds), 2022, 4th Edition, Oxford University Press
International Human Rights: Law and Practice, Bantekas, I., Oette, L., 2018, Cambridge University Press
International Human Rights, Alston P., 2025, New York University
Case summaries or excepts to be communicated by the instructor

courses.name: Business Law
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts and structure of law, with emphasis on civil law, obligations, consumer law, commercial law, competition law, intellectual property, company law, and negotiable instruments. It aims to help students understand how these areas relate to one another and how basic legal principles operate in business contexts.
courses.description: Introduction to law, basic concepts of civil law, rights, acquisition of rights via good faith, the principle of good faith, abuse of right, law of persons, capacity to have rights, capacity to act, the origins of obligation, the formation of contracts, validity requirements of contracts, invalidity of contracts, performance of obligations, performance modalities, legal consequences of non-performance, the concept of commercial enterprise and its legal structure, commercial affair and its legal consequences, legal consequences of being a merchant, selected topics concerning business and commercial law.
learning_outcomes: Distinguish legal rules from other social rules and evaluate the hierarchical structure of legal rules.
Understand the concepts of person and legal capacity and explain how rights may be exercised and limited.
Analyze the validity requirements of contracts and assess the legal consequences of forming a valid contract.
Understand the modalities of performance and reason about the consequences of non-performance or improper performance of obligations.
Recognize the basic concepts and main features of commercial law, consumer law, competition law, and intellectual property law.

Understand the basic principles of the establishment, operation, and termination of general partnerships, joint-stock companies, and limited liability companies, and identify the types and effects of negotiable instruments.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to law: the concept of law and its different meanings; law and justice; characteristics of legal rules and their differences from other social rules; the Turkish legal system as part of the Continental European legal system; sources of Turkish law and hierarchy of norms.

Hafta 2: Introduction to law: distinction between private law and public law; types of sanctions in law; types of legal rules: mandatory / supplementary / interpretive rules; application of legal rules in terms of place / time / wording / essence.

Hafta 3: Civil law and persons law: concept and types of rights; concept of person, natural and legal persons; beginning and end of personality; legal capacity and capacity to act; protection of personality.
Hafta 4: Civil law and obligations law: acquisition and exercise of rights; especially the right of ownership and its limits; legal events; concept and types of legal transactions.

Hafta 5: Law of obligations: concept of obligation and sources of obligations; freedom of contract and freedom of form; validity of contracts; general terms and conditions; obligation to contract; examples of contract types.
Hafta 6: Law of obligations: provisions of the obligation relationship; performance of obligations; consequences of non-performance of obligations.

Hafta 7: Consumer law: concepts of consumer and consumer transaction; necessity of consumer protection; unfair terms; important topics and examples: prohibition of refusal to sell / prohibition of imposing aggravating conditions / unsolicited goods / defective goods and services / right of withdrawal / subscription contracts.
Hafta 8: Commercial law: commercial enterprise, commercial transaction, merchant concepts; merchant natural persons and legal entities; consequences of being a merchant.
Hafta 9: Commercial law: trade name, business name and trademark; commercial books; assistants to merchants.

Hafta 10: Commercial law and competition law: unfair competition; general introduction to competition law and its difference from unfair competition; general introduction to intellectual and industrial property law.

Hafta 11: Intellectual and industrial property law: distinction between intellectual rights and industrial property rights; general overview of the establishment and protection of intellectual and industrial property rights.

Hafta 12: Company law: introduction and basic concepts; general partnership: characteristics / establishment / relations among partners / management and representation / termination and liquidation; joint-stock company: characteristics / establishment / organs / management and representation / termination and liquidation.

Hafta 13: Company law: limited liability company: characteristics / establishment / organs / management and representation / termination and liquidation; mergers and acquisitions of companies, general overview.
Hafta 14: Negotiable instruments law: concept of negotiable instruments; types of negotiable instruments; bills of exchange in general.
resources: Hukuka Giriş, Kemal Gözler, 2023, 20. Baskı, Ekin Yayınevi
Medeni Hukuk: Giriş / Kaynaklar/ Temel Kavramlar, M. Kemal Oğuzman / Nami Barlas, 2024, 30. Baskı, Vedat Kitapçılık
Borçlar Hukuku, Genel Hükümler / Özel Borç İlişkileri, Turgut Akıntürk / Derya Ateş, 2022, 30. Baskı, Beta Yayıncılık
Medeni Hukuk, Turgut Akıntürk / Derya Ateş, 2024, 28. Baskı, Beta Yayıncılık
Medeni Hukuk’un Temel Kavramları, Bilge Öztan / Damla Gürpınar, 2022, 47. Baskı, Yetkin Yayınları
Medeni Hukuk - Temel Bilgiler, Ahmet Kılıçoğlu, 2023, 9. Baskı, Turhan Kitabevi
Bankacılar İçin Şirketler Hukuku Bilgisi, Tuğrul Ansay / Aynur Yongalık, 2018, 22. Baskı, Banka ve Ticaret Hukuku Araştırma Enstitüsü

courses.name: Administrative Law
courses.purpose: To introduce the basic political and legal principles of Turkish administrative law and the basic principles of judicial control of the administration.
courses.description: Basic political and legal principles of Turkish administrative law and basic principles of judicial control of the administration. Sources of administrative law; structural and functional aspects of central and local administration and regulatory agencies; rule-making power of the administration; unilateral administrative acts; contracts of the administration and legal regime of public personnel. Organization of administrative courts and Council of State and their competences; judicial remedies; liability of the administration and its agents.
learning_outcomes: Identify the sources of administrative law and discuss the legality of regulations.
Determine the place of institutions and organizations within the administrative structure according to the legislation they are subject to, and interpret the duty and authority relations among them.
Classify administrative activities based on legal grounds and determine the legal regime applicable to them.
Classify the types of cases that can be brought before administrative courts and compare their basic characteristics.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: General information on administrative law; characteristics of administrative law
Hafta 2: Sources of administrative law
Hafta 3: Administrative regime; basic principles affecting the administration
Hafta 4: Basic principles affecting the administration
Hafta 5: Administrative organization – general principles – central administration
Hafta 6: Administrative organization – central administration
Hafta 7: Administrative organization – local administration
Hafta 8: Administrative act – administrative action
Hafta 9: Administrative contracts
Hafta 10: Public service
Hafta 11: Police power activities
Hafta 12: Liability of the administration
Hafta 13: General principles of administrative procedure law
Hafta 14: Types of cases in administrative litigation
resources: Türk İdare Hukuku (İİBF'ler için), Bahtiyar Akyılmaz, Murat Sezginer, Cemil Kaya, 2020, Savaş Kitabevi
İdare Hukukuna Giriş, Kemal Gözler- Gürsel Kaplan, 2021, 27. Baskı, Ekin

courses.name: Tax Law
courses.description: Basic principles of tax law, parties of taxation, process of taxation, valuation, depreciation and equivalents, tax penalties, tax enforcement law, income tax, corporate tax, expenditure taxes: value added tax, special consumption tax, wealth taxes.
learning_outcomes: Bu dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler vergi hukukunun temel alanları ile ilgili kavramsal bilgi ve anlayış çerçevesinde somut vergi problemleri ile bu alanlar arasında ilişki kurabilirler
Bu dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler vergi mevzuatını, vergi hukuku ilişkilerine uygulayabilirler
Bu dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler vergilendirme ile ilgili uygulamada ortaya çıkabilen problemlere, vergi hukukunun genel ve teorik esaslarına uygun çözümler getirebilirler

Bu dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler gelir, harcama, servet ve servet transferleri üzerinden alınan vergileri, bu vergilerin temel özelliklerini de değerlendirerek kaynağı itibariyle birbirlerinden ayırt edebilirler
Bu dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler mevcut vergi mevzuatını, olması gereken hukuk bakımından değerlendirerek, eleştirebilirler
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: "Vergi"nin tanımı ve farklı vergi türleri
Hafta 2: Vergi hukukunun temel prensipleri
Hafta 3: Vergi hukukunun kaynakları ve yorum
Hafta 4: Vergilendirme yetkisinin uluslararası alanda sınırları
Hafta 5: Vergi ödevi (mükellef ve sorumlu kavramları)
Hafta 6: Vergilendirme işlemleri
Hafta 7: Vergilendirme işlemleri (devam)
Hafta 8: Vergi borcunun sona ermesi
Hafta 9: Vergi suç ve cezaları
Hafta 10: Vergi uyuşmazlıklarında çözüm yolları
Hafta 11: Gelir vergisi
Hafta 12: Kurumlar vergisi
Hafta 13: Katma değer vergisi
Hafta 14: Emlâk vergisi
resources: Vergi Hukuku, Nami Çağan, Ahmet Kumrulu, Mualla Öncel, Cenker Göker, 2025, 34. Baskı, Turhan Kitabevi
Vergi Hukuku, Selim Kaneti, Esra Ekmekci Çalıcıoğlu, Gülsen Güneş, Mahmut Kaşıkçı, 2023, 3. Bası, Filiz Kitabevi
Comparative Tax Law, Victor Thuronyi, Kim Brooks, Borbala Kolozs, 2016, 2nd edition, Wolters Kluwer

courses.name: Criminal Procedure
courses.description: Jurisdiction; Evidence; Burden of proof; Commencement and conduct of proceedings; Legal remedy (review).

courses.name: Introduction to Contract Law
courses.description: The concept of contractual obligation, obligational relationship, obligation and liability, legal transactions, declaration of intention, formation of contracts, freedom of contract, form of legal transactions, validity of contracts, interpretation of declarations of intention, representation, standard contract terms, cause of transactions, performance of obligations, legal consequences of non-performance, cease of obligations.

courses.name: Enforcement and Bankruptcy Law
courses.description: The organization of execution office, court orders enforcement, forcing of payments of debts, order of payment, objection, sequestration, forced sale, enforcement for negotiable instruments and bankruptcy.
learning_outcomes: Students who successfully complete the course can explain how the state, which has the monopoly of force as a rule, enforces court decisions.
Students who successfully complete the course can determine how to initiate non-judgment enforcement proceedings even when there is no court decision for monetary and security claims.

Students who successfully complete the course can analyze which assets and rights of the debtor may be seized in order to collect a claim, how the seizure procedure varies according to the subject of the seizure, and identify the remedies against unlawful actions by the enforcement office.
Students who successfully complete the course can evaluate the conditions for bankruptcy proceedings, the procedure to be followed, and the effects on the bankrupt and creditors.

Students who successfully complete the course can understand how to proceed in order to improve deteriorated financial conditions or to avoid a possible bankruptcy, and how concordat and similar institutions can be used in this context.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: General information about enforcement law
Hafta 2: Complaint, enforcement fees and expenses, notification, time limits, holiday and suspension cases, request for enforcement through general attachment, payment order

Hafta 3: Objection, action for annulment of objection, removal of objection, actions for negative declaratory judgment and restitution, annulment and postponement of enforcement, declaration of assets
Hafta 4: Attachment, participation in attachment, procedure of claim of ownership
Hafta 5: Payment of debt in installments, sale
Hafta 6: Annulment of auction, distribution of money
Hafta 7: Certificate of insolvency
Hafta 8: Enforcement through attachment specific to negotiable instruments, eviction of leased immovables
Hafta 9: Enforcement based on a judgment
Hafta 10: Enforcement through foreclosure of pledge
Hafta 11: Provisional attachment

Hafta 12: General information on bankruptcy, bankruptcy proceedings, postponement of bankruptcy, general information on concordat, types, conditions, legal effects and consequences, restructuring by reconciliation of capital companies and cooperatives
Hafta 13: Action for avoidance of disposition
Hafta 14: Crimes under enforcement and bankruptcy law
resources: İcra ve İflas Hukuku, Arslan, Ramazan/Yılmaz, Ejder/Taşpınar Ayvaz, Sema/Hanağası, Emel, 2024 11. Baskı, Yetkin Yayınevi
2004 Sayılı İcra ve İflas Kanunu ve İlgili Mevzuat, Arslan, Ramazan/Yılmaz, Ejder/Taşpınar Ayvaz, Sema/Hanağası, Emel, 2024 11. Baskı, Yetkin Yayınları
İcra ve İflas Hukuku Pratik Çalışmalar, Arslan, Ramazan/Yılmaz, Ejder/Taşpınar Ayvaz, Sema, 2024 20. Baskı, Yetkin Yayınevi

courses.name: Public Procurement Contracts
courses.description: Public contracts, conclusion of public procurement contracts, invalidity and termination of public procurement contracts.

courses.name: Public Procurement Contracts
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the legal framework governing public procurement contracts and the main issues that arise in their formation, validity, and termination.
courses.description: Public contracts, conclusion of public procurement contracts, invalidity and termination of public procurement contracts.

courses.name: European Union Law
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the institutional and substantive framework of European Union law. It also seeks to develop an understanding of how EU law interacts with national legal systems.
courses.description: The course covers the origins and historical evolution of EU law, the institutions and competences of the European Union, legislative procedures and sources of EU law, and the principles governing the relationship between national law and EU law. It also examines the main aspects of the free movement of goods, workers, services and capital, as well as the area of freedom, security and justice.

courses.name: Spanish I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Spanish. It aims not only to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns but also to improve their reading, writing, speaking, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Spanish. It is geared to A1.1 and to part of A1.2 of the A1 level of CEFR.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his / her understanding simple and very common expressions, when people speak slowly and clearly and to demonstrate his /her understanding straightforward tasks and instructions, simple questions, simple information about an object. Able to demonstrate his /her understanding short conversations on subjects he/she is familiar with (school, family, hobbies)

Reading: Able to read, to demonstrate his/her understanding familiar names, words and very simple sentences, for example on notices and posters or in catalogues. Able to read and illustrate his/ her understanding short, simple messages, very short texts.

Speaking: Able to greet people, intoduce him/her self briefly, to order some thing to drink and eat and to use simple phrases and sentences to describe where I live and people I know. Able to ask and answer simple questions in areas of immediate need or on very familiar topics.

Writing: Able to write very simple sentences, able to write who he/she is and where he/she lives, to fill in forms with personal details, for example entering his/her name, nationality and address on a hotel registration form. Able to write very simple messages and questions for other people (e.g text messages or "post-its",cards) and write some personal information concerning him/ herself (age, address, hobbies), describe objects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: UNIT 1: "IN SPANISH" Giving personal information and asking names, telephone numbers and e-mails. Study of simple present tense to speak in Spanish in class. Use of necessary words to study with the book given. Learning the alphabet, nouns and articles: Gender, number and verbs SER and LLamarse.

Hafta 2: UNIT 1: Reading the dialogue, conjugation of new verbs and explaining the content of the dialogue. Reading numbers from 0 to ten. Learning names of places, countries, cities and nationalities of some countries.
Hafta 3: UNIT 2: ”MUCHO GUSTO” Greetings and introducing yourself. Exchanging some personal information. Talking about the languages we speak as mother tongue. Talking about jobs.

Hafta 4: UNIT 2: Introducing and identifying someone. Talking about marital status and professions. Reading the dialogue, conjugation of the new verbs and explaining the content of the dialogue. Definite and indefinite articles. Verbs: to be, to work, to speak, to cal. Talking about hobbies.

Hafta 5: UNIT 3: "PARTY WITH MY FAMILY" Exchanging personal information. Talking about families and family parties. Describing the physical appearance and character of people. Talking about age of people. Telling Time.

Hafta 6: UNIT 3: Learning months and holidays. Reading the dialogue, conjugation of new verbs and explaining the content of the dialogue. Possessive adjectives. Physical description and character. Quantifiers. Presentation.
Hafta 7: UNIT 4: “GOOD WEEKEND” Talking about leisure and free time habits. Comparison of tastes and preferences about cultural activities, sports, etc.

Hafta 8: UNIT 4: Adverbs of frequency. Verbs gustar y encantar. Reading the dialogue, conjugation of the new verbs and explaining the content of the dialogue. Making offers. Talking about free time activities. Irregular Verbs.
Hafta 9: UNIT 5: “CALLE MAYOR” Describing the neighborhood, public places and public services. Learning the verbs there be, have and to be.

Hafta 10: UNIT 5: Making offers an making an appointment. Asking and telling the time. Thanking and apologizing. Reading the dialogue, conjugation of new verbs and explaining the content of the dialogue.
Hafta 11: UNIT 5: Giving address and talking about the environment with new verbs.
Hafta 12: UNIT 5: Adjectives to describe a city. Quantifiers: very, a lot , / a little. Adverbs . Use of there is/there are and verb to be.

Hafta 13: UNIT 6: “THE MENU OF THE DAY” Talking about eating habits. Ordering food and drinks at a restaurant. Reading the menu and order. Reading the dialogue, conjugation of new verbs and explaining the content of the dialogue. Making offers.
Hafta 14: UNIT 6: Eating habits in Spain. Activities: Use of Comparatives. Obligations = have to + Infinitive. Meals, foods and Drinks.
resources: Aula Internacional Plus 1, Roberto Castón - Eva García - Isabel Ginés, 2021, Difusión

courses.name: Spanish II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 111 (Spanish I) and is intended for students with basic Spanish knowledge. It aims to help students use acquired grammatical patterns and further develop their communication skills in Spanish.
courses.description: The course covers Spanish language topics and structures at a basic-to-lower-intermediate level, including describing places and residences, talking about plans and holidays, professions and jobs, memories and past experiences, internet and media use, health, and food/cooking. It also practices grammar such as ir + a + infinitive, prepositions of place, adjectives and colors, reflexive pronouns, comparatives, simple past tense, direct and indirect objects, present perfect tense, and related vocabulary and functional expressions.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate understanding of the main points in simple, clear, short announcements and messages; extract important information from short radio broadcasts; and understand high-frequency vocabulary related to immediate personal relevance.
Reading: Able to read and understand simple texts, find specific predictable information in everyday materials, and understand short simple personal letters.
Speaking: Able to answer simple questions, interact in a simple way, express familiar feelings, have short telephone conversations, make simple purchases, and ask straightforward questions.
Writing: Able to write about everyday life, compose very simple cards and short greetings, and reply to invitations or change appointments using simple words.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: UNIT 7: “RURAL AREAS AND BEACHES” Description of natural areas and resorts. Talking about plans, wishes and intentions. Talking about the weather and climate of a place and holidays. Reading the dialogue, learning new verbs and explaining the content of the dialogue. Talking about the places to stay in holidays and the activities there.
Hafta 2: UNIT 7: Verb Ir + a + infinitive (be going to + Verb infinitive). Prepositions of place. Ways of transportation while going holidays and the period for a stay there.
Hafta 3: UNIT 8: "FLAT FOR RENT" Identification of residences (rooms, decoration, furniture, etc.). Talking about the rules to be followed at home. Name of the colors.
Hafta 4: UNIT 8: Adjectives and colors. Reflective pronouns with verbs: Wake up, get up, have a shower or have a bath, etc. Irregular verbs.

Hafta 5: UNIT 9: "WORK OR JOB" Definition of a profession: place, time, general duties. Asking and giving information about the requirements to do a job well. Giving opinion on the jobs. Agree and disagree. Reporting on professional knowledge or skills. Comparatives.
Hafta 6: UNIT 9: Required and not required. Comparatives and their use with verb to be. Occupations and definition of jobs. Work-related verbs. Reading CV.
Hafta 7: UNIT 10: “MEMORIES” Talk about important dates and moments in our lives. Ask about past experiences. Simple Past Tense.
Hafta 8: UNIT 10: Irregular verbs in Simple past tense. Direct and indirect object.
Hafta 9: UNIT 10: Travel memories. Activities in previous holidays.
Hafta 10: UNIT 1: CONNECTION THROUGH INTERNET. Frequency of using the internet and its reasons. Gaining information and talking about radio and television programs.
Hafta 11: UNIT 1: New irregular verbs and asking questions about daily activities. Conjunctions before and after. Interest in the news followed through Internet.
Hafta 12: UNIT 2: TALKING ABOUT HEALTH. Talking about symptoms, moods, illnesses and medication.
Hafta 13: UNIT 2: Talk about pains. Parts of the body. Present Perfect tense.

Hafta 14: UNIT 3: COOKING. Talk about food and cooking. Learn and write recipes. Give your opinions about the kitchen and shopping. Spices and sauces. Quantities. Beverages. Traditional cuisines. Restaurants and environments.
resources: Agencia Ele 1 Nueva edición A1 Libro de clase / Agencia Ele 2 Nueva edición A2 Libro de clase, José Amenós- Manuela Gil-Toresano-Inés Soria/ Manuel Gil-Toresano-Inés Soria-Aurora Duque, 2017, SGEL
Agencia Ele 1 Nueva edición A1 Libro de ejercicios-Agencia Ele 2 , José Amenós Pons, Mª Luisa Pascual Vallejo, Yolanda Pérez Sinusía, 2017, SGEL

courses.name: Spanish III
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 112 (Spanish II) and is intended for students with basic Spanish knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to a part of A2.1 and to A2.2 of the A2 level of CEFR. It covers vocabulary and grammar related to music, weekly plans, future actions, travel, past tense, physical descriptions, clothing and shopping, places and climate, lifestyles, permissions, accommodations, science, and life abroad.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding the main points of clear standard inputs on familiar topics about work, school, leisure, etc. Able to demonstrate his/her understanding of the most of radio or TV programs on current affairs or topics of interest personal or professional title if the delivery is relatively slow and clear.
Reading: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding essential information on advertisements, when they are clear and simple.

Speaking: Able to communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring an exchange of information simple and directions on familiar topics and activities. Able to handle very short social exchanges. Able to use a series of phrases and sentences to describe in simple terms his/her family and other people, his/her living conditions, educational background and present or most recent job.
Writing: Able to describe the daily activities or referring to the past. Also formulate projects and fix in writing of the action taken.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: UNIT 4: “MUSIC” Exchange information about tastes, preferences and interests related to music.
Hafta 2: UNIT 4: Express plans and intentions. Make an appointment. Propose, decline and accept invitations. Asking for some information about shows and buying a ticket.
Hafta 3: UNIT 5: "WHAT A WEEK!" Talk about our weekly agenda. Exchange information about actions about future actions: obligations and plans. Talk about news and react them.

Hafta 4: UNIT 5: Survey on the street. Read the dialogue, conjugate the new verbs, explain the content of the dialogue. Propose an activity. Use of “already and not yet” in Present Perfect tense. Temporal expressions for the future: within ..., the day after tomorrow ... Use of present tense in the news.

Hafta 5: UNIT 6: Types of travel and activities. Vocabulary for vacation. Past tense: regular and irregular verbs. Temporal changes for past tense. Conjunctions. Reading the dialogue, conjugate the new verbs, explaining the content of the dialogue. Propose an activity.

Hafta 6: UNIT 7: “TALK ABOUT PAST- used to + Verb” Physical description of the people in the past. Talk about earlier behaviors as habits. Describe changes in people over the years. Buying clothes in a store.
Hafta 7: UNIT 7: Physical description of people: clothes and shopping habits, the sales. Read the dialogue, conjugate the new verbs, explain the content of the dialogue. Regular and irregular verbs.

Hafta 8: UNIT 8: “PLANET AND WATER” Describe the places and the landscapes. Talk about the climate of a place. Protection of natural environment. Oral presentation to be made by the students about their environment.
Hafta 9: UNIT 8: Seasons. Geography and climate. Seasonal and climatic changes. Talk about responsibilities. Use of the gerund and pronouns.
Hafta 10: UNIT 8: Give instructions with imperatives. Irregular verbs. Read the dialogue, conjugate the new verbs, explain the content of the dialogue.

Hafta 11: UNIT 9: “WAYS OF LIVING” Exchange information about different lifestyles and compare them. Express wishes about the place of residence and housing. Ask permissions with modal auxiliaries and give answers using imperatives. Accommodations and reservation.

Hafta 12: UNIT 9: Language exchange. Read the dialogue, conjugate the new verbs, explain the content of the dialogue. Comparatives. Regular and irregular imperatives. Placement of pronouns with imperative. Express wishes: I would like + infinitive.
Hafta 13: UNIT 10: "SCIENCE". Biographical information about scientists: Talking and writing on their biography.
Hafta 14: UNIT 10: Life Abroad. Talk about school life and experiences.
resources: Agencia Ele 2 Nueva edición A2, Manuela Gil-Toresano, Inés Soria, Aurora Duque, Sonia Espiñeira, Nuria de la Torre, Antonio Vañó, 2017, SGEL
Agencia Ele 2 Nueva edición A2 Libro de ejercicios, José Amenós Pons, Mª Luisa Pascual Vallejo, Yolanda Pérez Sinusía, 2017, SGEL

courses.name: Intermediate Spanish IV
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 116 (Intermediate Spanish III) and is intended for students with third stage intermediate Spanish knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to the second stage of the B2 level of CEFR.

courses.name: Intermediate Spanish I
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 113 (Spanish III) and is intended for students with basic Spanish knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course is geared to the first stage of the B1 level of CEFR. It covers functional language use, grammar, vocabulary, and communication skills through thematic units such as learning strategies, work and generations, cinema, cities and the future, travel, giving advice, and life events.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to complete the task correctly after listening in relatively long discussions, if people involved do not talk too fast and use standard language.
Reading: Able to complete or answer to questions on advertising letters, formation leaflets, texts of varying lengths, personal letters giving a relatively detailed account of events and experiences.
Speaking: Able to give someone advice on simple matters, able to express his/her opinion on subjects with which he/ she is familiar.

Writing: Able to write an e-mail to describe every day things or events using complete sentences that are connected to one another, to write simple, relatively short texts he/she has experienced, without any help, to write a report on an important event or a personal experience in a school newspaper.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: UNIDAD 1: "VIVIR EN BABEL". Contenidos funcionales: Intercambiar opiniones sobre hábitos y experiencias de aprendizaje. Hablar de hábitos en el pasado. Redactar una biografía lingüística. Elegir estrategias de aprendizaje.

Hafta 2: UNIDAD 1: Gramática: Presente de indicativo. Pretérito imperfecto. Adverbios y expresiones de cantidad. Léxico: Biografía lingüística. Actividades en el aula. Aprender lenguas. Estrategias para aprender lenguas.

Hafta 3: UNIDAD 2: "TRABAJAR PARA VIVIR". Contenidos funcionales: Hablar de la experiencia generacional y del mundo del trabajo. Expresar preferencias y deseos. Redactar un informe y expresar porcentajes. Desarrollar estrategias para recordar vocabulario.

Hafta 4: UNIDAD 2: Gramática: Perífrasis verbales: seguir + gerundio y dejar de + infinitivo. El gerundio. Presente de subjuntivo. Conectores: pero, sin embargo, ahora bien, si bien, no obstante. Léxico: Generaciones. Adjetivos de carácter. Empleo.

Hafta 5: UNIDAD 3: "ME VA DE CINE". Contenidos funcionales: Contar curiosidades e historias de cine. Describir situaciones en el pasado. Contar anécdotas personales. Desarrollar estrategias para mejorar la fluidez en la expresión oral.

Hafta 6: UNIDAD 3: Gramática: Cuál, quién y qué. Verbos gustar, preferir. Tiempos del pasado: pretérito perfecto, pretérito imperfecto, pretérito indefinido. Pronombres relativos. Léxico: Cine. Géneros de cine. La expresión oral.

Hafta 7: UNIDAD 4: "CIUDADES PARA EL FUTURO". Contenidos funcionales: Intercambiar opiniones sobre la vida en las ciudades y los Juegos Olímpicos. Expresar situaciones futuras. Realizar una presentación oral. Reflexionar sobre las diferencias culturales.

Hafta 8: UNIDAD 4: Gramática: Presente con valor de futuro. Perífrasis ir a + infinitivo. Futuro simple: verbos regulares e irregulares. Léxico: Urbanismo. Prensa. Juegos Olímpicos. Diferencias culturales.

Hafta 9: UNIDAD 5: "¿VIAJAMOS?". Contenidos funcionales: Planificar las vacaciones y hablar sobre preferencias de viajes. Expresar planes, hipótesis y condiciones. Escribir cartas formales e informales. Conocer las diferencias entre tú, vos y usted en España y América Latina.

Hafta 10: UNIDAD 5: Gramática: Condicional simple. Oraciones condicionales. Conectores: sin embargo, pero, aunque, por eso, además. Léxico: Vacaciones. Problemas y soluciones para el futuro. Lugares. Relacionarse.
Hafta 11: UNIDAD 5: Léxico: Vacaciones. Problemas y soluciones para el futuro. Lugares. Relacionarse.

Hafta 12: UNIDAD 6: "YO EN TU LUGAR…". Contenidos funcionales: Dar y recibir consejos de forma oral y escrita. Describir personas y objetos. Relacionar hechos del futuro. Conocer el valor de los consejos en las culturas hispanoamericanas.
Hafta 13: UNIDAD 6: Gramática: Cuando + subjuntivo. Imperativo afirmativo. Complementos del verbo: complemento directo y complemento indirecto.
Hafta 14: UNIDAD 6: Léxico: Tener hijos. Consejos. Acontecimientos de la vida. Ser feliz. La casa.
resources: Aula Internacional 3, Roberto Castón, Eva García, Isabel Ginés, 2021, Difusión

courses.name: French I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic knowledge of French and helps students begin using the language in simple everyday situations. It also supports the development of foundational communication skills for further progress in French.
courses.description: This course introduces the basic knowledge of French. It aims not only to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns but also to improve their reading, writing, speaking, and listening comprehension skills. The course is geared to A1.1 and to a part of A1.2 of the A1 level of CEFR.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his / her understanding simple and very common expressions, when people speak slowly and clearly and to demonstrate his /her understanding straightforward tasks and instructions, simple questions, simple information about an object. Able to demonstrate his /her understanding short conversations on subjects he/she is familiar with (school, family, hobbies)

Reading: Able to read, to demonstrate his/her understanding familiar names, words and very simple sentences, for example on notices and posters or in catalogues. Able to read and illustrate his/ her understanding short, simple messages, very short texts.

Speaking: Able to greet people, intoduce him/her self briefly, to order some thing to drink and eat and to use simple phrases and sentences to describe where I live and people I know. Able to ask and answer simple questions in areas of immediate need or on very familiar topics.

Writing: Able to write very simple sentences, able to write who he/she is and where he/she lives, to fill in forms with personal details, for example entering his/her name, nationality and address on a hotel registration form. Able to write very simple messages and questions for other people (e.g text messages or "post-its",cards) and write some personal information concerning him/ herself (age, address, hobbies), describe objects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Meet and Greet; Syllabus / Introduction; Unité 0/Bienvenue ! Découverte du français – Premiers contacts – La langue de la classe; Unité 1/ Je suis…; Parlez en français !; Demander de se présenter, se présenter, les adjectifs de nationalité; Langues en Duo; Les articles, les loisirs, les nombres (32-69)

Hafta 14: Unité 7 / Chez moi; Vivre ensemble !; Chers voisins; Exprimer des règles de vie commune, Expliquer un problème domestique; Problèmes à la maison !; Vocabulaire, Vie Pratique

courses.name: Intermediate Spanish II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 114 (Intermediate Spanish I) and is intended for students with first stage intermediate Spanish knowledge. It aims to help students use the grammatical patterns they have acquired and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course continues the development of intermediate Spanish proficiency at the second stage of the B1 level of CEFR. It covers functional language use, grammar, vocabulary, and communicative tasks related to giving advice, making requests, reporting speech, expressing opinions, giving instructions, narrating past events, describing character and relationships, and expressing wishes and invitations.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to complete the tasks given about the content of most audio recordings or radio programmes that interest him/her, provided that people talk clearly and in the standard language.

Reading: Able to answer questions on short texts on topical or familiar subjects in which people give their opinions, assessment, points of view, and to scan straightforward factual texts in magazines, brochures or on the Internet.

Speaking: Able to ask people relatively spontaneous questions about a special experience or event, make a complaint in person, start up a conversation, keep it going, express his/her opinion on familiar subjects and ask other people questions.

Writing: Able to list the advantages and disadvantages of things, justify his/her opinion using simple words, write a curriculum vitæ in table form, and reply to an advertisement in writing and ask for further information on items of interest.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: UNIDAD 6: "YO EN TU LUGAR…" Functional content: giving and receiving advice orally and in writing; describing people and objects; relating future events; cultural value of advice in Hispanic American cultures. Grammar: cuando + subjunctive; affirmative imperative; direct and indirect object complements. Vocabulary: having children; advice; life events; being happy; the house.

Hafta 2: UNIDAD 7: "¿ME HACES UN FAVOR?" Functional content: expressing, accepting or rejecting a request; reporting someone else's words; cultural formulas of courtesy; asking for a favor and accepting or rejecting a request. Grammar: reported speech; verbal periphrases such as empezar a + infinitive, seguir + gerund, dejar de + infinitive, estar a punto de + infinitive, volver a + infinitive, llegar a + infinitive. Vocabulary: health; work and relationships.

Hafta 3: UNIDAD 8: "YO CREO QUE…" Functional content: exchanging opinions about press freedom and other rights; expressing opinions, agreement and disagreement; organizing a debate; reflecting on participation in debates in Hispanic American cultures. Grammar: ser/estar + adjective/adverb + infinitive / + que + indicative/subjunctive; creo + que + indicative; no creo + que + subjunctive; impersonality with se, one, and third person forms. Vocabulary: human rights; discrimination and equality; giving opinions.

Hafta 4: UNIDAD 9: "¿ME EXPLICAS CÓMO SE HACE?" Functional content: giving and understanding written and oral instructions; expressing purpose and possession; describing objects and how they work; talking about alternative ways of traveling; reflecting on giving orders and instructions. Grammar: imperative; deber/tener que + infinitive; para + infinitive; para que + subjunctive; possessives and definite article in special cases. Vocabulary: job offers; instructions; devices; coexistence.

Hafta 5: UNIDAD 10: "NO ME CUENTES CUENTOS" Functional content: telling and writing stories; narrating and describing people and places in the past; comparing; developing strategies for storytelling. Grammar: contrast between imperfect and preterite; pluperfect; comparative and superlative structures.

Hafta 6: UNIDAD 11: "PERSONAS CON CARÁCTER" Functional content: describing personality; talking about social relationships; apologizing and responding to apologies; giving opinions about cultural aspects. Grammar: adjectives of character; ser/parecer + adjective; tener + noun / + a noun with adjective; llevarse bien/mal; ser and estar. Vocabulary: changes; character; arguing; relationships.

Hafta 7: UNIDAD 12: "¡FIESTA!" Functional content: expressing wishes; inviting and making proposals; asking for and giving permission; expressing politeness; reflecting on justifications in Spanish. Grammar: imperfect subjunctive; expressing wishes with me encantaría + infinitive / que + subjunctive; ojalá + imperfect subjunctive. Vocabulary: celebrations; invitations.
resources: Agencia Ele 3 Nueva edición, Claudia Fernández, Florencia Genta, J. Lahuerta, I. Lerner, C. Moreno, Juana Ruiz, Juana Sanmartín, 2018, SGEL
Agencia Ele 3 Libro de ejercicios, Claudia Fernández (coordinadora pedagógica), Javier Lahuerta, Ivonne Lerner, Juana Sanmartín, 2018, SGEL

courses.name: French II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 121 (French I) and is intended for students with basic French knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to a part of A1.2 and to a part of A2.1 of the A2 level of CEFR. It covers French language topics and activities related to health, sports and well-being, travel and holidays, work and university life, memories, housing, and everyday communication.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding the main points in simple, clear and short announcements and messages about how to get somewhere, when someone explains slowly and clearly. Able to extract important information on short radio broadcasts. Able to understand phrases and the highest frequency vocabulary related to areas of most immediate personal relevance (e.g. very basic personal and family information, shopping, local area, employment).

Reading: Able to read and describe his understanding of texts written in simple language. Able to read very short, simple texts, find specific, predictable information in simple everyday material such as advertisements, prospectuses, menus and timetables and understand short simple personal letters.

Speaking: Able to answer simple questions using individual words, expressions or short sentences and to interact in a simple way. Able to express certain feelings that everyone is familiar with, using simple words. Able to have a short telephone conversation, with people arrange to meet them, e.g. able to make simple purchases, explain what he/she is looking for and ask how much an article costs. Able to ask straightforward questions on a subject or react to something that other people have said about it.

Writing: Able to write about people and things in his/her everyday life (school, family, hobbies, daily routine, projects...). Able to write a very simple card (e.g. to thank someone for a present). Able to use short sentences to say what he/she prefers or what he/she receives. Able to write a short greetings card. Able to reply to an invitation and cancel or change an appointment, using simple words (e.g. SMS messages).
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Meet and Greet; Syllabus / Introduction; Unité 8 / En Forme !; Forum Santé; Bonjour docteure !; Marcher, ça fait du bien; Docteur ?; Learn how to talk about your health, ask questions, express an emotion.
Hafta 2: Unité 8 / En Forme !; Sport et bien-être; Manger sain; Bougez au quotidien !; Learn about sport activities and healthy foods.

Hafta 3: Unité 9 / Bonnes vacances !; Qu’est-ce qu’on fait ?; Hôtel Atlantique, j’écoute !; À l’aéroport !; Vacances en Bretagne; Learn how to book a hotel room, describe a city and a country; Learn vocabulary related to the airport and a journey.
Hafta 4: Unité 9 / Bonnes vacances !; Visitez la France avec ses peintres !; Retour de vacances; Bonnjour de Marseille !; Learn how to describe a landscape and talk about the nature.

Hafta 5: Unité 10 / Au travail !; Rencontrez les étudiants ambassadeurs !; Visite de l’université !; L’année de césure !; Les études et après ?; Learn how to talk about their studies at university; Know how to tell their skills and personal projects.
Hafta 6: Unité 10 / Au travail !; L’exposition Hexagone; Talk about studies, university, skills, professional projects.
Hafta 7: Unité 10 / Au travail !; Pour ou contre le télétravail ?; A2 / Unité 1 / Nouvelles vies; Gael Faye, artiste multi-talents; Know how to talk about their jobs.
Hafta 8: A2 / Unité 1 / Nouvelles vies; Une histoire d’amour comme au cinéma; Vacances en famille; Les Journées du patrimoine; Talk about your background; Express your intention to do something.
Hafta 9: A2 / Unité 1 / Nouvelles vies; Les Jeux de la Francophonie; Envie de sortir ?; A2 / Unité 2 / Je me souviens; Saveurs de Corse; Talk about your tastes; Suggest, accept or refuse an outing.
Hafta 10: A2 / Unité 2 / Je me souviens; Revivre de beaux moments; Une vue de rêve; Telling a memory.
Hafta 11: A2 / Unité 2 / Je me souviens; Parlons météo !; Pourquoi on achète des souvenirs de vacances ?; La tendance rétro; Expressing liking and disliking; Asking about a memory.
Hafta 12: A2 / Unité 3 / Comme à la maison; Etudiants : comment trouver un toit ?; À louer; Learning how to describe a place to live.
Hafta 13: A2 / Unité 3 / Comme à la maison; Je suis intéressé par votre annonce; Rent a place to live.
Hafta 14: A2 / Unité 3 / Comme à la maison; Cent jours en bateau; La ville du quart d’heure; Entre voisins; Express disappointment; console somebody.
resources: Édito A1 Livre du cours (Unités 8-10), Lucie Mensdorff-Pouilly, Sergueï Opatski, Violette Petitmengin, Sylvie Pons, Caroline Sperandio, 2022, Didier
Édito A1- Cahier d'Activités, Roxane Amoravain Valérie Blasco Hélène Maspoli Lucie Mensdorff-Pouilly, 2022, Didier
Édito A2 - Methode de Français (Unités 1-2-3-4), Florence Gajdosova, Clémence Fafa, Alexandra Horquin, 2022, Didier
Édito A2- Cahier d'Activités, Roxane Amoravain Valérie Blasco Hélène Maspoli Lucie Mensdorff-Pouilly, 2022, Didier

courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 124 (Intermediate French I) and is intended for students with first stage intermediate French knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to the second stage of the B1 level of CEFR.

courses.name: French III
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 122 (French II) and is intended for students with basic French knowledge. It aims to help students use the grammatical patterns they have acquired and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course is geared to a part of A2.1 and to A2.2 of the A2 level of CEFR. It covers vocabulary, grammar, reading, writing, speaking, and listening activities through thematic units such as working, current affairs, health, going out, defending rights, discovering a foreign country, and living together.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding the main points of clear standard inputs on familiar topics about work, school, leisure, etc. Able to demonstrate his/her understanding of the most of radio or TV programs on current affairs or topics of interest personal or professional title if the delivery is relatively slow and clear.
Reading: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding essential information on advertisements, when they are clear and simple.

Speaking: Able to communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring an exchange of information simple and directions on familiar topics and activities. Able to handle very short social exchanges. Able to use a series of phrases and sentences to describe in simple terms his/her family and other people, his/her living conditions, educational background and present or most recent job.
Writing: Able to describe the daily activities or referring to the past. Also formulate projects and fix in writing of the action taken.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Unit 3: Working; Lesson 1 Finding work; Respond to a job offer; Choose a job; Lesson 2 Daily work; Lesson 3 Observe a rule.
Hafta 2: Unit 4: Find out about current affairs; Lesson 1 Announce an event; Leaf through a newspaper or consult an information site; Highlight information; Announce news.

Hafta 3: Lesson 2 Understanding a news item; Talk about a crime or a disaster; Write the story of a news item; Lesson 3 Give your opinion on a piece of information; Make a discovery; Say if a fact is true or false; Specify the moment of an action.
Hafta 4: Lesson 4 Describe the activity of a company; Name the professions; Project: Write a cover letter.

Hafta 5: Unit 4: Find out about current affairs; Lesson 1 Leafing through a newspaper or consulting an information site; Highlighting information; Announcing news; Announcing an event; Lesson 2 Understanding and telling a news story; Talking about a crime or a disaster; Writing a news story.
Hafta 6: Lesson 3 Make a discovery; Tell whether a fact is true or false; Specify the moment of an action; Unit 5: Stay in shape; Lesson 1 Explain a health problem; Express unease.

Hafta 7: Lesson 2 See a doctor; Make an appointment; Name the parts of the body; Present a health problem; Lesson 3 Telling an accident; Express concern; Reassure; Describe the circumstances of an accident; Express the duration.
Hafta 8: Lesson 4 Look after your form; Play sports; Relax; Project: Exchange health and wellness advice.
Hafta 9: Unit 6: Exit; Lesson 1 Plan an outing; Choose; Suggest an outing to someone; Lesson 2 Go to a restaurant; Choose a restaurant; Understand a menu; Order a meal; Solve a problem.

Hafta 10: Lesson 3 See a show; Talk about a movie; Lesson 4 Go to a party; Understanding information about a party; Telling a party; Unit 7: Defend Yourself; Lesson 1 Defend your own good; Express belonging; Claim your property; Claim your right.

Hafta 11: Lesson 2 Request authorization; Prohibit; Request an authorization; Give an authorization; Lesson 3 Enforce your rights; Judge an action; Read an opinion poll; Justify an opinion; Project: Make a complaint.

Hafta 12: Unit 8: Discover a foreign country; Lesson 1 Talking about a trip; Describe an itinerary; Describe an action; Lesson 2 Understanding information about the climate; Lesson 3 Compare lifestyles.
Hafta 13: Lesson 4 Tell the story of a tradition; Unit 9: Living together; Lesson 1 Be of service; Name household chores; Exchange services; Lesson 2 Meet new people; Describe a person.
Hafta 14: Lesson 3 Get along; Express disagreement; Project: Giving news; Oral comprehension.
resources: TENDANCES A2 Méthode de Français, Cahier d'activités.

courses.name: Intermediate French IV
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 126 (Intermediate French III) and is intended for students with third stage intermediate French knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing and listening comprehension skills.

courses.name: Intermediate French I
courses.purpose: This course is intended to build on prior basic French knowledge and help students use acquired grammatical patterns more effectively. It aims to improve speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills and prepare students for the first stage of the B1 level of CEFR.
courses.description: This course is a continuation of LNG 123 (French III) for students with basic French knowledge. It focuses on developing grammatical competence and improving the four language skills: speaking, reading, writing, and listening. The course is geared to the first stage of the B1 level of CEFR.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to complete the task correctly after listening in relatively long discussions, if people involved do not talk too fast and use standard language.
Reading: Able to complete or answer to questions on advertising letters, formation leaflets, texts of varying lengths, personal letters giving a relatively detailed account of events and experiences.
Speaking: Able to give someone advice on simple matters, able to express his/her opinion on subjects with which he/ she is familiar.

Writing: Able to write an e-mail to describe every day things or events using complete sentences that are connected to one another, to write simple, relatively short texts he/she has experienced, without any help, to write a report on an important event or a personal experience in a school newspaper.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Unit 0: Improving your learning. Lesson 1: Expressing yourself better orally; strategies for speaking a foreign language; Lesson 2: Read more easily; anticipating information and finding the meaning of unfamiliar words.
Hafta 2: Unit 0 Lesson 3: Exercise listening skills; distinguishing close sounds; Lesson 4: Improve your written expression; avoiding repetitions, organizing texts, and using right formulas.
Hafta 3: Unit 1: Getting informed with the media. Lesson 1: Respond to general information; understanding a news item; the present conditional.

Hafta 4: Unit 1 Lesson 2: Understanding political information; summarizing economic information; asking for details about an event; Lesson 3: Giving your opinion on facts or ideas; understanding arguments against the cell phone.

Hafta 5: Unit 1 Lesson 3 continued: Express doubt or certainty; appreciating the reality of a fact; Lesson 4: Questioning the media; understanding the work of a journalist; responding to a survey on media reliability.
Hafta 6: Unit 2: Take care of one's image. Lesson 1: Have personal projects; anticipating the future; the future anterior; phonetics on [y], [u], [i]; Lesson 2: Choosing your look; the French touch.
Hafta 7: Unit 2 Lesson 2 continued: Never without my coach; giving or taking advice; describing membership; Lesson 3: Be in physical shape; sport in the city and pollution.
Hafta 8: Unit 2 Lesson 3 continued: Health and the arrival of connected objects; practicing a sporting activity; the gerund.
Hafta 9: Unit 2 Lesson 4: Asserting yourself in a group; understanding the work of an actor; social networks; review.
Hafta 10: Unit 3: Make a trip. Lesson 1: Preparing for a trip; travel differently; choosing a type of trip; giving instructions; the passive subjunctive.
Hafta 11: Unit 3 Lesson 2: Getting around by car; driving lesson; driving a car and accident circumstances; making an accident report; the most perfect of the callsign.

Hafta 12: Unit 3 Lesson 3: Managing a problem; traffic problems in large cities; understanding an advertisement; posting an opinion on a tourism site; Lesson 4: Discuss means of transportation; travel at the best price; transports of tomorrow in Paris; giving precautionary advice.

Hafta 13: Unit 4: Maintaining friendly relations. Lesson 1: Recalling memories; understanding two emails and making a timeline; telling a personal anecdote; commenting on a souvenir photo; time indicators.

Hafta 14: Unit 4 Lesson 2: Facing a problem; making assumptions; disputes between friends; arguing and reconciling; past conditional; Lesson 3: Talking about friends; characterizing people; Lesson 4: Adapting to others; adapting to different behaviors; making friends in French-speaking countries.
resources: TENDANCES B1 Méthode de Français, Cahier d'activités., Jacky Girardet, Jacques Pécheur / Colette Gibbe-Marie-Louise Parizet, 2016, CLE INTERNATIONAL

courses.description: This course is geared to the second stage of the C1 level of CEFR.
