courses.name: German I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic knowledge of German. It aims not only to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns but also to improve their reading, writing, speaking, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to A1.1 and to a part of A1.2 of the A1 level of CEFR.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his / her understanding simple and very common expressions, when people speak slowly and clearly and to demonstrate his /her understanding straightforward tasks and instructions, simple questions, simple information about an object. Able to demonstrate his /her understanding short conversations on subjects he/she is familiar with (school, family, hobbies)

Reading: Able to read, to demonstrate his/her understanding familiar names, words and very simple sentences, for example on notices and posters or in catalogues. Able to read and illustrate his/ her understanding short, simple messages, very short texts.

Speaking: Able to greet people, intoduce him/her self briefly, to order some thing to drink and eat and to use simple phrases and sentences to describe where I live and people I know. Able to ask and answer simple questions in areas of immediate need or on very familiar topics.

Writing: Able to write very simple sentences, able to write who he/she is and where he/she lives, to fill in forms with personal details, for example entering his/her name, nationality and address on a hotel registration form. Able to write very simple messages and questions for other people (e.g text messages or "post-its",cards) and write some personal information concerning him/ herself (age, address, hobbies), describe objects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: MODUL 1/Lektion 1:identify himself and others; describe/ recognize information about people; use structures to talk about being, greeting and farewell.
Hafta 2: Lektion 2: explain his/her/own profession; describe/ recognize personal information; produce questions to ask people; create internet profile; write small texts.
Hafta 3: Lektion 3: describe/ recognize/ produce questions to ask for information about his/her/own family, languages she/he speaks, phone numbers, ages.
Hafta 4: MODUL 2/Lektion 4:operate conversations to understand and to ask for information about products; rate products positively and negatively; express/recognize prices.
Hafta 5: Lektion 5: operate conversations to understand and to ask for information about products; describe products; write information in forms.
Hafta 6: Lektion 6: recognize short e-mails, SMS and phone calls.
Hafta 7: MODUL 3/Lektion 7: describe/recognize information about activities, hobbies and abilities; express to pay and to respond compliments.
Hafta 8: Lektion 8: manipulate a conversation to fix an appointment, to react positively or negatively to the appointment; express/recognize time, date; write invitations/refusals.
Hafta 9: Lektion 9: express/recognize information about food and beverage.
Hafta 10: MODUL 4/Lektion 10: understand public announcements on train station, airport etc.; select information about schedules.
Hafta 11: Lektion 11: express/recognize information about the past; write about daily routine.
Hafta 12: Lektion 12: express/recognize information about events.
Hafta 13: Modul 5/ Lektion 13: Give and ask for directions; Understand the names of places and buildings in a city; Give information about the location of the buildings.
Hafta 14: Lektion 14: Wohnen: Read and understand the apartment advertisements; collect information about houses, recognize the rooms in an apartment.
Hafta 15: Lektion 15 : Read and understand texts about neighborhoods; tell about the advantages and disadvantages of the district.
resources: Momente A1 (Kursbuch), Sandra Evans, Angela Pude, Franz Specht, 2020 / 1, Hueber Verlag
Momente A1 (Arbeitsbuch), Sabine Glas-Peters, Angela Pude, Monika Reimann, 2020, Hueber Verlag

courses.name: German II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 131 (German I) and is intended for students with basic German knowledge. It aims to help students use the grammatical patterns they have acquired and further develop their communication skills in German.
courses.description: The course is geared to a part of A1.2 and to a part of A2.1 of the A2 level of CEFR. It covers grammar, vocabulary, and language use through speaking, reading, writing, and listening activities.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate understanding of the main points in simple, clear, and short announcements and messages; able to extract important information from short radio broadcasts; able to understand high-frequency vocabulary related to immediate personal relevance.

Reading: Able to read and understand texts written in simple language; able to find specific, predictable information in everyday materials such as advertisements, prospectuses, menus, and timetables; able to understand short simple personal letters.

Speaking: Able to answer simple questions using individual words, expressions, or short sentences and interact in a simple way; able to express familiar feelings using simple words; able to have short telephone conversations and ask straightforward questions.

Writing: Able to write about people and things in everyday life; able to write very simple cards and short greetings cards; able to reply to invitations and cancel or change appointments using simple words.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Modul 6 / Lektion 16: Termine; paraphrase questions to ask for support; express to offer support and to respond to requests; operate appointments.
Hafta 2: Lektion 17: express wishes; describe future projects; recognize wishes and projects.
Hafta 3: Lektion 18: explain pains; describe pains; apply for support and advices; reproduce and recognize advises.
Hafta 4: Modul 7 / Lektion 19: to ask for favour; express requests and response towards requests; describe problems and conflicts; to criticize and react towards critics.
Hafta 5: Lektion 20: define and recognize requests and orders about housekeeping; illustrate personal lifestyle.
Hafta 6: Lektion 21: identify rules; express positive and negative opinions; recognize newspaper articles.
Hafta 7: Module 8 / Lektion 22: express own surprising; make comparations.
Hafta 8: Lektion 23: evaluate the weather; express own opinion about weather; give reasons; best wishes for a pleasend trip.
Hafta 9: Lektion 24: talk about important days; congratulations; express wishes.
Hafta 10: A2.1 Modul 1 / Lektion 1: talk about previous events and the past; storytelling with phrases.
Hafta 11: Lektion 2: conversations when a car breaks down; to thank at certain occasions; to wish someone something; justify something; express importance; to evaluate something.
Hafta 12: Lektion 3: to evaluate something; give excusion tipps.
Hafta 13: Modul 2 / Lektion 4: distribute work orders and organisation duties; issue work orders; react positively and negatively against work orders; express the reason of an incident.
Hafta 14: Lektion 5: evaluate an event or a happening; make an offer; react against offers and express the reason; convince someone to do something.
resources: Kursbuch-Momente A1.2 / A2.1, Sandra Evans, Angela Pude, Franz Specht, 2021, Hueber Verlag
Arbeitsbuch Momente A1.2 / A2.1, Sandra Evans, Angela Pude, Franz Specht, 2021, Hueber Verlag

courses.name: German III
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 132 (German II) and is intended for students with basic German knowledge. It aims to help students use the grammatical patterns they have acquired and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course continues the German language sequence at the A2 level of CEFR, covering parts of A2.1 and A2.2. It focuses on developing grammatical competence and communicative skills in speaking, reading, writing, and listening through thematic units and classroom practice.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding the main points of clear standard inputs on familiar topics about work, school, leisure, etc. Able to demonstrate his/her understanding of the most of radio or TV programs on current affairs or topics of interest personal or professional title if the delivery is relatively slow and clear.
Reading: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding essential information on advertisements, when they are clear and simple.

Speaking: Able to communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring an exchange of information simple and directions on familiar topics and activities. Able to handle very short social exchanges. Able to use a series of phrases and sentences to describe in simple terms his/her family and other people, his/her living conditions, educational background and present or most recent job.
Writing: Able to describe the daily activities or referring to the past. Also formulate projects and fix in writing of the action taken.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Erste Kontakte. Modul 3 / Lektion 7 - SPORT UND FITNESS. Ask for advice, make suggestions. Talk about sports and fitness. Making a diet plan. Grammar: subjunctive II, temporal preposition between temporal adverbs.

Hafta 2: Modul 3 / Lektion 8 GESUNDHEIT UND KRANKHEIT. Expressing compassion, concern and hope. Talking about the health condition. Asking for advice on the Internet. Grammar: conjunctions because, therefore.

Hafta 3: Modul 3 / Lektion 9 - ARBEITSLEBEN. Express importance. Understand the production steps in a factory, talk about working conditions. Express advantages and disadvantages of a job. Grammar: Adjective declension after zero article. Modul 4 / Lektion 10 - Im Restaurant. Order, complain, pay at the restaurant. Express liking and disliking. Grammar: conjunction that.
Hafta 4: Modul 4 / Lektion 11 - FIRMENPORTRÄT. Rate something, congratulate, thank. Celebrate a company anniversary. Give a speech about the company's performance. Grammar: Reflexive verbs.

Hafta 5: Modul 5 -Lektion 12 - ERNÄHRUNG. Comparing something; expressing surprise, talking about the eating habits of German men and women, understanding a statistic. Grammar: conjunction if. Lektion 13- Sprachen lernen. Reporting language learning experiences, recognizing learner types. Grammar: conjunction as.
Hafta 6: Lektion 14- POST UND KOMMUNIKATION. Expressing joy, wrapping and sending a gift, understanding vocabulary at the post office. Grammar: Present passive.

Hafta 7: Modul 5 / Lektion 15- MEDIEN. Talk about TV habits, talk about favorite show, introduce a movie/series. Grammar: verbs with dative and accusative / position of objects. Modul 6 / Lektion 16- IM HOTEL. Booking a room; describing a path in a building. Grammar: indirect questions: whether, how long, local prepositions: opposite, past, through.

Hafta 8: Modul 6 / Lektion 17- REISEN UND VERKEHR. Talking about travel habits, retelling a trip chronologically, commenting on something. Grammar: local prepositions: by the sea, to the sea. Modul 6 / Lektion 18- WETTER UND KLIMA. Talking about the weather, describing the seasons and their characteristics. Grammar: verbs with prepositions: to be interested in questions and prepositional adverbs.
Hafta 9: Modul 7 / Lektion 19- KULTURELLE VERANSTALTUNGEN. To persuade someone, to inspire; to respond hesitantly to suggestions. Grammar: local prepositions: From where?- from/ from the.
Hafta 10: Modul 7 / Lektion 20- BÜCHER UND PRESSE. Expressing interest / disinterest. Talking about reading behavior. Grammar: past tense modal verbs: was allowed, could.
Hafta 11: Modul 7 / Lektion 21- STAAT UND VERWALTUNG. To ask for a report / description; to report / describe something. Grammar: which- demonstrative pronoun : this-, the, verb let.
Hafta 12: Modul 8 / Lektion 22 MOBILITÄT UND VERKEHR. Explaining something, talking about the use of transportation. Grammar: Conjunctions until, since ( the).

Hafta 13: Modul 8 / Lektion 23- AUSBILDUNG UND BERUF. Express satisfaction/dissatisfaction, report on the German school system. Grammar: relative pronouns and relative clause in the nominative and accusative case.
Hafta 14: Modul 8 / Lektion 24- ARBEITEN IM AUSLAND. Expressing enthusiasm/ disappointment. Grammar: past tense: came, said,...
resources: Menschen A2 (Kursbuch), Charlotte Habersack/ Angela Pude, 2017, Hueber Verlag
Menschen A2 (Arbeitsbuch), Anna Breitsameter, Sabine Glas -Peters, Angela Pude Hueber Verlag, 2017, Hueber Verlag

courses.name: Intermediate German IV
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 136 (Intermediate German III) and is intended for students with third stage intermediate German knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing and listening comprehension skills. The course is geared to the second stage of the B2 level of CEFR.
courses.description: This course continues the intermediate German sequence and focuses on further development of grammatical structures and language skills at the B2 level. Students practice speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension through the use of acquired patterns and course activities.

courses.name: Intermediate German I
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 133 (German III) and is intended for students with basic German knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course is geared to the first stage of the B1 level of CEFR. It covers topics such as friendship, work and profession, housing, customer service, future plans, invitations, advice, career choices, health, disappointment, happiness, and celebrations in the workplace. Students practice grammar and communication through listening, speaking, reading, writing, presentations, and exercises.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding of the main points in relatively long discussions, if people involved do not talk too fast and use standard language.

Reading: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding of advertising letters, formation leaflets, texts of varying lengths, and personal letters giving a relatively detailed account of events and experiences.
Speaking: Able to give someone advice on simple matters, and able to express his/her opinion on subjects with which he/she is familiar.

Writing: Able to describe everyday things or events using complete sentences that are connected to one another. Able to write simple, relatively short texts he/she has experienced, without any help. Able to write a report on an important event or a personal experience in a school newspaper.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Modul 1, Lektion 1, Freundschaft: Ihr seid einfach die Besten!; Hören/Sprechen: Personen beschreiben / Charaktereigenschaften; Adjektive als Nomen; n-Deklination; Lesemagazin: Die Freundefinder

Hafta 2: Lek. 2, Beruf und Arbeit: Er erledigte seine Aufgaben zuverlässig; Sprechen/Schreiben: etwas Vergangenes bewerten; Lesen: Praktikumsbericht, E-Mail, Bestätigung; Schreiben: Bericht; Gr. Präteritum

Hafta 3: Lek. 3, Wohnen: Mein Beruf ist meine Leidenschaft; Sprechen: über eine Statistik sprechen; Lesen: Magazintext, Statistik; Gram. Relativsätze im Dativ und mit Präpositionen; Filmstationen: Clip 1; Projekt Landeskunde: Flexibles Wohnen auf Zeit

Hafta 4: Modul 2, Lektion 4, Kundenservise: Obwohl ich Ihnen das erklärt habe,...; Hören/Sprechen: reklamieren; Gesprächsstrategien am Telefon; Lesen/Schreiben: Reklamation; Gram. Konjunktionen „obwohl, trotzdem“; Lesemagazin: Kommedia lädt ein
Hafta 5: Lek. 5, Zukunft: Bald wird in fast jedem Haushalt ein PC stehen; Hören/Sprechen: Vermutungen über Zukünftiges äußern; Lesen: Umfrage: Lesermeinungen; Gram. Futur I

Hafta 6: Lek. 6, Einladungen: Fühlen Sie sich wie zu Hause; Hören/Sprechen: halboffizielle Einladungen; Lesen: Ratgeber; Gram. Konjunktion „falls“; Film-Stationen: Clip 2; Projekt Landeskunde: Presselandschaft in Deutschland

Hafta 7: Modul 3, Lek. 7, Beratung: Kann ich Ihnen helfen?; Hören/Sprechen: Kundengespräche; Gram. Infinitiv mit „zu“; Lek. 8, Berufsfindung: Während andere lange nachdenken...; professions and psychological strengths/weaknesses

Hafta 8: Sprechen: Stellung nehmen; Lesen: Test; Gram. Konjunktionen „da, während, bevor“; Lek. 9, Gesundheit: Sport trägt zu einem größeren Wohlbefinden bei; Hören/Sprechen: eine Präsentation halten; Lesen: Programm zur Gesundheitsförderung; Gram. Adjektivdeklination mit Komparativ und Superlativ; Modul-Plus: Lesemagazin; Film-Stationen: Clip 3; Projekt Landeskunde: Arbeitgeberattraktivität

Hafta 9: Modul 4, Lek. 10, Verpasste Gelegenheiten: Hätte ich das bloß anders gemacht!; Hören/Sprechen: Enttäuschungen ausdrücken; auf Enttäuschung reagieren; Schreiben: Kommentar; Gram. Konjunktiv II Vergangenheit

Hafta 10: Lek. 11, Glücksmomente: Nachdem wir jahrelang Pech gehabt hatten, ...; Sprechen: etwas emotional kommentieren; Lesen/Schreiben: Blog-Beitrag; Gram. Plusquamperfekt mit „haben“ und „sein“; Konjunktion „nachdem“
Hafta 11: Lek. 12, Feiern im Betrieb: Ausflug des Jahres; Lesen/Schreiben: Briefe und E-Mails: Einladungen, Absagen, Zusagen; Gram. Genitiv; Adjektivdeklination im Genitiv; Präposition „trotz“
Hafta 12: Modul-Plus, Lesemagazin: Lebensfreude, Mut und Kraft schenken; Film-Stationen: Clip 4; Projekt Landeskunde: Glücksbringer
Hafta 13: Modultests / Wiederholung
Hafta 14: Modultests / Wiederholung
resources: Menschen B1.1 (Kursbuch), Julia Braun-Podeschwa, Charlotte Habersack, 2014, Hueber Verlag

courses.name: Communication Skills in German I
courses.purpose: This course aims to improve students' communication skills in German by building on previously acquired grammar and language knowledge. It supports the development of reading, writing, and speaking abilities at the first stage of the C1 level.
courses.description: This course is a continuation of LNG 137 (Intermediate German IV) and builds on students' knowledge and usage of previously acquired grammar by improving their reading, writing, and speaking skills. This course is geared to the first stage of the C1 level of CEFR.

courses.name: Intermediate German II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 134 (Intermediate German I) and is intended for students with first stage intermediate German knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing and listening comprehension skills.

courses.name: Italian I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Italian and helps students build a foundation for using the language in everyday situations. It also aims to develop the skills needed to understand and produce simple spoken and written Italian.
courses.description: The course covers basic Italian grammar, vocabulary, pronunciation, and communicative functions at CEFR A1.1 and part of A1.2. Topics include greetings, introductions, nationality, family and personal information, daily routines, telling time, directions, food and drink, shopping, invitations, weekend activities, and weather-related expressions.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate understanding of simple and very common expressions, straightforward tasks and instructions, simple questions, and short conversations on familiar subjects.
Reading: Able to read and understand familiar names, words, very simple sentences, short messages, and very short texts.
Speaking: Able to greet people, introduce oneself briefly, order food and drink, and ask and answer simple questions on familiar topics.
Writing: Able to write very simple sentences, fill in forms with personal details, write simple messages and questions, and provide basic personal information.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Unit I A-B; asking for names and identities; singular nouns; indefinite articles; classroom objects; greetings; present tense of essere.
Hafta 2: Unit I E-F-G; nationality; interrogative/affirmative/negative forms; possession and relationship expressions; Italian alphabet.
Hafta 3: Unit II A-C; definite articles; plural nouns; workplaces; c’è / ci sono; addresses, email, telephone numbers; possessive adjectives.
Hafta 4: Unit II D; asking name, age, city, profession, spoken languages; first conjugation verbs (-ARE); mutual acquaintance presentation.
Hafta 5: Unit III A-B; daily routine; parts of the day; everyday actions; three conjugations (-ARE, -ERE, -IRE); irregular verbs fare and andare; telling time.
Hafta 6: Unit III C-D; reflexive verbs; days of the week, months, seasons; presentation on self-introduction and daily routine.
Hafta 7: Unit IV A-B-C; city places and services; means of transport; prepositions of place; asking/giving directions; modal verbs; compound prepositions; timetable information.
Hafta 8: Unit V A-C; food and drink; ordering at a bar; adjectives of gender and number; menu; expressing likes; shopping vocabulary; restaurant dialogue.
Hafta 9: Unit VI A-B; invitations and responses; irregular verbs uscire, venire, stare; leisure activities; describing a weekend; adverbs of frequency.
Hafta 10: Unit VI C; weather conditions; describing how weekends are spent; free-time presentation.
resources: Volentieri! A1: Corso di lingua e cultura italiana, Monica Piantoni, Rosella Bozzone Costa, Luisa Fumagalli, 2021-2025, LOESCHER EDITORE

courses.name: Italian III
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 142 (Italian II) and is intended for students with basic Italian knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to a part of A2.1 and to A2.2 of the A2 level of CEFR.

courses.name: Italian II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 141 (Italian I) and is intended for students with basic Italian knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to a part of A1.2 and to a part of A2.1 of the A2 level of CEFR. It covers communication, grammar, and vocabulary topics including describing homes, clothing, past actions, family, personality, introducing oneself and others, occupations, free time activities, and Italian culture-related language use.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding the main points in simple, clear and short announcements and messages about, how to get some where, when someone explains slowly and clearly. Able to extract important information on short radio broadcasts. Able to understand phrases and the highest frequency vocabulary related to areas of most immediate personal relevance (e.g. very basic personal and family information, shopping, local area, employment)

Reading: Able to read and describe his understanding of texts written in simple language. Able to read very short, simple texts, find specific, predictable information in simple everyday material such as advertisements, prospectuses, menus and timetables and understand short simple personal letters.

Speaking: Able to answer simple questions using individual words, expressions or short sentences and to interact in a simple way. Able to express certain feelings that everyone is familiar with, using simple words. Able to have a short telephone conversation, with people arrange to meet them, e.g.: able to make simple purchases, explain what he/she is looking for and ask how much an article costs. Able to ask straightforward questions on a subject or react to something that other people have said about it.

Writing: Able to write about people and things in his/her every day life (school, family, hobbies, daily routine, projects...). Able to write a very simple card (e.g.to thank someone for a present ) Able to use short sentences to say what he/she prefers or what he/she receives. Able to write a short greetings card. Able to reply to an invitation and cancel or change an appointment, using simple words (e.g SMS messages).
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: UNITÀ 7 - COMMUNICATION: Talk about where you live and describe how much time is needed to do something, understanding short rental ads GRAMMAR: The present indicative of –care e –gare, use of two pronominal verbs Volerci and Metterci VOCABULARY: house types, housing ads

Hafta 2: UNITÀ 7 - COMMUNICATION: talk about homes and apartments, describe an apartment, talk about furniture, describe the location of an object in a space GRAMMAR: Prepositions to describe the location of objects in a space, articulated prepositions, direct object pronouns lo-la-li-le VOCABULARY: rooms and spaces in a house, part of building, home features and furniture

Hafta 3: UNITÀ 8 - COMMUNICATION: Describe an article of clothing, ask for and express opinions VOCABULARY: colors, articles of clothing, shoes, accessories, fabrics and fabric patterns, styles, sizes GRAMMAR: singular and plural forms of adjectives (colors) ending with –o and -e, colors with invariable ending, noun-adjective agreement, stare bene / male (a qualcuno) + indirect pronouns

Hafta 4: UNITÀ 8 - COMMUNICATION: talk about clothing styles, make purchases in clothing and shoe stores GRAMMAR: direct and indirect pronouns: forms and position, piacere + indirect pronouns VOCABULARY: some adjectives related to clothing, shoes and accessories

Hafta 5: UNITÀ 9 - COMMUNICATION: talking about past actions and understanding descriptions of past events GRAMMAR: past tense: passato prossimo, past participles of regular verbs, forms and agreement of the past participle, verbs taking avere as an auxiliary VOCABULARY: various types of the accommodation
Hafta 6: GRAMMAR: irregular past participles, verbs taking essere as an auxiliary, adverbs of time, the adverb ci
Hafta 7: COMMUNICATION: describe a vocation, talk about last weekend VOCABULARY: time indicators: ieri, l’altro ieri, ieri sera, ieri pomeriggio, scorso, fa, etc, holiday activities

Hafta 8: UNITÀ 10 - COMMUNICATION: describing a family tree, talking and writing about one’s family GRAMMAR: possessive adjectives (all singular and plural forms forms), possessive adjectives + nouns referring to family relationships VOCABULARY: names of family members, family relationships

Hafta 9: COMMUNICATION: Descriptions, describing one’s personality, talk about some most important stage of life GRAMMAR: irregular past participles, modal verb sapere to express ability VOCABULARY: some descriptive adjectives, adjectives to describe physical appearance, personality adjectives

Hafta 10: ( SPAZIO ITALIA A2 ) UNITÀ 1 - COMMUNICATION: introducing yourself and others, asking about someone’s place of origin GRAMMAR: venire + da + country of origin or place of provenance, VOCABULARY: adjectives of nationalities, country names

Hafta 11: COMMUNICATION: introducing yourself and others, asking for someone’s occupation, talking about free time activities, describing one’s work habits and working hours GRAMMAR: singular and plural nouns, present indicative: regular verbs, irregular verbs, modal verbs VOCABULARY: professions, workplaces

Hafta 12: GRAMMAR: ordinal numbers, direct pronouns, unstressed indirect pronouns (atoni) and stressed indirect pronouns (tonici), asking information about Italian phrases, expressions and words VOCABULARY: some Italian cities, some great Italian artists and their most famous works

Hafta 13: past tense: passato prossimo, past participles of regular verbs, forms and agreement of the past participle, verbs taking avere or essere as an auxiliary, verb piacere (present tense), verb piacere (past tense) COMMUNICATION: talk about famous people and their Works, talk about famous landmarks, monuments and places in Italy, reading the flyer of an art exhibit, reading a short biography
Hafta 14: UNITÀ 4 - GRAMMAR: past tense: passato prossimo form of reflexive verbs
resources: Volentieri! A1-A2: Corso di lingua e cultura italiana, Monica Piantoni, Rosella Bozzone Costa, Luisa Fumagalli, 2021-2025, Loescher

courses.name: Intermediate Russian II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 164 (Intermediate Russian I) and is intended for students with first stage intermediate Russian knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to the second stage of the B1 level of CEFR.

courses.name: Russian II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 161 (Russian I) and is intended for students with basic Russian knowledge. It aims to help students use acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course continues the study of Russian at the A1.2 to A2.1 level of CEFR. It develops grammatical patterns, vocabulary, and communicative competence through speaking, reading, writing, listening, drills, exercises, presentations, and assignments.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to understand the main points in simple, clear, and short announcements and messages, and extract important information from short radio broadcasts.
Reading: Able to read simple texts and find specific, predictable information in everyday materials such as advertisements, menus, timetables, and short personal letters.
Speaking: Able to answer simple questions, interact in a simple way, and handle short everyday conversations.
Writing: Able to write about everyday life, compose very simple cards and short messages, and respond to invitations or changes of appointment using simple words.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Repetition of the ninth lesson. Speech samples: I read in Russian. I work in a bank. How much does milk cost? Where do you study? What are you learning? Phonetic material: Practicing reading verbs. Grammatical material: The present tense of verbs of the first and second conjugations. Accusative case.
Hafta 2: Continuation of the accusative case. A compound sentence with the union THEREFORE. Speech patterns: I'm busy, sick. Construction: WHERE YOU CAN+INFO.
Hafta 3: Lesson ten. Speech patterns: I was busy. He was ill. Grammatical material: The past tense of the verb and the use of TO BE in the past tense. Verbs of movement GO in the present tense.

Hafta 4: Lesson ten. Speech patterns: What did you do? Where have you been? Yesterday, the day before yesterday, earlier. Features of the use of the verbs TO SEE and TO LOOK. Verbs of movement WALK and RIDE in the past tense.

Hafta 5: Lesson ten. Speech patterns: What did you drive? Were you happy? Phonetic material: The rhythm of words and phrases. Grammatical material: Verbs of movement WALK and RIDE in the past tense. Accusative case to express the direction of movement.

Hafta 6: Lesson eleven. Speech patterns: Was or was not. I'm walking. My sister invited... How often do you call home? Anna has already written a letter and put it in an envelope. Grammatical material: Verb types (fact, repeatability, process, result).

Hafta 7: Lesson eleven. Speech patterns: Why don't you repeat new words? Have you bought a hat yet? How often? What time is it now? Grammatical material: The use of NSV and SV in the past tense and the use of imperfect and perfect types in the past tense.

Hafta 8: Lesson eleven. Phonetic material: The rhythm of polysyllabic words and phrases. Grammatical material: Expression of time. Speech patterns: I want to go to the clinic to see a doctor. I can... Verb TO WANT + INF.

Hafta 9: Lesson eleven. Speech patterns: Who are you going to/going to? I CAN SPEAK RUSSIAN. Grammatical material: The dative case in the meaning of the direction of movement to any person and Verb CAN + INFINITIVE VERB.

Hafta 10: Lesson eleven and lesson twelve. Speech patterns: What are the plans? Let's meet. I wanted to take it, I took it. I can buy it. Let's meet at 6 o'clock. Grammatical material: Expression of time and repetition of the theme imperfect and perfect types.

Hafta 11: Lesson twelve. Speech patterns: I have / don't have. Victor doesn't have a sister. There is/is not in the room. Where are you from? Where did Jean come from? Where did Anna go? She went to the theater. Grammatical material: Genitive case of nouns in several meanings.

Hafta 12: Lesson twelve and lesson thirteen. Speech patterns: What happened? Unfortunately. Hour - per hour. We want to understand. I can't read it, there's no magazine. Where did you come from? Grammatical material: Verbs of movement and reading phrases.

Hafta 13: Lesson thirteen. Speech patterns: What will you do tomorrow? Tomorrow Ivan will read the article. Who will you give flowers to? How old is Ivan? Grammatical material: Future simple and complex tense; dative case.

Hafta 14: Lesson fourteen. Speech patterns: When I rest, I read. She came and cooked dinner. In the evening, Anna and Ivan were at the theater. Grammatical material: The use of imperfect and perfect verbs to express simultaneity and sequence of actions; creative case in the meaning of joint actions.
resources: Daroga v Rassiyu 1, Nakhabina, Safronova, Tolstkhıh, 2023, Zlatoust
Ya pişu po-russki, Belyayeva, Nahabina, 2023, Moskow
Russian In Exercises, Khavronina & Shirochenskaya, 2024, Russkiy Yazık

courses.name: Russian III
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 162 (Russian II) and is intended for students with basic Russian knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course is geared to a part of A2.1 and to A2.2 of the A2 level of CEFR. It covers Russian grammar and language use through lessons on declension systems, prepositional, accusative, and genitive cases, pronouns and adjectives, verbs of movement, direct and indirect speech, phonetics, reading texts, dialogues, listening activities, writing tasks, and oral presentation practice.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding the main points of clear standard inputs on familiar topics about work, school, leisure, etc. Able to demonstrate his/her understanding of the most of radio or TV programs on current affairs or topics of interest personal or professional title if the delivery is relatively slow and clear.
Reading: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding essential information on advertisements, when they are clear and simple.

Speaking: Able to communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring an exchange of information simple and directions on familiar topics and activities. Able to handle very short social exchanges. Able to use a series of phrases and sentences to describe in simple terms his/her family and other people, his/her living conditions, educational background and present or most recent job.
Writing: Able to describe the daily activities or referring to the past. Also formulate projects and fix in writing of the action taken.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Lesson one: The concept of the declension system of nouns with pronouns and adjectives (in the masculine gender). Speech samples: Ivan dreams of an interesting journey. Victor is studying at Moscow University. Grammatical material: The concept of the declension system of nouns with pronouns and adjectives (in masculine and feminine genders). Prepositional case (6). Object. Speech samples: Tell me, in which hand is the candy? In the right or in the left? I live in this new house... . Grammatical material: Prepositional case (6). Place. Reading the text "Wunderkinds".

Hafta 2: Lesson one: Speech samples: Where can I get a visa? In which embassy...? What can you advise me? ... Grammatical material: Prepositional case (6). Place (continuation of the topic). Dialogues p.23. Speech samples: In what year were you born? This year/month... Grammatical material: Prepositional case (6). Time.

Hafta 3: Lesson one: Speech patterns: Here is the Bolshoi Theater, where famous artists work ... . Phonetic material: The rhythm of polysyllabic words. Grammatical material: A complex sentence with the word "which" in the prepositional case. Speech samples: Meet my friend who studies with me.... Grammatical material: Prepositional case (repetition).

Hafta 4: Lesson two: Speech patterns: I remember my hometown. I will study at Moscow University.... . Phonetic exercises. Grammar material: repetition of the previous lesson. Listening to dialogues. Speech patterns: I often look at photos and remember my hometown, my family. Grammatical material: Accusative case . An object

Hafta 5: Lesson two: Speech patterns: I draw my friend, my girlfriend and my house .... Phonetic material: The rhythm of polysyllabic words and phrases. Grammatical material: YOUR pronoun in the accusative case. Reading the text "What I love". Lesson Two: Speech patterns: Which athlete? Which singer? ... Phonetic material: Rhythmics of polysyllabic words and phrases. Grammatical material: Accusative case . Looks like who? / for what? Dialogues.

Hafta 6: Lesson two: Speech patterns: After 10 years, I didn't recognize my city. Before the lesson/ flight/meeting.... Grammatical material: Accusative case. Time. Reading the text "Yuri Gagarin's Grandson". Lesson two: Speech samples: In a week Vladimir will go to the theater ... . Grammatical material: Verbs of movement: go / come / leave .

Hafta 7: Lesson two: Speech patterns: Now friends are going out of town... Grammatical material: Verbs of movement: go/walk/ride /ride Direct and indirect speech. Lesson two: Speech patterns: Oleg asked Irina.... Ira replied to Oleg that ... . Grammatical material: A complex sentence with the word "which" in the accusative case.
Hafta 8: Lesson Two: Speech patterns: There is a book in this store that I want to buy.... Grammatical material: Accusative case. The text "Breakfast".Preparation for written control work.

Hafta 9: Lesson three: Speech patterns: I met my older brother. He looks like his father. My son has blond hair... . Phonetic exercises. Listening to dialogues. Lesson three: Speech patterns: From whom? Which one (in the masculine gender)? Which one (in the feminine gender)?... Grammatical material: The genitive case of nouns. with pronouns and adj. Genitive case. Possession.

Hafta 10: Lesson three: Speech patterns: Which young man has blond hair or which one.... Grammatical material: Genitive case. Speech samples: There will be no youth festival this year... Grammatical material: Genitive case. The lack of.

Hafta 11: Lesson Three: Speech patterns: I RECEIVED A LETTER FROM MY FRIEND... Phonetic material: Practicing reading verbs. Grammatical material: The pronoun in the genitive case. The genetive case. Characteristics. Affiliation. Speech samples: Born on December tenth, one thousand nine hundred and eighty-five... Grammatical material: Genitive case. Time.

Hafta 12: Lesson Three: Speech patterns: How many people are in your family? ... Grammatical material: Genitive case. How much? Grammatical material: Continuation of the topic "genitive case". Reading letters written to the newspaper "My family".

Hafta 13: Lesson three: Speech patterns: I came to Novgorod to see the city. Grammatical material: A complex sentence with the word "which" in the genitive case. Grammatical material: Genitive case (continued). Reading the text "Call me...".
Hafta 14: Watching the film "Irony of Fate" in Russian and discussion. Repetition of the material covered throughout the semester. Presentation.
resources: Дорога в Россию 2 (Daroga v Rassiyu 2)., Antonova. Nakhabina, Tolsthıh, 2023, Zlatoust
Ya pişu po-russki, Belyayeva, Nahabina, 2023, Moskow, Belyayeva, Nahabina, 2023, Moskow
Russian In Exercises, , Khavronina& Shirochenskaya,, 2024, Russkiy Yazık

courses.name: Russian I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Russian. It aims not only to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns but also to improve their reading, writing, speaking, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to A1.1 and to a part of A1.2 of the A1 level of CEFR. It covers basic Russian speech patterns, phonetics, grammar, vocabulary, and simple communicative tasks such as introducing oneself, asking and answering questions, talking about family, numbers, daily routines, places, and basic needs.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his / her understanding simple and very common expressions, when people speak slowly and clearly and to demonstrate his /her understanding straightforward tasks and instructions, simple questions, simple information about an object. Able to demonstrate his / her understanding short conversations on subjects he/she is familiar with (school, family, hobbies)

Reading: Able to read, to demonstrate his/her understanding familiar names, words and very simple sentences, for example on notices and posters or in catalogues. Able to read and illustrate his/ her understanding short, simple messages, very short texts.

Speaking: Able to greet people, intoduce him/her self briefly, to order some thing to drink and eat and to use simple phrases and sentences to describe where I live and people I know. Able to ask and answer simple questions in areas of immediate need or on very familiar topics.

Writing: Able to write very simple sentences, able to write who he/she is and where he/she lives, to fill in forms with personal details, for example entering his/her name, nationality and address on a hotel registration form. Able to write very simple messages and questions for other people (e.g text messages or "post-its",cards) and write some personal information concerning him/ herself (age, address, hobbies), describe objects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the class. Lesson One: (pages 3-13) Speech patterns: This is Ivan. He's here. Is this Anna? Is Anna at home? Phonetic material: Vowels (a, o, u, e, i). Consonants (l, m, n, p, b, f, v, t, d). The rhythm of the word, stress, reduction.

Hafta 2: Lesson two: Speech patterns: When is the lesson? What is your name? Phonetic material: Consonants (s, z, r). Rhythm of a word, syllabus, stress, reduction. Comparison of IK-1 IK-2 IK-3 Grammatical material: The concept of parts of speech. Animate and inanimate. Concepts Lesson Three: Speech Patterns: Hello. Goodbye. How are you? Can? etc. Phonetic material: Consonants (ж,ш, й). Rhythm of a word, syllabus, stress, reduction. Constructions IK-1 IK-2 IK-3. Comparisons of internationalism words.

Hafta 3: Lesson Three: Speech Patterns: This is my friend. This is also my friend. This is my bag and this is your bag. Phonetic material: Rhythm of a word, syllabus, stress, reduction. Constructions IK1 IK2 IK 3 Grammatical material: Possessive pronouns. Dialogue over the phone. Lesson Four: Speech Patterns: Say. Sorry. Is this a pharmacy? No that's not ... . Phonetic material: The concept of hard and soft consonants. Grammar material: Possessive pronouns. Dialogues over the phone. We work in class with a partner. We make small dialogues.

Hafta 4: Lesson four: Speech patterns: Give / those. Show. Phonetic material: Rhythm of polysyllabic words. Grammar material: Negative constructions. Using the imperatives offıcıal and unofficial forms. Lesson four: Speech patterns: Can I? It is forbidden. Truth? Call. Phonetic material: The rule for reading the combinations ЧТ and ЧН. Grammar material: Constructions with the question Where? We work in class with a partner. We make small dialogues.

Hafta 5: Lesson Five: Speech Patterns: This is my country. This is my hometown. Breakfast in the morning? ... Phonetic material: The concept of hard and soft consonants. Grammar material: Possessive pronouns. Dialogues over the phone. Lesson Five: Speech Patterns: What's Your Name? Do you know who he is? ... Phonetic material: Consonant Ts. IK-4. Grammatical material: Plural. Dialogues over the phone. We work in class with a partner. We make small dialogues.

Hafta 6: Lesson Five: Speech Patterns: Let's get to know each other. Who is he? Who is she? ... Phonetic material: Rhythm of polysyllabic words and phrases. Grammar material: Paradigm of the first conjugation of the verb (know / think). Dialogues over the phone. We work in class with a partner.

Hafta 7: Lesson Five: Speech Patterns: Let's Get Acquainted. Who is he? Who is she? ... Phonetic material: Rhythm of polysyllabic words and phrases. Grammar material: Paradigm of the first conjugation of the verb (know / think). The question "whose" for the masculine, the feminine, the neuter, the plural genders. The text "My Family". Dialogues over the phone. We work in class with a partner. We make small dialogues.

Hafta 8: Lesson seven: Speech patterns: 2 + 2 how much? My mobile phone number ... Phonetic material: Practice reading numbers 1-20, 30, 40, 50 .... Grammar material: Numbers 1-1000. Lesson Seven: Speech Patterns: What It Is This is an interesting magazine. What street is it? ... Phonetic material: Construction of IK-2 with a question word What? Grammar material: Adjective. We work in class with a partner. We make small dialogues. Let's talk about ourselves.

Hafta 9: Lesson seven. Speech patterns: I like. Let's go. See you tomorrow.... Phonetic material: The rules of reading (I) in unstressed positions. Grammatical material: Demonstrative pronouns. Constructions "I, you, him, her, us, you, they like it. Lesson seven: Speech patterns: I like ... Let's go. See you tomorrow. Do you think it's cold today? .... Grammatical material: Complex sentences with conjunctions: that / because ...

Hafta 10: Lesson Seven: Speech patterns: I like (singular)/like (plural)... Let's go. See you tomorrow. Do you think it's cold today? .... Grammatical material: Complex sentences with conjunctions: what / because ... Lesson Eight: Speech patterns: I like ... Phonetic material: Working out the reading of numerals 1-100. Grammatical material: The present tense of verbs of the first conjugation.

Hafta 11: Lesson eight: Speech patterns: Who is working/playing/resting...? Phonetic material: The design of the Ik-5. Grammatical material: The present tense of verbs of the first conjugation. The use of adverbs. Lesson Eight: Speech patterns: Do you read in Russian? Grammatical material: The present tense of the verbs of the first and second conjugations (to speak).

Hafta 12: Lesson eight: Speech patterns: Where do you live / work ...? Phonetic material: Reading phrases with prepositions In and ON. Grammatical material: Prepositional case of nouns. Prepositional case Lesson Eight: (pages 134--142). Speech samples: Well, how? As usual. See you! Phonetic material: Reading phrases with prepositions In and ON. Grammatical material: The use of verbs to be, to work, to walk, to live, etc. Group work. Staging. We do dialogues in the market, on the street, at the post office, at the stadium, etc.

Hafta 13: Lesson nine: Speech patterns: I read in Russian. I work in a bank. How much does milk cost? ... Phonetic material: Practicing reading verbs. Grammatical material: The present tense of verbs of the first and second conjugations (talking, smoking, calling ...). Lesson nine: Speech patterns: Where do you study? What are you learning? Grammatical material: Verbs: Learn and teach. Accusative case: Who? What?

Hafta 14: Lesson nine: Speech patterns: Every day I listen to the news in Russian.... Grammatical material: Continuation of the topic "accusative case". Reading the text: Ivan journalist. Lesson nine: Speech samples: What good weather! With pleasure... Grammatical material: Continuation of the topic "accusative case". Lesson Nine: Speech patterns: What day is today? What do I like to do in my free time? Phonetic material: Practicing reading the days of the week. Grammatical material: A compound sentence with the conjunction THEREFORE.. Lesson nine: Speech patterns: I'm busy, sick... Grammatical material: Construction: WHERE YOU CAN + INFO.
resources: Required - Textbook: Дорога в Россию 1 (The road to Russia 1), Антонова,Нахабина, Сафронова, Толстых (Antonova, Nakhabina, Safronova, Tolstykh), 2023, Златоуст (zlatoust)
Required - Textbook: Daroga v Rassiyu 1, Antonova, Nakhabina, Safronova, Tolstykh, 2023, Zlatoust
Recommended - Textbook: RUSSİAN İN EXECİSES, Khavronına-Shirochenskaya, 2020, Russky Yazyk Publishers Moskow

courses.name: Arabic I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Arabic. It aims not only to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns but also to improve their reading, writing, speaking, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Arabic. It is geared to A1.1 and to a part of A1.2 of the A1 level of CEFR. The course covers Arabic letters, reading in Arabic, equational sentences, gender in pronouns and nouns, interrogatives, vocative, word order, noun cases, definite article, demonstrative phrases, agreement of predicate adjective, numerals, verbs in the perfect tense, idafa constructions, negation, plural forms, independent pronouns, pronoun suffixes, and related drills and conversations.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate understanding of simple and very common expressions when people speak slowly and clearly, and to understand straightforward tasks and instructions, simple questions, simple information about an object, and short conversations on familiar subjects.
Reading: Able to read and demonstrate understanding of familiar names, words, and very simple sentences, as well as short, simple messages and very short texts.

Speaking: Able to greet people, introduce oneself briefly, order something to drink and eat, use simple phrases and sentences to describe where one lives and people one knows, and ask and answer simple questions on very familiar topics.
Writing: Able to write very simple sentences, fill in forms with personal details, write very simple messages and questions, and provide some personal information and descriptions of objects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Reading and writing Arabic letters.
Hafta 2: Reading in Arabic.
Hafta 3: Equational sentences. Gender in pronouns, interrogatives, vocative, word order.
Hafta 4: Lesson 1: Basic text, vocabulary, interrogative in phrases, nunation in nouns, case: general, nominative case, gender in nouns. Additional text for the first lesson.

Hafta 5: Lesson 2: Basic text, vocabulary, vowels, definite article grammar and drills: the genitive case, object of proposition, helping vowels. Definite article, demonstrative phrases, agreement of predicate adjective, numerals. Additional text for the lesson 2.

Hafta 6: Drills and conversation of Lesson 1 and 2. Lesson 3: Basic text, vocabulary, grammar and drills, negative of equational sentences, interrogatives, word order, numerals. Additional text for lesson 3.

Hafta 7: Lesson 4: Basic text, vocabulary, grammar and drills, non-adjective phrases, pronoun of separation, nisba: the relative adjective, pronoun suffix with nouns. Additional text for the lesson 4.

Hafta 8: Lesson 4: Basic text, vocabulary, grammar and drills, non-adjective phrases, pronoun of separation, nisba: the relative adjective, pronoun suffix with nouns. Additional text for the lesson 4.
Hafta 9: Lesson 5: Basic text, vocabulary, grammar and drills, the definite article, verbs: perfect tense, verbal idafa construction, comprehension passages, general drills.
Hafta 10: Drills and conversation of Lessons 3-4-5. Additional text for the lesson 5.
Hafta 11: Lesson 6: Basic text, vocabulary, grammar and drills, perfect sentence, negation of words or phrases, demonstratives, continuation of numerals.
Hafta 12: General drills and conversations for the lessons before. Additional text for the lesson 6.
Hafta 13: Lesson 7: Basic text, vocabulary, grammar and drills, idafa: noun negative, plural form, helping vowels, comprehension passages. Additional text for the lesson 7.
Hafta 14: Lesson 8: Basic text, vocabulary, grammar and drills, idafa: noun phrases as second term, plural subject, independent pronouns, pronoun suffixes, negative plural forms, helping vowels.
resources: Elementary Modern Standard Arabic. Part I, Peter F. Abboud, Najim A. Bezirgan, Mounah A. Khouri and others, 1995, Cambridge University Press
Instructor's Nottice, Ahmet Simin BEYATLI

courses.name: Chinese I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Chinese. It aims not only to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns but also to improve their reading, writing, speaking, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Chinese. It is geared to A1.1 and to part of A1.2 of the A1 level of CEFR. The course covers Chinese pronunciation, Pinyin, tones, basic strokes and characters, numbers, family, dates and time, daily routines, money and shopping, actions in progress and future plans, movement, food, comparison, and ability.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate understanding of simple and very common expressions when people speak slowly and clearly, and to understand straightforward tasks and instructions, simple questions, and simple information about an object. Able to understand short conversations on familiar subjects such as school, family, and hobbies.

Reading: Able to read and understand familiar names, words, and very simple sentences, for example on notices and posters or in catalogues. Able to understand short, simple messages and very short texts.

Speaking: Able to greet people, introduce oneself briefly, order something to drink and eat, and use simple phrases and sentences to describe where one lives and people one knows. Able to ask and answer simple questions in areas of immediate need or on very familiar topics.

Writing: Able to write very simple sentences, write who one is and where one lives, fill in forms with personal details, and write very simple messages and questions for other people. Able to write some personal information and describe objects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Lesson 1-2: Introduction to China and Chinese language; Learning the four tones of Chinese (Mandarin); Learning Chinese Phonetic Alphabet (Pinyin)
Hafta 2: Lesson 3-4: Unit 1: Practicing Pinyin and tones
Hafta 3: Lesson 5-6: Unit 1: Learning basic strokes of Chinese characters; introducing themselves and others
Hafta 4: Lesson 7-8: Unit 1: Asking questions and talking about one’s name, identity, and nationality.
Hafta 5: Lesson 9-10: Unit 2: Describing numbers 0-99; asking and talking about age

Hafta 6: Lesson 11-12: Unit 2: Talking about family members and describing the number of them; describing the number of people and objects; asking the quantity of people and objects to describe one’s job or major; learning the Chinese zodiac.
Hafta 7: Lesson 13-14: Unit 3: Asking and describing the date and someone’s birthday; asking and describing the time
Hafta 8: Lesson 15-16: Unit 3: Describing something as either “a” or “b”; talking about daily routine; learning traditional Chinese festivals
Hafta 9: Lesson 17-18: Unit 4: Describing amounts of money; asking the price of an item; knowing the measure words
Hafta 10: Lesson 19-20: Unit 4: Describing degree; expressing a desire; knowing all necessary words about shopping
Hafta 11: Lesson 21-22: Unit 5: Describing an action in progress; describing future plans; describing a change or development
Hafta 12: Lesson 23-24: Unit 5: Describing movement to a certain place; describing the number of times an action or activity has occurred
Hafta 13: Lesson 25-26: Unit 6: Ordering food in a restaurant; asking someone about their favorite food or drink and if there are any foods they avoid
Hafta 14: Lesson 27-28: Unit 6: Describing a food’s flavor, offering a suggestion; comparing the qualities of “a” and “b”; describing something as the best in its category; describing ability
resources: Mastering Chinese 1 Listening and Speaking （会通汉语1听说）, Lu Fubo, 2015, People's Education Press
Mastering Chinese 1 Reading and Writing （会通汉语1读写）, Lu Fubo, 2015, People's Education Press

Reading: Able to read, to demonstrate his/her understanding familiar names, words and very simple sentences, for example on notices and posters or in catalogues. Able to read and illustrate his/ her understanding short, simple messages, very short texts.

Hafta 2: ‘Hangeul’characters Vowels, Consonants and Syllable 1
Hafta 3: ‘Hangeul’characters Vowels, Consonants and Syllable 2

Hafta 6: Lesson 2: Introducing Yourself 2: Sino-Korean numbers Asking and answering phone numbers and YES/NO questions

Hafta 10: Lesson 6: Buying things 1 : Talking about places and food

Hafta 13: Lesson 9: Time and Weather 2 : Weather and seasons

courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Japanese. It aims not only to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns but also to improve their reading, writing, speaking, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: This course introduces the basic knowledge of Japanese. It is geared to A1.1 and part of A1.2 of the A1 level of CEFR.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his / her understanding simple and very common expressions, when people speak slowly and clearly and to demonstrate his /her understanding straightforward tasks and instructions, simple questions, simple information about an object. Able to demonstrate his /her understanding short conversations on subjects he/she is familiar with (school, family, hobbies)

Reading: Able to read, to demonstrate his/her understanding familiar names, words and very simple sentences, for example on notices and posters or in catalogues. Able to read and illustrate his/ her understanding short, simple messages, very short texts.

Speaking: Able to greet people, intoduce him/her self briefly, to order some thing to drink and eat and to use simple phrases and sentences to describe where I live and people I know. Able to ask and answer simple questions in areas of immediate need or on very familiar topics.

Writing: Able to write very simple sentences, able to write who he/she is and where he/she lives, to fill in forms with personal details, for example entering his/her name, nationality and address on a hotel registration form. Able to write very simple messages and questions for other people (e.g text messages or "post-its",cards) and write some personal information concerning him/ herself (age, address, hobbies), describe objects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: [Lesson 1&2 Nihongo] Exchange Greeting / Recognize Hiragana & Katakana / Write your name/country in Japanese
Hafta 2: [Lesson 3 Myself] Give a simple self-introduction (countries, languages, occupations) / Recognize the parts of business card
Hafta 3: [Lesson 4 Myself] Talk briefly about your family (people, number) / Tell someone about your family, using a family photo
Hafta 4: [Lesson 5 Food] Talk about your favorite foods / Offer someone a drink / Talk about your breakfast / Start to recognize some basic kanji
Hafta 5: [Lesson 6 Food] Say what your favorite dish is / Talk with a friend where to go for lunch / Read a menu / Order food and drinks at a hamburger shop
Hafta 6: [Lesson 7 Home] Say what kind of home you live / Say what you have in your home / Write an E-mail inviting someone to your home
Hafta 7: [Lesson 8 Home] Ask/Say where to put things in the room / Visit/Welcome a friend / Show someone around your home / Recognize the name and address on signs
Hafta 8: [Lesson 9 Daily Life] Say the time you do something, Talk about your daily routine
Hafta 9: [Lesson 10 Daily Life] Talk about your schedule for this week / Talk about when to have a party / Write a birthday card

Hafta 10: [Lesson 11-12 Days off] Talk about your hobbies / Talk about what you do on your days off / Say whether or not you will attend an event / Talk about whether or not you can go out with your friend
Hafta 11: [Lesson 13 Towns] Recognize station and taxi signs / Show a taxi driver a note and say your direction / Say how to get a particular destination
Hafta 12: [Lesson 14 Towns] Say how to get to a destination, using a map / Say where you are now over the phone / Recognize opening/closing time on shop noticeboards
Hafta 13: [Lesson 15 Shopping] Talk about what you want to buy / Talk about where to shop for something you want
Hafta 14: [Lesson 16 Shopping] Make a brief comment on things in a shop / Read prices / Do some shopping

courses.name: Japanese II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 241 (Japanese I) and is intended for students with basic Japanese knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to a part of A1.2 and to a part of A2.1 of the A2 level of CEFR.
learning_outcomes: Listening: Able to demonstrate his/her understanding the main points in simple, clear and short announcements and messages about how to get somewhere, when someone explains slowly and clearly. Able to extract important information on short radio broadcasts. Able to understand phrases and the highest frequency vocabulary related to areas of most immediate personal relevance (e.g. very basic personal and family information, shopping, local area, employment).

Reading: Able to read and describe his understanding of texts written in simple language. Able to read very short, simple texts, find specific, predictable information in simple everyday material such as advertisements, prospectuses, menus and timetables and understand short simple personal letters.

Speaking: Able to answer simple questions using individual words, expressions or short sentences and to interact in a simple way. Able to express certain feelings that everyone is familiar with, using simple words. Able to have a short telephone conversation, with people arrange to meet them, e.g. able to make simple purchases, explain what he/she is looking for and ask how much an article costs. Able to ask straightforward questions on a subject or react to something that other people have said about it.

Writing: Able to write about people and things in his/her every day life (school, family, hobbies, daily routine, projects...). Able to write a very simple card (e.g. to thank someone for a present). Able to use short sentences to say what he/she prefers or what he/she receives. Able to write a short greetings card. Able to reply to an invitation and cancel or change an appointment, using simple words (e.g. SMS messages).
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: A1 [Lesson 17 Holidays] Read a short blog / Say what you did on your days off / Say briefly what you thought about your days off / Write a short blog about your days off
Hafta 2: A1 [Lesson 18 Holidays] Read a simple Email / Say what you did on your travels / Say where you want to go next time
Hafta 3: A2.1 [Lesson 1 My Family] Talk briefly about where you/your family live and what you /they do / Say what language you speak with your family and friend
Hafta 4: A2.1 [Lesson 2 Myself] Talk about your hobbies / Read short, simple comment about someone’s self-introduction on a website

Hafta 5: A2.1 [Lesson 3-4 Seasons and Weather] Talk about the change of seasons / Say what seasons you like and why / Greet people by talking about weather / Start a conversation over the phone by talking about the weather

Hafta 6: A2.1 [Lesson 5 My Town] Tell a friend about a place/area of your recommendation, using a map of your town / Tell a friend what a place that he/she is interested in is like and what to be careful about, using a map
Hafta 7: A2.1 [Lesson 6 My Town] Tell someone how to get to a place nearby / Correct some information misunderstood by someone / Describe the features of buildings seen in the distance
Hafta 8: A2.1 [Lesson 7 Going Out] Talk with a friend about the time and place you will meet / Read an Email from a friend saying he/she will be late / Apologize for being late and give a reason
Hafta 9: A2.1 [Lesson 8 Going Out] Invite a friend to visit a place of your recommendation / Respond to an invitation / Say that you would like to drop by somewhere

Hafta 10: A2.1 [Lesson 9 Languages and Cultures] Say what languages you have studied and when / Talk about foreign languages you have studied / Write down what language you have studied and when / Ask someone for help to understand or to learn a foreign language / Respond to a request for help

Hafta 11: A2.1 [Lesson 10 Languages and Cultures] Talk about your involvement in the culture of another country / Offer help to someone with a problem / Accept an offer for help / Read the program of an event

Hafta 12: A2.1 [Lesson 11-12 Eating Outdoors] Discuss what to take for a picnic / Write a memo to say who is taking what for a picnic / Ask/Say what specific food or drinks your friend/you would prefer for a picnic / Talk about food you don’t know much about / Comment briefly on the taste of food / Offer a dish to your friend / Respond to an offer

Hafta 13: A2.1 [Lesson 13 Business Trips] Talk about someone visiting your office on a business trip and the date of his/her visit / Greet a visitor arriving at the airport / Check the hotel room and tell your visitor if it is OK / Read a business trip schedule

Hafta 14: A2.1 [Lesson 14 Business Trips] Introduce your colleagues to a visitor / Ask to use things in the office / See a visitor off at the airport with some parting phrases / Read a message from a colleague in the oversea office when you return home from a business trip
resources: まるごと：日本のことばと文化　初級1 A2 ーかつどうー 1-14課 (Marugoto: Elementary 1 A2 - Activities- Lesson 1-14), 国際交流基金 (Japan Foundation), 2013, 三修社 (Sanshusha)
まるごと：日本のことばと文化　初級1 A2 ーりかいー 1-14課 (Marugoto: Elementary 1 A2 - Competences- Lesson 1-14), 国際交流基金 (Japan Foundation), 2013, 三修社 (Sanshusha)
ままるごと：日本のことばと文化　入門A1 ーかつどうー 17&18課 (Marugoto: Starter A1 - Activities- L17&18), 国際交流基金 (Japan Foundation), 2013, 三修社 (Sanshusha)
まるごと：日本のことばと文化　入門A1 ーりかいー 17&18課 (Marugoto: Starter A1 - Competences- L17&L18), 国際交流基金 (Japan Foundation), 2013, 三修社 (Sanshusha)

courses.name: Intermediate Japanese I
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 243 (Japanese III) and is intended for students with basic Japanese knowledge. It aims to help students use the grammatical patterns they have acquired and improve their speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to the first stage of the B1 level of CEFR.

courses.name: Readings in French I
courses.purpose: This course is designed especially for the Master's students of the Department of Archeology and History in accordance with the European Languages Common Framework Programme at the first stage of the level A1. It aims to teach basic French while primarily focusing on reading comprehension.
courses.description: Vocabulary and reading materials covered in this course are chosen from the discipline of Archeology and History.

courses.name: Intermediate Japanese II
courses.purpose: This course is a continuation of LNG 244 (Intermediate Japanese I) and is intended for students with first stage intermediate Japanese knowledge. The course aims to enable students to use the acquired grammatical patterns and improve their speaking, reading, writing and listening comprehension skills.
courses.description: The course is geared to the second stage of the B1 level of CEFR.

courses.name: Readings in German I
courses.purpose: This course is designed especially for the Master's students of the Department of Archeology and History at the first stage of the A1 level within the European Languages Common Framework Programme. It aims to teach basic German while primarily focusing on reading comprehension.
courses.description: Vocabulary and reading materials covered in this course are chosen from the discipline of Archeology and History.

courses.name: Readings in German II
courses.purpose: This course is designed especially for the Master's students of the Department of Archeology and History in accordance with the European Languages Common Framework Programme at the second stage of the level A1. While it aims to teach basic German, it is primarily directed towards the comprehension of readings.
courses.description: Vocabulary and reading materials covered in this course are chosen from the discipline of Archeology and History.

courses.name: Readings in French II
courses.purpose: This course is designed especially for the Master's students of the Department of Archeology and History in accordance with the European Languages Common Framework Programme at the second stage of the level A1. It aims to teach basic French and primarily focuses on reading comprehension.
courses.description: Vocabulary and reading materials covered in this course are chosen from the discipline of Archeology and History.

courses.name: Introduction to Business II
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' analytical and decision-making skills through practical data analysis and spreadsheet-based problem solving. It also introduces automation and basic programming concepts using VBA and Python.
courses.description: This hands-on course mainly focuses on data analysis, which the students will practice during their academic and business life. The students will gain the necessary skills to evaluate, interpret and display data, and additionally, make projections by selecting appropriate functions and decision-making tools (i.e. What-If Analysis) in spreadsheet applications. They will solve optimization problems with a linear programming approach by Solver add-in and practice TVM (Time Value of Money) functions. Moreover, the students will gain an insight into automated tasks that can be conducted with VBA (Visual Basic for Applications) and discover the Python environment and its instruments (modules) by basic programming concepts. At the end of the course, students will be able to improve their analytical skills and master algorithmic thinking.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to effectively use word processing tools in order to create well-formatted and professional-looking documents.
Students will be able to make meaningful inferences and decisions by analyzing and reporting with the given data.
Students will be able to retrieve required data from simple and complex tables using functions and tools.
Students will be able to make predictions and estimations about future cases using tools.
Students will be able to solve optimization problems with a linear programming approach by using Excel add-ins and practice financial functions for both calculation and decision making.
Students will be able to apply their understanding of an algorithmic structure by generating and customizing VBA macro codes to conduct automated tasks.
Students will be able to work with the Python environment and use its instruments (modules) by applying basic programming concepts.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Creating well-formatted and professional-looking documents by MS Word. Managing workbooks and worksheets. Importance of data readability, ways of formatting the necessary sections of a spreadsheet to represent data.

Hafta 2: Basic and advanced usage of Built-in functions [CountIf(s), AverageIf(s), SumIf(s) etc.] Representing data graphically, customization of charts. Writing formulas to manage data & solve problems. Steps of basic data analysis based on emphasis, organization and grouping (Sorting, Conditional Formatting and Filtering)
Hafta 3: Filtering vs Pivot Tables. Data analysis & interpretation with Pivot Tables & Charts.
Hafta 4: Data analysis & interpretation with simple and advanced LookUp functions. (VLookUp & HLookUp)
Hafta 5: Boolean Operators & Functions (AND/OR/NOT). Composing simple If Statements. Making decisions & predictions by forming NESTED IF statements.

Hafta 6: MS Excel Basic Financial Functions (i.e. Time Value of Money). Usage of PV, FV, RATE, NPER, PMT functions. Making decisions with Multiple Cash Flow analyses and forming an Amortization Table.

Hafta 7: Analyzing and predicting data by different options of the What-If Analysis tool (Ex: Break-Even Analysis by one/two-variable data tables; Calculating exact break-even utilizing Goal Seek and preparing Best/Expected/Worst case scenarios by using Scenario Manager tool)
Hafta 8: Linear Programming by Excel SOLVER add-in. Profit maximization/Cost minimization and optimization problems.
Hafta 9: Data analysis, interpretation and presentation by VBA Macros I (Working with String and Integer type of variables, Input and MsgBox functions; Assigning macro codes to buttons)
Hafta 10: Data analysis, interpretation and presentation by VBA Macros II (Applications with If….Then….Else/Nested If statements and iteration by For Each…..Next statement)
Hafta 11: Data analysis, interpretation and presentation by VBA Macros III (Preparing scenarios, making predictions and decision making by considering building blocks of algorithms)

Hafta 12: PYTHON I - Introduction to Python Environment. Installation & set-up process of Jupyter Notebook. Declaring and using variables. Importing and working with Python modules such as Math, Random & Numpy.
Hafta 13: PYTHON II - Understanding and developing iterative procedures (For and While loops), Boolean functions (AND, OR, NOT), and conditional statements (If, Elif, Else, and Nested If statements).
Hafta 14: PYTHON III - Analyzing and exploring MS Excel and CSV files by performing basic calculations (sum, average, max, min, etc.) and applying filters with the Pandas module.
resources: Next Generation Excel, Isaac Gottlieb, Second Edition, John Wiley & Sons Singapore Pte. Ltd.
Succeeding in Business with Microsoft Office Excel 2013: A Problem-Solving Approach, Debra Gross, Frank Akaiwa, Karleen Nordquist, 2013, Cengage Learning
VBA for Modelers: Developing Decision Support Systems Using Microsoft® Excel, S. Christian Albright, 2013, Cengage Learning

courses.name: Principles of Accounting I
courses.purpose: This course is designed to help law students understand how accounting information is used in legal practice. It aims to build familiarity with the basic concepts and principles needed to prepare and analyze financial statements in different legal business forms.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts of accounting, including Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), the users and uses of accounting information, the accounting cycle, and the reporting and analysis of financial statements. It also introduces accounting for sole proprietorships, partnerships, and corporations.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to list, define and explain the purpose of basic financial statements.
Students will be able to list and define the most current Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Students will be able to recognize Professional Code of Ethical Standards for accountants in Turkey and on the International Level.
Students will be able to identify major asset, liability, equity, revenue and expense items and explain how they are accounted for in the financial statements.
Students will be able to infer, deduce and recognize some assumptions for analyzing business transactions and selecting the appropriate accounts for recording them in the accounting books.
Students will be able to perform book keeping and prepare basic financial statements; classified balance sheet, income statement and cash flow statement.
Students will be able to calculate basic financial ratios (current and quick ratios; inventory, accounts receivable and accounts payable turnover ratios; net working capital; gross margin).
Students will be able to define retained earnings concept.
Students will be able to recognize some tax issues (corporate tax and Value Added Tax).
Students will be able to calculate the cost of inventory through inventory costing methods; FIFO, LIFO and Weighted Average methods.
Students will be able to calculate depreciation expense for property, plant and equipment account.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction & Chapter 1: Aim and Users of Accounting
Hafta 2: Chapter 2- The Fundamental Accounting Equation
Hafta 3: Chapter 2- The Accounting Cycle: Journalizing & Posting
Hafta 4: Chapter 3- Completing the Accounting Cycle: Adjusting Entries, Preparation of Trial Balance, Closing the Books
Hafta 5: Chapter 3- Financial Statements – Preparing and Interpreting Balance Sheet & Income Statement
Hafta 6: Chapter 4- Merchandise Transactions: Accounting for Purchases
Hafta 7: Chapter 4- Merchandise Transactions: Accounting for Sales & Preparing Multi-step Income Statement
Hafta 8: Chapter 5- Inventory Costing Methods
Hafta 9: Chapter 7- Current Assets, Uncollectible Accounts for Trade Receivables
Hafta 10: Chapter 10- Long Term Assets
Hafta 11: Chapter 10- Long Term Assets (cont.) - Depreciation Methods for PPE and Intangibles
Hafta 12: Chapter 8 & 11- Current Liabilities (Legal Obligations such as Warranty Liability, Legal Cases, and Corporate Tax) & Long-term Liabilities (Installment Loans, Leasing Obligations)
Hafta 13: Chapter 14- Financial Statement Analysis
Hafta 14: Chapter 13- Cash Flow Statement
resources: Principles of Financial Accounting - Conflux of Internationa, Can Şımga Muğan & Nazlı Hoşal Akman, 2001, Akademi Yayın Hizmetleri A.Ş.

courses.name: Elements of Finance
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the basic principles of finance and the role of financial managers in corporations. It aims to build a framework for understanding how financial decisions are made and how financial markets and institutions connect savings to investments.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamentals of corporate finance, including the time value of money, financial instruments, risk and return, capital budgeting, mergers and acquisitions, and elements of international finance. It also addresses agency problems, financial markets and institutions, and the valuation of firms, bonds, and stocks.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to understand investment and financing decisions that financial managers make.
Students will be able to define agency problems and specify ways to mitigate this problem in corporations.
Students will be able to explain the functions of financial markets and financial institutions and how they work.
Students will be able to apply the time value of money to calculate the value of bonds, common stocks and real investments.
Students will be able to measure and interpret different types of risk and rate of return.
Students will be able to understand capital budgeting techniques, such as net present value and internal rate of return.
Students will be able to describe ways that companies change their ownership or management.
Students will be able to understand basic concepts in international finance.
Students will be able to follow financial media in English.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Finance as a Profession, Governance and Agency Problems in Corporations
Hafta 2: Financial Markets & Financial Institutions
Hafta 3: Accounting and Finance
Hafta 4: Time Value of Money
Hafta 5: Time Value of Money (continued)
Hafta 6: Bond Valuation
Hafta 7: Firm (Stock) Valuation
Hafta 8: Capital Budgeting
Hafta 9: Capital Budgeting (continued)
Hafta 10: Introduction to Risk and Return
Hafta 11: Risk and Return (continued)
Hafta 12: Introduction to Corporate Financing
Hafta 13: Mergers and Acquisitions
Hafta 14: International Finance
