resources: Fundamentals of Corporate Finance, Brealey, Richard A., Stewart C. Myers, and Alan J. Marcus, 11th Edition, 2022, McGraw-Hill

courses.name: Introduction to Management Science
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to management science as a quantitative approach to managerial decision making. It helps students learn how to construct mathematical models and use spreadsheet-based tools to support business decisions.
courses.description: Management science is the application of mathematical modeling to decision making in various management contexts. This course introduces students to mathematical model construction, spreadsheet modeling using Excel Solver, and interpretation of Solver output. The topics also include other decision making tools such as decision trees and simulation.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to identify the need for quantitative managerial decision making tools to improve decision making in a business context and apply the appropriate tools.
Students will be able to demonstrate an understanding of building blocks of quantitative managerial decision making models.

Students will be able to build quantitative managerial decision making models which allow you to get maximum values from business strategies, recognize inherent assumptions, interpret the data and evaluate arguments when the firm has a single or multiple objectives and faces various constraints.
Students will be able to solve and analyze quantitative decision making models using spreadsheets.
Students will be able to analyze the best solution with respect to changes in the parameters of the problem, and evaluate solutions.
Students will be able to produce short and long reports in English and deliver an oral presentation suited for a professional audience.
Students will be able to show an awareness of ethical and diversity related issues.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Management Science & Modelling (Chapter 1)
Hafta 2: Modelling, cont. (Chapter 1)
Hafta 3: Linear Programming (LP): Graphical Solution Methods (Chapter 2)
Hafta 4: Linear Programming (LP): Computer Solution (Chapter 2)
Hafta 5: Using Excell Solver to solve LP problems (Chapter 2)
Hafta 6: LP Applications (Chapter 3)
Hafta 7: LP Applications cont. (Chapter 3)
Hafta 8: LP Sensitivity Analysis (Chapter 4)
Hafta 9: Mathematical Programming Models (Chapter 6)
Hafta 10: IP, NLP, Goal Programming Models (Chapter 6)
Hafta 11: Network models (Chapter 5)
Hafta 12: Project Management (Chapter 7)
Hafta 13: Simulation (Chapter 10)
Hafta 14: Simulation cont.(Chapter 10)
resources: Required - Textbook: Managerial Decison Modeling with Spreadsheets, Render, B., Stair R. M., and Balakrishnan, N., 2003, Prentice Hall
Recommended - Textbook: An Introduction to MANAGEMENT SCIENCE: Quantitative Approach, Anderson, Sweeney and Williams, 2001, West
Recommended - Software: Excel Solver and its Manual

courses.name: Market Research
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the ability to gather, analyze, and use market information systematically for managerial decision-making. It also helps students understand when and how to use primary and secondary data and how to communicate research findings effectively.
courses.description: The course introduces market research methods used in industry to solve organizational problems. It covers the organizational value and context of information gathering, the selection of appropriate data sources, data analysis techniques, and the reporting of research results.
learning_outcomes: Understand the organizational value and context of information gathering.
Know when to collect primary and secondary data.
Determine the appropriate data analysis technique.
Persuasively communicate and report research results.

courses.name: Organizational Behavior
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand how individuals and groups behave in organizations and what factors influence these behaviors. It also introduces the role of organizational behavior in organizational effectiveness.
courses.description: This course covers the application of concepts, theories, and empirical findings from the behavioral and social sciences to the study of behavior in and around organizations. Topics include individual differences, values, attitudes, moods and emotions, perception, motivation, interpersonal relationships, group dynamics, leadership, ethics, and diversity.
learning_outcomes: Define organizational behavior and explain how it affects organizational effectiveness.

Identify behavioral phenomena in organizations, including individual differences, values, attitudes, moods and emotions, perception and attribution, learning, motivation, interpersonal relationships, group dynamics, and leadership.
Apply organizational behavior knowledge to critically analyze real-life situations and suggest constructive recommendations.
Produce written reports in English and deliver an oral presentation suited for a professional audience.
Assess organizational scenarios in terms of ethical and diversity-related issues.
Function better in team settings.
Think critically.
Show awareness of the ethical components of a business decision.
Show awareness of and commitment to respect for others' opinions and beliefs, and equal treatment of individuals regardless of gender and other characteristics.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: What is organizational behavior?
Hafta 3: Diversity & ethics
Hafta 4: Attitudes & Job satisfaction
Hafta 5: Personality
Hafta 6: Values
Hafta 7: Perception & individual decision making
Hafta 8: Midterm
Hafta 9: Motivation-1
Hafta 10: Motivation-2
Hafta 11: Teams & groups-1
Hafta 12: Teams & groups-2
Hafta 13: Leadership
Hafta 14: Semester wrap-up
resources: Organizational Behavior, Robbins, S. P. & Judge, T. A., 18th Edition/Global Edition, Pearson

courses.name: Financial Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a solid understanding of the theory and practice of corporate finance. It prepares students to analyze financial decisions and communicate effectively in finance-related contexts.
courses.description: MAN 307 is an intermediate level course on the theory and practice of corporate finance.
learning_outcomes: Master financial vocabulary to communicate effectively with professionals in finance.
Draw pro-forma financial statements and apply investment rules to make investment decisions.
Recognize the differing objectives of the firms' stakeholders and evaluate how these differing objectives may affect the decision-making of financial managers.
Recognize and evaluate the effect of financing decisions on firm value.

courses.name: Managerial Accounting
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to introduce the students to the main issues in management accounting. Special emphasis will be put on decision making at different levels of management, and on data and reports to facilitate the decision making process.
courses.description: Topics covered include cost volume profit analysis, cost behavior, costing systems, budgeting, unit cost calculations, pricing, variance analysis, responsibility accounting and performance evaluation.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to differentiate the financial and managerial accounting.
Students will be able to state the work of management in an organization.

Students will be able to recognize the importance of ethical standards issued by Union of Chambers of Certified Public Accountants of Turkey and identify ethical issues within a situation in a business environment.
Students wil be able to ıdentify costs in terms of cost behaviour (variable vs fixed costs) and relevance (opportunity costs vs sunk costs).
Students will be able to distinguish between product/manufacturing (material, labor and overhead costs) and period costs
Students will be able to calculate and prepare schedule of cost of goods manufactured and cost of goods sold.
Students will be able to distinguish between different costing systems. e.g. Job Order Costing, Process Costing.

Students will be able to apply decision making concepts in cost volume analysis scenarios and calculate break-even points in quantity and monetary value, margin of safety, & degree of operating leverage.
Students will be able to adopt knowledge and skills of managerial accounting though spreadsheets.
Students will be able to identify and compute the types of standard costs and interpret them for decision making purpose.
Students will be able to infer, deduce and recognize some assumptions for identifying relevant cost information at different environments and making CVP Analysis.
Students will be able to produce quality professional written document on an ethical issue of a business decision.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Cost terms concepts and classifications (Appendix included)
Hafta 3: Job order costing
Hafta 4: Job Order and Process Costing
Hafta 5: Holiday
Hafta 6: Cost Behavior Analysis
Hafta 7: Cost Behavior Analysis and Use
Hafta 8: Cost-Volume-Profit Relations
Hafta 9: Variable Costing
Hafta 10: Activity Based Costing and Pricing
Hafta 11: Profit Planning
Hafta 12: Standard costs
Hafta 13: Flexible Budgets and Overhead Analysis
Hafta 14: Relevant Costs for Decision Making
resources: Managerial Accounting, 18th ed., Garrison, McGraw Hill (Connect)

courses.name: Business Forecasting
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide the concepts and principles of a variety of forecasting models. Main emphasis is on the establishment of a process for effective forecasting.
courses.description: Within this framework, various smoothing techniques, regression analysis, ARIMA models, and judgmental forecasting issues are discussed in detail.

courses.name: Corporate Finance
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the students to the world of finance through fundamental concepts such as time value of money, risk, return, and asset valuation.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts of corporate finance, including the time value of money, risk and return, asset valuation, financial performance analysis, bond and stock valuation, and capital budgeting techniques.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to identify the goal of the corporation.
Students will be able to measure and analyze the financial performance of a firm’s management.
Students will be able to apply the time value of money in order to solve related problems and to value financial and real asset investments.
Students will be able to define and measure risk and rate of return.
Students will be able to calculate values of bonds and common stocks.
Students will be able to apply capital budgeting techniques, such as net present value and internal rate of return to evaluate projects.
Students will be able to interpret and evaluate the results of ratio analysis.
Students will be able to follow financial press in English.
Students will be able to calculate and interpret the weighted average cost of capital.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Overview and Financial Environment
Hafta 2: Measuring Financial Performance
Hafta 3: Measuring Financial Performance
Hafta 4: Time Value of Money
Hafta 5: Time Value of Money
Hafta 6: Time Value of Money & Mid-term Exam I
Hafta 7: Bond Valuation
Hafta 8: Bond Valuation
Hafta 9: Stock Valuation
Hafta 10: Stock Valuation & Mid-term Exam II
Hafta 11: Risk and Return
Hafta 12: Risk and Return
Hafta 13: Capital Budgeting
Hafta 14: Capital Budgeting
resources: Fundamentals of Corporate Finance, Stephen A. Ross, Randolph W. Westerfield, and Bradford A. Jordan, 2022, McGraw-Hill Irwin

courses.name: Money and Banking
courses.purpose: This course is designed to introduce the theory and practice of money and banking in developing countries.
courses.description: In the first part of the course, money demand and money supply processes and the role of money in an economy will be emphasized. In the second part, the macroeconomics framework will be developed. And finally in the third part, the course will focus on the models of bank behavior and management. In addition, a wide range of topics from financial institutions to government intervention in financial markets will be covered.

courses.name: Marketing Principles
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with an analytical understanding of marketing as a major business function and its role in organizational decision-making.
courses.description: An analytical study of marketing as a major business function. Topics include an overview of the marketing system, the marketing concept, market research, market analysis, marketing strategies (segmentation, targeting, positioning), and marketing programs. The course also examines how effective marketing strategy and program decisions are based on an analysis of buyer behavior, market structure, and competition.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to demonstrate an understanding of the fundamental concepts and terminology used in marketing.
Students will be able to identify and apply appropriate marketing tools.
Students will be able to make marketing strategy decisions by using critical thinking skills.

Students will be able to develop analytical reasoning in a marketing management setting, especially with respect to understanding social, political, economic and competitive environments, their inter-relationships, and their influences on marketing actions.
Students will be able to produce qualified marketing plans.
Students will be able to improve presentation skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: INTRODUCTION
Hafta 2: STRATEGIC MARKETING
Hafta 3: MARKETING ENVIRONMENT & GLOBALIZATION
Hafta 4: CONSUMER BEHAVIOR
Hafta 5: COMPETITION
Hafta 6: SEGMENTATION, TARGETTING AND POSITIONING
Hafta 7: PRODUCT DECISIONS
Hafta 8: SERVICE DECISIONS
Hafta 9: DISTRIBUTION CHANNELS
Hafta 10: RETAILING & EMARKETING
Hafta 11: INTEGRATED MARKETING COMMUNICATIONS
Hafta 12: ADVERTISING
Hafta 13: PUBLIC RELATIONS, PERSONAL SELLING & SALES PROMOTION
Hafta 14: PRICING
resources: Principles of Marketing, P. Kotler & G. Armstrong, 2014, Pearson

courses.name: Fundamentals of Marketing
courses.purpose: The course introduces students to the fundamental concepts, tools, and activities that comprise the marketing function. It also provides an overview of the development of marketing thought and contemporary issues in marketing.
courses.description: Topics include the marketing concept and orientation, segmentation and positioning, buyer behavior analysis, and marketing mix decisions related to product, price, promotion, and distribution.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to demonstrate an understanding of the fundamental concepts and terminology used in marketing.
Students will be able to identify and apply appropriate marketing tools.
Students will be able to make marketing decisions by critically thinking the information provided to them (i.e. by evaluating arguments).

Students will be able to develop their analytical reasoning and problem solving skills in a marketing management setting, especially with respect to understanding social, political, economic and competitive environments, their inter-relationships, and their influences on marketing actions, and evaluating marketing decision variables in the design of marketing strategies, and showing awareness of the ethical components of marketing decision making.
Students will be able to develop a foundation for further study in marketing and other business areas.

Students will be able to practice interpersonal communication and writing skills consistent with contemporary marketing management and business practices. More specifically, students will be able top produce quality marketing plans, and deliver a quality professional presentation of their marketing plan to the class using appropriate technology.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Defining Marketing; Overview of the course
Hafta 2: Marketing Basics and Strategy
Hafta 3: Marketing Research
Hafta 4: Consumer Markets
Hafta 5: Business-Organizational Markets
Hafta 6: Marketing Strategy
Hafta 7: Midterm
Hafta 8: Product/Service Decisions
Hafta 9: New Product Development
Hafta 10: Pricing Decisions
Hafta 11: Distribution Decisions
Hafta 12: Promotion Decisions
Hafta 13: Course wrap-up
Hafta 14: Project Presentations
resources: Principles of Marketing, Philip Kotler; Gary Armstrong; Sridhar Balasubramanian, 19th Edition, Pearson

courses.name: Managerial Economics
courses.purpose: This course introduces economic concepts used in managerial decision making. It aims to equip students with basic microeconomic tools and experience in applying them to questions about consumer behavior, competition among firms, and government policy.
courses.description: Cases and problems illustrate how economic concepts can be applied to improve corporate strategies.

courses.name: Organization Theory
courses.purpose: This course is to help students obtain in-depth understanding of organizations and organizational effectiveness. By introducing the basic concepts and recent theoretical approaches, the course will help students to develop the capability of understanding, designing, and managing organizations.
courses.description: The course emphasizes both the macro characteristics of organizations such as their structures, technology and environment, and internal processes such as organizational culture, managerial decision making, politics and conflicts.
learning_outcomes: Compare different perspectives in organization theory, and be able to apply them in analysing and solving organizational problems.
Elucidate intra-organizational processes, including politics, conflict, decision-making, innovation, and culture.
Evaluate organizational structures and strategies, and investigate the interplay between different organizational choices.
Conduct environmental and organizational analysis.
Identify stakeholders, relationships between organizations and the environments and assess the outcomes and implications of the possible relationships on one another.
Evaluate and facilitate innovation and change processes in organizations.
Assess the effects of size, life cycle, technology and information control processes on organizations.
Show awareness of the ethical components of an organizational design.
Improve oral and written communication skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: What is organization theory?
Hafta 2: Perspectives on organizations
Hafta 3: Strategy, organization design & effectiveness
Hafta 4: Fundamentals of organization structure
Hafta 5: The external environment
Hafta 6: Interorganizational relationships
Hafta 7: Midterm 1
Hafta 8: Manufacturing and Service Organizations
Hafta 9: Organization size, life cycle & decline
Hafta 10: Organizational culture & ethical values
Hafta 11: Innovation & change
Hafta 12: Decision-making
Hafta 13: Conflict, power & politics
Hafta 14: Semester wrap up and additional cases
resources: Organization Theory & Design: An International Perspective, R. L. Daft, J. Murphy & H. Willmott, 2020/4th Edition, Cengage Learning

courses.name: Investment Analysis
courses.purpose: The main objective of this course is to help students develop a basic understanding of the theory and practice of investment analysis.
courses.description: The characteristics of financial markets and financial instruments, security trading mechanisms, investment process, pricing models, equity and bond valuation are studied. Students apply what they learn in class to the Istanbul Stock and Bond Markets.

courses.name: International Business
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a framework for understanding the challenges and opportunities of doing business across national borders. It develops analytical tools for evaluating international business decisions and for formulating effective strategies, structures, and processes in the global environment.
courses.description: International Business differs in important ways from business conducted within national borders. The course covers analytical frameworks for international business decisions and combines concepts from strategy, international finance, international trade and investment, cross-cultural issues, international politics and law, marketing, human resource management, and related areas.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to understand the Pros and Cons of Globalization and Modes of Entry.
Students will be able to understand the Role of Economic, Political, and Cultural Environments in IB.
Students will be able to understand the Role of International Trade & Regional Economic Integration.
Students will be able to understand the strategies of internationalization (Multi-domestic, Global, Transnational, International).
Students will be able to understand the Role of Ethics/CSR in IB.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course and Understanding Globalization
Hafta 2: National Differences in Political, Economic & Legal Systems and Institutional Development
Hafta 3: Differences in Culture
Hafta 4: International Trade Theories
Hafta 5: Government Policy and International Trade
Hafta 6: Foreign Direct Investment
Hafta 7: Midterm Exam Review & Midterm Exam
Hafta 8: International Finance & Foreign Exchange Market
Hafta 9: Strategy of International Business
Hafta 10: Entry Strategy & Alliances
Hafta 11: Exporting & Importing
Hafta 12: Global Production, Outsourcing and Logistics
Hafta 13: Global Marketing
Hafta 14: Final Exam Review
resources: Global Business Today, Charles W.L. Hill, 12. edition, McGraw-Hill Irwin.

courses.name: Production Management
courses.purpose: This course introduces the functional area of production management and its role in designing, operating, and improving the processes and systems used to create and deliver products and services. It aims to survey the operating practices and procedures found in manufacturing and service delivery firms.
courses.description: Production management is a functional field of management covering the design, operation, and improvement of the processes and systems employed in the creation and delivery of an organization's products and services. This course intends to be a survey of the operating practices and procedures found in both manufacturing and service delivery firms.
learning_outcomes: Students should be able to identify, define and formulate operations management problems.
Students should be able to identify the available solution alternatives and/or develop novel alternatives.
Students should be able to apply mainly quantitative techniques (sometimes using spreadsheet applications) to solve some of these problems.
Students will be able to identify the strategic issues in operations management.
Students will be able to identify the issues in quality management and apply the seven tools of TQM.
Sudents will be able to identify the issues in product/service design and compare the possible alternatives for design and development.
Studenyts will be able to identify the issues in production/service system design and apply the basic analysis and solution tools in this area.
Students will be able to identify the cost issues in supply chain management and apply the basic decision models to problems in this area.
Students will be able to identify the ethical issues at hand in operations management decisions and those parties impacted.
Students will be able to identify, formulate and solve OM problems that you may encounter in their professional career.
Students will be able to enhance their key skills in time management, taking initiative and responsibility, communicating ideas especially in written form.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction What is P/OM?
Hafta 2: Productivity, Competitiveness and Strategy
Hafta 3: Productivity, Competitiveness and Strategy
Hafta 4: Process Selection and Capacity Planning
Hafta 5: Location Planning
Hafta 6: Facilities Layout
Hafta 7: Design of Work Systems & Facilities Layout (revisited)
Hafta 8: Learning Curves
Hafta 9: Quality Management
Hafta 10: Quality Assurance and Process Improvement
Hafta 11: Quality Control
Hafta 12: Product and Service Design
Hafta 13: Reliability, Maintenance and Warranties
Hafta 14: Review
resources: Production/Operations Management, W. J. Stevenson, 14the Ed., McGrawHill
Selected readings on reserve

courses.name: Business Plan Development
courses.purpose: This course focuses on business plans as a necessary element of starting a business and prepares students to participate in business planning in large institutions.
courses.description: The course goes through the process of preparing successful business plans, including determining the contents of a plan and reviewing an actual plan. It is designed to help students incorporate the contents of the core management courses.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the components of a business plan.
Prepare the components of a business plan.

courses.name: Business Strategy
courses.purpose: This course provides a comprehensive overview of the analytical tools, methods, and processes used to develop, implement, and evaluate business strategy. It aims to help students understand how top-level management can identify and leverage a firm's resources and competences to create long-term direction and sustainable competitive advantage.
courses.description: Business strategy refers to the long-term direction and scope of a firm's activities. The course covers the analytical tools, methods, and processes involved in developing, implementing, and evaluating business strategy. It also examines how firms' resources and competences are used within competitive, industry, and macro-environmental contexts.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to consider the business enterprise as a total system in a global environment.
Students will be able to analyse and evaluate the problems, opportunities, and ethical responsibilities which face the chief executive of a business firm.
Students will be able to formulate and implement various strategies.
Students will have gained experience in using the technique of case problem analysis.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Business strategy: Introduction, overview and key concepts
Hafta 2: Analysis of a firm's resources, competences and strategic position
Hafta 3: Analysis of a firm's resources, competences and strategic position (cont'd)
Hafta 4: Strategic analysis of the external environment and stakeholders
Hafta 5: Strategic analysis of the external environment and stakeholders (cont'd)
Hafta 6: Business-level competitive strategy options
Hafta 7: Business-level competitive strategy options (cont'd) MIDTERM
Hafta 8: Corporate-level strategy options
Hafta 9: Corporate-level strategy options (cont'd)
Hafta 10: Evaluating strategic effectiveness
Hafta 11: Evaluating strategic effectiveness (cont'd)
Hafta 12: Strategy development process
Hafta 13: Course synopsis and team presentations
Hafta 14: Course synopsis and team presentations
resources: Exploring Strategy: Text and Cases, Johnson, G. et. al., 2017, Pearson
Various business case studies (some to be purchased)
Various business articles and book excerpts

courses.name: Innovation Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand why innovation is critical for organizational competitiveness and how it can be managed systematically. It also seeks to develop awareness of the structures, systems, and incentives that support creativity, innovation, and corporate entrepreneurship.
courses.description: The course covers all aspects of managing innovation, including recognizing the need and desire to be creative and innovative, using imagination to add value, and developing structures, systems, and incentives that encourage and implement innovation. It centers on three themes: ideation/creativity generation techniques, innovation models, dimensions, degrees and sources of innovation, and strategic management of innovation. Real company cases are presented and discussed to help students understand the significance of leadership and corporate entrepreneurship in managing innovation.

courses.name: Financial Statement Analysis
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop an ability to analyze financial information as an aid to financial decision making. The emphasis is on understanding the usefulness of financial statement information for various groups such as investors, security analysts, and creditors.
courses.description: This course covers basic financial statement analysis tools, including ratio analysis, cross-sectional and time-series analysis, and statistical forecasting models. It also examines research and empirical evidence on the impact of financial statement information on asset pricing, capital market efficiency, debt ratings, and corporate restructuring.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to define basic financial statements.
Students will be able to locate information within financial statements and notes to the financial statements.
Students will be able to evaluate financial position, profitability and cash flow performance of a business entity.
Students will be able to research and collect financial information about a business entity.
Students will be able to write and present a formal financial analysis report.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Overview of Financial Statements and Analysis
Hafta 3: The Balance Sheet-assets
Hafta 4: The Balance Sheet-assets
Hafta 5: The Balance Sheet-liabilities and shareholders' equity
Hafta 6: Income statement and statement of shareholders' equity
Hafta 7: Statement of cash flows
Hafta 8: A Guide to Earnings and Financial Reporting Quality
Hafta 9: The Analysis of Financial Statements
Hafta 10: Ratio Analysis
Hafta 11: Midterm
Hafta 12: Accounting for Mergers and Acquisitions
Hafta 13: Company Valuation
Hafta 14: Company Valuation
resources: Understanding Financial Statements, Lyn M. Fraser, Aileen Ormiston, 8th, Pearson Prentice Hall

courses.name: Marketing Strategy and Innovation
courses.purpose: To develop students' understanding of marketing as a strategic process driven by value creation for customers, and to build their ability to design and manage marketing strategies in competitive and rapidly changing markets.
courses.description: Marketing Strategy and Innovation integrates critical aspects of marketing and innovation and presents marketing as a strategic process driven by value creation for customers. The course focuses on the design and management of all elements of business necessary to define, develop and deliver customer value in the challenging environment of global competition and rapidly changing markets. It combines theoretical and practical perspectives to support the conception, development, and execution of a marketing strategy.

courses.name: Capital Markets and Institutions
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the theory and practice of financial intermediation. It aims to help students understand how financial institutions and markets operate and how they are affected by economic conditions and regulation.
courses.description: The course covers the structure and functioning of the financial system, the special role of financial institutions, commercial banking, regulation and recent developments in Turkish banking, financial markets in Turkey, financial statement analysis of financial institutions, and the main risks faced by financial institutions. It also includes interest rate risk, derivative securities, and off-balance-sheet risk.
learning_outcomes: Analyze how changing economic conditions and regulations are affecting financial institutions in the world and in Turkey.
Read and interpret financial statements of commercial banks.
Recognize and analyze the risks that financial institutions face.
Recognize how money markets, bond markets, stock markets and foreign exchange markets work.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Overview of the Financial System
Hafta 2: Why are financial institutions special?
Hafta 3: Commercial banks overview
Hafta 4: Regulation and recent developments in Turkish commercial banking
Hafta 5: Regulation and recent developments in Turkish commercial banking
Hafta 6: Case Discussion
Hafta 7: Project Presentations
Hafta 8: Financial Markets in Turkey
Hafta 9: The Historical Background of Financial Markets
Hafta 10: The Structure of Capital Markets
Hafta 11: Regulation in Markets
Hafta 12: Financial Statement Analysis of Financial Institutions
Hafta 13: Risks that financial institutions face
Hafta 14: Interest rate risk
Hafta 15: Managing interest rate risk
Hafta 16: Derivative Securities and off-balance sheet risk
Hafta 17: Project Presentations
resources: Financial Markets and Institutions Management, Anthony Saunders and Marcia M. Cornett

courses.name: International Finance
courses.purpose: This course introduces the financial issues associated with operating a firm in the international environment.
courses.description: This course introduces financial issues associated with the operation of a firm in the international environment. Specifically, the workings of the foreign exchange markets, the impact of economic policy on exchange rates, the nature of foreign exchange risk, and important aspects of financial management of the multinational corporation will be introduced. The connection to current events as reported in newspapers and journals will be emphasized.
learning_outcomes: Students are expected to analyze, interpret and understand the fundamentals of forecasting foreign exchange (FX) movements.
Students are expected to define purchasing power parity (PPP) and interest rate parity (IRP).
Students are expected to analyze financial and strategic impact of FX risk on the corporations.
Students are expected to calculate transnational and economic (transactional and operational) risk exposures.
Students are expected to define the fundamentals of FX risk management.
Students are expected to identify international investment risks.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: What is "Special About International Finance"?
Hafta 2: Exchange Rate Systems and Impossibility of Trinity
Hafta 3: Balance of Payments and Exchange Rate Systems
Hafta 4: Foreign Currency Market
Hafta 5: Exchange Rate Determination
Hafta 6: International Parity Conditions and Carry Trade
Hafta 7: Review and Midterm Exam
Hafta 8: Economic Exposure
Hafta 9: Transaction Exposure and Financial Hedging Alternatives
Hafta 10: Lufthansa Case, Voyages Soleil Case and Richard May's Option Case
Hafta 11: Operational Exposure
Hafta 12: Operational Exposure
Hafta 13: International Capital Budgeting and Groupe Ariel Case
Hafta 14: International Capital Budgeting and Centralia Case
resources: International Finance , Cheol S. Eun, Bruce G. Resnick (Author) , 2015, McGraw Hill
Harvard Business School Cases - Articles from "The Economist"

courses.name: Auditing
courses.purpose: The primary objective of the course is to distinguish between accounting and auditing by familiarizing students with basic auditing concepts.
courses.description: Topics such as types of audits and auditors, audit reports for financial statements, professional ethics, evidence accumulation and verification procedures, internal control, and auditing engagements are discussed.

courses.name: Anthropological Marketing
courses.purpose: Students learn how to do qualitative research in order to understand markets and consumers. The course emphasizes ethnography and field-based inquiry as methods for identifying unmet needs, generating insights, and supporting marketing and business decisions.
courses.description: The course covers qualitative research methods used to understand markets and consumers, with a focus on ethnography, observed everyday behavior, ethical and effective field practices, and the application of research findings to marketing and business decisions. It also includes research assignments designed to improve writing and presentation skills.
learning_outcomes: Conduct qualitative research to understand markets and consumers.
Apply ethnographic and field research methods ethically and effectively.
Use research findings to inform marketing and business decisions.
Improve writing and presentation skills through research assignments.
resources: Mariampolski textbook (ethnography and qualitative research)

courses.name: Risk Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the risk management and the derivatives markets. The concept of risk management and the question of why risk management is vital for modern financial institutions will be explored.
courses.description: The course covers the main concepts and instruments used in risk management and derivatives markets, including forwards, futures, swaps, options, hedging, portfolio insurance, and value at risk.
learning_outcomes: Students will develop an ability to define and understand financial derivatives like futures, forwards, options.
Students will be able to develop hedging strategies.
Students will develop an ability to define and understand the concepts of speculation, hedging and arbitrage.
Students will develop an ability to understand basic investment strategies involving derivative instruments.
Students will be able to understand the composition and structure of an investment or a risk management strategy.
Students will be able to collect or find data for analysis.
Students will be able to analyse the situation using the relevant tools for the purpose.
Students will be able to suggest a hedging or investment strategy using the analysis and the solutions.

Students will develop teamwork skills such as taking responsibility within the group, working in harmony with team members, time management skills and motivation and taking initiative within the group.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Basic Concepts in Risk Management
Hafta 2: Futures and Forwards Trading
Hafta 3: Forward Pricing
Hafta 4: Volatility Estimation
Hafta 5: Risk Management through Hedging with Futures and Forwards
Hafta 6: Swaps
Hafta 7: Option Trading
Hafta 8: Review and Midterm
Hafta 9: Spring Break
Hafta 10: Binomial Option Pricing
Hafta 11: Black-Scholes Option Pricing
Hafta 12: Risk Management through Hedging with Options
Hafta 13: Introduction to Value at Risk (VAR)
Hafta 14: VAR Calculations with Derivatives
resources: Fundamentals of Futures and Options Markets, John C. Hull, 2005, Prentice Hull
An introduction to Derivatives, Don M. Chance, 1998, Dryden
Value at Risk: The New Benchmark for Controlling Derivative, Phillippe Jorion, 2000, Irvine Professional Publishers
Risk Management, Michel Crouhy, R. Mark, and D. Galai, 2000, McGraw-Hill Trade
Return to RiskMetrics - the Evolution of a Standard, Jorge Mina and Jerry Y. Xiao, 2001, RiskMetrics Group

courses.name: Consumer Behavior
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand why consumers behave as they do and how that behavior can be analyzed and predicted. It also emphasizes how consumer insights can be applied to the development, evaluation, and implementation of effective marketing strategies.
courses.description: A study of the nature and determinants of consumer behavior. The course examines how individuals and groups acquire, consume, and dispose of goods, and explores the influence of psycho-sociological factors such as personality, cognitive characteristics, beliefs, social class, and culture on consumers' attitudes and purchasing behavior. It also focuses on understanding and predicting marketplace behavior and on applying these insights to marketing strategy.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to identify and explain the psychological and sociological theoretical underpinnings that contribute to and shape the behavior of people as consumers.
Students will be able to analyze the complexities of external and internal influences on consumers' behavior and relate them to strategic implications for marketers.
Students will be able to assess and apply the principles of consumer segmentation, positioning, and targeting for effective marketing management.
Students will be able to critically and ethically evaluate market offerings and campaigns as relating to consumers.
Students will be able to investigate a consumer-related issue and develop a strategic initiative to address that issue to benefit consumers and a business entity or a policy-making institution.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction_Syllabus
Hafta 2: Consumer Behavior and Value - Critical thinking & Perspective
Hafta 3: Consumer Research - Consumer Learning: Perception
Hafta 4: Consumer Learning: Memory - Consumer Motivation and Emotion
Hafta 5: Attitudes and Attitude Change
Hafta 6: Personality and Consumer Lifestyles - Construction of The Self
Hafta 7: Group and Interpersonal Influence
Hafta 8: Written Progress Report & Presentation - Culture
Hafta 9: Consumer Culture and Globalization - Field Trip
Hafta 10: Microcultures
Hafta 11: Social Stratification
Hafta 12: Consumer Decision Making, Value and Satisfaction
Hafta 13: Consumer Insight & Wrap up & discussion of project related Qs
Hafta 14: Exam _Research Project Work
resources: Consumer Behavior: Buying, Having, Being, 14th edition (Global Edition), Michael R. Solomon, 2024, Pearson
CB (Consumer Behavior) 7th ed., Babin, B. and Harris, E. G., 2015, Cengage Learning
Principles of Marketing, Philip T. Kotler & Gary Armstrong, 2024, Pearson

courses.name: Services Marketing
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to provide strategic insights to the marketing managers of service firms.
courses.description: The course focuses on the unique characteristics of service products that differentiate them from manufactured goods and classifies services according to many different variables such as the nature of the service act and method of service delivery. Alternative strategies that can be executed to achieve organizational objectives by the marketing managers of different types of service firms are discussed in detail.

courses.name: Integrated Marketing Communications
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' understanding of how marketing communication tools are strategically planned, integrated, and evaluated. It also seeks to build analytical skills for assessing and managing communication needs in a competitive business environment.
courses.description: This course introduces students to various tools such as advertising, sales promotions, and sponsorships, that marketers use while communicating with their customers. The emphasis rests on strategic planning and development of marketing communications campaigns, their integration and evaluation. The course combines theory and practice and aims to develop analytical skills necessary for effectively assessing and managing communication needs of companies operating in today's competitive business environment.
learning_outcomes: Understand the consumer’s perspective including the consumer decision making process and how marketing communications influence this process.
Understand the different types of communication tools and how they are combined into an integrated communications campaign.
Analyse the key drivers for successful marketing communications programs.
Critically evaluate various media channels and identify their uses, benefits, and drawbacks.
Apply specific tools and approaches to evaluate the effectiveness of communication campaigns from the business, regulatory, social & ethical points of view.
Get familiar with the advertising industry and the structure and functions of the organizations involved.
Design an effective marketing communication campaign; learn how to write a client brief, strategy report, media planning report; and identify the strengths and weaknesses of alternative strategies.
Promote critical and analytical thinking, enhance decision-making and communication skills (vocally, visually, and written) and encourage students to get practical with their creative skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction/Course Overview & Integrated Marketing Communications
Hafta 2: IMC Program Situation Analysis
Hafta 3: Analyzing the Communication Process
Hafta 4: Creative Strategy
Hafta 5: Media Planning & Strategy
Hafta 6: Advertising Media Selection
Hafta 7: Direct Marketing
Hafta 8: Digital Marketing & Social Media
Hafta 9: Sales Promotion
Hafta 10: Public Relations & Sponsorship
Hafta 11: Measuring the Effectiveness of the Promotional Program
Hafta 12: IMC Ethics / Evaluating an Integrated Marketing Program
Hafta 13: Project Presentations
Hafta 14: Project Presentations
resources: Advertising and Promotion, Belch & Belch, 13, McGraw Hill

courses.name: New Product Development
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the tools and techniques needed to analyze market opportunities and to design, test, and introduce new products and services. It also prepares students to understand how consumers, customers, and competitors shape new product development decisions.
courses.description: The course covers the new product development process, market entry strategies, idea generation for new products, mapping customer perceptions, segmentation, product positioning, forecasting market demand, and product design. Both quantitative and qualitative approaches are included.

courses.name: Retail Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with foundational knowledge of the retail industry and the strategic decisions retailers make in areas such as market, financial, location, and store management. It also introduces channel design, retail operations, and current trends in retailing.
courses.description: Foundational knowledge of the retail industry. Strategic decisions made by retailers including market, financial, location, and store management strategies. Channel design and management. Retailer activities including the use of big data and analytical methods, assortment planning, pricing, promotions. A discussion of the latest trends in retailing.
learning_outcomes: Students will be proficient in developing a Project Management Life Cycle by considering & applying the PMI standarts.
Students will be able to develop a project scope while considering factors such as customer requirements and internal/external goals.
Students will have acquired advanced knowledge of the following PM process groups: Initiating, Planning, Executing, Monitoring and Control, and Closing.
Students will manage the scope, cost, timing, and quality of the project, at all times focused on project success as defined by project stakeholders.
Students will identify project goals, constraints, deliverables, performance criteria, control needs, and resource requirements in consultation with stakeholders.

Students will have acquired advanced knowledge of tools for project scheduling (by using MS Project), templates for project management and communication, and in depth knowledge of techniques such as Earned Value to measure schedule variance and cost variance.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction – Syllabus Review & What is Retailing?
Hafta 2: The Science of Shopping
Hafta 3: Introduction to Retail Marketing Strategy
Hafta 4: Introduction to E-Commerce and Digital Retailing
Hafta 5: Introduction to Buying Process
Hafta 6: Introduction to Financial Management
Hafta 7: Introduction to the Importance of Customer Service and Relationship Management (CRM)
Hafta 8: Introduction to Retail Human Resources
Hafta 9: Introduction to Innovations in Retail
Hafta 10: Introduction to Franchising
Hafta 11: Luxury in Retailing
Hafta 12: Promotions
Hafta 13: Consumer Act Rights and Responsibilities
Hafta 14: Responsibility/Sustainability in Retailing
resources: Retail Management, A Strategic Approach, Barry Berman, Joel R. Evans, Patrali Chatterjee, 13th Edition, Pearson

courses.name: Global Marketing and Emerging Markets
courses.purpose: This course aims to apply marketing concepts and methods to the international marketplace and to develop an understanding of the problems and decisions involved in marketing across national boundaries.
courses.description: Application of marketing concepts and methods to the international marketplace. Problems and decisions involved in marketing across national boundaries are discussed. While focusing on export marketing, the course also covers the international environment, export market selection, export market entry strategies, export marketing mix decisions, financing and methods of payment, and the export order and physical distribution.

courses.name: Revenue Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the tools and conceptual frameworks of revenue management and its applications in diverse industries such as tourism, hospitality, manufacturing and fashion.
courses.description: Revenue management is concerned with two types of demand decision: quality (how to allocate capacity to different market segments, when to withhold a product from sale etc.) and price (how to set prices, how to price across product categories, over time etc.).

courses.name: Project Management
courses.purpose: This course provides an integrative view of project management and explains why project management is important for achieving strategic goals in organizations.
courses.description: The course covers project selection, project organization, budgeting and cost estimation, progress and performance measurement and evaluation, and project auditing. Project planning and control techniques such as CPM and PERT are also included.
learning_outcomes: Be proficient in developing a Project Management Life Cycle by considering and applying the PMI standards.
Be able to develop a project scope while considering factors such as customer requirements and internal/external goals.
Have acquired advanced knowledge of the following PM process groups: Initiating, Planning, Executing, Monitoring and Control, and Closing.
Manage the scope, cost, timing, and quality of the project, at all times focused on project success as defined by project stakeholders.
Identify project goals, constraints, deliverables, performance criteria, control needs, and resource requirements in consultation with stakeholders.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Project, Project Management, Project Life Cycles, Project Stakeholders, Project
Hafta 2: Project Financing, Project Selection
Hafta 3: Project Contract Management
Hafta 4: Project Planning
Hafta 5: Project Time Management
Hafta 6: Project Cost Management
Hafta 7: Using Software to Assist in Time and Cost Management (Microsoft Project)
Hafta 8: Project Monitoring Evaluation and Control
Hafta 9: Project Procurement Management
Hafta 10: Project Quality Management
Hafta 11: Project Risk Management
Hafta 12: Project Teams Leadership, Communications, Team Building, Cultural Considerations
Hafta 13: Project Termination & Sustaibilty of Project
Hafta 14: Project Presentations
resources: Project Management, Erik W. Larson, Clifford F. Gray, 2021/8th edition, McGraw Hill

courses.name: Decision Analysis
courses.purpose: This course is designed as an introduction to the basic concepts, principles and methods of decision making under uncertainty.
courses.description: The course covers decision trees as a modeling tool. It also discusses the role and value of information in decision making, the concept of risk, modeling risk attitudes with utility theory, measuring utility functions, and alternatives to utility theory.

courses.name: Cross-Cultural Management
courses.purpose: This course is designed for persons who expect to do business in an unfamiliar country or interact with people from other cultures. It aims to build awareness of fundamental cultural differences that shape everyday life and business practice.
courses.description: The course applies literature from cultural anthropology to practical business topics such as negotiation, the role of deadlines and time orientation, organizational structure, business ethics and corruption, stress management, and business etiquette. Specific countries as well as general principles are discussed, and student groups investigate a culture of their choice and present their findings to the class.

courses.name: European Union and Turkey: Trade and Policies
courses.purpose: This interdisciplinary course introduces students to the European Union and Turkey-EU relations.
courses.description: After a review of EU history and institutional and legal framework, the course emphasizes external and internal trade law and the implementation of the Customs Union Decision between Turkey and the EU. Full membership criteria are also discussed.

courses.name: Entrepreneurial Management
courses.purpose: The course aims to give additional tools and knowledge to graduating students to create their own business, or to acquire share of already running business or to become top executives who shape the future of companies in which they work.
courses.description: Lectures will be augmented by case discussions. Groups of students will do a term project in which an entrepreneurial example will be evaluated in detail. Throughout the semester, prominent businessmen and top executives will be invited to the class to share their experience through active participation of the students.

courses.name: Entrepreneurship and Venture Capital
courses.purpose: This course introduces the methodologies used in entrepreneurial finance and uses the case method to study venture capital deals. It aims to help students understand how venture capital supports start-up financing, innovation, valuation, and organizational restructuring.
courses.description: The course covers entrepreneurial opportunities, early-stage investments by angel investors, early-stage investments by venture capital funds, venture capital exits, and investments in VC funds. It also examines issues related to structuring VC funds and analyzes VC investment opportunities from both qualitative and quantitative perspectives.

courses.name: Human Resources Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with an academic understanding of human resource management (HRM), especially those with little or no prior knowledge of the area. It also seeks to help students understand why HRM is one of the most significant management functions and who performs HRM in organizations.
courses.description: The course explores the development of HRM as an academic field in its historical context, with emphasis on the differences between personnel management and contemporary HRM approaches. It defines and discusses various functions and dimensions of HRM using case studies and real examples from Turkey and other countries. Current issues in HRM are also examined.

courses.name: Real Estate Finance
courses.purpose: This course introduces the central issues in real estate finance and investment, with emphasis on how residential, industrial, and commercial properties are financed and evaluated.
courses.description: This course deals with the central issues in real estate finance and investment. Typical policies and procedures used in financing of residential, industrial, and commercial properties are discussed. Topics include methods of measuring rates of return, feasibility and appraisal processes, risk analysis, equity and debt financing vehicles. Real estate investment trusts and mortgage backed securities are also covered.

courses.name: Discrete Mathematics
courses.description: Sets and propositions: Finite and infinite sets, mathematical induction, propositions. Permutations, combinations, and discrete probability. Relations and functions: binary, equivalence relations, partitions, partial ordering, functions. Graphs: weighted graphs, paths and circuits, shortest paths. Eulerian and Hamiltonian paths. Trees. Abstract algebra: groups, cosets, Lagrange's theorem, Boolean algebra.

courses.name: Finite and Discrete Mathematics
courses.description: Sets and propositions. Mathematical induction. Recurrence relations. Graph theory: trees, Euler paths, planar graphs, graph isomorphism. Enumerative combinatorics: binomial coefficients, pigeonhole principle, relations and incidence matrices, functions, isomorphism of posets, Hasse diagrams, equivalence relations, Stirling numbers of the second kind.
learning_outcomes: Use mathematical induction to prove statements about integers
Solve linear recurrence relations of degree two
Find a minimal spanning tree in a weighted graph
Identify planar graph using Kuratowsky theorem
Find an Euler’s circuit and Euler’s path in a graph when it exists
Construct a linear code and estimate its parameters. Use encoding and decoding procedures for linear codes
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Overview of combinatorial counting principles. Rules of Sum and Product. Permutations and Combinations. (1.1--1.3)
Hafta 2: Binomial Theorem and applications. Combinations with Repetition. Introduction to Catalan Numbers. (1.4-1.5)
Hafta 3: Relations and Functions. (5.1--5.6)
Hafta 4: Enumerative aspects of relations, functions, Stirling Numbers of the Second Kind. (7.1--7.4)
Hafta 5: The Principle of Inclusion and Exclusion (PIE) and derangements.(8.1--8.5)
Hafta 6: Generating Functions and applications. Exponential generating functions. (9.1--9.5). Review and Midterm I.
Hafta 7: Recurrence relations and their applications: The First-Order Linear Recurrence Relation. (10.1--10.3)
Hafta 8: The Second-Order Linear Homogeneous Recurrence Relation with Constant Coefficients, Integer and set partitions. (10.4--10.6)
Hafta 9: Basic concepts of graph theory, subgraphs, and graph isomorphism. Euler Trails/Circuits. (11.1--11.3)
Hafta 10: Planar graphs, Characterization of planar graphs. (11.4)
Hafta 11: Review and Midterm II.
Hafta 12: Hamiltonian paths and cycles. Graph Coloring and Chromatic polynomials. (11.5--11.6)
Hafta 13: Definitions and properties of trees, rooted trees. (12.1--12.2)
Hafta 14: Trees in sorting and weighted trees. (12.3--12.4)
resources: Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics, 5th edition., R. P. Grimaldi, 2004, Pearson

courses.name: Linear Algebra I
courses.description: Solving systems of linear equations. Vector spaces and linear transformations. Bases, matrix representations and coordinate transformations. Rank-Nullity Formula. Cayley-Hamilton Theorem. Eigenspaces and generalized eigenspaces. Determinants. Jordan canonical forms.

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts and methods of linear algebra, which are essential for understanding and solving problems in mathematics, science, and engineering. It develops the theoretical framework and computational tools needed to work with vectors, matrices, and linear transformations.
courses.description: Introduction to matrices. Fields and vector spaces, linear transformations, change of basis. Linear equations, existence and classification of solutions, Gaussian elimination and LU decomposition. Characteristic equation of a matrix: eigenvalues, eigenvectors and the Jordan form. Numerical techniques for computing eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Inner product spaces, quadratic forms.
learning_outcomes: Solve application problems of systems of linear equations
Calculate determinants using row operations, column operations
Use effectively the relationships between the invertibleness, determinant and the rank of a matrix
Find the kernel, rank, range and nullity of a matrix and the associated linear transformation
Calculate eigenvalues, eigenvectors and eigenspaces
Determine if a matrix is diagonalizable, and if it is, diagonalize it
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Systems of linear equations. Matrices (1.1-1.5)
Hafta 2: Echelon form of a matrix. Nonsingular matrices (2.1-2.3)
Hafta 3: Elementary matrices (2.3-2.4)
Hafta 4: Determinants (3.1-3.5)
Hafta 5: Applications
Hafta 6: Vector spaces; subspaces (4.1-4.4)
Hafta 7: Linear independence; basis and dimension (4.5-4.6)
Hafta 8: Coordinates. Homogeneous systems (4.7-4.8)
Hafta 9: Rank of a matrix (4.9). Standard inner product (5.1-5.2)
Hafta 10: Inner product spaces; Gram-Schmidt process (5.3-5.4)
Hafta 11: Orthogonal complement (5.5). Linear transformations (6.1)
Hafta 12: Kernel and range of a matrix (6.2-6.3). Similarity (6.5)
Hafta 13: Diagonalization. Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors (7.1-7.3)
Hafta 14: Applications
resources: Elementary Linear Algebra with Applications, Bernard Kolman and David R. Hill, 2014, 9th ed., new int. ed., Pearson
Lecture Notes - LMS (Moodle, etc)

courses.name: Introduction to Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts and methods of linear algebra, which are essential for further study in mathematics and its applications. It aims to develop students' ability to model and solve problems involving systems of linear equations, matrices, vector spaces, and linear transformations.
courses.description: Introduction to matrices, basic definitions and properties. Linear equations; inverse and rank of a matrix; existence and classification of solutions; Gaussian elimination. Characteristic equation of a matrix; eigenvalues, eigenvectors. Numerical techniques. Applications.
learning_outcomes: Solve systems of linear equations
Calculate determinants by using row reductions and apply to Cramer's Rule
Describe lines and planes in 3-space
Describe linear transformations and find the row space, column space, null space, and calculate rank and nullity
Calculate eigenvalues and eigenspaces
Discuss vector spaces, linear independence, basis and dimension
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Systems of linear equations. Required textbook section 1.1.
Hafta 2: Gaussian and Gauss-Jordan elimination 1.2, Matrices 1.3.
Hafta 3: Elementary matrices, inversion of matrices by row operations, 1.4, 1.5. Existence and uniqueness of solutions 1.6, 1.7.
Hafta 4: Applications of Linear Systems, 1.8. Leontief Economic Models 1.9, 10.8.
Hafta 5: Determinants, their algebraic properties, their evaluation by row reduction and by cofactor expansion 2.1.
Hafta 6: Evaluation of determinants by row reduction, 2.2, 2.3.
Hafta 7: Real vector spaces, 4.1.
Hafta 8: Subspaces, spanning, linear independence, basis, dimension, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4.
Hafta 9: Change of basis, 4.6. Row and column spaces, rank and nullity, 4.6, 4.7.
Hafta 10: Linear transformations, 4.9. Eigenvalues and eigenvectors, 5.1.
Hafta 11: Diagonalization, 5.2.
Hafta 12: Inner product spaces, 6.1, 6.2.
Hafta 13: Gram-Schmidt process, 6.3.
Hafta 14: If time permits, Markov chains 4.12, 10.5. Review.
resources: Elementary Linear Algebra, Bernard Kolman, 2014 / 9th edition, Prentice-Hall
Elementary Linear Algebra, Howard Anton, Anton Rorres, 2015, 11th edition, John Wiley & Sons Inc.
Introduction to Linear Algebra, Gilbert Strang, 1993, Wellesley-Cambridge Press
Differential Equations and Linear Algebra, Stephen W. Goode, 2000, Prentice-Hall

courses.name: Introduction to Probability
courses.description: Basic concepts of probability, conditional probability. Random variables, expectation and variance. Binomial, geometric, negative binomial, hypergeometric, Poisson random variables. Moment generating functions. Continuous random variables: Uniform, Exponential, Gamma, Normal, Beta, Weibull distributions. Bivariate and marginal distributions. Conditional distributions, covariance and correlation coefficient, conditional expectations.
learning_outcomes: Apply knowledge of probability and random variables
Assess proposed solutions to a probability problems
Communicate effectively in writing
Contribute effectively in problem solving dialogue and teamwork
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to probability. Randomness concept, sets. Definition of probability, counting rules.
Hafta 2: Conditional probability, independence.
Hafta 3: Rules of probability.
Hafta 4: Random variables, discrete random variables. Functions of random variables. Expectation and variance.
Hafta 5: Bernoulli, Binomial random variables.
Hafta 6: Geometric, negative binomial, hypergeometric, Poisson random variables.
Hafta 7: Continuous random variables.
Hafta 8: Uniform, exponential distributions.
Hafta 9: Gamma, normal random variables.
Hafta 10: Weibull distribution.
Hafta 11: Bivariate Distributions. Marginal distributions.
Hafta 12: Conditional distributions, Independent random variables.
Hafta 13: Covariance and correlation coefficient.
Hafta 14: Conditional expectations.
resources: Modern Mathematical Statistics with Applications, Jay L. Devore, Kenneth N. Berk, Second Edition, Springer
Probability and statistics for Engineers, Fourth Edition, Richard L. Scheaffer, James T. McClave, Duxbury Press

courses.name: Advanced Calculus II
courses.description: Euclidean spaces, topology of Rn. Differentiability on Rn, differentials, inverse and implicit function theorems. Riemann integral on Rn, Jordan regions, change of variables. Vector calculus, curves and surfaces, Green, Gauss, Stokes theorems.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of multidimensional spaces and topology, able to apply limits and continuity and to communicate mathematics in writing
Apply multidimensional differentiability to particular cases, to test hypotheses, to formulate alternatives, and to communicate mathematics in writing
Apply multidimensional analysis knowledge, demonstrate knowledge of abstract structures, able to communicate mathematics in writing
Test hypotheses and formulate alternatives, in a given short time
Use multidimensional analysis knowledge in particular cases, able to organize study, to plan projects, to review information, and to communicate orally and in writing
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Euclidean Spaces and Norms
Hafta 2: Topology and Geometry of R^n, Inner Products, Linear Maps on Euclidean Spaces
Hafta 3: Continuous Functions, Directional and Partial Derivatives, Total Differentiability
Hafta 4: Chain Rule, Mean Value Property
Hafta 5: Clairaut's Theorem, Taylor's Theorem
Hafta 6: Inverse and Implicit Function Theorems
Hafta 7: Integration
Hafta 8: Integration, continued
Hafta 9: Integration, continued
Hafta 10: Line Integrals
Hafta 11: Vector Calculus
Hafta 12: Green's Theorem
Hafta 13: Stokes and Gauss Theorems
Hafta 14: Applications of Surface Integrals
resources: Lecture Notes: Advanced Calculus, Runde Volker, 2023, Unpublished
Multivariable Calculus with Applications, P. D. Lax and M. S. Terell, 2017, Springer
Real Mathematical Analysis (Chapter 5), Charles C. Pugh, 2015, Springer
The Elements of Real Analysis, Robert G. Bartle, 2nd Edition, Wiley
Introduction to Analysis, W.R. Wade, 4e/2010, Pearson
Mathematical Analysis (Chapters 12,13,14), Tom M. Apostol, 1973, Second edition
Calculus II (Chapters 8, 10, 11, 12), Tom M. Apostol, 1975, John Wiley & Sons
Foundations of Analysis, J.L. Taylor, 2012, Amer. Math. Soc.

courses.name: Statistical Methodology
courses.description: Organization and description of data. Basic concepts of probability. Binomial, Poisson distributions. The normal distribution. X2, t and F distributions. Simple and multiple regression. Analysis of categorical data. Some nonparametric tests. Biological and medical science applications using a statistical software such as MINITAB or SAS.
learning_outcomes: Produce and interpret graphical representations of data
Demonstrate knowledge of the basic concepts of probability and random variables
Infer properties of a population from a sample
Demonstrate the ability to do statistical tets
Demonstrate the ability to do linear regression analysis
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Organization and Description of Data. Counting Rules.
Hafta 2: Probability.
Hafta 3: Conditional Probability and Independence.
Hafta 4: Random variables.
Hafta 5: Continuous Random Variables. The Normal Distribution. The Normal Approximation to the Binomial.
Hafta 6: Sampling Distributions. The Central Limit Theorem.
Hafta 7: Point and Interval Estimation of a Population Mean.
Hafta 8: Testing hypotheses about a population mean. Inferences about a Population Proportion.
Hafta 9: Small-Sample Inferences for Normal Populations. Student's t Distribution.
Hafta 10: Inferences about a Population Mean (small sample size).
Hafta 11: Inferences about the Standard Deviation (The Chi-square distribution).
Hafta 12: Comparing Two Treatments - Independent Random Matched Pair Comparisons.
Hafta 13: Linear Regression. The Method of Least Squares.
Hafta 14: Analysis of Categorical Data.
resources: Statistics: Principles and Methods, Richard A. Johnson & Gouri K. Bhattacharyya, Sixth Edition, Wiley

courses.name: A Concise History of Mathematics
courses.purpose: To introduce the emergence and development of mathematical ideas and techniques over the last few millennia, from early Egyptian papyri to twentieth-century mathematics, and to place mathematics in its philosophical and cultural context.
courses.description: The emergence and development of mathematical ideas and techniques during the last few millennia starting with the early Egyptian papyri and ending with the twentieth century mathematics. Tracing the philosophical and cultural setting of mathematics through some concrete problems which include the unit fractions, perfect numbers, Diophantine equations, ruler and compass constructions, polynomial roots, limits, infinity and some unsolved problems of elementary mathematics.

courses.name: Topology
courses.description: Topological spaces, connected and compact spaces, continuous functions, product spaces, the Tychonoff theorem, separation axioms, separation by continuous functions, complete metric spaces, applications. Fundamental group and covering spaces: homotopy, fundamental group, covering spaces.

courses.name: Statistics for Social Sciences
courses.description: Introduction to statistics with special emphasis on the utilization of statistical methods in social sciences: Organization of data, measures of center and variability. Basic probability concepts. Discrete and continuous random variables and their distributions. Inferences about the mean. Applications using statistical computer programs.
learning_outcomes: Summarize and analyze the data
Make inferences on social sciences
Use mathematical expressions appropriately in statistical analysis
Use McGraw-Hill CONNECT (Online learning and assessment system)
Perform statistical summary and analysis
Use class participation
Recognize and conclude the facts of the statistics
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Chapter 1 -- INTRODUCTION: Types of Statistics, Types of Variables, Levels of Measurement

Hafta 2: Chapter 2 -- DESCRIBING DATA GRAPHICALLY: Constructing Frequency Tables, Graphic Presentation of Qualitative Data, Constructing Frequency Distributions, Graphical Presentation of a Distribution
Hafta 3: Chapter 3 -- DESCRIBING DATA NUMERICALLY: Measures of Location, Measures of Dispersion
Hafta 4: Chapter 4 -- DESCRIBING DATA by DISPLAYING: Measures of Position, Box Plots, Skewness, Describing Relationship between Two Variables, Contingency Tables
Hafta 5: Chapter 5 -- PROBABILITY: Assigning Probabilities, Rules of Addition, Rules of Multiplication: Special, General, Contingency Tables and Tree Diagrams, Bayes’ Theorem, Principles of Counting
Hafta 6: Chapter 5 -- continued
Hafta 7: Chapter 6 -- DISCRETE PROBABILITY DISTRIBUTIONS: Random Variables, Expected Value, Bernoulli trials, Binomial & Hypergeometric distributions
Hafta 8: MIDTERM
Hafta 9: Chapter 7 -- CONTINUOUS PROBABILITY DISTRIBUTIONS: Normal & Standard Normal Distribution

Hafta 10: Chapter 8 -- SAMPLING AND THE CENTRAL LIMIT THEOREM: Sampling Distribution of the Sample Mean, Central Limit Theorem, Standard Error of the Mean, Sampling Distribution of the Sample Mean and Sample Proportion
Hafta 11: Chapter 9 -- CONFIDENCE INTERVALS: Point Estimate, Confidence Intervals, Appropriate Sample Size, Finite-Population Correction Factor
Hafta 12: Chapter 9 -- continued
Hafta 13: Chapter 10 -- ONE-SAMPLE HYPOTHESIS TESTS: Six-Steps, Type I and Type II Error, One- and Two-Tailed, p-Value, Power of the Test
Hafta 14: Chapter 10 -- continued
resources: Statistical Techniques in Business and Economics, LIND/MARCHAL/WATHEN, 19th. Edition, (McGraw-Hill)

courses.name: Orthogonal Functions
courses.description: Linear spaces. Finite-dimensional linear spaces. Infinite-dimensional linear spaces. Inner products. Orthogonal functions and vectors. Orthogonal sequences. Differential operators. Integral operators. Convolution. Fourier series. Sine and cosine series. The Gibbs phenomenon. Local convergence of Fourier series. Divergent series. Generalized functions. Legendre polynomials.

courses.name: Interpolation and Approximation
courses.description: Chebyshev polynominals. Polynominal interpolation. Divided differences. Lebesgue constants. Hermite interpolation and Birkhoff interpolation. Bernstein polynominals. Uniform approximation of functions. Characterization of the best approximation. Uniqueness of the best approximation, Haar systems. Fekete points, Leja sequence and approximation of the Green function. Polynomial inequalities. Some extremal problems. Degree of approximation. Jackson and Bernstein theorems. Interpolation and approximation by splines.

courses.name: Complex Analysis II
courses.description: Evaluation of sums by residues. Argument principle, Rouche theorem, open mapping theorem. Conformal mapping, mapping by elementary functions. Riemann sphere, linear fractional transformations. Schwarz lemma, automorphisms of the disc and the upper half plane. Schwarz-Christoffel transformations. Applications to fluid flow and electrostatics. Harmonic functions, Poisson integral formula. Infinite products, entire and meromorphic functions, gamma and zeta functions.

courses.name: Representation Theory
courses.description: Module theory, semisimple rings and modules, Artin-Wedderburn theorem. Characters, orthonormality properties of character tables. Induction, restriction and inflation of characters. Calculating character tables. Burnsides' solubility theorem, Frobenius' normal complement theorem.
learning_outcomes: Apply ring theory to problems in ordinary character theory
Apply ordinary character theory to examples and concrete problems
Apply ordinary character theory to group theory
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Representations of finite groups
Hafta 2: Group algebras
Hafta 3: Characters
Hafta 4: Inner product and orthogonality relations
Hafta 5: The character table
Hafta 6: Proofs of orthogonality relations
Hafta 7: Techniques for constructing character tables
Hafta 8: The character ring and algebraic integers
Hafta 9: Modules over group algebra and structural results
Hafta 10: Restriction and induction
Hafta 11: Mackey theory and irreducibility criteria
Hafta 12: Burnside's p-alpha-q-beta theorem
Hafta 13: Frobenius normal complement theorem
Hafta 14: Clifford theory
resources: Groups and Representations, J. L. Alperin, R. B. Bell, 1995, Springer

courses.name: Principles of Applied Mathematics
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental ideas of applied mathematics and the main approaches used to build and analyze mathematical models of real-world phenomena.
courses.description: Fundamental ideas of applied mathematics. Modeling approaches, deterministic and stochastic models. Transformation and approximation methods. Dimensional analysis, similarity solutions, perturbation methods and asymptotic expansions. ODE and PDE models, reaction-diffusion equations, pattern formation, wave phenomena. Variational principles. Applications to physical and biological sciences.

courses.name: Introduction to Soliton Theory
courses.description: The Korteweg-de Vries equation, properties of the KdV solutions, integrability of the KdV equation, initial value problem for the KdV equation, inverse scattering theory, the Lax method, multi-soliton solittions, geometrical approach to integrable models, the Toda lattice, Zakharov-shabat formulation.

courses.name: Introduction to Dynamical Systems
courses.description: Systems of linear differential equations, phase portraits. Nonlinear equations. Existence and uniqueness theorems. Flows and limit sets. Equilibrium and periodic solutions. Local stability and the linearization principle. Global stability and the Liapunov method. Planar systems and the Poincaré-Bendixson theorem. Invariant manifolds. Bifurcations. Discrete dynamical systems. Strange attractors and chaos. Numerical methods. Applications in biology, mechanics, and engineering.

courses.name: Differential Geometry II
courses.description: Intrinsic geometry of surfaces. Riemannian geometry. Geodesics. Gauss-Bonnet theorem. Global structure of surfaces.

courses.name: Functional Analysis
courses.description: Normed spaces. Compactness in Banach spaces. Linear operators. Dual spaces, reflexivity. Hilbert spaces. Orthogonal sequences in Hilbert spaces. Operators in Hilbert spaces. Hahn-Banach theorem. Uniform boundedness principle. Open mapping and closed graph theorems. Weak and weak* convergence.

courses.name: Differential Geometry I
courses.description: Euclidean spaces and differential forms, frames, calculus on surfaces. Shape operators. Gaussian and mean curvatures.
learning_outcomes: Obtain intrinsic properties of a curve
Determine an equation of the curve whose curvature and torsion are given
Compute first and second fundamental forms a surface
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Parametrized Curves, Inner Product, Acceleration, Reparametrization (Sections 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4)
Hafta 2: Curvature, Plane Curves (Sections 1.5, 1.6)
Hafta 3: Space Curves, Frenet Frame, Rigid Motions, Curvature Formulas (Sections 1.7, 1.8, 1.9)
Hafta 4: Theorems of Hopf and Jordan, Differential Forms, Total Curvature, Green's Theorem (Sections 2.1, 2.3, 2.4)
Hafta 5: Derivatives of a Function from R^m to R^n, Regular Surfaces, Tangent Planes (Sections 3.1, 3.2, 3.3)
Hafta 6: Surface Area, Orientable Surfaces (Sections 3.4, 3.5, 3.6)
Hafta 7: Isometries, First Fundamental Form, Conformal maps (Sections 3.7, 3.8, 3.9)
Hafta 8: Gauss Map, Shape Operator, Gaussian Curvature, Mean, Curvature, Normal Curvature (Sections 4.1, 4.2, 4.3)
Hafta 9: Geometric Characterization of Gausssian Curvature, Second Fundamental Form (Sections 4.4, 4.5)
Hafta 10: Geodesics, Exponential Map, Gauss's Theorem (Sections 5.1, 5.2, 5.3)
Hafta 11: Complete Surfaces, Parallel Transport, Covariant Derivative (Sections 5.4, 5.5)
Hafta 12: Geodesics in Local Coordinates, Gaussian Curvature Measures Infinitesimal Holonomy (Sections 5.6, 5.7)
Hafta 13: Local/Global Gauss-Bonnet Theorem (Sections 6.1, 6.2)
Hafta 14: Compact Surfaces, Other Global Theorems (Sections 6.3, 6.4)
resources: Required - Textbook: Differential Geometry of Curves and Surfaces, Kristopher Tapp, 2016, Springer
Recommended - Textbook: Elementary Differential Geometry, Barrett O'Neill, 1997, Academic Press
Recommended - Textbook: Differential Geometry of Curves and Surfaces, Manfredo P. do Carmo, 1976, Prentice Hall

courses.name: Mathematical Foundations of Statistical Mechanics
courses.description: System of Statistical Mechanics, Statistical Ensembles. Random fields, Product measures, finite and infinite volume Gibbs Distributions. Markov property. Probabilistic methods: Borel-Cantelly lemma and Kolmogorov type 0-1 laws. Phase transitions. ferromagnetical and antiferromagnetical Ising model. Pierls inequality. Correlation functions and Griffith inequalities. Thermodynamic limit. Transfer matrix. Cluster expansions.

courses.name: Analysis of Differentiable Functions
courses.description: Algebra of smooth functions. Faa di Bruno formula. Critical values. Sard theorem. Real analytic functions. Pringsheim-Boas criterion. Extension of differentiable functions. Whitney functions. Partition of unity. Polynomial interpolation. Lebesgue constants. Approximation by polynomials, smooth and analytic functions. Elements of harmonic analysis.
