Hafta 10: Association Claim Multivariate Correlational Research (Chapter 9)
Hafta 11: Causal Claim: Introduction Simple Experiments (Chapter 10)
Hafta 12: Causal Claims: More on Experiments: Confounding and Obscuring Variables (Chapter 11)
Hafta 13: Causal Claims: Experiments with More Than One Independent Variable (Chapter 12)
Hafta 14: Quasi Experiments and Small-N Designs (Chapter 13) & Replicability, Generalization and the "Real World" (Chapter 14)
resources: Research Methods in Psychology: Evaluating a World of Information, Beth Mohrling, 2020, 4th edition, W. W. Norton & Company

courses.name: Developmental Psychology
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the major theoretical perspectives and research findings in developmental psychology. It helps students understand how human development unfolds from conception through the end of life and what biological, environmental, and social factors shape it.
courses.description: The course covers the development of the mind across the lifespan, with emphasis on major topics such as Piaget's model of cognitive development and its critique, language acquisition, social interaction, attachment, parenting styles, and adolescent development.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate introductory knowledge of major theoretical perspectives in developmental psychology
Demonstrate knowledge of how biological and environmental features (including culture) contribute to child development
Demonstrate knowledge of important research findings in the major sub-fields of developmental psychology and the connection that such findings have with theory
Demonstrate knowledge of how developmental psychologists design experiments and collect data
Demonstrate knowledge of basic statistical methods and scientific tools that developmental psychologists use to conduct research and analyze data
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Welcome & Greetings, Introducing the Course and Syllabus
Hafta 2: The Nature of Child Development (chapter-1)
Hafta 3: Biological Beginnings (chapter-3, Mod-4) & Prenatal Development and Birth (chapter-4)
Hafta 4: Motor, Sensory, and Perceptual Development (chapter-6)
Hafta 5: Historical Foundations of Cognitive Developmental Theories (chapter-7)
Hafta 6: QUIZ-1 and MIDTERM EXAM-1
Hafta 7: Language Development (Chapter -10)
Hafta 8: Emotional Development (chapter -11)
Hafta 9: No classes - Spring Break & Feast of Ramadan holiday
Hafta 10: The Self and Identity (chapter-12)
Hafta 11: QUIZ-2 and MIDTERM EXAM-2
Hafta 12: Gender (chapter-13)
Hafta 13: Moral Development (chapter-14)
Hafta 14: Families (chapter-15) & QUIZ-3
resources: Child Development, Santrock, J., Deater-Deckard, K., & Lansford, J., (2021, 15th edition), McGraw Hill

courses.name: Mirroring Processes in Social Cognition
courses.purpose: This course is designed to introduce the key functions of the mirror neuron system in social cognition. It aims to help students understand how this neural system contributes to action understanding, imitation, empathy, language processing, social interaction, and learning.
courses.description: Since their discovery in monkeys in 1992, mirror neurons and the putative mirror neuron system (MNS) in humans have been the focus of extensive debate in cognitive psychology and neuroscience. The course examines the main neuroanatomy, physiology, and clinical evidence for this neural structure, with emphasis on its proposed roles in social cognition.
learning_outcomes: Understand the main underlying mechanism of social cognition in the brain.
Explain the key functions attributed to the mirror neuron system.
Describe the main neuroanatomical and physiological evidence related to mirror neurons.
Discuss clinical evidence and debates surrounding the mirror neuron system.

courses.name: Perception, Attention, and Action
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the study of perception, with a particular emphasis on visual perception. It aims to show how perception research helps explain broader cognitive capacities by examining the brain’s role in sensory processing.
courses.description: The course covers the physiological structure of vision, modularity, attentional influences, spatial vision, color vision, perceptual learning, categorical perception, and motion perception.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of organizational principles in sensory systems (vision, touch, smell, taste, proprioception)
Demonstrate knowledge of techniques and approaches of measuring sensation and perception
Demonstrate knowledge of important problems and research findings in the field of perception
Demonstrate knowledge of the interactions of sensory and motor systems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Perception
Hafta 2: The beginnings of the perceptual process
Hafta 3: Neural processing; Cortical organization
Hafta 4: Perceiving objects and scenes; Visual attention
Hafta 5: Taking action; Visual motion
Hafta 6: Perceiving color
Hafta 7: Perceiving depth and size
Hafta 8: Midterm exam
Hafta 9: Hearing
Hafta 10: Speech perception
Hafta 11: Cutaneous senses
Hafta 12: Cutaneous senses
Hafta 13: Chemical senses
Hafta 14: Review
resources: Sensation and Perception, Bruce Goldstein, 11th edition, Wadsworth Cengage; ISBN 978-1-133-95849-9
Cognitive Neuroscience: the Biology of the Mind, Gazzaniga et al., 4th edition, 2014, Norton; ISBN 978-0-393-91348-4

courses.name: Cognitive Neuroscience and Rehabilitation
courses.purpose: This course is designed to introduce the main features of the mirror neuron system as a novel approach for training in the rehabilitation of patients with neurological disorders.
courses.description: Mental rehearsal of actions (motor imagery) and action observation treatment have recently gained attention as promising rehabilitation tools for patients with neurological disorders. The neurophysiological basis of these innovative tools is represented by the activation of the mirror neuron system in humans.
learning_outcomes: Understand that the underlying mechanism of these new strategies involves the mirror neurons.
Recognize that dysfunction of this system can lead to problems in social interaction and communication skills.

courses.name: Applied Social Psychology
courses.purpose: This course introduces the application of social psychological theories and findings to real-world social issues and problems. It aims to help students understand how social psychology can be used to analyze and address topics such as immigration, national identity, inter-group relations, and conflict resolution.
courses.description: Social psychology has accumulated a wealth of knowledge as well as theories to predict human behavior in social settings. This knowledge is fast becoming crucial in understanding social problems such as immigration, national identity, inter-group relations, and conflict resolution. The course introduces studies and applications of social psychological findings to social issues and problems.

courses.name: Learning and Memory
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain how learning and memory contribute to cognition and how psychologists study the processes by which information is acquired, stored, retrieved, and used. It also helps students understand major theories and findings in the learning and memory literature.
courses.description: The course covers the basic concepts, theories, and research findings in learning and memory. Topics include classical conditioning, operant conditioning, memory systems, episodic and autobiographical memory, self-regulated learning, constructive and false memory, eyewitness testimony, and forgetting. The course also introduces how psychologists design experiments, collect data, and analyze and interpret results in these areas.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of the important concepts of learning and memory such as classical conditioning, operant conditioning and different memory systems.
Demonstrate knowledge of important theories and theoretical perspectives in learning and memory literature systems.
Demonstrate knowledge of important research findings in the major sub-fields of learning and memory.
Demonstrate knowledge of how psychologists design experiments, collect data, and analyze and interpret results in major sub-fields of learning, remembering and thinking.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to learning, memory, and the course
Hafta 2: Classical conditioning
Hafta 3: Classical conditioning applications
Hafta 4: classical conditioning principles and theories
Hafta 5: operant conditioning
Hafta 6: operant conditioning principles
Hafta 7: Theories of Operant Conditioning
Hafta 8: Exam
Hafta 9: INtroduction to memory and its structure
Hafta 10: Episodic memory and self-regulated learning
Hafta 11: Autobiographical memory
Hafta 12: Long-term memory memory: constructive and false memory
Hafta 13: Eyewitness testimony
Hafta 14: Forgetting
resources: Human Learning and Memory, David A. Lieberman, 2012, Cambridge University Press

courses.name: Advanced Cognitive and Social Development
courses.purpose: This course elaborates on the previous year's developmental psychology course, providing a more in-depth analysis of problems and up-to-date findings in cognitive and social development. The object is to see the relationship between developmental processes and the adult mind.
courses.description: Main topics covered are development of logic and reasoning, memory, spatial cognition, perception, face recognition, prosocial behavior, sociocultural approaches.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of major theoretical perspectives in Cognitive and Social Development: Action-based constructivism, socio-cultural constructivism, Neuroconstructivism

Demonstrate knowledge of important topics and concepts in the major sub-fields of Cognitive and Social Development: Development of symbolic thought, representation of self and others, development of executive functioning, development of emotions and socialization

Demonstrate knowledge of important research findings in the major sub-fields of cognitive and social development: Development of symbolic thought, representation of self and others, development of executive functioning, development of emotions and socialization
Demonstrate knowledge of how developmental psychologists design experiments and collect data
Demonstrate knowledge of basic statistical methods and scientific tools that psychologists use to conduct research and analyze data
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: WELCOME AND COURSE INTRODUCTION
Hafta 2: CONSTRUCTIVIST APPROACHES TO CHILDREN'S THINKING
Hafta 3: SOCIOCULTURAL APPROACHES TO CHILDREN'S THINKING
Hafta 4: Neuro-Constructivism
Hafta 5: Midterm 1
Hafta 6: Executive Functions
Hafta 7: Social Cognition, Theory of Mind
Hafta 8: Imagination
Hafta 9: Children's Lies
Hafta 10: MIDTERM 2
Hafta 11: Attachment
Hafta 12: Prosocial Behavior
Hafta 13: Peer Status
Hafta 14: Discussion

courses.name: Behavioral and Cognitive Neuroscience
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the techniques and findings that have expanded our understanding of brain processes underlying perception, hearing, thinking, speech, and dreaming. It also familiarizes students with recent neuroimaging methods and the research findings obtained through them.
courses.description: This course covers the techniques and findings that have allowed us to know much more about what happens in our brains when we see, hear, think, talk, and even dream. Recent technological advances such as fMRI brain imaging techniques are covered and findings from studies using such techniques are discussed.

courses.name: Individual Differences and Personality
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain why individuals differ from one another in intelligence, aptitude, personality traits, and self-concept. It also introduces the major theoretical perspectives and assessment methods used in the study of personality and individual differences.
courses.description: This course examines individual differences in psychology, focusing on intelligence, aptitude, personality traits, and the development of self-concept. It addresses major questions about the nature-nurture debate and explores how psychologists assess intelligence and personality.
learning_outcomes: Describe what facets of psychology and behavior define “personality,” “individual differences” and their statistical structure
Demonstrate an understanding of personality operationalization, measurement, and assessment.
Define construct validity, including the sub-concepts of face validity, convergent validity, discriminant validity, and external validity
Demonstrate a nuanced understanding of personality’s consistency across context and stability across time
Discuss different models for the origins of personality differences
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course Overview
Hafta 2: What is Personality?
Hafta 3: How is Personality Studied and Assessed?
Hafta 4: Psychoanalytic Aspects of Personality
Hafta 5: Neo-Analytic and Ego Aspects of Personality
Hafta 6: Neo-Analytic and Ego Aspects of Personality (continued)
Hafta 7: Midterm
Hafta 8: Biological Aspects of Personality
Hafta 9: Behaviorist and Learning Aspects of Personality
Hafta 10: Cognitive and Social-Cognitive Aspects of Personality
Hafta 11: Trait Aspects of Personality
Hafta 12: Humanistic, Existential, and Positive Aspects of Personality
Hafta 13: Person–Situation Interactionist Aspects of Personality
Hafta 14: In-Class Presentations of the Group Project
resources: Personality: Classic theories and modern research, Friedman, H. S., & Schustack, M. W., 2014, 5th Edition - New International Edition, Pearson

courses.name: Directed Research in Psychology
courses.purpose: This course provides students with the opportunity to conduct an individual research project under the supervision of a faculty member. It is designed to guide students through the full research process and help them develop independent research skills.
courses.description: This course involves conducting an individual research project under the supervision of a faculty member. All phases of the research enterprise are covered including: a review of the literature, development of hypotheses, developing a methodology to test the hypotheses, conducting the research, analyzing the results and discussing the implications of their work. The course will be graded pass/fail.
learning_outcomes: Develop research plan
Conduct research study
Write up research activity.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course
Hafta 2: Literature Search
Hafta 3: Literature Search
Hafta 4: Determination of specific research question
Hafta 5: HYpothesis Generation
Hafta 6: Methodological design of the study
Hafta 7: Pilot study
Hafta 8: Data collection
Hafta 9: Data collection
Hafta 10: Data coding
Hafta 11: Data analyses
Hafta 12: Write-up of the report introduction
Hafta 13: Write-up of the report method and results
Hafta 14: Write-up of the discussion & APA style

courses.name: Introduction to FMRI
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a basic understanding of the physical and biological concepts underlying fMRI, as well as the principles of data collection, scanner operation, and experimental design.
courses.description: This course covers the basic principles of Functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI), including the physical principles of signal generation in MRI and the relation of neuronal activity with the blood-oxygen-level-dependent (BOLD) signal. The course emphasizes techniques to conduct experiments investigating the functional activity of the nervous system, and statistical analysis of the fMRI data. Weekly hands-on sessions are held using the in-campus MRI scanner.
learning_outcomes: Gain a basic understanding of the physical and biological concepts of fMRI.
Acquire basic knowledge of fMRI data collection and scanner operation through weekly hands-on sessions.
Develop a basic knowledge of fMRI experimental design.

courses.name: Cognitive Aging
courses.purpose: This course is designed to give students an accurate understanding of the behavioral/cognitive, physiological, and biological changes that people experience as they grow older. It also introduces the theories underlying these changes and possible interventions that might prevent or reduce them.
courses.description: Students examine the behavioral/cognitive, physiological, and biological changes associated with aging, along with the theories that explain why these changes occur. The course also considers possible interventions and includes reading and presenting relevant research articles on later-life change.

courses.name: Selected Topics in Cognitive Psychology
courses.purpose: This course brings final year students up to date with a current research area and provides them an opportunity to continue academic study in this or other areas.
courses.description: Members of academic staff cover a research topic that they are working on, presenting an in-depth, advanced understanding of the research problem, data that have been collected by the instructor or colleagues, and a discussion of these results in relation to the bigger questions.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate depth and breadth of knowledge in important concepts in the selected sub-field of cognition
Demonstrate depth and breadth of knowledge of important theories and theoretical perspectives in the selected sub-field of cognition
Demonstrate depth and breadth of knowledge of important research findings in the selected sub-field of cognition
Demonstrate depth and breadth of knowledge of how psychologists design experiments, collect data, and analyse and interpret results in the selected subfield of cognition
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to course, syllabus
Hafta 2: Early language development
Hafta 3: Input (quality and quantity)
Hafta 4: Learning language in atypical circumstances
Hafta 5: Language and the brain
Hafta 6: Working memory
Hafta 7: Learning to read
Hafta 8: Dyslexia
Hafta 9: Reading comprehension
Hafta 10: Developmental coordination disorder and dyspraxia
Hafta 11: ADHD/ADD
Hafta 12: Autism spectrum disorder
Hafta 13: Developmental Language Disorders
Hafta 14: Presentations

courses.name: Selected Topics in Social Psychology
courses.purpose: Members of academic staff cover a research topic that they are working on, presenting an in-depth, advanced understanding of the research problem, data that have been collected by the instructor or colleagues, and a discussion of these results in relation to the bigger questions. This course brings final year students up to date with a current research area and provides them an opportunity to continue academic study in this or other areas.
courses.description: Members of academic staff cover a research topic that they are working on, presenting an in-depth, advanced understanding of the research problem, data that have been collected by the instructor or colleagues, and a discussion of these results in relation to the bigger questions.

courses.name: Selected Topics in Developmental Psychology
courses.purpose: Members of academic staff cover a research topic that they are working on, presenting an in-depth, advanced understanding of the research problem, data that have been collected by the instructor or colleagues, and a discussion of these results in relation to the bigger questions. This course brings final year students up to date with a current research area and provides them an opportunity to continue academic study in this or other areas.

courses.name: Neuropsychology
courses.purpose: This advanced course aims to provide a deeper understanding of the brain and the nervous system in relation to mind and behavior. It also helps students connect neuropsychological findings with broader questions about cognition, behavior, free will, and self-determination.
courses.description: This advanced course is a more in-depth study of the brain and the nervous system in relation to mind and behavior. Specifically, disorders of the brain, lesions resulting from accidents and strokes, and the effects of these on mental capacity and behavior are discussed. The implications of such findings are discussed particularly in relation to philosophical questions such as free-will and self-determination.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of the aims, approaches, limitations and methods in Neuropsychology
Demonstrate knowledge of the anatomy of the brain and the associated cortical vasculature system
Demonstrate skills to identify cognitive deficits in a potential stroke patient
Demonstrate skills to localize a potential area of stroke, given specific cognitive deficits
Demonstrate skills to read original research articles critically
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Neuropsychology, Basic Issues, Neuropsychological Assessment
Hafta 2: Paralysis and other Motor Disorders
Hafta 3: Alzheimer's and Other Dementias, Parkinson's Disease
Hafta 4: Problems of Attention, Traumatic Brain Injury
Hafta 5: Problems of Vision
Hafta 6: Somatosensory Problems
Hafta 7: Amnesias
Hafta 8: Disorders related to the Frontal Lobe
Hafta 9: Aphasias and Language Disorders
Hafta 10: Disconnection Syndromes. Emotion Related Problems.
Hafta 11: Problems related to Social Cognition
Hafta 12: Brain Plasticity and Recovery after Brain Damage 1
Hafta 13: Brain Plasticity and Recovery after Brain Damage 2
Hafta 14: Course Wrapup
resources: Fundamentals of human neuropsychology, Kolb & Whishaw
Fractured Minds: A Case-Study Approach to Clinical Neuropsychology, Jenni A. Ogden

courses.name: Selected Topics in Cognitive Science
courses.purpose: This course introduces selected topics in cognitive science and examines the field as an interdisciplinary study of the mind. It aims to provide students with a conceptual understanding of how psychology, neuroscience, philosophy, computer science, and linguistics contribute to the study of cognition.
courses.description: Cognitive Science is the interdisciplinary study of the mind that draws theoretically and methodologically on several core disciplines: psychology/neuroscience, philosophy, computer science, and linguistics. Despite differences in which disciplines are emphasised, Cognitive Science is unified by its focus on conceptual issues related to the nature of representation, reasoning, learning, and development, especially as they relate to long-standing conceptual issues such as the nativist-empiricist debate.

courses.name: Selected Topics in Cognitive Neuroscience
courses.purpose: Purpose of this course is to enable the students have a good grasp of the most recent advances, and a critical assessment of the literature in the field of cognitive neuroscience. In this course, through weekly reading and writing assignments students will gain essential perspective on the relevant recent studies and important classical studies in literature.
courses.description: Topics will include methods in cognitive neuroscience, models in cognitive neuroscience, perception, memory, learning, and consciousness.

courses.name: Selected Topics in Neurobiological Psychology
courses.purpose: The main goal of this course is to provide students with a biological framework within which some of the "hard problems" or "key issues" of modern cognitive psychology can be discussed in a comparative way. More specifically, this course presents a holistic perspective by bridging the gap between the cognitive processes of humans and non-human organisms with respect to some of the interesting topics in the philosophy of mind.
courses.description: The course draws parallels and, when necessary, elaborates the distinctions between human and non-human minds for each conceptual topic in neurobiological psychology and the philosophy of mind.

courses.name: Psychological Testing and Measurement
courses.purpose: This course provides training in the techniques of psychological assessment, with emphasis on intelligence testing and personality testing. It also addresses the problems involved in measuring human mind and behavior and the methods used to deal with them.
courses.description: This course covers techniques of psychological assessment in areas such as intelligence testing and personality testing. It discusses the problems associated with measuring human mind and behavior and the methods used in dealing with these issues.
learning_outcomes: To master terminology, concepts and theories of testing and assessment
To learn the legal-ethical principles of testing and assessment
To understand the importance of reliability and validity in psychometric assessment
To comprehend how tests are developed
To master the issues pertaining to testing and assessment in different settings (laboratory, clinical, educational)
To master the issues pertaining to testing and assessment of different psychological attributes (e.g., intelligence, personality, clinical/counseling, neuropsychological)
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Issues of psychological testing and assessment (Ch 1)
Hafta 2: Historical, cultural, and legal/ethical considerations
Hafta 3: A statistics refresher - Tests and testing
Hafta 4: Reliability
Hafta 5: Validity & Utility
Hafta 6: Test development (Ch 8)
Hafta 7: Gottman Relationship Check-up
Hafta 8: Intelligence and its measurement (Ch 9)
Hafta 9: WAIS & WISC Tests and Their Subtests
Hafta 10: Personality assessment: An overview (Ch 11)
Hafta 11: Personality assessment methods (Ch 12)
Hafta 12: MMPI, TAT
Hafta 13: Clinical and counselling assessment (Ch 13) Suicide & Risk Assessment
Hafta 14: Neuropsychological assessment (Ch 14) MMSE MoCA
resources: Psychological testing and assessment: An introduction to tests & measurement, R. J. Cohen, M. E. Swerdik, 2022, 10th edition, New York: McGraw Hill

courses.name: Abnormal Psychology
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the study of mental disorders and psychological problems. It aims to build an understanding of how abnormal behavior has been viewed historically and how contemporary approaches have advanced the field.
courses.description: This course covers the study of mental disorders and problems. It includes the history of the understanding of mental health and introduces major advances in the field. The course provides an in-depth understanding of disorders such as schizophrenia, depression, phobias, addiction, and sexual health.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of historical and contemporary viewpoints on abnormal behavior
Demonstrate knowledge of various disorders
Demonstrate knowledge of clinical assessment
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Overview to Understanding Abnormal Behavior (Chapter 1)
Hafta 2: Diagnosis and Treatment (Chapter 2)
Hafta 3: Assessement (Chapter 3)
Hafta 4: Anxiety, Obsessive-Compulsive, Trauma, and Stressor-Related Disorders (Chapter 8)
Hafta 5: Somatic Symptoms and Dissociative Disorders (Chapter 9)
Hafta 6: Review & Midterm
Hafta 7: Mood Disorders and Suicide
Hafta 8: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders (Chapter 6)
Hafta 9: Personality Disorders (Chapter 14)
Hafta 10: Neurodevelopmental and Neurocognitive disorders (Chapter 5)
Hafta 11: Disruptive, Impulsive-Control, and Conduct Disorders (Chapter 10); Substance Related Disorders (Chapter 12)
Hafta 12: Eating Disorders (Chapter 10)
Hafta 13: Paraphilic Disorders, Sexual Dysfunctions, and Gender Dysphoria (Chapter 11)
Hafta 14: Ethical and Legal Issues (Chapter 15)
resources: Abnormal Psychology: Clinical Perspectives on Psychological Disorders, Susan Krauss Whitbourne, 10th Edition, 2024, McGraw Hill Education
Slides
Videos
Exercises

courses.name: Child and Adolescent Psychopathology
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with an overview of psychopathology from childhood through adolescence and to help them understand how psychological disorders are identified and conceptualized across development.
courses.description: This course provides an overview of psychopathology in childhood through adolescence from various perspectives. DSM descriptions, etiology, phenomenology and diagnosis for major disorders are covered. Diagnoses include disruptive behavior disorders, anxiety disorders, affective disorders, attention disorders and psychotic disorders.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of the causes of problems in children
Demonstrate knowledge of psychological problems in children
Demonstrate knowledge of the learning disorders in children
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Orientation and Course Introduction; Understanding Abnormal Development: Theoretical Perspectives
Hafta 2: Risks, Protective Factors and Culturally Diverse Youth
Hafta 3: Standards in Ethical Code Relevant to Children and Youth & Issues in Diagnosis, Assessment and Treatment
Hafta 4: Intellectual Developmental Disorder (IDD) & Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)
Hafta 5: Specific Learning Disabilities (SLD)
Hafta 6: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD)
Hafta 7: Midterm
Hafta 8: Internalizing Problems: Anxiety Disorders and Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD)
Hafta 9: Mood Disorders: Depression & Bipolar Disorders
Hafta 10: Externalizing Problems and Disorders
Hafta 11: Problems with Onset in Later Childhood or Adolescence: Eating Disorders
Hafta 12: In-class Assignment/Essay; Problems with Onset in Later Childhood or Adolescence: Substance Abuse Disorders
Hafta 13: Stress, Trauma-Related Disorders and Child Maltreatment
Hafta 14: Experiential Play Therapy: Foundations and Applications in Child Psychopathology
resources: Abnormal Child and Adolescent Psychology: A Developmental Perspective, Wilmshurst, L., 2017, Taylor & Francis

courses.name: Industrial and Organizational Psychology
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the main advances in industrial and organizational psychology and helps them understand the psychological aspects of working in organizations. It aims to address questions about effective organizations, workplace behavior, job fit, and career development.
courses.description: This course covers the main topics and methods of industrial and organizational psychology, including research methods, job analysis, performance appraisal, assessment methods, selection, training, motivation, job attitudes, positive and counterproductive work behavior, teams, leadership, and occupational psychology.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of the various areas of industrial/organizational psychology.
Describe the methods used by I/O psychologists.
Conduct a job analysis.
Compare major theories in I/O psychology.
Demonstrate how psychology is applied in industry.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction
Hafta 2: Introducing & research methods
Hafta 3: Job Analysis
Hafta 4: Performance Appraisal
Hafta 5: Assessment methods
Hafta 6: Selection (1st midtrem)
Hafta 7: Training
Hafta 8: Motivation
Hafta 9: Job Attitude
Hafta 10: Positive & Counterproductive & Work Behavior (quiz)
Hafta 11: Teams
Hafta 12: Leadership
Hafta 13: occupational psychology (job analysis project submission)
Hafta 14: review
resources: Industrial and Organisational Psychology, Spector, P.S., 5th, John Wiley and Sons
Work in the 21st century: An introduction to Industrial and Organizational Psychology, Landry, F. J. & Conte, J. M., 2013, John Wiley and Sons, Inc: Hoboken, NJ.

courses.name: Clinical Psychology
courses.purpose: This course introduces clinical psychology as one of the major applications of psychology. It aims to explain how clinical psychologists understand, assess, and intervene in mental disorders and related problems, while also connecting these topics to health psychology.
courses.description: The course covers abnormal behavior and mental disorder, the development of clinical psychology, basic features of clinical assessment, interviewing and observation, psychological testing, and major clinical interventions. It also includes psychodynamic, humanistic, behavioral, and cognitive-behavioral psychotherapies, alternative intervention modes, research on clinical intervention, clinical child psychology, health psychology, forensic psychology, and professional issues in clinical psychology.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate knowledge of various types of therapies
Demonstrate knowledge of clinical assessment
Demonstrate knowledge of professional issues in clinical psychology
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: What is Clinical Psychology?
Hafta 2: Clinical Psychology: Past and Present
Hafta 3: Basic Features of Clinical Assessment
Hafta 4: Interviewing and Observation in Clinical Psychology
Hafta 5: Testing in Clinical psychology
Hafta 6: Basic Features of Clinical Interventions (Exam 1)
Hafta 7: Psychodynamic and Humanistic Psychotherapies
Hafta 8: Behavioral and Cognitive Behavioral Psychotherapies
Hafta 9: Alternative Modes of Clinical Intervention
Hafta 10: Research on Clinical Intervention
Hafta 11: Clinical Child Psychology (Exam 2)
Hafta 12: Health Psychology
Hafta 13: Forensic Psychology
Hafta 14: Professional Issues in Clinical Psychology
resources: Introduction to Clinical Psychology: Bridging Science and Practice (10th ed), Bernstein, D. A., Teachman, B. A., Olatunji, B. O., & De Los Reyes, A., 2024, Cambridge: Cambridge University Press.

courses.name: Interpersonal Relationships
courses.purpose: The course aims to familiarize students with cutting-edge theories and research in relationship science and to explain the basic structure, functions, dynamics, and formation of human affectional ties.
courses.description: The course examines interpersonal relationships primarily from a social psychological perspective, drawing on research from other fields of psychology including cognitive, developmental, clinical, and personality psychology as well as social-cognitive neuroscience. It surveys topics central to relationship science such as interpersonal attraction and mate selection, attachment bond formation, social-cognitive processes in interpersonal relationships, jealousy and infidelity, relationship dissolution, loneliness, social rejection, and the effects of social relationships on mental and physical well-being.

courses.name: Visual Neuroscience
courses.purpose: This course explores the visual system as a model for understanding complex brain functions and the methods used to study them. It aims to help students develop a solid understanding of visual processes and critically engage with recent literature on visual perception and its neuronal correlates.
courses.description: This course covers the basics of the visual system in detail through review of recent literature. It examines retinal mechanisms, visual pathways, subcortical and cortical processing, lightness and color, motion perception, object recognition, eye movements, attention, and the architecture and evolution of visual systems across animal species. The course also discusses how visual information is processed to guide behavior and create perceptual experiences.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate a solid knowledge of the visual processes, such as lightness, color, motion perception, and effects of high level cognitive functions, such as attention, on these processes.
Demonstrate critical assessment of the recent literature on visual perception and its neuronal correlates.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course and visual neuroscience: Overview of the visual system.
Hafta 2: Photoreceptors and transduction mechanisms.
Hafta 3: Early visual processing (retina and equivalent structures in invertebrates/insects) - Part 1.
Hafta 4: Early visual processing - Part 2.
Hafta 5: Early visual processing - Part 3.
Hafta 6: Early visual processing - Part 4 and intermediate stages of visual processing.
Hafta 7: Overview of central visual processing in the cortex of mammals and high brain structures in invertebrates/insects.
Hafta 8: Parallel pathways for motion vision, depth perception, color vision and polarized light vision - Part 1.
Hafta 9: Parallel pathways for motion vision, depth perception, color vision and polarized light vision - Part 2.

courses.name: Mind in Evolution
courses.purpose: This course examines the place of evolutionary thinking in contemporary psychology and cognitive science. It aims to introduce students critically to influential evolutionary approaches, the kinds of questions they address, their assumptions, their limitations, and the theoretical contexts in which they developed.
courses.description: What is the place of evolution in contemporary psychology and cognitive science? The course focuses on influential evolutionary approaches in psychology, the questions they are suited to address, their assumptions, their limitations, and the specific theoretical climates in which each emerged. It provides a critical introduction to the continued evolution of evolutionary approaches in psychology.
learning_outcomes: Understanding of the various ways evolution has been used to approach psychological questions
Understanding of the main critiques and limitations of evolutionary approaches in psychology
Understanding of the key assumptions made in evolutionary approaches, what motivates them, and their consequences
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and Course Mechanics
Hafta 2: What Is Evolution? A Brief Introduction
Hafta 3: The Evolution of Perceptual Systems
Hafta 4: Attention and Its Evolutionary Roots
Hafta 5: Learning and Evolutionary Preparedness
Hafta 6: Comparative Approaches to Memory Evolution
Hafta 7: Language
Hafta 8: The Evolution of Concept Formation and Categorization
Hafta 9: Higher Cognitive Abilities in Evolution: Causal Inference and Reasoning
Hafta 10: Evolution of Sociocognitive Skills
Hafta 11: The Evolution of Mate Choice & Mating Systems
Hafta 12: The Evolutionary Roots of Emotions & Moods
Hafta 13: Is Consciousness Everywhere? An Evolutionary Perspective
Hafta 14: Course Review and Wrap-Up
resources: No textbook. Readings consist of select chapters from various sources.
Comparative Cognition, Mary C. Olmstead, Valerie A. Kuhlmeier, 2015, Cambridge University Press
Evolutionary Psychology (5th Edition), Lance Workman, 2025, Cambridge University Press

courses.name: Introduction to Cognitive Psychology for Teachers
courses.purpose: This course is intended to provide an introductory overview of theories and findings in the field of cognitive psychology, with an emphasis on how these topics relate to education.
courses.description: Cognition is a process composed of various phases like transferring sensory information to memory, retention, reasoning, logic, and problem solving. The main topics include perception, attention, memory, problem solving, and reasoning, examined in relation to education.

courses.name: French I
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to develop basic communication skills in French for social and professional use. Students will be exposed to basic communication situations related to everyday communication through which they will learn the basic structures of French grammar and acquire essential notions of French culture.

courses.name: French II
courses.purpose: This course expands on the skills acquired in SFL 101 and emphasizes language skills needed to describe and carry out typical tasks in the workplace.
courses.description: Special attention will be given to terminology and pronunciation.

courses.name: French III
courses.purpose: This course emphasizes language skills needed to interact socially in a professional environment and aims to further develop language awareness and accuracy in language use.
courses.description: Students engage with texts, both written and audio, from different contexts. The course focuses on describing a company, following instructions, writing effectively, and developing the ability to present information related to work settings cohesively and coherently both orally and in writing, as well as expressing ideas and opinions effectively.

courses.name: Theory of Mind
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce and examine the mental competence known as Theory of Mind, which allows humans to represent and predict true and false mental states. It also seeks to situate this ability within broader cognitive and social development.
courses.description: Theory of Mind (or ToM) is the term for the mental competence that enables humans (and possibly other primates) to represent and predict true and false mental states. This is a rapidly growing interdisciplinary area of study. The class will survey and analyze current theories and research in the evolution and development of ToM in relation to other cognitive and social abilities, in both typical and atypical populations.

courses.name: French IV
courses.purpose: This course aims at developing the ability to listen and read complex texts for gist and detail and to apply this knowledge and understanding to speaking and writing.
courses.description: Emphasis will be given to texts related to banking, finance and accounting and case studies will focus on customer relationships and banking services.

courses.name: French V
courses.purpose: This course builds on the skills acquired in SFL 201.
courses.description: Besides professional skills and specific terminology concerning investment opportunities and types of credit, students will also deal with the intercultural aspect of business.

courses.name: Interpersonal Communication in Business Settings
courses.purpose: To introduce theories and practice in verbal and nonverbal communication with a focus on interpersonal relationships, and to improve students' communication competence in business-related exchanges.
courses.description: The course covers theories and practice in verbal and nonverbal communication with an emphasis on interpersonal relationships and business-related communication contexts.

courses.name: English in Business Communication
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop communicative competency in writing short reports, conducting meetings, and delivering presentations through assignments designed to meet real organizational needs.
courses.description: The course focuses on business communication skills, especially writing short reports, conducting meetings, and delivering presentations in professional contexts.

courses.name: English in Organizational Communication
courses.description: Covering a range of communication issues and practices within businesses and other organizations, emphasis is placed on effective communication through oral and written reporting, proposals, negotiations and team presentation.

courses.name: French in Financial Communication I
courses.purpose: This course aims to improve students' ability to communicate effectively in a wide-range of accounting and financial contexts. Realistic case studies related to finance, banking operations, investments and economics will be used to develop language skills.
courses.description: Emphasis will be given to a variety of topic areas such as financial reporting, corporate governance, mergers and acquisitions, insurance, investment banking.

courses.name: French in Financial Communication II
courses.purpose: This course aims to improve students' ability to communicate effectively in a wide-range of accounting and financial contexts.
courses.description: Realistic case studies related to finance, banking operations, investments and economics will be used to develop language skills. Emphasis will be given to a variety of topic areas such as financial reporting, corporate governance, mergers and acquisitions, insurance, investment banking.

courses.name: Written Business Communication
courses.purpose: Develops understanding of communication theories and builds skills in written communication emphasizing style and audience awareness.
courses.description: Practical applications center on external and internal business correspondence. Letters and memos that inform, persuade, grant, and refuse are stressed.

courses.name: Intercultural Business Communication
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' ability to communicate effectively in intercultural business settings. It also helps students analyze cultural differences and produce appropriate business messages for specific audiences.
courses.description: This course focuses on business communication in intercultural environments. Cross-cultural communication theories and a cultural framework will be used to assist in the analysis, evaluation and creation of appropriate business messages addressed to specific audiences in both English and French. Practical applications center on designing a multilingual/multicultural website.

courses.name: Integrated Marketing Communications (IMC)
courses.description: This course introduces students to the areas that comprise IMC: public relations, advertising, direct marketing, sales promotions, events promotions and online communication. Using these communication tools, students learn how to target an audience (in English + French), assess communication options and formulate IMC plans for organizations.

courses.name: French in Corporate Communication
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop communications competency in writing short reports, conducting meetings and delivering presentations through assignments designed to meet real organizations.

courses.name: Acting Techniques II-Singing
learning_outcomes: Aquire and develop the basic acting skills
Construct a method and approach to rehearse and perform simple realistic scenes
Ascertain the overall structure and events of dramatic action

Hafta 2: Acting exercises
Hafta 3: Acting exercises
Hafta 4: Acting exercises
Hafta 5: Improving acting skılls through a text.

resources: MARIA CALLAS

courses.name: Art of Pastry
courses.purpose: This course will prepare students for careers or post-secondary programs related to the baking and pastry culinary business and industry.
courses.description: The student will apply the knowledge and skills of how basic ingredients function, baking/pastry vocabulary, and mixing techniques to produce baking/pastry products based on industry standards. Students will develop skills in basic bread and pastry techniques to produce breads, muffins, biscuits, pies, cakes, pastries, and specialized desserts. Attention to detail and artistic flair are key skills that begin to develop during this class.

courses.name: Food and Beverage Culture
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the knowledge and practical skills needed for effective management of food and beverage services. It also prepares students for careers or further study related to food and beverage culture and service.
courses.description: The course covers the basic knowledge required for food and beverage service management. It is conducted in two sessions: theoretical preparation before service and practical experience in a fine dining room bar area. Students learn how basic ingredients function, how technologies are used, and how to complete service with proper ritual and mixology skills.
learning_outcomes: Apply knowledge and skills related to the management of food and beverage services.
Understand the function of basic ingredients and technologies used in food and beverage service.
Develop practical mixology skills.
Complete service with appropriate ritual and professional standards.

courses.name: Organizational Communication in French
courses.purpose: To develop effective communication skills in French for use in business and organizational settings, with emphasis on oral and written reporting, proposals, negotiations, and team presentations.
courses.description: Covers a range of communication issues and practices within business and other organizations, emphasizing effective communication in French through oral and written reporting, proposals, negotiations, and team presentations.

courses.name: Digital Marketing
courses.purpose: This course provides a solid foundation in the key concepts of digital marketing and explains how digital technologies have transformed marketing and communication with customers and the public.
courses.description: The course covers strategy formulation for digital marketing, relationship marketing using digital platforms, campaign planning for digital media, marketing communications through digital channels, and the evaluation and improvement of digital channel performance. Case studies are used to introduce best-practice frameworks for developing a digital marketing strategy and to examine success factors for techniques such as conversion rate optimization and social media communications including Instagram and Facebook.

courses.name: Voice and Speech I
courses.purpose: This course is devoted to the examination and practice of the basic principles of voice and speech, with emphasis on breathing, resonance, articulation, diaphragm support, pitch, rhythm, expressiveness, placement, and diction.
courses.description: The course covers the basic principles and practical application of voice and speech techniques, including breathing, resonance, articulation, diaphragm support, pitch, rhythm, expressiveness, placement, and diction.

courses.name: Voice and Speech II
courses.purpose: The course aims to help students integrate voice, breath, and text. It concentrates on the formation of the individual sounds of spoken Turkish and the development of ability to speak clearly, expressively and without impediments.
courses.description: The course focuses on voice and speech training for performance, with emphasis on spoken Turkish sounds, vocal clarity, articulation, breath control, and expressive delivery. Students work on practical exercises and performance-based application.
learning_outcomes: Aquire practical knowledge of voice, speech and performance related theories
Demonstrate vocal projection, articulation and clear speech for the stage
Demonstrate proficency in clear voice and speech through performance of poems and verse text

courses.name: Movement and Combat II
courses.purpose: The course continues the fundamentals of theatrical stage movement for actors begun in THR 105. It aims to advance students' knowledge and mastery of these exercises and to help them apply the principles to individual and ensemble movement-improvisation.
courses.description: The course continues the fundamentals of theatrical stage movement for actors begun in THR 105. Students further develop and master movement exercises and apply these principles in individual and ensemble movement-improvisation.
learning_outcomes: Develop greater mind and body coordination by advancement of balance and timing
Demonstrate physical exercises of fencing, attacks and counter-attacks
Implement physical movements within a composition and acting scene

courses.name: Voice and Speech III
courses.purpose: The course is a continuation of the first-year work, which now becomes integrated into the rehearsal of the second-year acting studio.
courses.description: Development of the student's vocal instrument as an integrated organic function of self and character.

courses.name: Movement and Combat III
courses.purpose: Building upon the techniques learned in THR 105-106, this course develops students' movement and combat skills through compositional exercises based on Mary Overlie's Viewpoints.
courses.description: Students combine the techniques learned in THR 105-106 into compositional exercises based on Mary Overlie's Viewpoints.

courses.name: Voice and Speech IV
courses.purpose: This course continues the first-year work and integrates it into the rehearsal process of the second-year acting studio. It aims to develop the student's vocal instrument as an integrated and organic function of self and character.
courses.description: The course is a continuation of the first-year work, which now becomes integrated into the rehearsal of the second-year acting studio with Shakespeare and Early Modern texts. Development of the student's vocal instrument as an integrated organic function of self and character.
learning_outcomes: Aquire practical knowledge of voice, speech and performance related theories
Demonstrate vocal projection, articulation and clear speech for the stage
Demonstrates proficency in clear voice and speech through performance in shakespearean text

courses.name: Movement and Combat I
courses.purpose: This course develops the fundamental theatrical stage movement skills needed by actors. It focuses on building physical control and readiness through movement training rooted in Allan Wayne Work and developmental movement patterns.
courses.description: The course is based on the fundamentals of theatrical stage movement for actors, rooted in the techniques of Allan Wayne Work and developmental movement patterns. Students learn exercises to increase strength, flexibility, coordination, and stamina.

courses.name: History of Theater I: Origins to Renaissance
courses.description: Through a combination of lecture and discussion the course is an exploration of the history of world theatre from the Greeks to Shakespeare. Students learn about staging practices, the cultural use of theatrical space, the changing status of theatre within various cultures, and the role of the audience, the playwright, and the actor in theatrical performance.

courses.name: Movement and Combat IV
courses.purpose: This course builds on the techniques learned in THR 205 and develops students' ability to use imagination, character, and story within non-verbal physical theater.
courses.description: Students work on non-verbal physical theater through movement and combat training, with an emphasis on devising, rehearsing, and presenting a physical theatre project. The course includes duel fencing and hand-to-hand combat scenes, dramatic action, and performance preparation.
learning_outcomes: Aquire proficiency in duel fencing and hand-to-hand combat scenes from Shakespeare
Aquire proficency in duel fencing scenes combined with dramatic action
Develop and perform duel fencing scenes combined with dramatic acting for an invited audience
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review syllabus

courses.name: History of Theater III: 20th Century
courses.purpose: This course explores the major plays and playwrights of the 20th century in order to examine how theatre relates to the actor, the audience, and the broader culture. It aims to clarify what theatre means and why it remains important in the modern world.
courses.description: Focusing primarily on the most significant plays and playwrights of the 20th century, the course examines the relationship between actor and audience and between theatre and the larger culture.

courses.name: Textual Interpretation and Analysis I
courses.purpose: To develop students' ability to closely read dramatic texts and source material and to understand the principles of dramaturgical practice. The course also introduces the historical and theoretical foundations of dramaturgy from Ancient Greece to the Renaissance.
courses.description: A close study of dramatic texts and source material, with emphasis on dramaturgical praxis, including an overview of the history/theory of dramaturgy from Ancient Greece to Renaissance.

courses.name: History of Theater II: Renaissance to 20th Century
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with an understanding of the development of Western theatre from the 17th century to the 20th century. It emphasizes major theatrical ideas, dramatic forms, theatre architecture, the actor-audience relationship, and productions from these historical periods.
courses.description: A study of the development of Western theatre from the 17th century to the 20th century. The course focuses on the idea of theatre, the development of dramatic forms, the evolution of theatre architecture, the relationship between actor and audience, and productions of these historical periods in Western theatre.
learning_outcomes: Students will demonstrate knowledge of theatrical history and literature from renaissance to 20th century
Explain the origin and development of dramatic texts as the basis of theatrical performance
Explain different periods, genres, and styles of drama from the Renaissance to the 20th century
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review Syllabus
Hafta 2: English Theatre and Drama, 1576-1642: Theatre in Elizabethan Era
Hafta 3: French Theatre, 1630-1700: Neo-Classicism. Moliere. Racine. Corneille.
Hafta 4: Neo-Classicism Presentations
Hafta 5: The Enlightenment; German Theatre and Drama; Romanticism and Melodrama, 1800-1880.

courses.name: Textual Interpretation and Analysis II
courses.purpose: The course aims to make analysis of dramatic texts, with special emphasis on play structure, plot, character, dialogue, ideas, and various other elements essential to effective theatrical interpretation and realization from Renaissance to Realism.
courses.description: The course focuses on the analysis of dramatic texts, emphasizing play structure, plot, character, dialogue, ideas, and other elements essential to theatrical interpretation and realization from Renaissance to Realism.
learning_outcomes: Students will demonstrate knowledge of textual interpretation and analysis from renaissance to 20th century
Students will analyze, appreciate, and interpret significant works from renaissance to 20th century
Students will review, analyze and interpret information, and independently generate conclusions

courses.name: Voice and Speech V
courses.purpose: The course focuses on building power and range by developing breath muscles of the ribs and diaphragm, limbering the resonators of the chest, mouth, teeth, sinus, nasal, and skull, and working on the entire range of the voice. It aims to help students work through spoken text through their own creative writing, sonnets, and monologues and dialogues, which are rehearsed and performed.
courses.description: The course develops voice production and speech skills through breath control, resonance, and vocal range exercises. Students work with spoken text, including creative writing, sonnets, monologues, and dialogues, and rehearse and perform these materials.

courses.name: On Camera Acting
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' fundamental skills in acting for the camera and help them transition from theatre performance to screen performance. It also prepares students to respond to the technical and expressive demands of film and television acting.
courses.description: This course offers instruction and practice in the basics of acting for the camera. It introduces on-camera performance in various genres and addresses technical requirements of TV and film acting such as playing to the camera, shooting out of sequence, blocking, and other production considerations. The course includes on-camera scene-work, character development, script analysis, live tapings of selected materials, and exercises designed to expand emotional, intellectual, physical, and vocal expressiveness for the camera.
learning_outcomes: Aquire the fundamental skills of acting for the camera
Reconcile the acting and technical requirements used in film and tv acting
Aquire the skills to interpret and prepare scenes for on camera acting
Collaborate with director, director of photography and other film and tv crew members

courses.name: Theatre Project
courses.purpose: Working with a director and being involved in the whole process to constitute a play or scene project.
courses.description: Working with a director and being involved in the whole process to constitute a play or scene project.

courses.name: Alternative Theatre Lab
courses.purpose: To explore several alternative styles and forms of postmodern theatre and develop practical experience through studio work.
courses.description: Exploration of several alternative styles and forms of postmodern theatre. Culmination with a theatre presentation for the Bilkent Community.

courses.name: Textual Interpretation and Analysis III
courses.purpose: This course aims to define different roles and different tools for analyzing scripts for dramatic production, and to help students choose among a range of analytical methods.
courses.description: A survey of diverse ways of analyzing scripts for dramatic production. The course is based on theatrical interpretation and realization from Realism to Avant-garde, and explores critical methods based on psychoanalysis, cognitive science, Marxism, the various feminisms, and historicism.

courses.name: Playwriting
courses.purpose: The primary goal of this course is to study the art and craft of playwriting. Students learn how to develop ideas into dramatic form, create conflict, build characters, and shape narrative structure. The course also encourages students to find their own unique voice while writing a self-contained short play.
courses.description: This course covers the process of writing a play from the initial idea stage through premise, synopsis, character and location development, structure, dialogue, and formatting. Students workshop their ideas and drafts, give feedback to peers, and revise their work through successive writing assignments until they complete a 10 to 15 minute play.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the community; review the syllabus; appreciating short plays and films as a storyteller; conflict as the essential ingredient of drama; playwriting versus screenwriting; theatre versus poetry and music; collaboration between your many selves; passion; the importance of endings: personal vision and theme.

Hafta 2: View short films and plays; discussion of short play ideas; choosing which idea to pursue; short play or full length; screenplay or play; or a short story; the process of storytelling from idea to premise to synopsis to play.
Hafta 3: Discussion of the reading and writing assignments; looking at character and point of view; workshop premises.
Hafta 4: Discussion of the reading and writing assignments; looking at structure; on endings: the key elements of theme; cultivating personal vision; workshop synopses.
Hafta 5: Discussion of the reading and writing assignments; key elements of scenework and dialogue; key elements of play form and format; workshop characters and locations.
Hafta 6: Discussion of the reading and writing assignments; workshop synopsis.
Hafta 7: Discussion of the reading; workshop first five pages of plays.
Hafta 8: Discussion of the reading; workshop first five pages of plays.
Hafta 9: Workshop plays.
Hafta 10: Discussion of the reading; workshop plays.
Hafta 11: Exercise on pitching your play.
Hafta 12: Guest speaker; workshop with the guest speaker.
Hafta 13: Workshop; finish revising short plays; final form of the play is due next week; work on the pitch.
Hafta 14: Pitching your short play.

courses.name: Textual Interpretation and Analysis IV
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce and compare diverse methods of analyzing scripts for dramatic production. It helps students understand different interpretive approaches and select appropriate analytical tools for theatrical realization.
courses.description: A survey of diverse ways of analyzing scripts for dramatic production. The course is based on theatrical interpretation and realization from Realism to Avant-garde. It explores critical methods based on psychoanalysis, cognitive science, Marxism, various feminisms, and historicism.

courses.name: Senior Design Project I
courses.purpose: Capstone design project. Technical and innovative group project emphasizing engineering design principles on a specific topic in any field of computer science and engineering.
courses.description: Documentation on the specifications, analysis, and requirements of the project. Presentation and demo.

courses.name: Senior Design Project II
courses.purpose: Continuation of the capstone design project started in CS 491, enabling students to complete a technical and innovative group project on a specific topic in computer science and engineering. The course emphasizes engineering design principles, teamwork, documentation, and presentation of the completed project.
courses.description: Technical and innovative group project emphasizing engineering design principles on a specific topic in any field of computer science and engineering. Students work with the same team from CS 491 to complete the detailed design and implementation of the project, and prepare documentation, a presentation, and a demo.
learning_outcomes: Apply engineering design process to produce solutions that meet specified needs
Communicate effectively in writing
Perform effective oral communication
Prepare a user manual for non-technical users
Recognize ethical and professional responsibilities in engineering situations
Make informed judgments in engineering situations that consider the impact of engineering solutions in global, economic, environmental, and societal contexts
Contribute and function effectively on a team
Help creating a collaborative and inclusive team-work environment
Take lead role and shares leadership on a team
Work with team mates to establish goals and plan tasks
Acquire new knowledge using appropriate learning strategies
Apply new knowledge as needed
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Submit Detail Design Report
Hafta 2: Submit Final Report
Hafta 3: Oral presentation

courses.name: History of Turkish Theater
courses.description: This module focuses on the changes and evolution of Turkish Theatre from 1839 to the present and Traditional Turkish Theatre.
learning_outcomes: Students will demonstrate knowledge of the history of the Turkish theatre
Explain different periods, genres, and styles of drama from the Turkish theatre
Explain the origin and development of dramatic texts as the basis of theatrical performance in Turkey
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review syllabus
Hafta 2: Texts reviews, readings, and discussion.
Hafta 3: Texts reviews, readings, and discussion.

Hafta 7: Midterm- Short Paper. History of Turkish Theatre 1839- 1900
Hafta 8: Texts reviews, readings, and discussion.
Hafta 9: Texts reviews, readings, and discussion.

Hafta 11: Texts reviews, readings, and discussion.
