an ability to develop and conduct appropriate experimentation, analyze and interpret data, and use engineering judgment to draw conclusions
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Statistical learning and its applications
Hafta 2: Linear regression and its linear algebra fundamentals
Hafta 3: Extensions of Linear Models in Linear Regression
Hafta 4: Classification and use of Linear Models
Hafta 5: Resampling Techniques and Model Selection
Hafta 6: Compressive Sensing and its applications
Hafta 7: Principal Component Analysis
Hafta 8: Nonlinear Regression
Hafta 9: Tree Based Methods for Classification
Hafta 10: Support Vector Machine for Binary Classification and its Applications
Hafta 11: Clustering Techniques and Applications
Hafta 12: Learning From Data: Deep Neural Netwotrks and Convolutional Neural Nets
Hafta 13: Learning From Data: Backpropagation and the Chain Rule
Hafta 14: Learning From Data: The World of Machine Learning
resources: Linear Algebra and Learning from Data, Gilbert Strang, 2019, Wellesley-Cambridge Press. ISBN: 9780692196380
An Introduction to Statistical Learning with Applications in Python, Gareth James, Daniela Witten, Trevor Hastie, Robert Tibshirani, Jonathan Taylor, 2023

courses.name: Deep Generative Networks
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the principles and methods used in deep generative modeling for images and videos. It focuses on how modern neural network architectures can be used to generate, transform, and reconstruct visual data.
courses.description: Image generation and manipulation algorithms with deep learning. Convolutional neural networks, generative adversarial networks. (multi-modal) image to image translation. Image inpainting. Texture synthesis. Unsupervised feature learning via image reconstruction. 3D image learning. Video generation.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Deep Generative Networks
Hafta 2: Convolutional Neural Network Architectures
Hafta 3: Generative Adversarial Networks
Hafta 4: Discovering Interpretable Latent Codes
Hafta 5: Transformers, Attention Models
Hafta 6: Image to Image Translation
Hafta 7: Multi-modal Image to Image Translation
Hafta 8: Presentations
Hafta 9: Image Inpainting
Hafta 10: Texture Synthesis
Hafta 11: Unsupervised Feature Learning via Image Reconstruction
Hafta 12: Self-supervised 3D Image Learning
Hafta 13: Video Generation
Hafta 14: Project Presentations

courses.name: Hardware Acceleration for Machine Learning
courses.purpose: This course introduces the hardware and software foundations needed to accelerate machine learning workloads efficiently. It aims to help students understand how different computing platforms and design choices affect performance, power, and implementation trade-offs for deep neural networks.
courses.description: A brief review of DNNs and different architectures, software and hardware platforms for neural networks (DNNs), accelerator taxonomy: CPU, GPU, FPGA, ASIC, comparison of hardware platforms, low-power hardware design, custom logic hardware implementation tools, DNN acceleration/optimization techniques on FPGAs/ASICs, systolic arrays, TPU, dataflow types, co-design of DNN hardware and algorithms. Case studies. Review of recent architecture papers.

courses.name: Wireless Networking Technologies and Applications
courses.description: Wireless networking fundamentals. Wireless channel characteristics. Wireless LANs. Medium access control in WLANs. Bluetooth and WPANs. WiFi/Bluetooth coexistence. Ad hoc networks. MANET routing algorithms. Mobile IP, DHCP, NAT. Wireless sensor networks. Real-time OS for wireless sensor applications. Network programming for mobile applications. Energy-efficient routing and scheduling in sensor networks. TCP performance in wireless networks.

courses.name: Digital Communications
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a fundamental understanding of digital communication systems and the principles behind transmitting information over noisy channels. It also prepares students to analyze system performance and understand the limits and techniques of reliable communication.
courses.description: Measure of information, entropy, source coding, rate distortion. Sampling theorem, waveform coding. AM, FM signals. Signal representation. Noise processes, white noise and Gaussian processes. Digital modulation/demodulation, receiver design, matched filter. Performance analysis of digital modulation schemes. Channel coding, linear block codes, convolutional codes. Noise figure, link budget analysis.
learning_outcomes: Develop understanding of measure of information and concept of entropy; learn about lossless and lossy data compression algorithms and their implementation
Use concept from random processes to model noise in communication systems; apply basic mathematical principles to quantify its effects
Develop fundamental understanding of how digital information can be transmitted over noisy channels; analyze communication systems
Quantify the fundamental limits of communication over noisy channels and learn about different channel coding techniques to approach these limits
Conduct computer simulations to study different aspects of digital communications and to implement different algorithms; Report on the results
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Communications overview
Hafta 2: Digital Representation of Sources
Hafta 3: Digital Representation of Sources
Hafta 4: Digital Representation of Sources
Hafta 5: Analog Communications
Hafta 6: Random Processes
Hafta 7: Random Processes
Hafta 8: Random Processes
Hafta 9: Linear Modulation
Hafta 10: Linear Modulation
Hafta 11: Linear Modulation
Hafta 12: Linear Modulation
Hafta 13: Capacity and Reliable Transmission
Hafta 14: Capacity and Reliable Transmission
resources: Fundamentals of Digital Communication Systems, T. M. Duman, 2025, Cambridge University Press

courses.name: Reinforcement Learning and Dynamic Programming
courses.description: Markov chains. Markov decision processes. Dynamic programming: policy iteration, value iteration. Model-free reinforcement learning: Monte Carlo, Temporal Difference, Q-learning. Policy gradient methods. Model-based reinforcement learning: classical multi-armed bandits, stochastic multi-armed bandits, adversarial multi-armed bandits.
learning_outcomes: Ability to formulate a given engineering problem involving sequential decision-making within the dynamic programming framework
Ability to formulate a given engineering problem involving sequential decision-making under uncertainty and limited-model knowledge within the reinforcement learning framework
Ability to choose the right dynamic programming or reinforcement learning methods to solve the sequential decision-making problem formulated
Ability to implement dynamic programming and reinforcement learning methods, such as Q-learning and policy gradient, on modern software environments or programming languages
Demonstrate engineering skills by successfully implementing a complete project
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and Probability Review
Hafta 2: Markov Chains
Hafta 3: Markov Decision Processes
Hafta 4: Bellman Equation
Hafta 5: Bellman Optimality Equation
Hafta 6: Value Iteration & Policy Iteration
Hafta 7: Monte Carlo Methods
Hafta 8: Multi-armed Bandits
Hafta 9: Stochastic Approximation
Hafta 10: Temporal Difference Methods
Hafta 11: Value Function Methods & Function Approximation
Hafta 12: Policy Gradient Methods
Hafta 13: Actor-Critic Methods
Hafta 14: Project Demos
resources: Required - Textbook: Mathematical Foundation of Reinforcement Learning, Shiyu Zhao, 2025, Springer
Recommended - Textbook: Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction, Sutton and Barto, 2020/2nd Edition, MIT Press

courses.name: Introduction to Robotics
courses.description: Robot arm kinematics (forward and inverse kinematics); robot arm dynamics (equations of motion, equivalent formulations); planning of manipulator trajectories; range sensing (time-of-flight and triangulation systems, nown target size, optical flow), proximity sensing (optical, magnetic, capacitive, inductive, ultrasonic), tactile (touch) sensing, force and torque sensing, dead reckoning (odometry and inertial sensing); mobile robots (localization, mapping, path planning, navigation, obstacle avoidance, object classification); multi-sensor data fusion.
learning_outcomes: understanding rigid body transformations, properties of matrix transformations, basic translational, rotational, and scaling transformations, formulating homogeneous transformation matrices and composite transformations.

understanding Denavit-Hartenberg (D-H) representation and identifying D-H parameters; deriving the forward kinematics equations of a robot arm; being able to design more complicated kinematic chains based on simple and basic modules such as the 3-D wrist and the planar arm.
determining the existence and the number of inverse kinematics solutions for a robot arm; finding inverse kinematics solutions of manipulators analytically and geometrically.
deriving equations of motion (dynamics equations) of manipulators using two equivalent approaches (Lagrange-Euler and Newton-Euler methods).
mastering different approaches to trajectory planning in Cartesian and joint spaces.
learning the characteristics and principles of operation of different sensing modalities for robots and applications of sensing in the field of robotics.
acquiring working knowledge of numerical tools such as MATLAB Robotics Toolbox.
using numerical tools to formulate and solve robotics problems with computers.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: nomenclature, robot manipulators, kinematic chain, links, joints, actuators, degrees of freedom, overview of forward and inverse kinematics
Hafta 2: rigid body transformations, properties of matrix transformations, translational, rotational, and scaling transformations, composite transformations
Hafta 3: center of rotation, Euler angles, homogeneous coordinates and transformations, composite homogeneous transformation matrix
Hafta 4: Denavit-Hartenberg representation (links, joints, and their parameters), arm matrix
Hafta 5: kinematic chain examples, forward kinematics equations
Hafta 6: inverse manipulator kinematics: methods of solution (numerical, closed-form)
Hafta 7: examples to algebraic and geometric solutions, workspace (dexterous, reachable), 6-DOF manipulator solution
Hafta 8: velocity kinematics and the Jacobian, derivation of the Jacobian, linear and angular velocity Jacobians, singularities
Hafta 9: Midterm Exam
Hafta 10: robot arm dynamics, forward and inverse dynamics, equivalent formulations, Lagrange-Euler formulation, equations of motion
Hafta 11: Newton-Euler and Generalized d'Alambert formulations to robot arm dynamics, forward and backward recursive equations
Hafta 12: planning of manipulator trajectories in joint space and Cartesian space, knot points, cubic spline trajectories, trajectories with polynomial segments, bounded deviations method

Hafta 13: introduction to sensing, classification of sensors, time-of-flight range sensors (ultrasonic, laser-based ranging), triangulation systems, proximity sensing (inductive, capacitive, magnetic, ultrasonic, optical)
Hafta 14: dead-reckoning systems (odometry, potentiometers, optical encoders, inertial sensing: gyroscopes, accelerometers, tilt sensors), multi-sensor data fusion
resources: Robot Modeling and Control, M.W. Spong, S. Hutchinson, and S. Vidyasagar, 2006, John Wiley
Introduction to Robotics, Analysis, Sytems, Applications, S. B. Niku, 2001, Pearson Education
Robotics: Control, Sensing, Vision, and Intelligence, K.S. Fu, R.C. Gonzalez, C.S.Lee, 1987, McGraw-Hill

courses.name: Statistical Learning and Data Analytics
courses.purpose: This course introduces the goals and tools of machine learning and data analytics. It aims to build a foundation for understanding how statistical learning methods are used to analyze data and solve engineering problems.
courses.description: Introduction to the goals and tools of machine learning and data analytics. Overview of machine learning on diverse data acquired by sensor networks, physiological devices, and similar sources. Fundamental learning models and applications such as decision support, computer vision, and recommender systems. Performance analysis using probabilistic approaches. Bayesian and frequentist machine learning. Classification and regression, including linear regression, ridge regression, and lasso. Parameter estimation and Bayesian regression. Generalized linear models, neural networks, learning from unlabeled data, probabilistic clustering, blind signal separation, feature extraction, graphical models, techniques for handling missing and corrupted data, deep learning, transfer learning, and online learning.
learning_outcomes: Ability to apply probability and linear algebra knowledge to analyze the performance of statistical learning algorithms.
Ability to train and test machine learning algorithms on a given dataset.
Ability to implement machine learning algorithms on modern software environments or programming languages.
Demonstrate engineering skills by successfully implementing a complete project.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Probability review for machine learning and data science (random variables, random vectors, Bayes rule, independence, law of large numbers, concentration inequalities)
Hafta 2: Bayesian and frequentist machine learning (credible intervals, confidence intervals) and their use in data science
Hafta 3: Linear regression, ordinary least squares
Hafta 4: Ridge regression, lasso, Bayesian linear regression
Hafta 5: Generalized linear models
Hafta 6: Perceptron, neural networks and backpropagation
Hafta 7: Blind signal separation
Hafta 8: Probabilistic clustering, mixture of Gaussians and expectation maximization
Hafta 9: Feature extraction and feature selection
Hafta 10: Inference and learning in graphical models
Hafta 11: Naive Bayes, Restricted Boltzmann Machines, Contrastive Divergence
Hafta 12: Deep learning
Hafta 13: Reinforcement learning
Hafta 14: Online learning
resources: The elements of statistical learning, T. Hastie, T. Tibshirani, J. Friedman, 2003, Springer
An introduction to statistical learning, James G, Witten D, Hastie T, Tibshirani R., 2013, Springer
Machine learning: A probabilistic perspective, Murphy, Kevin P, 2012, MIT Press
Probabilistic graphical models: principles and techniques, Koller, Daphne, and Nir Friedman, 2009, MIT press
Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning, Christopher M. Bishop, 2011, Springer

courses.name: Neural Networks
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts and methods of neural networks, from biological inspiration and neuron models to modern deep learning architectures. It aims to build the mathematical and conceptual foundation needed to understand how neural networks learn, generalize, and are applied in engineering problems.
courses.description: Biological neural networks. Artificial neural networks. Basic neuron. Modeling the single neuron. Feed-forward architectures. Recurrent neural networks. Restricted boltzmann machines. Learning algorithms. Back propagation. Contrastive divergence. Optimization and generalization for neural networks. Autoencoders. Deep belief networks. Convolutional neural networks. Long short-term memory networks. Self-attention and transformers. Adversarial networks.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic neuron structure, related mathematical neuron models and basic neural network models.
Understand the basics of learning in neural systems, formulate various learning rules and investigate their properties.
Understand the relation between dynamical systems and some neural network structures, utilize feedback control systems ideas to investigate their properties.
Apply the neural network structures in solving some engineering problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Primer on Linear Algebra, Probability and Optimization for Machine Learning / Introduction to Neural Networks (NNs)
Hafta 2: Neurons: Biophysical and Mathematical Models
Hafta 3: Neural Network Structures: Perceptrons
Hafta 4: Learning Algorithms: Supervised, Unsupervised, Reinforcement Learning
Hafta 5: Training Single Layer NNs
Hafta 6: Training Multilayer NNs: Back Propagation, Empirical Risk Minimization
Hafta 7: Optimization Methods and Generalization
Hafta 8: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs): BP Through Time, LSTM
Hafta 9: Restricted Boltzmann Machines (RBMs): Contrastive Divergence
Hafta 10: Autoencoders: Denoising, Contractive, Stacked NNs
Hafta 11: Deep Learning: Deep Autoencoders, Deep Belief Networks
Hafta 12: Vision Models: Convolutional and Transformer Networks for Computer Vision
Hafta 13: Language Models: RNNs and Transformers for Natural Language Processing
Hafta 14: Generative Modeling: Adversarial Models, Variational Models, Diffusion Probabilistic Models
resources: Deep Learning, Ian Goodfellow and Yoshua Bengio and Aaron Courville, 2016, The MIT Press
Neural Networks and Learning Machines, Simon S. Haykin, 2009, Upper Saddle River: Pearson Education
Introduction to Artificial Neural Systems, J. M. Zurada, 1992., West Pub. Co

courses.name: Introduction to Data Science
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a broad understanding of data science fundamentals, techniques, and applications. It also seeks to develop hands-on experience through projects and computer assignments.
courses.description: Introduction to data science fundamentals, techniques and applications; data collection, preparation, storage and querying; parametric models for data; models and methods for fitting, analysis, evaluation, and validation; dimensionality reduction, visualization; various learning methods, classifiers, clustering, data and text mining; applications in diverse domains such as business, medicine, social networks, computer vision; breadth knowledge on topics and hands-on experience through projects and computer assignments.

courses.name: Modeling and Methods in Optimization
courses.purpose: This course aims to extend students' understanding of linear programming to broader optimization methodologies. It prepares students to model and solve discrete optimization problems using exact and heuristic methods.
courses.description: Extension of linear programming to different methodologies including network models, integer programming and dynamic programming. Discrete optimization: local search heuristics.
learning_outcomes: Recognize network structure in optimization problems and use/modify/develop algorithms to solve network flow problems
Construct good models for discrete optimization problems
Solve discrete optimization problems using exact algorithms such as branch-and-bound, cutting planes, branch-and-cut algorithms and dynamic programming
Develop heuristic algorithms for discrete optimization problems
Use a general purpose mixed integer programming solver
Design an experiment to compare models, report outcomes
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to integer optimization
Hafta 2: Formulations and modeling tricks
Hafta 3: Easily solved special cases: Network optimization revisited
Hafta 4: Shortest path and maximum flow problems, Algorithms
Hafta 5: Optimal trees, matchings and coverings
Hafta 6: Models requiring exponential number of constraints: TSP
Hafta 7: Branch and Bound Algorithms
Hafta 8: Branch and Bound continued
Hafta 9: Cutting Plane Algorithms
Hafta 10: Heuristics
Hafta 11: Dynamic Programming, Shortest paths revisited and other examples
Hafta 12: Dynamic Programming continued, Project planning and scheduling with CPM and PERT
Hafta 13: Project Scheduling
Hafta 14: Review
resources: Operations Research: Applications and Algorithms, W. Winston, 2004, Duxbury Press

courses.name: Introduction to Stochastic Processes
courses.description: Random variables, conditional expectation. Bernoulli and poisson processes. Discrete time Markov chains: classification of states, ergodic properties. Random walks, branching processes. Continuous time Markov processes. Kolmogorov differential equations. Birth and death processes, renewal process.

courses.name: Stochastic Models
courses.description: Markov chains. Basic queuing models and applications. Stochastic inventory models: periodic and continuous review. Introduction to stochastic maintenance models.
learning_outcomes: Understand randomness and the statistical relationships between random events
Understand value of information accumulated over time under uncertainty
Learn the properties of fundamental stochastic processes
Calculate probabilities and expectations related to stochastic processes
Model using stochastic processes and solve the inventory, reliability, maintenance, financial engineering problems under uncertainty.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Sample space, probability, random variables, indicator r.v.'s, expectations, discrete distributions
Hafta 2: Conditional distributions and expectations, random sums
Hafta 3: Markov chains, Markov property, n-step transition probabilities
Hafta 4: Markov chains: modelling examples
Hafta 5: First step analysis with examples
Hafta 6: Classification of the sates, long-run behavior of Markov chains, limiting probabilities
Hafta 7: Long-run behavior of Markov chains (cont'd)
Hafta 8: Poisson processes and related distributions
Hafta 9: Superposition and decomposition theorems
Hafta 10: Examples with Poisson processes, non-homogeneous Poisson processes
Hafta 11: Continuous-time Markov chains, transition rates and probabilities, sojourn times
Hafta 12: Continuous time Markov chains : Limiting probabilities
Hafta 13: Birth and death processes, introduction to queueing models
Hafta 14: Basic queueing models
resources: An Introduction to Stochastic Modeling, Mark Pinsky and Samuel Karlin, 2010/4th Edition, Academic Press

courses.name: Forecasting Methods and Applications
courses.description: Basic quantitative methods of forecasting, time series decomposition, regression methods, exponential smoothing, moving average (MA), autoregressive (AR) and autoregressive integrated moving average (ARIMA) models, brief introduction to autoregressive conditional heteroskedasticity (ARCH) and generalized ARCH (G-ARCH) models.

courses.name: Introduction to Nonlinear Optimization
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental ideas and methods of nonlinear optimization. It aims to build the theoretical background needed to analyze and solve continuous optimization problems, including unconstrained and constrained cases.
courses.description: Nonlinear optimization, optimality conditions for unconstrained optimization, line search, convex sets and functions, convex optimization, constrained optimization, Karush-Kuhn-Tucker conditions, duality.
learning_outcomes: To understand the optimality conditions for continuous optimization problems
To solve optimization problems using optimality conditions and existence results
To understand the duality theory for nonlinear optimization problems in particular for convex optimization problems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and motivation: Examples of nonlinear programming problems
Hafta 2: Mathematical preliminaries: Inner products and norms, eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Hafta 3: Optimality conditions for unconstrained optimization: global and local optima
Hafta 4: First and second order optimality conditions, quadratic functions
Hafta 5: Convex sets, definition and examples, operations with convex sets
Hafta 6: Convex functions, definition and examples, first order characterization
Hafta 7: Second order characterization of convex functions, operations preserving convexity
Hafta 8: Convex optimization: Definition and examples
Hafta 9: Weak duality, strong duality in the convex case, examples
Hafta 10: Optimality conditions: KKT conditions for linearly constrained problems
Hafta 11: KKT conditions for the convex case
Hafta 12: KKT conditions for inequality and equality constrained problems
Hafta 13: Solving problems using KKT conditions: Examples
Hafta 14: Review
resources: Introduction to Nonlinear Optimization: Theory, Algorithms and Applications with MATLAB, Amir Beck, 2014, Society fro Industrial and Applied Mathematics and the Mathematical Optimization Society

courses.name: Applied Data Analysis
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to modern applied statistical learning methods and their use in analyzing real-world data. It prepares students to model complex data, compare alternative approaches, and interpret results for decision-making in industrial and service systems.
courses.description: Introduction to exploratory data analysis, multivariate regression, semiparametric regression, scatterplot smoothing, linear mixed models, generalized linear models, recursive partitioning, and hidden Markov models through the applications on real data sets using the statistical software R. Applications to consumer choice models, modeling the number of emergency room visits, building e-mail spam filters, detecting fraudulent transactions, and other applications from manufacturing and service systems illustrating big data analytics.
learning_outcomes: Recognize the properties of fundamental statistical models
Explore features of a data sets through graphics and summary statistics
Fit a sensible statistical model to a given dataset
Take advantage of bias-variance trade-off and avoid over-fitting
Select the best model for a given dataset
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to statistical learning and R, overview of regression and classification problems

Hafta 2: Linear regression (Illustrations: effects of budgets allocated for TV, newspaper, radio advertisement on annual sales, prediction of credit card balance from income, limit, rating, age, number of cards, and education level)
Hafta 3: Linear regression continued and k-nearest neighbour regression (Illustration: conjoint analysis from marketing science; how can you design a new product with a higher market penetration?)
Hafta 4: Logistic regression (Illustration: loan default probability estimation from credit card balance, income, occupation)
Hafta 5: Multinomial and Poisson regressions (Illustration: would it have been possible to predict the Challenger diasaster? https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Shuttle_Challenger_disaster)
Hafta 6: Linear discriminant analysis (Illustrations: revisit credit card default probability estimation and Challenger disaster)

Hafta 7: Cross-validation, linear model selection, subset selection (Illustrations: what are the variables among income, limit, rating, age, number of cards, and education level that explain the credit card balance or default probability best? Is logistic regression or linear discriminant model best for predicting the loan default probability?)

Hafta 8: Shrinkage methods, ridge regression and lasso (What if the number of predictors is large--comparable to number of examples? Illustration: prediction of salaries of baseball players from various measures of their performances in the past games)

Hafta 9: Polynomial regression, regression splines, smoothing splines (Illustration: modeling the wage as a function of age, the amount pollutants in a residential area as a function of its distance from employment centers)
Hafta 10: Local regression, generalized additive models for quantitative and categorical variables (Illustrations: revisit wage and pollutant examples)
Hafta 11: Regression trees (Illustrations: predict the baseball player salaries, car-seat sales)
Hafta 12: Classification trees (Illustrations: email spam filtering--when is an email message spam? Predict crime rate in a residential area)
Hafta 13: Bagging, random forests, boosting (Illustrations: revisit baseball player salary email spam, crime-rate examples)
Hafta 14: Principal component analysis, k-means and hierarchical clustering (Illustrations: handwritten digit recognition, clustering cancer cell according to micro-array data, market-basket data)
resources: An Introduction to Statistical Learning, G. James, D. Witten, T. Hastie, R. Tibshirani, 2013, Springer

courses.name: Reinforcement Learning and Dynamic Programming
courses.description: Markov chains. Markov decision processes. Dynamic programming: policy iteration, value iteration. Model-free reinforcement learning: Monte Carlo, Temporal Difference, Q-learning. Policy gradient methods. Model-based reinforcement learning: classical multi-armed bandits, stochastic multi-armed bandits, adversarial multi-armed bandits.

courses.name: Computational Optimization
courses.description: Strong models and valid inequalities. Extended formulations. Cutting plane and column generation algorithms. Decomposition approaches in deterministic and stochastic optimization. Applications in production planning, network design and logistics.

courses.name: Complex Analysis
courses.description: Algebra of complex numbers, topology of the complex plane. Analytic functions, Cauchy-Riemann equations, power series. Complex line integrals, Cauchy theorem, Cauchy integral formula, Taylor series. Mean value property, Liouville theorem, isolated zeros, uniqueness theorem, maximum modulus principle. Isolated singularities, singularities at infinity, Laurent series. Residues, evaluation of improper integrals.

courses.name: Quantum Optimization
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental ideas of quantum computation and quantum mechanics needed to understand how quantum methods can be applied to optimization problems. It aims to equip students with the ability to model, analyze, and benchmark quantum optimization approaches using current tools and hardware constraints.
courses.description: Basics of quantum computation and fundamental principles of quantum mechanics. Introduction to gate-based and adiabatic models of computation. Ising and quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO) models. Algorithms for quantum optimization - quantum annealing, quantum approximate optimization algorithm (QAOA), Grover search, amplitude amplification. Essentials for benchmarking on quantum hardware with practical implementations using simulators available in IBM Qiskit, D-Wave Ocean SDK and OpenJij. Current quantum hardware and limitations, introduction to quantum error correction and logical qubits.
learning_outcomes: Explain the fundamental principles of quantum computation effectively applied to solving optimization problems.
Transform classical combinatorial optimization problems into quantum-compatible formulations, such as Ising and QUBO models.
Demonstrate a strong understanding of leading NISQ-era algorithms, specifically Quantum Annealing and QAOA.
Construct and execute quantum algorithms using industry-standard Python toolkits like Qiskit, Ocean SDK, and OpenJij.
Critically evaluate quantum and quantum-inspired solutions against classical methods, considering the constraints of current quantum hardware.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Classical optimization & quantum context
Hafta 2: Quantum mechanics essentials
Hafta 3: Models of quantum computation
Hafta 4: Quantum circuits and Qiskit
Hafta 5: Ising and QUBO models
Hafta 6: Adiabatic computing - quantum annealing
Hafta 7: Midterm Exam
Hafta 8: Variational quantum algorithms - VQE
Hafta 9: Variational quantum algorithms - QAOA
Hafta 10: Grover search & amplitude amplification
Hafta 11: Simple implementations with Qiskit, Ocean SDK and OpenJij
Hafta 12: Defining quantum advantage - metrics for benchmarking
Hafta 13: Current quantum hardware & limitations - annealing devices, NISQ devices
Hafta 14: Efforts towards fault-tolerant quantum computation - quantum error correction, logical qubits
resources: Recommended - Lecture Notes: Quantum Algorithms for Optimizers, Giacomo Nannicini, 2025
Recommended - Lecture Notes: Quantum Integer Programming, David E. Bernal, Sridhar Tayur and Davide Venturelli, 2020
Recommended - Textbook: Quantum Computation and Quantum Information, Michael A. Nielsen and Isaac L. Chuang, 2010, Cambridge University Press
Recommended - Textbook: Quantum Computing: A Gentle Introduction, Eleanor Rieffel and Wolfgang Polak, 2011, The MIT Press

courses.name: Advanced Calculus I
courses.description: The real number system, least upper bound property. Sequences in R, Cauchy sequences, limsup and liminf. Limit and continuity in R, uniform continuity. Differentiation in R. Riemann integral in R, fundamental theorem of calculus. Infinite series of numbers, absolute convergence. Sequences and series of functions, uniform convergence, power series.

courses.name: Network Flows
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental models and algorithms used to solve optimization problems on networks. It aims to help students formulate real-world problems as network flow problems and analyze efficient solution methods.
courses.description: Flow problems on networks. Transportation and assignment problems, special purpose algorithms and advanced computational techniques. Maximum flow problem, theory, algorithms, and applications. Shortest paths. Minimum cost flows. Network simplex method. Multicommodity flow problems. Generalized networks.
learning_outcomes: Model various problems as network flow problems
Conduct worst case complexity analysis
Learn about different data structures and their use in designing efficient algorithms
Learn about the state of the art network flow algorithms for shortest paths, maximum flows, minimum cost flows
Understand the combinatorial algorithms for matching and spanning tree problems
Compare the performances of polynomial algorithms against linear programming formulations of network flow problems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Network optimization, routing models, background, history and applications; Network and graph terminology, fundamentals, formulations; Data Structures for networks
Hafta 2: Algorithms and Complexity; Worst case complexity analysis; Polynomial versus exponential time complexity, practical implications
Hafta 3: Shortest Paths; Introduction, assumptions; Types of shortest path problems
Hafta 4: Shortest Paths; Reaching algorithms, Dijkstra, Label-correcting, Floyd-Warshall algorithms
Hafta 5: Maximum Flows; Max flow/ min cut theorem
Hafta 6: Maximum Flows; Flow augmenting, preflow-push algorithm
Hafta 7: Minimum Cost Flows; Cycle-canceling, successive shortest path and network simplex algorithms; the relationships, similarities and differences
Hafta 8: Minimum Cost Flows; Residual networks, reduced costs, complementary slackness, optimality conditions
Hafta 9: Assignment and Matching; Bipartite cardinality matching as maximum flows
Hafta 10: Assignment and Matching; Bipartite weighted matching as minimum cost flows
Hafta 11: Minimum Spanning Trees; Kruskal’s and Prim’s algorithms
Hafta 12: Multicommodity Flows; Applications
Hafta 13: Multicommodity Flows; Optimality Conditions
Hafta 14: Multicommodity Flows; Lagrangean Relaxation
resources: Network Flows: Theory, Algorithms and Applications, R.K. Ahuja, T.L. Magnanti and J.B. Orlin, 1993, Prentice-Hall

courses.name: Linear Algebra II
courses.description: Canonical forms, minimal polynomial. Dual spaces and adjoints. Inner product spaces. Orthonormal bases and Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization. Bilinear forms, quadratic forms and Sylvester's Law of Inertia. Symmetric, hermitian, orthogonal and unitary operators and their spectral theorems.
learning_outcomes: Calculate primary decomposition of a linear operator or a matrix.
Find a Jordan normal form of a matrix (over complex field).
Calculate orthogonal projection in an inner product space.
Find the best approximation to a vector.
Find Gaussian solution of an incompatible linear system.
Transform a normal operator into a diagonal form.
Find spectral decomposition of a Hermitian operator.
Transform a quadratic form into diagonal one.
Test a real bilinear form for positivity.
Find signature of a real quadratic form.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Summary of Math 223
Hafta 2: Characteristic Values, Annihilating Polynomials, Invariant Subspaces, Simultaneous Triangulation; Simultaneous Diagonalization
Hafta 3: Characteristic Values, Annihilating Polynomials, Invariant Subspaces, Simultaneous Triangulation; Simultaneous Diagonalization
Hafta 4: Simultaneous Diagonalization, Direct-Sum Decompositions, Invariant Direct Sums, The Primary Decomposition Theorem
Hafta 5: The Primary Decomposition Theorem, Cyclic Decompositions and the Rational Form
Hafta 6: The Primary Decomposition Theorem, Cyclic Decompositions and the Rational Form
Hafta 7: Computation of Invariant Factors, Summary; Semi-Simple Operators
Hafta 8: Forms on Inner Product Spaces, Positive Forms
Hafta 9: More on Forms, Spectral Theory, Further Properties of Normal Operators
Hafta 10: Symmetric Bilinear Forms, Skew-Symmetric Bilinear Forms, Groups Preserving Bilinear Forms
Hafta 11: Symmetric Bilinear Forms, Skew-Symmetric Bilinear Forms, Groups Preserving Bilinear Forms
Hafta 12: Further Properties of Normal Operators, Bilinear Forms, Symmetric Bilinear Forms
Hafta 13: Symmetric Bilinear Forms, Skew-Symmetric Bilinear Forms, Groups Preserving Bilinear Forms
Hafta 14: Groups Preserving Bilinear Forms
resources: Linear algebra, K. Hoffmann & R. Kruze, 1971, Prentice Hall

courses.name: Differential Equations
courses.description: First and second order differential equations, existence and uniqueness of solutions. Linear homogeneous differential equations. Linear nonhomogeneous equations: the methods of undetermined coefficients and variation of parameters, reduction of order. Series solution of second order equations. Systems of first order differential equations. Solution by Laplace transforms. Numerical solution techniques.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate basic skills in the treatment of ordinary differential equations (ODEs) and the understanding of how such equations are used in engineering and science
Apply ODEs in various situations and use correct mathematical terminology and notation
Demonstrate an understanding of the theory of ODEs
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Differential Equations. First order DEs. Linear DEs and integrating factors (Parts of Ch. 1 and Sec. 2.1)
Hafta 2: Separable DEs. Mathematical models. Applications of first order DEs (2.2, 2.3, 2.5)
Hafta 3: Exact DEs and integrating factors. Existence and uniqueness of solutions (2.6, 2.4)
Hafta 4: Homogeneous constant coefficient linear DEs. Wronskian. Linear independence (3.1, 3.2)
Hafta 5: Linear DEs with constant coefficients. Reduction of order (3.3, 3.4)
Hafta 6: Method of undetermined coefficients (3.5)
Hafta 7: Variation of parameters (3.6)
Hafta 8: Applications of second order DEs (3.7, 3.8)
Hafta 9: Higher order linear DEs (Ch. 4)
Hafta 10: The Laplace transform (6.1, 6.2)
Hafta 11: Laplace transform method for solving DEs (6.3-6.5)
Hafta 12: The convolution integral (6.6). Equations with non-constant coefficients. Euler equations (5.4)
Hafta 13: Power series (5.1). Series solutions of DEs (5.2-5.3)
Hafta 14: Series solutions of DEs (5.3-5.5)
resources: Elementary Differential Equations and Boundary Value Problems, W. E. Boyce, R. C. DiPrima, D. B. Meade, 2017, 11th global edition, Wiley

courses.name: Real Analysis I
courses.description: Metric spaces, sequences, completeness, Baire's theorem. Continuity. Completion of a metric space. Compactness and connectedness. Contraction mapping theorem and its applications. Product spaces.

courses.name: Introduction to Number Theory
courses.description: Divisibility, congruences, quadratic reciprocity, arithmetical functions, irrational numbers, simple continued fractions, Diophantine equations.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate proficiency of proof techniques and use them to give rigorous proofs of simple statements
Demonstrate knowledge of number theoretic algorithms to detect and solve a relevant numerical problem
Given a problem demonstrate knowledge by identifying facts that are relevant to its solution
Apply theoretical knowledge in practical settings such as applications to cryptography
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Chapter 1: Mathematical induction (and other proof techniques)
Hafta 2: Chapter 2. Divisibility theory
Hafta 3: Chapter 3: Primes and the fundamental theorem of arithmetic
Hafta 4: Chapter 4: Theory of congruences
Hafta 5: Chapter 5: Fermat's little theorem and Wilson's theorem
Hafta 6: Chapter 6: Number theoretic functions
Hafta 7: Chapter 6: Number theoretic functions
Hafta 8: Chapter 7: Euler's phi function and applications
Hafta 9: Chapter 8: Primitive roots and indices
Hafta 10: Chapter 9: Primitive roots and indices
Hafta 11: Chapter 9: Quadratic residues
Hafta 12: Chapter 9: The quadratic reciprocity law
Hafta 13: Chapter 13: Representation of integers as sums of squares
Hafta 14: Problem solving and review. Some special topics can be covered depending on audience interest
resources: Elementary Number Theory, David M. Burton, 2010/7th edition, ISBN 978-0073383149, McGraw-Hill Education
Elementary Number Theory, J. Strayer, 2001/1st edition, ISBN 978-1577662242, Waveland Press

courses.name: Real Analysis II
courses.description: Lebesgue outer measure, measurable sets. Lebesgue integral, convergence theorems. Functions of bounded variation, absolutely continuous functions. Lp spaces. General measure and integration theory. Radon-Nikodym theorem. Product measures, Fubini's theorem.

courses.name: Algebra II
courses.description: Symmetries of polynomial equations. Rings and modules. Quotient rings and polynomial rings. Field Extensions. Ruler-and-compass constructions. Euclidean domains, principal ideal domains, unique factorization domains. Finitely generated modules of principal ideal domains. Eisenstein's Criterion. Galois groups. The Fundamental Theorem of Galois Theory. The Unsolvability of the Quintic.

courses.name: Introduction to Statistics
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental ideas and methods of statistics needed to summarize data, make inferences from samples, and build basic statistical models. It prepares students to understand estimation, hypothesis testing, correlation, and regression analysis.
courses.description: Descriptive statistics, sampling and sampling distributions. Introduction to estimation theory, method of maximum likelihood and method of moments, interval estimation. Tests of hypotheses, two population problems, correlation. Simple linear regression analysis, nonlinear regression. Multiple regression analysis.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate the ability to use the statistical software MINITAB to do descriptive statistics and to verify Central Limit Theorem
Find estimators for unknown parameters of distribution using mathematical methods; such as the Method of Moments and the Method of Maximum Likelihood
Demonstrate the ability to do interval estimation for parameters of population, distribution and regression model
Demonstrate the ability to do statistical hypothesis testing with one or two samples
Demonstrate the ability to perform Chi-squared tests for categorical data
Demonstrate the ability to do linear regression analysis
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Descriptive Statistics.
Hafta 2: Statistics and Their Distributions.
Hafta 3: Some General Consepts of Point Estimation. Methods of Point Estimation.
Hafta 4: Statistical Intervals Based on a Single Sample.
Hafta 5: Tests of Hypotheses Based on a Single Sample
Hafta 6: Inferences Based on Two samples
Hafta 7: Inferences Based on Two samples (Cont. )
Hafta 8: Review Applications & MIDTERM
Hafta 9: Simple Linear Regression and Correlation.
Hafta 10: Simple Linear Regression and its Applications
Hafta 11: Nonlinear and Multiple Regression Analysis.
Hafta 12: Multiple Regression Analysis and its Applications
Hafta 13: Goodness-of-Fit Tests and Categorical Data Analysis.
Hafta 14: Goodness-of-Fit Tests and Categorical Data Analysis (Cont.)
resources: Modern Mathematical Statistics with Applications, Jay L. Devore and Kenneth N. Berk, Third Edition, Springer

courses.name: Introduction to Cryptography
courses.purpose: This course is designed as an introduction to public key cryptology. It focuses on the computational aspects of the subject and the implementation of core algorithms rather than rigorous proofs.
courses.description: Topics include Diffie-Hellman Key Exchange, Standard and Elliptic ElGamal Public Key Cryptosystem, RSA Public Key Cryptosystem, the Knapsack Cryptosystem, Digital Signatures, and Hash Functions. The course reviews the necessary mathematical background as needed.

courses.name: Topics in Quantum Information Theory
courses.purpose: This course introduces selected topics in quantum information theory and develops the conceptual and mathematical tools needed to understand quantum information processing.
courses.description: Selected topics in quantum information theory. Quantum operations, distance measures for quantum states and channels. Quantum error-correction: Shor code, Calderbank-Shor-Steane codes and stabilizer codes. Quantum entropy and its properties. Information-theoretic protocols: quantum data compression and noisy quantum channels. Quantum cryptography: quantum key distribution, BB84 protocol and its security proof.

courses.name: Mathematical Foundations of Data Science
courses.description: Review of eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Fundamental subspaces, matrix factorization techniques, principal components and best low-rank matrices; the structure of neural nets for deep learning. Convergence concepts and limit theorems in probability, basic inequalities of probability, tail bounds, the concentration of measures phenomena, empirical process. Maximum likelihood estimation, regularized regression, the Lasso and its variations. Optimization methods, gradient descent, stochastic gradient descent, convolutional neural nets.

courses.name: Introduction to Bioinformatics
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the use of computers and computational methods in molecular biology and genetics, preparing them to work with biological data and online resources.
courses.description: Computer use in molecular biology, access to online databases, sequence alignment, phylogenetics, RNA expression analysis, GWAS statistics, gene networks.

courses.name: Fundamentals of Molecular Genetics
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental molecular mechanisms underlying gene regulation, DNA repair, recombination, transposition, retroposition, and protein synthesis in prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems.
courses.description: Molecular biology of gene regulation, fundamentals of DNA repair and recombination, detailed analysis of transposition and retroposition in eukaryotes, molecular mechanisms of eukaryotic and prokaryotic protein synthesis.
learning_outcomes: Discriminate prokaryotic mechanisms
Define fundamental concepts of molecular genetics
Propose a biomedical tool and design
Criticize and evaluate peer presentations
Debate research findings related to course material
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course
Hafta 2: Replication Is Connected to the Cell Cycle (Chapter 9)
Hafta 3: The Replicon: Initiation of Replication (Chapter 10)
Hafta 4: DNA Replication (Chapter 11)
Hafta 5: Extrachromosomal Replicons (Chapter 12)
Hafta 6: Midterm I
Hafta 7: Homologous and Site-Specific Recombination (Chapter 13)
Hafta 8: Repair Systems (Chapter 14)
Hafta 9: Prokaryotic Transcription (Chapter 17)
Hafta 10: Midterm II
Hafta 11: Translation (Chapter 22)
Hafta 12: Using the Genetic Code (Chapter 23)
Hafta 13: The Operon (Chapter 24)
Hafta 14: Phage Strategies (Chapter 25)
resources: Genes XII, Krebs, Goldstein, Kilpatrick, 2018
Genetics Analysis & Principles, Robert J Brooker, 6th or newer
Molecular Cell Biology, Harvey Lodish Arnold Berk S Lawrence Zipursky Paul Matsudaira David Baltimore and James Darnell, 4th or newer
Gene Cloning and DNA Analysis: An Introduction, T. A. Brown, 8th

courses.name: Principles of Genetics
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental principles of genetics and how hereditary information is transmitted and altered. It aims to build a foundation for understanding classical and molecular genetics and their applications in biology.
courses.description: Transmission genetics, gene and chromosomal mutations, linkage and mapping, molecular genetic applications, population and quantitative genetics, molecular evolution, model organism genomics and bioinformatics.

courses.name: Introduction to Computational Biology
courses.purpose: This course introduces computational biology methods and their applications in analyzing biological data.
courses.description: Introduction to computational Biology: Methods and exercises in phylogenetics, microarray data analysis, systems biology.

courses.name: Flexible Robotics
courses.purpose: This course introduces the principles and methods used in the design and analysis of flexible robotic mechanisms. It aims to connect mechanics, biomechanics, and bioinspiration with robot design, modeling, and validation.
courses.description: Design and manufacturing of flexible mechanisms. Biomechanics and bioinspiration. Kinematics and dynamics of robots and mechanisms. Mechanics and the effects of compliance on robot dynamics. Design optimization and system identification for flexible robots. Experimental validation of flexible robots.

courses.name: Mobile Robots
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts of mobile robotics, including locomotion, kinematics, maneuverability, holonomicity, motion control, perception, localization, path planning, and obstacle avoidance.
courses.description: An introductory course on mobile robotics fundamentals. Legged and wheel locomotion basics. Kinematic models, mobile robot maneuverability, holonomicity, and motion control of mobile robots. Perception in mobile robots. Localization of mobile robots. Path planning and obstacle avoidance strategies.

courses.name: Fundamentals of Design for Reliability
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts and methods of reliability engineering for mechanical and related systems. It aims to develop an understanding of how reliability is defined, analyzed, tested, and improved throughout the design and production phases.
courses.description: Comprehensive definition of reliability. Introduction to reliability engineering, its basic definitions, applications and benefits. Confidence intervals and levels. Concept of target reliability. Case histories from aerospace, military, process control and power systems applications. Basic statistical and analytical functions and distribution types used by reliability engineers. Reliability analysis during the design phase. Qualification tests for components and assemblies. Interfaces of reliability, maintainability and risk engineering. Design guidelines for reliability, maintainability and quality. Statistical reliability testing. Reliability during the production phase. Standards about aerospace, defense and nuclear engineering. System reviews and related checklists. Construction of fault trees and fault tree analysis. Design of reliability testing. Failure causes and types. Effects of environmental conditions, age, mission time and stress. Mission profile and requirements. Failure modes, effects and criticality analysis and two different methods supported by examples. Ageing testing methods. Role of reliability departments in engineering organizations and systems projects.
